# Parts Hub — Comprehensive Project Knowledge Base

> **Purpose**: Complete extraction of every detail from every source document for the Parts Hub project. This document is designed to be uploaded at the start of a new Claude conversation to restore full project context. Nothing is summarized away — all granular details preserved verbatim where possible.
>
> **Source documents**: Proposal v1.3 (60-page PDF + DOCX), SRS v1.3 (265-page PDF + 40k-char DOCX), Business Requirements Analysis DOCX (59 tables, 549 reqs), RTM (5-sheet Excel), User Stories Compiled (132 stories with full AC), Vehicle & Parts Hierarchy doc (20 tables), Garage Onboarding Master Structure (14 tables), Supplier Onboarding Master Structure (17 tables), Garage Onboarding Classification, Mobile App proposal v1.1 (48k chars), Mobile App Feature Strategy (14.7k chars), Automation Phase-1 doc, Final Out of Scope, Web vs Mobile Feature matrix (3 sheets), Onboarding Verification Checklist, Venue Master Concept, Dashboard spec, Reports spec, Subscription Features matrix, Use Case Analysis, Insurance/Garage/Supplier Flow design docs, Design Questionnaire, JIRA export (768 issues, 51 epics), 28 MoMs (Nov 2025–Feb 2026), 60 transcripts (Jan-Apr 2026), 9 latest May 2026 transcripts, Pre-Project Zoom meeting & requirements discovery transcript, 15 Priya Email PDFs (OCR'd), Brand Guidelines PDF, Links doc, Licensing Authorities xlsx, Payment Milestones xlsx, Resource Planning xlsx, PBS with Time Estimation xlsx, Development Plan xlsx (1269 API-level tasks), Partshub Estimation xlsx, HTML Estimation xlsx, Wireframe & UI/UX Estimation xlsx, High Level Project Plans (2 docs), Parts Hub Development Schedule (3 MPP files), DOC-20230928 (early architecture overview).
>
> **Last updated**: 2026-05-14
>
> **Owner**: Navsoft team (Soumya Roy)
>
> **Total source extraction**: 72 sections, ~5+ MB of structured markdown covering every word from every source document INCLUDING substantive content from all 60+ meeting transcripts, all email correspondence, infrastructure details, estimation breakdowns, and project planning artifacts.

## 3. Proposal — Executive Summary

> Verbatim from proposal pages 7-10:

The Dubai based client 'Parts Hub' wants to build a platform that would act as a broker between insurance companies, garages, and suppliers. The platform would be powered by AI and machine learning, and would allow insurance companies to request quotations for spare parts and select which type of part they want. The client also wants to create a repository of all the parts of different vehicles and acting as a marketplace for the auto industry.

Navsoft will build a scalable and integrated broker platform which will be used by the insurance companies, garages, and suppliers. This AI powered broker platform will be used by the insurance companies for evaluating the quotes by garages when a customer files a claim against his/her broken vehicle.

Navsoft has suggested Python or .NET core as a technology stack for developing the proposed broker platform and the Compiling language would be helpful for implementing deep learning using the TensorFlow and data models.

This BRD translates the findings during the requirement gathering session with the PM from client's side. It details how this platform will execute the operation of Insurance companies, garages & suppliers based on several use cases from onboarding, claim requests, RFQ Generation, Quote Selection, Order Generation, Invoicing and Payment capture. By implementing AI DRIVEN BROKER PLATFORM, Parts Hub aims to cut off the extra cost getting paid by the Insurance companies, streamline operations, data accuracy, enforce consistent workflows, and gain real-time visibility across Insurance company, Garages & Suppliers, ultimately cutting off the offline broker services so that insurance company can reduce their claim settlement by 30%.

---

## 4. Proposal — Functional Scope

### A. INSURANCE COMPANY

**1. Onboarding Process**
- **Registration** — Insurance company registers with: First Name, Last Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, Password
- **Login** — Email + Password; options: Remember Me, Forgot Password
- **Additional Details** — Users add personal, company, licenses & registration, bank details
- **Account Approval Process** — Admin approves/rejects based on document verification

**2. Dashboard**
- Role-based access (subscription plan defines visible stats)
- Latest Claim Requests: Customer Name, Date & Time of Claim, Policy Number, Vehicle Make/Model, Status
- Active Claims, Pending Quotes, Completed Claims
- Notifications panel
- Quick actions (CTAs)

**3. Claim Management**
- Create new claim request
- Upload damage photos / videos
- Vehicle details (plate number, make, model, year, VIN)
- Customer details
- Accident description
- Send RFQ to garages
- Track claim status
- View garage quotations
- Approve/reject quotations
- Send RFQ to suppliers
- Generate orders

**4. Order Management**
- View garage orders
- View supplier orders
- Track order status (Placed, Shipping, Delivered, Completed)
- Replacement / refund requests
- Order history

**5. Garage Management** (HQ only)
- View network garages
- Invite new garages (email)
- Manage favorite garages
- View garage performance

**6. Branch Management** (HQ only)
- View branches (read-only metadata)
- Edit Branch Address, Manager Name, Contact Info
- Cannot create/delete branches (branches self-onboard)

**7. Inventory** — Parts & Vehicles (auto-populated from closed claims)

**8. Auction** — Create only (cannot participate)

**9. Hot Deals** — Create only (cannot participate)

**10. Subscription** — Manage Insurance subscription

**11. Reports** — Operational reports

**12. My Account / Profile**

### B. GARAGE FEATURES

- Onboarding with cascading hierarchy: Vehicle Type → Origin → Brand Segment → Brand + Specialties
- Dashboard
- Receive Claim RFQs
- Accept/Reject RFQs
- Inspect vehicle
- Submit Quotation (labor cost, parts cost, taxes, estimated repair time mandatory)
- Track quotation status
- Job execution & repair tracking
- Purchase parts (from suppliers/other garages)
- Insurance Partners view
- Auction — create AND participate
- Hot Deals — create AND participate
- Inventory (parts & vehicles)
- Subscription
- My Account / Profile

### C. SUPPLIER FEATURES

- Onboarding (Type gates inventory condition: new-only vs refurbished)
- Receive RFQs from Insurance after garage quote approval
- Quote builder per part (price, qty, delivery time, taxes, remarks)
- Order management & fulfilment
- Inventory (with CSV import/export added in May)
- Auction — create AND participate
- Hot Deals — purchase
- Subscription
- My Account / Profile

### D. PLATFORM ADMINISTRATOR FEATURES

- Manage Insurance Companies (approve/reject/suspend)
- Manage Garages
- Manage Suppliers
- Manage Claim Requests (governance/dispute)
- Manage Subscription Plans
- Manage User Subscriptions
- Manage Feature Groups
- Manage Review & Rating
- Manage Contact Inquiry & Tasks
- Manage Auction
- Manage Hot Deals
- Manage Admin Users, Roles, Groups
- Manage Master Attributes (vehicle hierarchy, parts catalog, damage categories)
- Manage Orders (centrally)
- Manage Payments
- Platform Admin Onboarding

## 5. Proposal — AI/ML Phased Roadmap

The proposal defines a 5-phase AI/Data Science roadmap. Phase 1 is included in the USD 86,500 cost; Phases 2-5 are future:

### Phase 1: Solving the Manual Evaluation Problem

**Step 1: Computer Vision for Damage Assessment**
- Requirement: Replace physical garage visits with photo-based evaluation
- Develop Damage Detection CNN Model
  - Train on vehicle damage images (front, side, rear, interior damage)
  - Classify damage severity: Minor, Moderate, Severe, Total Loss
  - Identify damage types: Collision, Scratch, Dent, Glass damage, Structural
- Multi-angle Analysis System
  - Process multiple photos from different angles
  - Create 3D damage mapping for comprehensive assessment
  - Validate consistency across multiple images
- Confidence Scoring Algorithm
  - Assign confidence levels to AI assessments
  - Flag low-confidence cases for human review
  - Reduce false positives/negatives
- **Expected Outcome**: Eliminate need for physical garage gatherings

**Step 2: Automated Parts Identification**
- Vehicle Parts Recognition Model: Object detection to identify specific car components (bumper, headlight, door, etc.); cross-reference with VIN/make/model databases; generate OEM part numbers automatically
- Damage-to-Parts Mapping Algorithm: ML model linking damage locations to affected parts; consider cascade effects; predict hidden damage from visible patterns
- Parts Categorization System: Classify as Body / Mechanical / Electrical / Interior; determine repair vs replacement; estimate labor complexity

### Phase 2: Cost Optimization (future)
- ML-based price prediction
- Supplier scoring
- Anomaly detection for inflated claims

### Phase 3: Time Efficiency (future)
- Automated workflow routing
- SLA prediction
- Bottleneck identification

### Phase 4: Parts Sourcing Challenge (future)
- Smart supplier matching
- Inventory optimization
- Lead time prediction

### Phase 5: Continuous Learning System (future)
- Feedback loops
- Model retraining
- A/B testing framework

### Implementation Roadmap (per proposal)
- 5-6 month estimated development time (Phase 1 only)

- **Backend**: Python (FastAPI/Django) or .NET Core
- **ML/AI**: TensorFlow
- **Frontend**: HTML UI (Angular 17+ in practice per build)
- **Architecture**: Microservices, modular APIs
- **Cloud**: Cloud-first (AWS EKS / Azure)

  2. Receiving acceptance / sign-off, OR
  3. Expiry of a calendar month from delivery without further communication, OR
  4. Project deliverables transferred / uploaded onto client's designated server

DEVELOPMENT → UAT → DEVOPS → DATA MIGRATION → GO LIVE

(Navsoft scope = Development only. UAT, Data Migration, DevOps, Go-Live not in Time Estimation.)

## 8. Proposal — Roles & Responsibilities

### Phase I — Inception
| Activity | Client Responsibility | Navsoft Responsibility | Deliverables |

| Approve Proposal | Agree to project scope | — | Approved proposal |

### Phase II — Elaboration
| Activity | Client | Navsoft | Deliverables |

| Analyze Requirements + HTML UI | Provide queries & feedback; Knowledge transfer on business logic; Approve & baseline | Study & analyze; Resolution for queries; Baseline scope doc + prototype | Approved Artifacts |
| Approved Prototypes (GUI) | Review & feedback | Include feedback & release updated versions | SRS, SDD Documents |
| Wireframes, Clickable Prototypes | Resolution of intermediate queries | Intermediate website progress demo with basic features | — |

### Phase III — Development
| Activity | Client | Navsoft | Deliverables |
|---|---|---|---|
| Approve Functional Modules | Resolution of intermediate queries | Design changes / Code construction / QA (unit + integration) | Source Codes, DB Schema, Flow Diagrams, UI/UX Files, CSS, JavaScripts, Page Contents |
| Approve Functional Flow | Carry out review based on intermediate delivery | Fixing of defects detected in Acceptance Testing | — |

### Phase IV — Transition, UAT
| Activity | Client | Navsoft | Deliverables |
|---|---|---|---|
| Handover All Source Files & Binaries | Final acceptance testing; Provide acceptance on final deliverable | Provide support to Client for fixing defects during acceptance | Reviewed and tested source files, support files, training materials |

### Project Governance (per SRS)
- **Project Sponsor (Client)**: Strategic direction, business priorities, approve budget, approve major changes
- **Project Steering Committee**: Resolve escalated conflicts, monthly governance meetings, review progress/risks/milestones
- **Client Project Manager**: Day-to-day client coordinator, internal resource availability, client-side activities/approvals
- **Navsoft Project Manager** (Implementation Partner): Overall delivery responsibility, manage Navsoft team, coordinate project plan / tasks / timelines / dependencies
- **Navsoft BA**: BRD & FSD delivered with requirement gathering; coordinate with Navsoft PM based on business needs
- **Solution Architect (Navsoft)**: Own solution design, module configuration, Wireframe, Flowchart; review business reqs and align with functional specs
- **Technical Leads (Navsoft)**: Configure modules, develop functionalities, manage technical testing and deployment
- **End-User Representatives (Client)**: Participate in process design reviews, feedback during UAT, early system champions

### Communication Plan (per SRS)
| Communication | Audience | Frequency | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| Project Kick-off Meeting | All Stakeholders | Once at start | Align expectations, scope, deliverables, timelines |
| Weekly Status Meetings | Core Project Team | Weekly | Review tasks completed, issues, blockers, upcoming activities |
| Steering Committee Review | Steering Committee | Monthly | Review overall project health, milestone achievement, risk management |
| Functional Demo | Functional Leads | As per schedule | Deep-dive requirement discussions, solution validations |
| UAT Planning & Execution | Functional Users | Per UAT cycle | User testing, issue logging, feedback capture, defect resolution |
| Change Request Management | Project Managers | As Needed | Manage scope changes, evaluate impact on timelines and cost |
| Training Sessions | End Users | Per training schedule | End-user enablement, process walkthroughs, system navigation |
| Go-Live Readiness Reviews | Core Team | Pre-Go-Live | Review readiness checklist and cut-over plan |

### Change Control Process
- All scope, schedule, or budget deviations follow formal Change Control Process
- Change Requests (CR) documented, evaluated for impact, approved by Project Steering Committee before execution
- Change Control Register maintained and reviewed periodically
- Documentation & Knowledge Management: Google Drive shared repo with version control; standard templates for Meeting Minutes, Risk Registers, Change Requests, Test Cases and Results

### SRS Section 1 — Executive Summary (verbatim):

The Dubai based client 'Parts Hub' wants to build a platform that would act as a broker between insurance companies, garages, and suppliers. The platform would be powered by AI and machine learning, and would allow insurance companies to request quotations for spare parts and select which type of part they want. The client also wants to create a repository of all the parts of different vehicles and acting as a marketplace for the auto industry.

Navsoft will build a scalable and integrated broker platform which will be used by the insurance companies, garages, and suppliers. This AI powered broker platform will be used by the insurance companies for evaluating the quotes by garages when a customer files a claim against his/her broken vehicle. Navsoft has suggested Python or .NET core as a technology stack for developing the proposed broker platform and the Compiling language would be helpful for implementing deep learning using the TensorFlow and data models.

This BRD translates those findings during the requirement gathering session with the PM from client's side. It details how this platform will execute the operation of Insurance companies, garages & suppliers based on several use cases from onboarding, claim requests, RFQ Generation, Quote Selection, Order Generation, Invoicing and Payment capture. The document is structured to guide internal stakeholders, the client's management, and the Navsoft implementation team through the project's goals, scope, detailed requirements by department, technical needs, and a recommended implementation roadmap. By implementing AI DRIVEN BROKER PLATFORM, Parts Hub aims to cut off the extra cost getting paid by the Insurance companies, streamline operations, data accuracy, enforce consistent workflows, and gain real-time visibility across Insurance company, Garages & Suppliers, ultimately cutting off the offline broker services so that insurance company can reduce their claim settlement by 30%.

---

## 11. SRS — Business Goals & Objectives

### 11.1 Purpose
Ensure all stakeholders aligned on business goals, functional & non-functional requirements, objectives for AI DRIVEN BROKER PLATFORM implementation.

**Key focus areas**:
- Defining success criteria
- AI DRIVEN BROKER PLATFORM strategy
- Business Requirements based on current scope
- Use Cases and Flow Chart

### 11.2 Business Objectives

Primary objective: Create a centralized, intelligent, and automated ecosystem that connects insurance companies, garages, and spare parts suppliers within the automotive industry. The platform will act as a digital intermediary, eliminating dependency on offline brokers and enabling real-time, transparent, and data-driven collaboration among stakeholders.

Through AI and machine learning, the system will optimize the quotation and procurement process for vehicle spare parts during insurance claim settlements. It will enable insurance companies to efficiently manage claim requests, RFQ generation, quotation evaluation, order confirmations, and payment settlements with full traceability and reduced manual intervention.

**Business goals**:
- **Reduce insurance claim settlement costs by up to 30%** by removing third-party broker commissions and inefficiencies
- Enhance operational efficiency by automating RFQ and quotation workflows across multiple stakeholders
- Provide data accuracy and transparency through centralized records of parts, quotations, and transactions
- Build a scalable parts repository and marketplace that supports multiple vehicle brands and models
- Leverage AI-driven analytics to recommend optimal suppliers, finalize product list, and identify fraudulent claims
- Enable real-time visibility and decision-making for all stakeholders via dashboards and performance KPIs
- Ultimately, position Parts Hub as a digital backbone for the UAE auto-claims ecosystem

### 11.3 Business Goals

- Eliminate manual and offline brokerage processes, reducing turnaround time and dependency on intermediaries
- Optimize claim settlement efficiency by enabling insurance companies to instantly receive, compare, and approve quotations from verified garages and suppliers
- Reduce claim settlement costs by up to 30%, ensuring financial efficiency and transparency for insurers
- Centralize vehicle parts data to build a structured, searchable repository that supports future marketplace functionality
- Leverage AI and machine learning for predictive analytics, intelligent quote evaluation, and fraud detection
- Ensure data-driven decision-making through real-time dashboards and KPI monitoring
- Provide scalability for seamless integration with external systems and expansion across new markets
- Position Parts Hub as the leading digital broker and marketplace for the UAE auto-claims industry

---

## 12. SRS — Success Criteria & Strategy

### 12.1 Strategic Considerations

**Alignment with Business Vision**:
- Establish Parts Hub as leading digital intermediary between insurance, garages, suppliers across GCC region
- Support client's vision of reducing insurance claim costs and improving transparency, turnaround time, service quality
- Create sustainable, AI-powered marketplace that can evolve into revenue-generating platform beyond insurance claims

**Technology Strategy**:
- Scalable architecture on Python or .NET Core
- Integrate TensorFlow-based models for ML tasks (price prediction, fraud detection, quote evaluation)
- Microservices + modular APIs for easy integration with external insurance/garage/supplier systems
- Cloud-first deployment (Azure/AWS) for high availability, security, scalability

**Data & AI Strategy**:
- Centralized repository of vehicle parts enriched with metadata (brand, model, compatibility, pricing)
- ML algorithms for:
  - Intelligent quote comparison and recommendation
  - Predictive pricing trends and supplier scoring
  - Anomaly detection to flag inflated or fraudulent claims
- Data analytics and dashboards for insurance companies (performance, cost savings, claim turnaround)

**Operational Strategy**:
- Role-based user access structure for Insurance, Garage, Supplier
- Standardize workflow: RFQ → quotation → order → invoicing → payment
- Automated notifications and approvals
- Multi-lingual and region-compliant design for UAE diverse market

**Partnership & Ecosystem Strategy**:
- Onboard key garages and suppliers early
- Strategic alliances with major insurance companies for adoption
- API-based partnerships with external claim management / ERP systems for long-term scalability

**Risk & Compliance Strategy**:
- Comply with UAE data privacy laws (PDPL) and international standards like GDPR
- Secure authentication and encryption protocols
- Audit trails and activity logs for all financial and transactional activities

### 12.2 Defining Success for the Project

**Operational Success**:
- End-to-end automation of RFQ → Quotation → Order → Invoice → Payment cycle with minimal manual intervention
- **Reduction in claim processing time by at least 40–60%** vs current offline process
- Complete removal of offline brokers — all communication through platform
- Real-time visibility of quotations, orders, supplier performance, claim status

**Financial Success**:
- **Reduction in insurance claim settlement cost up to 30%**
- Cost savings via accurate, data-driven supplier selection
- Scalable revenue model (transaction fees, subscription, commissions, marketplace income)

**User Adoption & Experience**:
- Successful onboarding of: all participating insurance companies, selected garages, verified suppliers
- **75%+ user adoption within first three months after go-live**
- **Positive user satisfaction scores (4/5 or above)** for usability, speed, transparency
- Reduction in errors, disputes, communication delays

**Technology & Platform Success**:
- **>99% uptime** scalable, secure, high-availability platform
- Successful integration of AI/ML models: Quote evaluation, Price prediction, Fraud detection, Supplier scoring
- Comprehensive parts repository covering all major UAE vehicle brands/models
- Support large transaction volumes without performance issues

**Compliance & Data Integrity**:
- Complete adherence to UAE PDPL, international security standards, insurance industry compliance
- Audit trails, activity logs, encryption ensuring secure data handling
- **Zero critical security vulnerabilities** at production launch

**Strategic & Long-Term Success**:
- Establish Parts Hub as recognized digital broker in UAE automotive ecosystem
- Future expansion into full-scale spare parts marketplace with predictive analytics
- Foundation supporting future AI advancements, global scaling, enterprise integrations

### 12.3 Current Systems
Navsoft is implementing PartsHub's current process — building the business and system from scratch (no legacy system to replace).

### 12.4 Process Optimization and Best Practices

**Workflow Standardization**:
- Define and enforce standard workflows for RFQ creation, quotation submission, quote comparison, order generation, invoicing, payment processing
- System-driven validations, mandatory fields, controlled user actions to minimize errors
- Role-based access control (RBAC)

**Automation & AI Enablement**:
- AI/ML algorithms automate repetitive tasks: quotation matching, anomaly detection, supplier recommendations
- Auto-calculation rules, pricing algorithms, part compatibility checks, real-time status updates
- Smart notifications and reminders

**Data Accuracy & Integrity**:
- Centralized, structured parts repository with standardized naming conventions, categorization, OEM vs non-OEM classifications, pricing ranges
- Clean, version-controlled master data
- Data validation rules for uploaded parts, documents, quotations

**Performance Monitoring & Continuous Improvement**:
- KPIs and dashboards: quotation turnaround times, supplier performance, claim cycle time, pricing trends, cost savings
- ML insights to identify bottlenecks
- Periodic platform audits, user feedback reviews, workflow enhancements

**Communication & Transparency**:
- Centralized communication through in-platform messaging, notifications, activity logs
- Full audit trails for every action (RFQs, quotes, selections, orders)
- Reduce reliance on manual channels (email, WhatsApp, phone)

**Compliance & Security**:
- UAE PDPL-aligned data privacy and protection (encryption, secure authentication, protected document storage)
- Insurance industry standards through logged approvals, timestamped actions, secured financial workflows
- Fraud detection AI models for inflated pricing, duplicate submissions, anomalous claims

**Platform Scalability & Maintainability**:
- Modular microservices architecture
- API-first development for future ERP/marketplace integrations
- CI/CD, version control, automated testing

**User-Centric Approach**:
- UX-first design for all stakeholders regardless of technical background
- Guided forms, tooltips, intuitive navigation
- Training materials, onboarding guides, customer support tools

---

## 13. SRS — 11 Modules Assessment

The SRS Section 4 evaluates each functional module for alignment with business objectives, user expectations, and technical feasibility:

### Module 1: User Management & Onboarding Module
- Supports onboarding, authentication, profile management for Insurance Companies, Garages, Suppliers, Platform Administrators
- Role-based registration, login, approval workflows
- Document verification and admin approval where required
- Profile updates follow verification rules
- Depends on stable identity and access management controls
- **Risk identified**: Incomplete or incorrect user-submitted data may delay approvals

### Module 2: Subscription Management Module
- Governs feature access, billing cycles, plan management across all user roles
- Plan creation, assignment, upgrades, downgrades, history tracking
- Feature access based on active subscription plans
- Integrates directly with payment gateway for subscription payments
- **Wallet management is excluded by design**
- **Dependency**: Accurate subscription-to-feature mapping is critical

### Module 3: Claim Management Module
- Handles end-to-end lifecycle of insurance claim requests
- Claim creation, assignment, acceptance, rejection, closure
- Enables garages to submit quotations with parts and labor costs
- Allows admins to monitor all claims across stakeholders
- Integrated with AI-based damage assessment for decision support
- **Risk**: Low-quality image uploads may impact AI accuracy

### Module 4: Inventory & Master Data Management Module
- Centralizes all items, categories, vehicle masters
- Admin-only control over master items and categories
- Inventory management for insurance and suppliers
- Prevents unauthorized creation of master data
- Approval-based item requests from other users
- **Risk**: Delays in master item creation may impact downstream workflows

### Module 5: Orders Management Module
- Manages orders between insurance, garages, suppliers
- Order creation, tracking, replacements, refunds
- Complete order history and status
- Integrated with payment gateway
- Reporting and exports for reconciliation
- **Dependency**: Payment confirmation accuracy is essential

### Module 6: Payments & Refunds Module
- All financial transactions via payment gateway integration
- Payments for orders, subscriptions, auctions, deposits
- Refunds directly through payment gateway
- Audit logs and transaction history
- **Does not support wallet or internal balance features**
- **Risk**: Gateway downtime may delay payment confirmation

### Module 7: Auction Module
- Auction creation and participation
- Insurance users create auctions, manage items
- Garages and suppliers participate after deposit payment
- Bid tracking and order generation
- Admin oversight ensures policy compliance
- **Dependency**: Real-time bid updates are critical

### Module 8: Hot Deals Module
- Time-bound deals for inventory liquidation
- Creation, management, purchase of hot deal items
- Search, filters, order tracking
- Integrates with payment and order modules
- **Time-based validations are critical to prevent misuse**

### Module 9: Review & Rating Module
- Feedback between users
- Submit ratings and reviews post-transaction
- Admin can moderate, edit, delete inappropriate content
- Reviews displayed contextually against orders or services
- **Risk**: Manual moderation may be required for disputes

### Module 10: Admin Management & Configuration Module
- Full administrative control
- Manages users, roles, groups, masters, subscriptions, reports
- Search, filters, exports, audit logs
- Platform configuration and access rules
- **Central dependency for governance and compliance**

### Module 11: Reporting & Analytics Module
- Operational visibility
- Reports across claims, orders, payments, subscriptions
- Standard format exports
- Role-based report access
- Relies on data consistency across modules

### Module Assessment Summary
All modules are logically structured, role-aligned, integrated for end-to-end business workflows. Design emphasizes scalability, security, controlled data governance. Key risks: data quality, dependency sequencing, third-party payment gateway availability — addressed through validations, approvals, audit mechanisms.

### Future Considerations
Phased roadmap allowing incremental enhancement without disrupting existing functionality. Phase 1 = core operational stability and transactional workflows. Phase 2 = AI-driven automation and advanced intelligence.

---

## 14. SRS — Project Governance & Communication

### Project Governance Structure

| Role | Responsibilities |
|---|---|
| **Project Sponsor (Client)** | Strategic direction, business priorities. Approve project budget and major changes. Resolve escalated conflicts. |
| **Project Steering Committee** | Conduct monthly governance meetings. Review project progress, risks, milestones. Make critical decisions impacting project direction. |
| **Client Project Manager** | Serve as day-to-day client coordinator. Facilitate internal resource availability. Manage client-side activities and approvals. |
| **Navsoft Project Manager** (Implementation Partner) | Overall delivery responsibility. Manage Navsoft implementation team. Coordinate project plan, tasks, timelines, dependencies. |
| **Navsoft BA** | BRD & FSD delivered along with Requirement Gathering. Coordinate with Navsoft PM based on business needs. |
| **Solution Architect (Navsoft)** | Own solution design, module configuration, Wireframe, Flowchart. Review business requirements and align with functional specifications. |
| **Technical Leads (Navsoft)** | Configure modules. Develop functionalities. Manage technical testing and deployment activities. |
| **End-User Representatives (Client)** | Participate in process design reviews. Provide feedback during UAT. Become early system champions. |

### Communication Plan

| Communication Type | Audience | Frequency | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| Project Kick-off Meeting | All Stakeholders | Once (Project Start) | Align expectations, scope, deliverables, timelines |
| Weekly Status Meetings | Core Project Team | Weekly | Review tasks completed, issues, blockers, upcoming activities |
| Steering Committee Review | Steering Committee | Monthly | Review overall project health, milestone achievement, risk management |
| Functional Demo | Functional Leads | As per schedule | Deep-dive requirement discussions, solution validations |
| UAT Planning & Execution | Functional Users | Per UAT cycle | User testing, issue logging, feedback capture, defect resolution |
| Change Request Management | Project Managers | As Needed | Manage scope changes, evaluate impact on timelines and cost |
| Training Sessions | End Users | Per training schedule | End-user enablement, process walkthroughs, system navigation |
| Go-Live Readiness Reviews | Core Team | Pre-Go-Live | Review readiness checklist and cut-over plan |

### Change Control Process
- All scope, schedule, or budget deviations follow formal Change Control Process
- Change Requests (CR) documented, evaluated for impact, approved by Project Steering Committee before execution
- Change Control Register maintained and reviewed periodically

### Documentation & Knowledge Management
- All key project documentation maintained in secure shared repository (e.g., Google Drive)
- Document versions, approvals, historical revisions controlled
- Standard templates: Meeting Minutes, Risk Registers, Change Requests, Test Cases and Results

### 15.3 Insurance Requirements (Section 5.1, 115 items)

#### 15.3.1 Onboarding & Registration Requirements (ONB-001 to ONB-014)

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ONB-001 | Registration Flow | Insurance companies can register either through sales team invitation or self-registration via website link. | High |
| ONB-002 | Registration Validation | Registered users cannot access system until admin approves their profile. | High |
| ONB-003 | Mandatory Information | Personal details required: Name, Email, Contact Number. | High |
| ONB-004 | Company Details | Company details required: Company Name, Speciality, Trade License Number, License Copy, Licensing Authority and to mention if they are head quarter or branch. | High |
| ONB-005 | Licensing Authority | Licensing Authority should be a dropdown; list to be provided by client. | Medium |
| ONB-006 | Required Documents | Trade License copy and VAT Certificate must be uploaded. | High |
| ONB-007 | Branch Handling | Each branch registers separately because each branch has its own trade license. | High |
| ONB-008 | Parent–Child Mapping | Branch should be able to select Parent Company during registration if not available then they can update it later. | High |
| ONB-009 | Admin Verification | Admin manually verifies license through licensing authority website. | Medium |
| ONB-010 | Notification to Admin | On new registration, admin receives web and email notification for approval. | High |
| ONB-011 | Rejection Handling | Admin provides rejection reason; insurer can edit details and resubmit. | High |
| ONB-012 | Notification to Insurance Company | Insurance company receives rejection notification via email and web in-app notifications. | High |
| ONB-013 | Admin Platform | Admin will use only web-based portal; responsive UI required. | High |
| ONB-014 | Post-Approval Access | Once approved, insurer gains access to all relevant modules. | Medium |

#### 15.3.2 Dashboard (ID-001) — Composite requirement covering 5 sub-sections:

1. **Top KPI Summary Cards (Always Visible — Subscription Based)**

| KPI | What it shows | Why needed |
|---|---|---|
| Total Active Claims | Claims currently in progress | Immediate workload visibility |
| New Claims (Today / This Week) | Fresh incoming claims | Ops monitoring |
| Claims Awaiting Action | Stuck or pending claims | Prevent delays |
| Claims with Garages | Claims under garage assessment | Track bottlenecks |
| Claims with Suppliers | Parts sourcing stage | Cost & time risk |
| Completed Claims | Successfully closed | Throughput health |

2. **Latest / Priority Claims Table** — Should show High value & long pending claims. Fields: Claim ID, Policy Holder, Policy Number, [more].

3. **Quick Actions (CTA Section)** — Top 5 frequent actions (Create Claim, View Open RFQs, Approve Quotations, Send Supplier RFQ, View Pending Payments).

4. **Subscription & Usage Awareness (Subtle)** — Banner with current plan, RFQ usage this month, expiry countdown.

5. **Insurance Dashboard – Auctions & Hot Deals** — Snapshot of auction/hot deal participation (if enabled).

#### 15.3.3 Claim Request Requirements (CLM-001 to CLM-009)

| Req ID | Description |
|---|---|
| CLM-001 | Insurance can create claim request with vehicle details, customer details, accident description, photos, videos, documents |
| CLM-002 | Claim ID auto-generated (CLM + 8 digits, expandable) |
| CLM-003 | Claim form has multiple sections: Policy Details, Vehicle Details, Accident Details, Damage Details, Documents, Inspection Venue |
| CLM-004 | Save as Draft supported (claim stays in DRAFT state) |
| CLM-005 | On Submit, claim status changes to SUBMITTED |
| CLM-006 | Sub-claim creation supported — link to parent_claim_id; only photos + reason required, rest auto-pulled |
| CLM-007 | Edit allowed before submission; after submission only admin can edit via dispute path |
| CLM-008 | Search/filter on claims listing — by status, date range, policy number, customer, vehicle |
| CLM-009 | CSV export honors active filters |

#### 15.3.4 RFQ to Garage Requirements (GAR-001 to GAR-020)

20 requirements covering:
- GAR-001: Send RFQ to Garage button on Claim Detail page
- GAR-002: Display eligible garages list (Network + Independent)
- GAR-003: Filter eligible garages by vehicle brand/model, specialty, location
- GAR-004: Manual selection of multiple garages
- GAR-005: System sends RFQ in parallel to all selected garages
- GAR-006: Each garage receives web + email notification
- GAR-007: Claim status updates to "RFQ Sent to Garages"
- GAR-008 to 020: Including reminder dispatch, deadline tracking, "No Response" auto-marking, ability to add more garages later (with indication of previously-sent), garage favorite priority, garage rating display

#### 15.3.5 RFQ to Supplier Requirements (SRFQ-001 to SRFQ-021)

21 requirements covering:
- SRFQ-001: Supplier RFQ auto-created after garage quote approval
- SRFQ-002: Parts list + quantities reused from approved garage quote (no retyping)
- SRFQ-003: Supplier RFQ goes to ALL eligible suppliers (no manual selection)
- SRFQ-004: Eligibility filter (part category, vehicle compatibility, delivery capability, active status)
- SRFQ-005: Supplier inventory auto-fills price + availability
- SRFQ-006: Delivery timeline per part is MANDATORY
- SRFQ-007: Quotes without delivery timeline auto-rejected
- SRFQ-008: Split sourcing across multiple suppliers allowed
- SRFQ-009: Partial availability: available parts proceed, rest stays open
- SRFQ-010 to 021: Quote comparison dashboard, supplier identity hidden until order creation, supplier rating display, favorite supplier priority

#### 15.3.6 Orders (ORD-001 to ORD-011, 11 requirements)
- ORD-001: Insurance can view all orders
- ORD-002: Orders list shows orders for parts AND services
- ORD-003: Orders categorized as Garage Order (labor) or Supplier Order (parts)
- ORD-004: Order summary fields: Order ID, Date, Type, Vendor, Amount, Status
- ORD-005: Filter orders by date, order type, status
- ORD-006: Search orders by Order ID, Claim ID, Customer
- ORD-007: Order details show full info — Order Date, Vendor Details, Part Details (Part Name, Quantity, Unit Price, Total), Labor Cost, Taxes, Grand Total
- ORD-008: Insurance can give reviews and ratings post-order
- ORD-009: Replacement requests for defective parts
- ORD-010: Refund requests via payment gateway
- ORD-011: Order audit log

#### 15.3.7 Garage Management within Insurance (Section 5.1, "Garage" sub-section)
- GAR-001 to GAR-012 (note these are duplicated IDs — context: garage network management within insurance portal)
  - HQ-only feature
  - Add garage to network (via invitation)
  - Mark favorite
  - View garage performance metrics
  - Suspend/remove garage from network
  - Display garage details: Name, Address, Specialty, Active claims count, Past claims count, Rating

#### 15.3.8 Branches (BR-001 to BR-007)

| Req ID | Description |
|---|---|
| BR-001 | HQ can view list of all branches |
| BR-002 | Branches listed with: Branch Name, Address, City, State, Country, Subscription Plan, Status |
| BR-003 | HQ can search and filter branches |
| BR-004 | Branches self-onboard (separate registration flow with parent company selection) |
| BR-005 | HQ can edit Branch Address, Manager Name, Contact Information only |
| BR-006 | HQ cannot edit subscription_plan_id or trade_license_no |
| BR-007 | Each branch maintains independent subscription |

#### 15.3.9 Inventory (INV-001 to INV-015)
- INV-001: Insurance has inventory module for parts and vehicles
- INV-002: Auto-populated from closed claims (replaced parts return to insurance inventory)
- INV-003: Manual addition allowed for items NOT in master (status PENDING_ADMIN_REVIEW until admin approves)
- INV-004: Listing fields: Part Name, OEM Number, Quantity, Branch Location, Status
- INV-005 to 015: Search/filter, edit quantity, mark for auction/hot deal, transfer between branches, audit log

#### 15.3.10 Auction (AUC-001 to AUC-010)
- AUC-001: Insurance can create auctions (NOT participate)
- AUC-002: Auction items from inventory
- AUC-003: Set start date, end date, starting bid, reserve price
- AUC-004: Deposit required for participants
- AUC-005: View live bidding
- AUC-006: Auto-close at end time
- AUC-007 to 010: Reserve-not-met handling (seller decides B1 sell anyway or B2 cancel), winner notification, order auto-generation, manual pre-close

#### 15.3.11 Hot Deals (HD-001 to HD-008)
- HD-001: Insurance creates hot deals from inventory
- HD-002: Time-bound (start, end)
- HD-003: Fixed price (not auction)
- HD-004: Quantity available
- HD-005 to 008: Image upload, view orders, extension allowed (D+14 → D+30), manual close

#### 15.3.12 Reports & Analytics (Section 5.1 reports)
Reports across:
1. Claims Analytics — total claims, avg processing time, settlement rate, declined claims, cost trends
2. Garage Performance Analytics (Phase 1) — RFQ response rate, avg quote turnaround, win rate, dispute rate
3. Supplier Performance Analytics (Phase 1) — RFQ response rate, on-time delivery, partial fulfillment rate, dispute rate
4. Auction & Hot Deal Analytics (Phase 1) — auction success rate, avg bid count, hot deal sell-through rate
5. Outstanding Payments & Liability Analytics (Phase 1) — pending payments, payment delays, avg payment delay

#### 15.3.13 Insurance Subscription (ISUB-001 to ISUB-014, 14 requirements)
- ISUB-001: View available plans (Basic/Silver/Gold)
- ISUB-002: Plan durations: 90/180/360 days
- ISUB-003: Plan selection per HQ; branch has independent subscription
- ISUB-004: RFQ credits — 30,000 to garages/month + 30,000 to suppliers/month per plan
- ISUB-005: Tier-gating — Starter tier excludes inventory, auction create, hot deal create
- ISUB-006: Mid-cycle upgrade prorated
- ISUB-007: Expiry banner with days remaining
- ISUB-008: 30-day grace period after expiry (login allowed, features locked)
- ISUB-009 to 014: Payment gateway integration, plan history, downgrade rules, payment failure handling

### 15.4 Garage Requirements (Section 5.2, 86 items)

#### 15.4.1 Garage Onboarding (GON-001 to GON-010)
- GON-001: Self-registration via website
- GON-002: Personal + Company + Garage Details (Speciality, Vehicle Hierarchy support)
- GON-004: Trade License upload + Licensing Authority
- GON-005: Cascading vehicle support: Vehicle Type → Origin → Brand Segment → Brand (per Apr 28 decision)
- GON-006 to GON-010: VAT, bank details, admin approval, OCR verification (~80% threshold), notification flow

#### 15.4.2 Garage Dashboard (GD-001) — Composite
KPIs: New RFQs, In-progress jobs, Pending quotations, Completed jobs, Payment pending. Latest RFQs table. Quick actions. Subscription awareness.

#### 15.4.3 Garage Claim RFQ (GCR-001 to GCR-011)
- GCR-001: Receive RFQ with claim details + photos/videos
- GCR-002: Accept or Reject RFQ (confirmation modal required)
- GCR-003: View vehicle inspection details (date, time, venue) if inspection required
- GCR-004: Auto-add inspection to garage calendar on acceptance
- GCR-005 to 011: Submit quotation (labor + parts + taxes + repair time mandatory), Add parts one-by-one with photos/condition/action, Diagnosis field before parts, Multiple labor cost types, Track quotation status, Quote due-date colored red when 1-2 days remaining

#### 15.4.4 Garage Auction Participation (GAP-001 to GAP-010)
- GAP-001: View all live auctions
- GAP-002: Pay deposit to participate
- GAP-003: Place bid
- GAP-004 to 010: View bid status, Track wins, Auto-debit on win, Order creation, Refund on loss

#### 15.4.5 Garage Hot Deals (GHD-001 to GHD-009)
- GHD-001 to 009: Browse, Filter, Buy, Track order, Payment, View past purchases

#### 15.4.6 Garage Inventory (G-001 to G-008)
- Similar to insurance inventory; parts + vehicles; supports both purchase and listing for auction/hot-deal

#### 15.4.7 Garage Purchase Parts (GPP-001 to GPP-008)
- GPP-001: Browse parts from supplier inventories
- GPP-002: Add to cart
- GPP-003: Checkout via payment gateway
- GPP-004 to 008: Track order, Receive parts, Replacement requests, Order history

#### 15.4.8 Garage Subscription (GSUB-001 to GSUB-014)
Similar plan structure to Insurance — Basic/Silver/Gold, 90/180/360-day durations, tier-gating

#### 15.4.9 Garage Account (GACC-001 to GACC-004)
Profile, password, notifications, bank details (view-only on mobile)

### 15.5 Supplier Requirements (Section 5.3, 77 items)

#### 15.5.1 Supplier Onboarding (SON-001 to SON-010)
- SON-001: Self-registration
- SON-002 to 010: Type gating (New parts only vs Refurbished/Used allowed), Trade License, VAT, bank details, admin approval

#### 15.5.2 Supplier Dashboard (SD-001)
New RFQs, Confirmed orders, Pending dispatches, Pending payments

#### 15.5.3 Supplier RFQ (SUP-001 to SUP-007)
- SUP-001: Receive RFQ with parts list (from Insurance after garage quote approval)
- SUP-002: View parts: Name, OEM Number, Quantity, Condition Type (OEM/Aftermarket/Used), Repair-vs-Replace indicator
- SUP-003: Quote builder per part — Unit Price, Available Quantity, Delivery Timeline (MANDATORY), Taxes, Remarks
- SUP-004: Auto-fill prices/availability from supplier inventory
- SUP-005: Submit quote
- SUP-006: Cannot submit without delivery timeline
- SUP-007: Track status (Submitted/Under Review/Accepted/Rejected/Revision Requested)

#### 15.5.4 Supplier Inventory (SIN-001 to SIN-007)
- SIN-001 to 007: Manage items, stock quantity, enable/disable, CSV import + export (added May 11), bulk operations (web-only)

#### 15.5.5 Supplier Auction (SAUC-001 to SAUC-012)
Both create AND participate in auctions

#### 15.5.6 Supplier Hot Deals (SHD-001 to SHD-007)
Both create AND purchase from hot deals

#### 15.5.7 Supplier Subscription (SSUB-001 to SSUB-014)
Similar plan structure to other roles

#### 15.5.8 Supplier Account (SACC-001 to SACC-004)
Profile management

### 15.6 Platform Administrator Requirements (Section 5.4, 261 items — largest)

#### 15.6.1 Platform Administrator Onboarding (ADM-OB-001 to ADM-OB-006)
- Admin onboarding flow (super-admin invites)
- Role assignment
- 2FA required
- Activity log capture

#### 15.6.2 Manage Insurance Companies (ADM-INS-001 to ADM-INS-018)
Approve/reject/suspend/reactivate; view detailed insurance company info; filter; CSV export; audit log

#### 15.6.3 Manage Garages (ADM-GAR-001 to ADM-GAR-015)
Similar to Insurance management

#### 15.6.4 Manage Suppliers (ADM-SUP-001 to ADM-SUP-016)
Similar to Insurance management

#### 15.6.5 Manage Claim Requests (ADM-CLM-001 to ADM-CLM-007)
- View all claims across stakeholders
- Intervene in disputes
- Reassign claim
- Override status (with audit)

#### 15.6.6 Manage Subscription Plans (ADM-SP-001 to ADM-SP-005)
- Create, edit, archive subscription plans
- Tier configuration (features mapped to plans)

#### 15.6.7 Manage User Subscriptions (Section, no prefix; covered under ADM-SP)
- Override subscription dates
- Refund subscriptions
- Custom plan assignment

#### 15.6.8 Manage Review & Rating (ADM-RR-001 to ADM-RR-009)
- View all reviews/ratings
- Moderate, edit, delete inappropriate content
- Hide review (soft delete)
- Audit log

#### 15.6.9 Manage Contact Inquiry & Tasks (ADM-CT-001 to ADM-CT-007, T-001 to T-009)
- Contact form submissions queue
- Tasks assignment to admin agents
- Status tracking

#### 15.6.10 Manage Auction (ADM-AUC, covered under AUC- prefix)
- View all auctions across stakeholders
- Force-close with reason (DISPUTE/FRAUD/REGULATORY/OTHER) + justification ≥30 chars
- Audit log

#### 15.6.11 Manage Hot Deals (ADM-HD-001 to ADM-HD-006)
- View all hot deals
- Force-close
- Audit

#### 15.6.12 Manage Admin Roles (Section, T- prefix for tasks)
- Create roles
- Assign permissions
- Group users to roles
- 3 user stories: Admin Users, Roles, Groups

#### 15.6.13 Manage Payments (ADM-PAY-001 to ADM-PAY-007)
- View all transactions
- Refund initiation
- Reconciliation reports
- Dispute handling

#### 15.6.14 Manage Orders (ADM-MO-001 to ADM-MO-009)
- View all orders centrally (Insurance, Garage, Supplier, Auction, Hot Deal orders)
- Filter by type, status, stakeholder
- Intervene in disputes

#### 15.6.15 Manage Item Sub-Category (M-001 to M-009)
- Master attributes management
- Parts categories, sub-categories, types
- Vehicle hierarchy administration (PH-UC-ADM-014 covers 20 sub-stories)

#### 15.6.16 Manage Damage Sub-Category (ADM-DC-001 to ADM-DC-009)
- Damage Category master (Collision, Dent, Scratch, Glass, Structural, Electrical)
- Sub-categories

#### 15.6.17 Manage Vehicle Master (ADM-VM-001 to ADM-VM-009)
- 10-level vehicle hierarchy administration
- Add/edit/disable at each level

#### 15.6.18 Manage Vehicle Verification (ADM-VV-001 to ADM-VV-009)
- VIN verification
- Plate number cross-reference
- Aggregator API integration

#### 15.6.19 Reports & Analytics (Admin reports section)
- Platform-wide KPIs
- Revenue analytics
- User adoption
- Transaction volumes

#### 15.6.20 Languages (LAN-001 to LAN-009)
- Multi-language support framework (English Phase-1; future Arabic)

#### 15.6.21 Service Requirements (SER-001 to SER-011)
- API documentation
- Webhook subscriptions
- Integration points

### 15.7 End-to-End Automation (Section 5.6)
See Section 25 of this document for full Automation_phase1.md content.

### 15.8 Considered Requirements (Section 5.7, CR-001 to CR-010)

These are 10 Change Requests considered in the current phase:

| Req ID | Use Case | Description |
|---|---|---|
| CR-001 | PH-UC-CR-001 | Credit / Payment Terms Management |
| CR-002 | PH-UC-CR-002 | Revenue Management |
| CR-003 | PH-UC-CR-003 | Subscription Management (Advanced Logic & Custom Plans) — Custom subscription plans for admin, Subscription Grace Period Handling |
| CR-004 | PH-UC-CR-004 | Insurance User Management & Per-User Subscription Model — Assign role (basic role structure), Claim owner inside insurance, Ability to reassign claims between users. Per-User Subscription: Insurance subscriptions per user not per company. All users under same subscription. |
| CR-005 | PH-UC-CR-005 | Comment Box / Chat Box (Insurance ↔ Garage) — Insurance asking clarification questions to garage |
| CR-006 | PH-UC-CR-006 | Independent Garage & Supplier Operations |
| CR-007 | PH-UC-CR-007 | Inventory Stock Management (Insurance & Garage) — Use own stock in claims, Decide if Use Existing Stock or Buy From Supplier |
| CR-008 | PH-UC-CR-008 | Favourite Garage & Supplier — Option to make garage and supplier as favourite. When Insurance sends Claim RFQ, favorites appear first (Garages, Suppliers) |
| CR-009 | PH-UC-CR-009 | Eligibility vs Favourite Separation — Recommendation Display Order: 1) Favorite Garages → 2) System Recommended → 3) All Eligible. Favorites bypass recommendation ORDER but not eligibility rules. |
| CR-010 | PH-UC-CR-010 | Quotation Of Alternate / Substitute Parts |



---

## 16. Use Cases — All 55 with Full Workflows

This section contains all 55 use cases from SRS Section 6 in full detail. Each use case includes Description, Creator, Actors, Date Created, Preconditions, Triggers, Basic Workflow, Alternative Flows, Postconditions, Exceptional Flow, Assumptions, and Notes & Comments — verbatim from the SRS.

Use case count by stakeholder:
- AUTH: 1 (PH-UC-AUTH-001)
- Insurance: 12 (PH-UC-INS-001 through 014, with 006 and 009 omitted in SRS)
- Garage: 9 (PH-UC-GRG-001 through 009)
- Supplier: 6 (PH-UC-SUP-001 through 006; SUP-007 mislabeled in SRS L15243 as GRG-007 — actually My Account Supplier Profile)
- Platform Admin: 16 (PH-UC-ADM-001 through 016)
- Change Requests / Cross-cutting: 10 (PH-UC-CR-001 through 010)
- **Total: 55** (note SRS numbering gaps)



### PH-UC-AUTH-001 — Website Login & Signup Access

```
Description
This use case describes how a visitor accesses the system from the website
homepage by logging in as an existing user or signing up as a new user (Insurance
Company, Garage, or Supplier). It includes homepage access, login, signup role
selection, and forgot password flow.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Date Created:
17/11/2025
* Website and homepage must be live.
Preconditions:
* User must have an existing approved account to log in.
* Signup feature must be enabled for all three categories (Insurance, Garage,
Supplier).
Triggers:
* Visitor opens the website and sees the homepage with “Login” and “Sign
Up” buttons
* Homepage Access:
o​ Visitor navigates to the website.
o​ System displays the homepage with Login and Sign Up buttons at
the top-right corner.
* Existing User Login:
o​ Visitor clicks Login.
o​ System displays Login form with fields: Email ID, Password, and
“Forgot Password”.
o​ User enters valid Email ID and Password.
o​ System authenticates the credentials.
o​ If successful and account is approved → User is redirected to
their system.
Basic Workflow:
* New User Signup:
o​ Visitor clicks Sign Up.
o​ System displays Role Selection Page with three options:
▪​
Insurance Company
▪​
Garage
▪​
Supplier
o​ Visitor selects one role.
o​ System redirects the visitor to the appropriate registration form for that
role.
o​ User fills the registration form and submits.
o​ System stores the data and triggers the onboarding/approval workflow
(based on role).
* A1: Incorrect Login Credentials
o​ User enters wrong email or password.
o​ System displays “Invalid credentials”.
o​ User retries.
* A2: Forgot Password
o​ User clicks Forgot Password.
o​ System shows “Enter registered email” input.
Alternative
Flows:
o​ User enters the email.
o​ System sends password reset link to email.
o​ User clicks the link and creates a new password.
o​ User returns to login screen and signs in.
* A3: User Attempts Login but Not Yet Approved
o​ User enters valid email/password but account is pending admin
approval.
o​ System displays: “Your account is pending approval. You will be
notified once approved.”
o​ User cannot enter system.
* Login Successful:​
User is redirected to their system according to their role.
Postconditions:
* Signup Successful:​
User registration is created, awaiting admin review
* Forgot Password Completed:​
User has a valid new password
* EX-1: Unregistered Email in Login:​
User enters an email not found in system.​
System displays: “Email not registered. Please sign up.”
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX-2: Email Already Exists During Signup:​
User enters email already registered.​
System prevents registration and displays: “This email is already registered.
Please login.”
* User type based home page exist for Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users.
* Admin approval is mandatory for onboarding users (e.g., Insurance
Assumptions:
Company, Garage, Supplier).
* Same email cannot be used across users.
* Password reset emails always reach the registered mailbox.
* Homepage design will include a clear CTA for Login/Signup in top
navigation.
Notes &
Comments
* User type selection is mandatory for signup because each user has a
different registration details.
* Authentication should follow secure standards.
6.2​ Insurance
* Insurance Company & Branch Registration & Approval
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-001 — Insurance Company & Branch Registration & Approval

```
Description
This use case describes the end-to-end registration and approval process for
insurance companies and their individual branches, covering both
sales-team-initiated registration and self-registration. It includes document
submission, parent–branch mapping, admin verification, approval/rejection,
resubmission, and system access enablement.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Date Created:
17/11/2025
* Registration page and onboarding system must be active.
* Licensing Authority dropdown list must exist (provided by client).
Preconditions:
* Sales team should have sent invitation links manually.
* Branches must have valid individual Trade Licenses and VAT certificate.
* Insurance company or branch gets a sales team manual invitation to register​
OR
Triggers:
* Insurance company or branch starts self-registration using the public
onboarding link.
* Actor opens the registration link (via sales invitation or website
self-registration).
* Actor enters Personal Details: Name, Email, and Contact Number.
* Actor enters Company Details:
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
Company Name
Trade License Number
Licensing Authority (dropdown)
Uploads Trade License Copy
Uploads VAT Certificate
* Actor specifies whether the entity is Head Quarter (HQ) or Branch.
* If registered as Branch:
o​ System displays a dropdown list of available HQ companies.
o​ Actor selects the parent HQ.
o​ If HQ not available in the dropdown, actor proceeds and may
update it later after approval.
Basic Workflow:
* Actor submits the registration form.
* System sends web and email notifications to Admin about new
*
*
*
*
registration.
Admin reviews the submitted details and documents.
Admin manually verifies the license using the Licensing Authority
website.
Admin chooses to Approve or Reject the registration.
If approved:
o​ System activates the insurer’s account and grants access to
relevant modules.
* If rejected:
o​ Admin enters a rejection reason.
o​ System notifies the insurer through email notification with the
reason and allows them to edit details and resubmit.
* A1: Branch cannot find Parent HQ in dropdown
o​ Actor selects "Branch" but HQ not listed in dropdown.
o​ Actor continues registration without selecting parent HQ.
o​ After approval, actor can update the parent HQ later from their
Alternative
Flows:
profile settings.
* A2: User receives Sales Team Invitation
o​ Sales team sends onboarding invitation link to insurer.
o​ Insurer opens pre-generated registration link.
o​ User proceeds with normal registration workflow.
* If Approved:​
The insurer or branch can log in and use all authorized modules.
Postconditions:
* If Rejected:​
Insurer profile remains inactive until corrected and resubmitted.
* System stores complete audit logs of registration, approval, rejection, and
resubmission.
* EX-1: Uploaded documents invalid or unreadable​
Admin marks document as invalid.​
System rejects application with instructions to upload correct documents.
* EX-2: Licensing Authority verification unavailable​
Exceptional
Flow:
Admin unable to validate license due to website downtime.​
Admin marks verification as pending.​
System keeps insurer in “Pending Review” state until manual verification
completes.
* EX-3: Duplicate Registration Attempt​
Actor attempts to register using an email or company name already
registered.​
System alerts the user and blocks duplicate registration.
* Branch and HQ registrations are treated independently due to separate
Trade Licenses.
* Parent-child mapping does not influence approval decision; each entity
follows the same approval workflow.
Assumptions:
* Admin's manual license verification method is outside system automation.
* Notification service (email + web alerts) is configured correctly.
* Sales team will be able to send the invitation to the insurance company
outside the system only.
* Insurance companies cannot create branches from their portal; only branch
users self-register.
Notes &
Comments
* The HQ list must be maintained accurately by Admin to ensure proper
parent-branch mapping.
* Approval workflow is mandatory; no user can access the system without
Admin approval.
* Insurance Claim Request Creation & Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-002 — Insurance Claim Request Creation & Management

```
Description
This use case covers the end-to-end process for insurance users to create, edit,
publish, preview, search, and manage claim requests after receiving accident details.
It includes uploading required documents, recording policyholder details, providing
damage information, and managing claim statuses.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Date Created:
17/11/2025
* Insurance user must have an approved and active account.
Preconditions:
* Accident details (photos, videos, witness information, etc.) are available to
be uploaded.
* Claim status master list will be provided by client and configured in system.
Triggers:
* Insurance user receives accident information from customer or Police and
initiates a new claim request based on their inspection.
*
*
*
*
Insurance user logs into the system.
User navigates to Claim Request section.
User clicks Create New Claim.
System displays the Claim Form containing fields for:
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
Basic Workflow:
Policyholder Name
Policy Number
Contact Information
Damage Description
Witness details
Date of Claim
Reason for Claim
Claim Status
* User uploads mandatory documents:
o​
o​
o​
o​
Police Report
Photos
Videos
Witness details
* User uploads additional supporting documents using “Add More
*
*
*
*
Documents”.
User reviews the entered information in Preview/Draft Mode.
User clicks Publish / Submit Claim.
System saves the claim request and assigns a unique Claim ID.
Claim enters the default status (client will define exact status list, e.g.,
“Submitted”, “Under Review” based on clients status details).
* System shows confirmation message with Claim ID.
* A1: User Edits Claim Before Publishing
o​ User clicks Edit while reviewing in Preview Mode.
o​ System returns user to form.
o​ User makes corrections and updates uploads.
o​ User saves and publishes again.
* A2: User Saves Claim as Draft
o​ User decides not to submit immediately.
Alternative
Flows:
o​ User clicks Save as Draft.
o​ System stores the claim in “Draft” status.
o​ User can later open the draft, edit, and publish.
* A3: Search & Filter Claims
o​ User navigates to Claim List page.
o​ User enters keywords, Claim ID, Policy Number, or Policyholder
Name.
o​ User applies filters (status, date range, user, policy number, etc.).
o​ System displays matching claims.
* A new claim request is successfully created and stored in system.
* Claim has a unique Claim ID and a valid status.
Postconditions:
* All uploaded documents are stored in secure document storage.
* Claim becomes available for further processing (internal review, sending
RFQ to garages, etc.).
* EX-1: Required Documents Missing​
User attempts to publish without uploading mandatory documents.​
System displays error: “Police Report, Photos, and Videos are required to
submit the claim.”​
User must upload missing documents before proceeding.
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX-2: Invalid or Corrupted Files​
User uploads an unreadable or unsupported file.​
System rejects the file and prompts for a valid file format.
* EX-3: Policy Number Not Found​
After creating of a new claim, if policy number is seen as not valid, claim will
get cancelled.
* Insurance companies follow internal guidelines for claim creation; system
does not auto-validate accident authenticity.
Assumptions:
* Claim status workflow may include many stages (e.g., Submitted →
Reviewing → RFQ Sent → Approved), but final list will be provided by client.
* Large files (videos/images) must follow platform size limits.
* Preview feature ensures user validates all fields before final submission.
* Claim records should be searchable by multiple parameters for operational
Notes &
Comments
efficiency.
* Claim editing allowed only until certain workflow stage (if required, can be
defined later).
* System should maintain full audit logs for create/edit/publish actions.
* Send RFQ to Garages After Claim Creation
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-003 — Send RFQ to Garages After Claim Creation

```
Description
This use case describes how an insurance user selects one or multiple garages
(network or independent) and sends an RFQ (Request for Quotation) for a created
claim. Garages receive notifications and can later respond with quotations.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Date Created:
19/11/2025
* A claim must already be created and available in the System.
* Insurance user must be logged in with required permissions.
Preconditions:
* Network and independent garages must be registered and active in the
platform.
* Claim has necessary information and attachments to request a quote.
Triggers:
* Insurance user opens a submitted claim and selects the option to Send RFQ
to Garages.
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
Basic Workflow:
Insurance user opens the Claim Details Page.
User clicks Send RFQ to Garage(s).
System displays a list of eligible garages (Network + Independent).
User manually selects one or multiple garages from the list.
User confirms the RFQ send action.
System generates RFQ associated with the claim.
System sends RFQ notifications to selected garages through:
o​ Web notifications
o​ Email notifications
* Garages receive RFQ with claim information and required documents.
* System updates claim status automatically (e.g., "RFQ Sent to Garages").
* System logs RFQ actions and stores timestamp and list of garages selected.
* A1: RFQ Sent to Only One Garage
o​ Insurance user selects a single garage.
o​ System sends RFQ only to that garage.
o​ Same flow continues with status update & logging.
* A2: RFQ Sent to Multiple Garages Simultaneously
o​ Insurance user selects multiple garages.
o​ System sends the same RFQ details and documents to all selected
Alternative
Flows:
garages.
o​ Garages independently receive notifications and respond later.
* A3: User Wants to Add More Garages Later
o​ User returns to the claim and opens the Claim.
o​ User clicks on the Send RFQ to Garage(s) to send RFQ to more
Garages.
o​ User selects new garages and sends RFQs and in the list those
garages will not be visible to whom RFQ is already sent.
o​ System logs new RFQs.
* RFQ is successfully sent to selected garages.
* Selected garages receive claim details and notification alerts.
Postconditions:
* Claim status is updated to reflect RFQ dispatch.
* System logs RFQ action for audit and tracking.
* EX-1: No Garage Selected​
Exceptional
Flow:
User attempts to send RFQ without selecting a garage.​
System displays error: “Please select at least one garage to continue.”​
No RFQ is sent.
* RFQ details include all necessary documents and information associated
Assumptions:
with the claim.
* RFQ timestamps and garage selection history must be maintained for audit.
* Garage selection is fully manual by insurance user (no AI or auto-suggestion
Notes &
Comments
for now).
* Network & independent garages must be clearly marked in the selection
list.
* Send RFQ to Suppliers After Parts Finalisation
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-004 — Send RFQ to Suppliers After Parts Finalisation

```
Description
This use case defines how an insurance user sends RFQs to one or multiple suppliers
after finalizing the garage and parts list, receives and compares supplier quotations,
manually selects one or more supplier quotes for a claim, and creates orders based
on the selected quotations.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Date Created:
19/11/2025
* Claim must be created and active.
* Garage must be selected for the claim.
Preconditions:
* Parts list must be finalized for the claim.
* Suppliers must be on boarded and active in the system.
Triggers:
* Insurance user finalizes parts list and selects Send RFQ to Supplier.
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
Basic Workflow:
*
*
*
*
Insurance user opens the Claim Details page.
User reviews and confirms the finalized Parts List.
User clicks Send RFQ to Supplier.
System displays list of eligible suppliers.
User manually selects one or multiple suppliers.
System sends RFQ notifications to selected suppliers with part details.
Supplier receives RFQ notification and opens RFQ.
Supplier submits quotation with following details for each part:
o​ Part Name
o​ Unit Price
o​ Total Cost
System stores supplier quotations against the claim.
Insurance user views all received quotations.
System provides quotation comparison view, highlighting lowest price per
part.
Insurance user manually evaluates and selects one or multiple supplier
quotes for the claim.
User confirms quote selection.
System creates Order(s) based on the selected supplier quotations.
*
*
* System updates claim and order statuses accordingly.
* A1: Parts Sourced from Multiple Suppliers
o​ Insurance user selects different supplier quotes for different parts.
o​ System creates multiple orders, one per supplier.
o​ All orders remain linked to the same claim.
* A2: RFQ Sent to Single Supplier
o​ Insurance user selects only one supplier.
Alternative
Flows:
o​ Supplier submits quotation.
o​ User selects quotation and order is created.
* A3: Partial Quote Selection
o​ Supplier provides quote for only some parts.
o​ User selects quoted parts from one supplier and sources remaining
parts from another supplier.
o​ System allows mixed supplier selection.
* RFQs are successfully sent to selected suppliers.
* Supplier quotations are recorded and linked to the claim.
Postconditions:
* One or more supplier orders are created based on selected quotations.
* Orders are traceable to the originating claim and garage.
* EX-1: No Supplier Selected​
User attempts to send RFQ without selecting any supplier.​
System displays error:​
“Please select at least one supplier to proceed.”
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX-2: Supplier Quote Missing Required Fields​
Supplier submits quote without unit price or total cost.​
System rejects submission and prompts supplier to complete required
fields.
* EX-3: No Supplier Quotes Received​
No suppliers respond within defined timeframe.​
Insurance user may resend RFQ or select different suppliers.
* Quote evaluation and selection is fully manual due to pricing complexity.
* Multiple supplier quotations can be selected for a single claim.
Assumptions:
* Order creation is directly dependent on selected quotations.
* System highlights lowest price but does not auto-select.
* Supplier availability is determined externally or manually.
* Future enhancement may include automated quote ranking, SLA-based
supplier selection, or partial automation.
Notes &
Comments
* Each supplier order should contain clear part-to-claim mapping.
* Audit trail must capture RFQ sending, quote submission, quote selection,
and order creation events.
* Order Management for Insurance (Supplier & Garage Orders)
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-005 — Order Management for Insurance (Supplier & Garage Orders)

```
Description
This use case describes how insurance users view and manage all orders created for
a claim, including supplier (parts) and garage (labour) orders. It covers order listing,
filtering, visibility rules (HQ vs branch), detailed order view, billing information,
status timeline, reviews & ratings, and replacement/refund workflows.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Date Created:
19/11/2025
Actors:
Insurance Company
* Orders must already be created from selected supplier quotations or garage
approvals.
Preconditions:
* Insurance user must be logged in with appropriate role (HQ or Branch).
* Order statuses and workflow are configured as per client-provided list.
Triggers:
* Insurance user navigates to the Orders module from the system menu.
* Part A – Orders List View
o​ Insurance user opens the Orders module.
o​ System displays a consolidated Orders List containing:
▪​
Supplier Orders (Parts)
▪​
Garage Orders (Labor)
o​ Each order row shows summary fields:
▪​
Order ID
▪​
Order Date
▪​
Supplier / Garage Name
▪​
Order Type (Supplier / Garage)
▪​
Order Status
o​ System enforces visibility rules:
▪​
HQ users see all orders across branches.
▪​
Branch users see only orders created for their branch.
* Part B – Search & Filter Orders
o​ Insurance user applies filters by:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Date range
▪​
Order Type (Supplier / Garage)
▪​
Supplier / Garage
▪​
Order Status (Pending, Confirmed, Dispatched, Delivered,
Cancelled)
o​ Insurance user searches orders using:
▪​
Order ID
▪​
Supplier / Garage Name
▪​
Claim ID
o​ System refreshes list with matching results.
* Part C – Order Details View
o​ Insurance user clicks on an Order ID.
o​ System displays Order Details, including:
▪​
Order ID & Order Date
▪​
Supplier / Garage Name
▪​
Contact Details
▪​
Billing Address
▪​
Shipping Address
o​ System displays Item Details:
▪​
Part Name
▪​
Part Description
▪​
Quantity
▪​
Unit Price
▪​
Total Price
* Part D – Billing & Status Timeline
o​ System displays Billing Information:
▪​
Cost Breakdown
▪​
Taxes
▪​
Additional Charges (e.g., delivery fees)
o​ System displays Order Status Timeline, showing chronological
status changes (as per client-defined statuses).
* Part E – Reviews & Ratings
o​ After order completion, insurance user submits Review & Rating
for supplier or garage linked to the claim.
o​ System stores the rating and recalculates aggregated rating for
that supplier/garage.
* Part F – Replacement / Refund Request
o​ Insurance user raises a Replacement or Refund Request for
defective or incorrect parts.
o​ User provides reason and supporting details.
o​ System sends request to supplier for approval.
o​ Supplier reviews and approves or rejects the request.
o​ System updates order status and logs decision.
* A1: View Only Garage Orders
o​ User applies filter: Order Type = Garage.
o​ System shows only labor-related orders.
* A2: View Only Supplier Orders
Alternative
Flows:
o​ User applies filter: Order Type = Supplier.
o​ System shows only parts-related orders.
* A3: Replacement Instead of Refund
o​ Supplier approves replacement request.
o​ System updates order with replacement shipment details.
o​ Status timeline reflects replacement activity.
* Insurance user can fully track supplier and garage orders.
* All order activities are logged with complete audit history.
Postconditions:
* Reviews and ratings impact aggregated supplier/garage score.
* Replacement/refund actions are traceable and status-driven.
* EX-1: Unauthorized Order Access​
Branch user attempts to access HQ-level or other branch orders.​
System blocks access and displays authorization error.
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX-2: Replacement/Refund without Reason​
User submits request without reason.​
System prompts: “Reason is mandatory for replacement/refund.”
* EX-3: Supplier Rejects Replacement/Refund​
Supplier rejects request with justification.​
System updates order timeline and notifies insurance user.
* Status definitions and transitions will be provided by client.
* Ratings calculation logic is average-based unless specified otherwise.
Assumptions:
* Replacement/refund workflow applies only to supplier (parts) orders.
* Garage labour disputes are handled outside this workflow.
* Orders module is a central operational dashboard for insurance users.
Notes &
Comments
* Status timeline improves traceability and dispute resolution.
* Review & rating feature supports long-term supplier and garage
performance evaluation.
* Garage Management, Claim Invitation & Assignment
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-007 — Garage Management, Claim Invitation & Assignment

```
Description
This use case describes how insurance companies manage garages on the platform,
including linking network garages, viewing a combined garage directory, searching
and filtering garages, inviting garages to claims for quotations, assigning claims
based on garage quotes, handling repair cost review and payment, and generating
garage invoices.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Date Created:
19/11/2025
* Insurance user must be logged in with an approved and active account.
* Garages (network and independent) must be registered or available in the
platform.
Preconditions:
* Claim must exist to invite garages.
* Claim and garage quotation statuses are configured as per client-provided
list.
Triggers:
* Insurance user navigates to the Garage module to add and manage network
garages.
* Part A – Add & View Garages
o​ Insurance user opens the Garage Management module.
o​ User adds Network Garages by entering or selecting garage
details (as per platform rules).
o​ System displays a combined garage list, including:
▪​
Network garages
▪​
Independently registered garages
o​ For each garage, system displays:
▪​
Garage Name
▪​
Garage ID
▪​
Specialization
▪​
Contact Person & Contact Details
▪​
Address
▪​
Licensing Details
o​ System enforces visibility rules:
▪​
HQ users see all garages across all branches.
▪​
Basic Workflow:
Branch users see only garages tied to their branch
operations.
* Part B – Search & Filter Garages
o​ Insurance user searches garages using:
▪​
Garage Name
▪​
Garage ID
▪​
Specialization
▪​
Location
▪​
License Number
o​ Insurance user filters garages by:
▪​
Network / Non-Network
▪​
Specialization
▪​
Region
▪​
Activity Status
o​ System refreshes the list with matching garages.
* Part C – Claim-Garage Association
o​ Insurance user opens a Claim Request.
o​ System displays all garages already invited or participating in that
claim.
o​ Insurance user clicks Invite Garages to Claim.
o​ User selects one or multiple garages from the garage list.
o​ System sends claim invitation notifications to selected garages.
* Part D – Quotation Review & Claim Assignment
o​ Garages submit quotations for repair/labor costs.
o​ Insurance user reviews submitted quotations.
o​ Based on quotation evaluation, insurance user selects a garage.
o​ System assigns the claim to the selected garage.
o​ Claim status is updated accordingly (as per client-defined
workflow).
* Part E – Payment & Invoice Handling
o​ Assigned garage completes repair work and submits repair cost
details.
Insurance user reviews the submitted repair costs.
Insurance user processes payment to the garage.
Upon successful payment, system generates Garage Invoice.
Invoice is linked to the claim and available for future reference.
* Part F – Claim Search
o​ Insurance user searches claims using claim status, such as:
o​
o​
o​
o​
▪​
Pending
▪​
In Progress
▪​
Approved
▪​
Completed
o​ System displays matching claim records with assigned garages.
* A1: Invite Multiple Garages but Assign Only One
o​ Insurance user invites multiple garages to a claim.
o​ Only one garage is selected based on quotation.
o​ Other garage invitations are marked as “Not Selected”.
* A2: Network Garage Preference
Alternative
Flows:
o​ Insurance user filters garages by Network.
o​ Only network garages appear for selection and invitation.
* A3: Payment Delayed
o​ Garage submits repair cost.
o​ Insurance user delays payment due to internal review.
o​ Invoice is not generated until payment is completed.
* Garages are successfully managed and categorized (network /
independent).
Postconditions:
* Claims are assigned to selected garages based on quotations.
* Garage payments and invoices are properly generated and logged.
* Claim and garage activities are fully traceable.
* EX-1: No Garage Selected for Invitation​
Insurance user attempts to invite garages without selection.​
System displays error:​
“Please select at least one garage to continue.”
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX-2: Branch Access ​
Branch user attempts to view or assign garages outside its scope.​
Every insurance user can see every garage present in the platform.
* EX-3: Payment Failure​
Payment to garage fails.​
System marks payment as failed and does not generate invoice.​
User is notified to retry payment.
* Garage quotation format and approval rules are standardized.
* Invoice generation is strictly payment-driven.
Assumptions:
```
