```
* Claim status lifecycle is defined and configurable by the client.
* Insurance users manually evaluate garage quotations.
* Garage module is tightly integrated with Claims, RFQ, Orders, and
Payments.
Notes &
Comments
* Clear HQ vs Branch access control is critical for governance.
* This use case can be further split into Garage Master Management, Garage
RFQ, and Garage Payment use cases if needed.
* Branch Management for Insurance Company (HQ Only)
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-008 — Branch Management for Insurance Company (HQ Only)

```
Description
This use case defines how the Insurance Company HQ views and manages its
registered branches. It covers branch listing, visibility and access control,
operational statistics, branch search, Branch complete visibility, editing and filtering,
and subscription independence. Branch creation is strictly handled via branch
self-onboarding and not through HQ.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Date Created:
19/11/2025
Actors:
Insurance Company
* Insurance HQ user must be logged in with an active and approved HQ
account.
Preconditions:
* One or more branches must have completed self-onboarding and been
approved by Admin.
* Branch subscription data must be available in the system.
Triggers:
* HQ user navigates to the Branches section from the system menu.
* Part A – Branch List View
o​ HQ user opens the Branches module.
o​ System displays a list of all branches registered under the
insurance company.
o​ Each branch record displays:
▪​
Branch Name
▪​
Branch ID
▪​
Branch Address
▪​
Branch Manager Name
▪​
Contact Information
* Part B – Branch Statistics
o​ HQ user selects a branch.
o​ System displays operational statistics for the selected branch
(details to be provided by client).
* Part C – Access Control
o​ System enforces role-based access:
▪​
HQ users can access the Branches module.
▪​
Basic Workflow:
Branch users do not see the Branches section in the UI
and can only access their own data.
* Part D – Search & Filter Branches
o​ HQ user searches branches using:
▪​
Branch Name
▪​
Branch ID
▪​
Branch Manager Name
▪​
Phone Number
o​ System filters and displays matching branch records.
* Part E – Edit Branch Details
o​ HQ user selects Edit Branch.
o​ System allows editing of permitted fields such as:
▪​
Branch Address
▪​
Branch Manager Name
▪​
Contact Information
o​ System restricts editing of:
▪​
Subscription details
▪​
Licensing details
o​ HQ user saves changes.
o​ System updates branch details.
* Part F – Subscription Awareness
o​ HQ user views branch subscription status.
o​ System indicates that each branch maintains an independent
subscription, separate from HQ.
* A1: Branch Has No Active Subscription
o​ HQ user views a branch with inactive or expired subscription.
o​ System displays subscription status warning.
Alternative
Flows:
o​ Branch functionality remains limited until renewal (as per business
rules).
* A2: Search Returns No Results
o​ HQ user searches with unmatched criteria.
o​ System displays “No branches found”.
* HQ has complete visibility into all registered branches.
* Branch data is updated where permitted.
Postconditions:
* Access control rules are strictly enforced.
* Subscription independence per branch is clearly maintained.
* EX-1: Unauthorized Access Attempt​
Exceptional
Flow:
Branch user attempts to access Branches module.​
System blocks access and hides the Branches section.
* EX-2: Edit Restricted Fields​
HQ user attempts to edit subscription or licensing fields. ​
System prevents action and displays permission error.
* Branch operational statistics will be defined and finalized by the client.
Assumptions:
* Branch creation and deletion are out of scope for HQ users.
* Subscription purchase and renewal are handled at branch level.
* Branch self-onboarding ensures regulatory and licensing compliance.
Notes &
Comments
* HQ’s role is limited to governance and oversight, not branch creation.
* This use case supports scalability for multi-branch insurance companies.
* Inventory Management for Insurance (Parts & Vehicles)
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-010 — Inventory Management for Insurance (Parts & Vehicles)

```
Description
This use case describes how insurance users manage inventory items (Parts and
Vehicles). It covers adding inventory, maintaining quantity and pricing, listing and
editing items, deleting unavailable inventory, and using inventory items to create
Auctions and Hot Deals. Inventory access and control are restricted to insurance
users only.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Date Created:
19/11/2025
* Insurance user must be logged in with an active and approved account.
Preconditions:
* Inventory module must be enabled for the insurance company.
* Parts and Vehicle master data must already exist in the system (for
read-only fields).
Triggers:
* Insurance user navigates to the Inventory module from the system menu.
* Part A – Add Inventory Item
o​ Insurance user clicks Add Inventory Item.
o​ System asks user to select Item Type:
▪​
Parts
▪​
Vehicle
o​ If Parts is selected:
▪​
System displays part details in read-only mode.
▪​
User enters:
​ Available Quantity
​ Unit Price
o​ If Vehicle is selected:
▪​
Basic Workflow:
System displays vehicle details (as configured).
o​ User saves the inventory item.
o​ System adds the item to inventory with active status.​
Note: Any part or vehicle intended for Auction or Hot Deal must
first be added to inventory.
* Part B – Inventory Listing
o​ Insurance user opens Inventory List.
o​ System displays all inventory items with the following fields:
▪​
Item Name
▪​
Item ID
▪​
Item Type (Vehicle / Parts)
▪​
Quantity Available (static/manual entry)
▪​
Status (Available, Used, Inactive, etc.)
* Part C – Edit Inventory Item
o​ Insurance user selects an inventory item.
o​ User edits permitted fields such as:
▪​
Quantity Available
▪​
Unit Price
▪​
Status
o​ User saves changes.
o​ System updates inventory details.
* Part D – Delete / Remove Inventory Item
o​ Insurance user selects an inventory item no longer available.
o​ User clicks Delete / Remove.
o​ System removes or marks the item as inactive (as per
configuration).
* Part E – Create Auction / Hot Deal
o​
o​
o​
o​
Insurance user selects an inventory item.
User chooses Create Auction or Create Hot Deal.
System validates that the item exists in inventory.
System redirects user to Auction or Hot Deal creation flow with item
details prefilled.
* A1: Inventory Added for Immediate Auction
o​ User receives a new part/vehicle.
Alternative
Flows:
o​ User adds item to inventory.
o​ User can create an Auction or Hot Deal from the inventory item.
* A2: Zero Quantity Inventory
o​ User marks item as unavailable or inactive.
* Inventory items are accurately maintained and tracked.
Postconditions:
* Only valid inventory items are used for Auctions or Hot Deals.
* Inventory reflects correct quantity, pricing, and status.
* EX-1: Unauthorized Access​
Insurance user can edit, or delete items from their own inventory only.​
System will not allow them to edit other insurance user’s inventory.
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX-2: Invalid Quantity or Price​
User enters negative or invalid values.​
System displays validation error and prevents saving.
* EX-3: Attempt to Create Auction/Hot deal Without Inventory​
User attempts to create Auction/Hot Deal without adding item to inventory.​
System blocks action and prompts user to add inventory first.
* Inventory quantity is a manual/static entry and not auto-deducted unless
defined later.
Assumptions:
* Read-only item details are sourced from master data.
* Auction and Hot Deal workflows are handled in separate use cases.
* Inventory is the single source of truth for Auctions and Hot Deals.
Notes &
Comments
* Strict access control ensures data integrity.
* This use case can be extended later to include automated stock movement
and reconciliation.
* Auction Creation, Bidding & Order Generation
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-011 — Auction Creation, Bidding & Order Generation

```
Description
This use case describes how insurance companies create and manage auctions using
inventory items, allow suppliers and garages to participate in bidding, maintain bid
history, and automatically generate orders for winning bids. It also covers auction
item management before go-live and auction order visibility in the Insurance Orders
module.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Date Created:
19/11/2025
* Insurance user must be logged in with an active and approved account.
* Inventory must contain Parts or Vehicles intended for auction.
Preconditions:
* Auction configuration and bidding rules are enabled in the system.
* Suppliers and garages must be registered and active to participate in
auctions.
Triggers:
* Insurance user selects Create Auction from the Auction module.
* Part A – Create Auction
o​ Insurance user navigates to the Auction module.
o​ User clicks Create New Auction.
o​ System displays Auction Creation form with mandatory fields:
▪​
Auction Name / Title
▪​
Start Date & Time
▪​
End Date & Time
▪​
Seller Information (Insurance Company / Branch)
▪​
Item Information
▪​
Reserve Price
▪​
Starting Bid
o​ User selects auction items only from Inventory.
o​ User saves the auction in Draft status.
* Part B – Manage Auction Items (Before Go-Live)
o​ Before auction start time, insurance user can:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Add items from Inventory
▪​
Edit item details
▪​
Delete items from auction
o​ System allows item changes only while auction status is Draft /
Scheduled.
o​ User publishes the auction.
* Part C – Auction Details & Bidding
o​ Auction goes live at scheduled start time.
o​ System displays Auction Details Page showing:
▪​
Auction information
▪​
Linked items with their current status
o​ Suppliers and garages can view auction and place bids.
o​ System records every bid with bidder details, bid amount, and
timestamp.
o​ System maintains full bid history for audit and transparency.
* Part D – Auction Closure & Order Creation
o​ Auction closes automatically at end date/time.
o​ System identifies the highest valid bid for each item.
o​ System automatically generates an Order for the winning bid.
o​ Order includes:
▪​
Order Amount
▪​
Buyer Details (Supplier / Garage)
▪​
Item Information
▪​
Payment Details
▪​
Order Status
o​ System categorizes the order as Auction Order.
o​ Auction order appears in the Insurance Orders module under
Auction category.
* Part E – Order Visibility
o​ System enforces order visibility rules:
▪​
HQ users can view all auction orders across branches.
▪​
Branch users can view only their own auction orders.
* A1: No Bids Received
o​ Auction closes with no bids.
o​ System marks auction as Closed – No Bids.
Alternative
Flows:
o​ No order is created.
* A2: Auction Item Removed Before Go-Live
o​ Insurance user deletes item from Inventory before auction starts.
o​ System removes item from auction successfully.
o​ System will restrict that auction to get published.
* Auction is successfully created and completed.
* Bid history is preserved for audit.
Postconditions:
* Winning bids result in automatically generated orders.
* Auction orders are visible in Insurance Orders module.
* EX-1: Attempt to Add Non-Inventory Item​
Exceptional
Flow:
User attempts to add an item not present in Inventory. ​
System blocks action and displays error:​
“Auction items must be selected from Inventory.”
* EX-2: Unauthorized Auction Modification​
User attempts to edit auction after it goes live.​
System blocks modification.
* Bidding rules (increment, extension, etc.) are configurable.
* Auction orders follow standard order lifecycle.
Assumptions:
* Payment handling is managed post order creation.
* One order is created per winning item unless otherwise defined.
* Auction module is tightly coupled with Inventory and Orders.
Notes &
Comments
* Full bid audit trail is mandatory for regulatory and dispute handling.
* This use case can be extended to support multi-item bundled auctions.
* Hot Deal Management & Order Creation
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-012 — Hot Deal Management & Order Creation

```
Description
This use case defines how insurance companies create and manage Hot Deals using
inventory items, allow buyers to purchase items at fixed deal prices, and
automatically generate orders upon purchase. It also covers Hot Deal item
management before go-live and order visibility rules for HQ and branch users.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Date Created:
19/11/2025
* Insurance user must be logged in with an active and approved account.
* Inventory must contain Parts or Vehicles intended for Hot Deals.
Preconditions:
* Hot Deal configuration is enabled in the system.
* Suppliers and garages must be registered and active buyers.
Triggers:
* Insurance user selects Create Hot Deal from the Hot Deals module.
* Part A – Create Hot Deal
o​ Insurance user navigates to the Hot Deals module.
o​ User clicks Create New Hot Deal.
o​ System displays Hot Deal creation form with mandatory fields:
▪​
Hot Deal Name
▪​
Start Date & Time
▪​
End Date & Time
▪​
Deal Price
▪​
Item Information
o​ User selects Hot Deal items only from Inventory.
o​ User saves the Hot Deal in Draft status.
* Part B – Manage Hot Deal Items (Before Go-Live)
o​ Before Hot Deal start time, insurance user can:
▪​
Add items from Inventory
▪​
Edit item details
▪​
Basic Workflow:
Delete items from Hot Deal
o​ System allows item changes only while Hot Deal status is Draft /
Scheduled.
o​ User publishes the Hot Deal.
* Part C – Hot Deal Details & Purchase
o​ Hot Deal goes live at scheduled start time.
o​ System displays Hot Deal Details Page showing:
▪​
Hot Deal information
▪​
Linked items with their status
o​ Supplier or Garage selects an item and purchases at the deal
price.
* Part D – Order Creation & Visibility
o​ Upon successful purchase, system automatically generates an
Order.
o​ Order includes:
▪​
Item Details
▪​
Item Type (Parts or Vehicle)
▪​
Deal Price
▪​
Buyer Details
▪​
Order Status
o​ System categorizes the order as Hot Deal Order.
o​ Hot Deal orders appear in the Insurance Orders module under
Hot Deals category.
o​ System enforces visibility rules:
▪​
HQ users can view all Hot Deal orders across branches.
▪​
Branch users can view only their own Hot Deal orders.
* A1: Item Sold Out
o​ Items related to hot deal got purchased.
o​ User will mark that item as unavailable or inactive.
* A2: Hot Deal Ends Without Purchase
o​ Hot Deal end time is reached.
Alternative
Flows:
o​ System marks Hot Deal as Expired.
o​ No orders are created.
* A3: Item Removed Before Go-Live
o​ Insurance user deletes item from Inventory before Hot deal starts.
o​ System removes item from hot deal successfully.
o​ System will restrict that hot deal to get published.
* Hot Deals are successfully created and executed.
Postconditions:
* Orders are automatically generated for purchased items.
* Hot Deal orders are tracked and visible in Orders module.
* EX-1: Attempt to Add Non-Inventory Item​
User attempts to add an item not present in Inventory. ​
System blocks action and displays error:​
“Hot Deal items must be selected from Inventory.”
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX-2: Unauthorized Auction Modification​
User attempts to edit Hot Deal after it goes live.​
System blocks modification.
* EX-3: Purchase Failure​
Buyer payment or confirmation fails.​
System cancels transaction and no order is created.
* Hot Deals are fixed-price offers (no bidding).
Assumptions:
* Payment handling is managed through order workflow.
* One order is created per successful purchase.
* Hot Deals complement Auctions for quick liquidation of inventory.
Notes &
Comments
* Tightly coupled with Inventory ensures item availability control.
* This use case can be extended to include limited-time discounts, bulk
purchases, or auto-expiry notifications.
* Insurance Subscription Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-013 — Insurance Subscription Management

```
Description
This use case defines how an insurance company views available subscription plans,
purchases a plan, upgrades or downgrades subscriptions, renews plans, views
subscription history, and how the system enforces access control, counters,
notifications, and audit logging.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Date Created:
Preconditions:
*
*
*
*
Triggers:
* Insurance user navigates to the Subscription module.
19/11/2025
Insurance user must be registered, approved, and logged in.
Admin must have created and published subscription plans.
Payment gateway must be active.
User must have permission to purchase/manage subscription.
* Part A – View Available Subscription Plans
o​ Insurance user opens the Subscription Plans page.
o​ System displays a list of Active & Published plans only.
o​ Each plan displays:
▪​
Feature Group
▪​
Plan Name
▪​
Price
▪​
Billing Cycle
▪​
Plan Benefits
▪​
Counters
▪​
Validity
o​ Inactive or unpublished plans are not visible.
Basic Workflow:
* Part B – Purchase Subscription
o​ Insurance user selects a subscription plan.
o​ User clicks Buy / Subscribe.
o​ System redirects user to payment flow.
o​ User completes payment using available payment options.
o​ System validates payment success.
o​ System activates the subscription and sets:
▪​
Start Date
▪​
End Date
▪​
Counters
▪​
Status = Active
o​ System generates:
▪​
Payment receipt
▪​
Transaction ID
▪​
Invoice
o​ User receives confirmation notification.
* Part C – View Current Subscription
o​ Insurance user navigates to Current Subscription section.
o​ System displays:
▪​
Plan Name
▪​
Start Date
▪​
End Date
▪​
Counters (used / remaining)
▪​
Subscription Status
* Part D – Upgrade Subscription
o​ Insurance user selects Upgrade Plan.
o​ System displays higher-tier plans only.
o​ User selects a new plan and proceeds with payment.
o​ Upon successful payment:
▪​
New plan becomes active immediately
▪​
Previous plan is closed
o​ System updates counters based on upgraded plan.
o​ System sends upgrade confirmation notification.
* Part E – Downgrade Subscription
o​ Insurance user selects Downgrade Plan.
o​ System allows downgrade selection anytime.
o​ System schedules downgraded plan to activate after current plan
expiry.
o​ Current plan remains active until expiry.
o​ User receives downgrade scheduling notification.
* Part F – Renewal Subscription
o​ Insurance user selects Renew Plan before expiry.
o​ User completes payment.
o​ Renewed plan is queued.
o​ System activates renewed plan automatically after current plan
expires.
o​ Counters reset (no carry forward).
o​ System sends renewal confirmation notification.
* Part G – Subscription History
o​ Insurance user navigates to Subscription History.
o​ System displays:
▪​
Past and current plans
▪​
Payment status
▪​
Receipts and invoices
▪​
Usage logs
* Part H – Access Control & Counters Enforcement
o​ Insurance user performs an action tied to subscription counters.
o​ System checks remaining counters.
o​ If counters are available, action is allowed.
o​ If counters are exhausted, system blocks action and displays
upgrade/renew message.
o​ Unused counters do not carry forward to next cycle.
* Part I – Notifications & Audit
o​ System sends notifications for:
▪​
Plan activation
▪​
Expiry reminders
▪​
Renewal confirmation
▪​
Upgrade / Downgrade
▪​
Payment failure
o​ System logs all subscription actions in audit trail for compliance.
* A1: Payment Failure
o​ Payment fails or is cancelled.
o​ Subscription is not activated.
o​ System displays failure message and sends notification.
* A2: Attempt to Use Feature Beyond Counters
Alternative
Flows:
o​ User exceeds plan counter limit.
o​ System blocks the action.
o​ System suggests upgrade or renewal.
* A3: Expired Subscription
o​
o​
o​
o​
Subscription reaches end date without renewal.
System marks subscription as Expired.
System restricts access to subscription-based features.
User can renew their subscription through the app or manually pay
amount to the Admin, they can activate their plan.
* Subscription lifecycle is correctly managed (purchase, upgrade, downgrade,
Postconditions:
renewal).
* Access is enforced strictly based on counters and plan validity.
* Subscription history and invoices are stored.
* Audit trail is maintained for compliance.
* EX-1: Inactive Plan Access​
Exceptional
Flow:
If a plan becomes inactive, it is not displayed for new purchases.
* EX-2: Unauthorized Access​
If user is not permitted or approved, subscription actions are blocked.
Assumptions:
Notes &
Comments
*
*
*
*
Subscription is mandatory to access core platform features.
Payment gateway handles financial validation.
Counter logic applies uniformly across all subscribed modules.
HQ and Branch subscriptions are independent.
* Branch subscriptions are separate from HQ subscriptions.
* This use case supports future enhancements like trial plans, discount
coupons, and auto-renewal.
* My Account – Profile
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-INS-014 — My Account – Profile

```
Description
This use case describes how a logged-in user (Insurance, Garage, or Supplier) can
view and update their account profile details, upload or update registration
documents, and manage profile changes while ensuring that restricted and unique
identifiers remain protected and approval workflows are enforced where required.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Preconditions:
Date Created:
19/11/2025
* User must be registered, approved, and logged in.
* User account must be active.
* Admin approval workflow must be enabled for profile updates impacting
compliance.
Triggers:
* User navigates to My Account from the system menu.
* Part A – View Profile Details
o​ User opens My Account section.
o​ System displays all profile details captured during registration,
including:
▪​
Personal details
▪​
Company details
▪​
Contact information
▪​
Uploaded documents
* Part B – Edit Profile Details
o​ User clicks Edit Profile.
o​ System enables editing for allowed fields only.
o​ User updates permitted profile details.
o​ System restricts editing of the following fields:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Email ID
▪​
License Number
▪​
Any other system-defined unique identifiers
o​ User saves profile changes.
* Part C – Company Type Restriction
o​ User attempts to change Company Type (Insurance / Garage /
Supplier).
o​ System blocks direct modification of Company Type.
o​ System displays a message stating that company type cannot be
changed once the account is created.
o​ If business-critical profile details are updated that impact
compliance, system flags the profile for Admin Review and
Re-Approval before changes take effect.
* Part D – Admin Approval (If Applicable)
o​ If uploaded or edited details require re-verification:
▪​
System marks profile as Pending Approval.
▪​
Admin receives notification.
o​ Admin reviews updated profile or documents.
o​ Admin approves or rejects the changes.
o​ System updates profile status accordingly and notifies user.
* A1: Edit Restricted Fields
o​ User attempts to edit Email ID or License number for an approved
Alternative
Flows:
Postconditions:
account.
o​ System disables the field and prevents saving changes.
*
*
*
*
User profile is updated with valid, permitted information.
Restricted identifiers remain unchanged.
Updated documents are stored securely.
Admin approval is enforced where required.
* EX-1: Unauthorized Profile Access​
Exceptional
Flow:
If an unauthenticated user attempts to access My Account, system redirects
to login.
* EX-2: Admin Rejection​
If Admin rejects updated details, system restores previous approved profile
data and notifies user with rejection reason.
* Email ID and License Number are system-wide unique identifiers.
* Certain profile updates may require regulatory compliance review outside
Assumptions:
the system.
* Admin approval workflow is configurable.
* This use case ensures data integrity, regulatory compliance, and controlled
Notes &
Comments
profile changes.
* Future enhancements may include change request tracking and profile
version history.
6.3​ Garage
* Garage Onboarding & Registration
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-GRG-001 — Garage Onboarding & Registration

```
Description
This use case defines the end-to-end process for garage users to register on the
platform, complete profile and compliance details, upload mandatory documents,
and receive admin approval before gaining access to system functionalities.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Garage User
Date Created:
17/12/2025
* Garage user is not already registered with the same unique identifiers
(Email, Mobile, License).
Preconditions:
* Platform registration is active.
* Admin approval workflow is enabled.
Triggers:
* Garage user clicks Sign Up from the website Garage login page.
* Actor opens the registration link (via sales invitation or website
self-registration).
* Actor enters Personal Details: Name, Email, and Contact Number.
* Actor enters Additional Details:
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
Personal details
Company details
Garage speciality
Registration and licensing details
Bank details
Uploads Trade License Copy
Uploads VAT Certificate
* System validates mandatory fields & document formats and sizes.
* Actor submits the registration form.
* System sends web and email notifications to Admin about new
Basic Workflow:
*
*
*
*
registration.
Admin reviews the submitted details and documents.
Admin manually verifies the license using the Licensing Authority
website.
Admin chooses to Approve or Reject the registration.
If approved:
o​ System activates the Garage’s account and grants access to relevant
modules.
* If rejected:
o​ Admin enters a rejection reason.
o​ System notifies the Garage through email notification with the reason
and allows them to edit details and resubmit.
* Until approval is granted, garage user:
o​ Can log in
o​ Cannot access operational modules of the platform.
* A1 – Duplicate Registration Attempt
o​ User attempts to register with an Email ID or Mobile Number
already existing in the system.
o​ System blocks registration and displays validation error.
* A2 – Weak Password
Alternative
Flows:
o​ User enters a password not meeting security standards.
o​ System displays password policy message and prevents submission.
* A3 – Incomplete Profile Submission
o​ User submits registration without completing mandatory additional
details.
o​ System prompts user to complete required fields before
submission.
* Garage user profile is either approved or rejected with reason.
Postconditions:
* Approved garage users can access the platform.
* Rejected users can correct details and resubmit.
* EX-1: System Failure during Submission​
Exceptional
Flow:
If system error occurs, registration is not saved and user is prompted to
retry.
* EX-2: Admin Action Timeout​
If admin does not act, registration remains in Pending Approval state.
* Email ID and Mobile Number are unique identifiers system-wide.
Assumptions:
* Admin approval is mandatory for all garage registrations.
* Bank details are required for future payment processing.
* Garage users cannot transact or participate in claims until approved.
Notes &
Comments
* This use case aligns with regulatory and compliance requirements.
* Future enhancements may include auto-verification of licenses.
* Garage Claim Request for Quotation (RFQ) Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-GRG-002 — Garage Claim Request for Quotation (RFQ) Management

```
Description
This use case describes how a garage views claim requests sent by insurance
companies, reviews claim details, accepts or rejects RFQ requests, prepares and
submits repair quotations including parts and labour costs, uploads supporting
documents, and receives notifications throughout the quotation lifecycle.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Garage User
Date Created:
17/12/2025
* Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.
Preconditions:
* Insurance company must have sent a claim RFQ to the garage.
* Claim request must be in a valid RFQ state.
Triggers:
* Garage user navigates to the Claim Requests / RFQ module or receives a
notification for a new claim RFQ.
* Part A – View Claim RFQ List
o​ Garage user opens the Claim RFQ List.
o​ System displays all claim requests sent to the garage.
o​ Claim list shows the following details:
▪​
Claim ID
▪​
Vehicle Details
▪​
Insurance Company Name
▪​
Request Date
▪​
Claim Status
o​ System segregates claims based on status (status list to be
provided by client).
* Part B – View Claim Details
o​ Garage user selects a claim from the list.
o​ System displays complete claim details including:
▪​
Vehicle information
▪​
Damage photos
▪​
Request notes from insurer
▪​
Basic Workflow:
Documents submitted by insurance company
* Part C – Accept or Reject Claim RFQ
o​ Garage user reviews the claim details.
o​ Garage user selects one of the following actions:
▪​ Accept RFQ
▪​ Reject RFQ
o​ If Rejected:
▪​
Garage provides rejection reason.
▪​
System updates claim status and notifies insurer.
o​ If Accepted:
▪​
Claim moves to quotation preparation stage.
* Part D – Prepare Quotation
o​ Garage user adds required parts by selecting parts from the
system.
o​ Garage enters unit price and quantity for each part.
o​ Garage enters Labour Cost for repair services.
o​ System calculates total quotation amount (parts + labour).
* Part E – Upload Supporting Documents
o​ Garage user uploads supporting documents such as:
▪​
Estimate files
▪​
Additional damage photos
▪​
Any relevant repair documents
o​ System validates file type and size.
* Part F – Submit Quotation
o​ Garage user reviews the quotation details.
o​ Garage user submits the final quotation.
o​ System updates claim status to Quotation Submitted.
o​ Insurance company receives notification of quotation submission.
* Part G – Activity Logging & Notifications
o​ System logs all garage actions, including:
▪​
Accept / Reject RFQ
▪​
Adding parts
▪​
Entering labour cost
▪​
Quotation submission
o​ Garage receives notifications for:
▪​
New RFQ request
▪​
RFQ acceptance or rejection
▪​
Quotation selection by insurance
* A1 – Partial Quotation Save (Draft)
o​ Garage user saves quotation as draft without submission.
o​ System retains draft for later editing.
Alternative
Flows:
* A2 – Reject RFQ After Review
o​ Garage decides not to participate.
o​ Garage submits rejection reason.
o​ System updates status and notifies insurer.
* Quotation is submitted or rejected by the garage.
Postconditions:
* Insurance company can view and evaluate submitted quotations.
* All quotation actions are logged for audit.
* EX-1: Unauthorized Access​
If garage user attempts to access RFQ not assigned to them, system blocks
access.
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX-2: Incomplete Quotation Submission​
If labour cost are missing, system prevents submission and prompts
completion.
* System Error during Submission​
If system failure occurs, quotation is not submitted and user is notified.
* Status list for RFQ lifecycle will be provided by the client.
Assumptions:
* Pricing entered by garage is exclusive of taxes unless specified.
* Once submitted, quotation can be edited only if insurance allows revision.
* RFQ handling is a critical garage interaction point with insurers.
Notes &
Comments
* Transparent activity logs ensure auditability.
* This use case integrates with Claim Assignment and Payment workflows.
* Garage Auction Participation & Order Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-GRG-003 — Garage Auction Participation & Order Management

```
Description
This use case describes how garage users view ongoing and upcoming auctions,
review auction and item details, pay the required deposit, participate in bidding,
track bids, and manage auction orders won through the platform, including
payment, refund and notifications.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Garage User
Preconditions:
*
*
*
*
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.
Auction must be active or scheduled.
Deposit fee rules must be configured for auction items.
Payment gateway must be available.
* Garage user navigates to the Auctions module or receives auction
Triggers:
notification.
* Part A – View Auctions
o​ Garage user opens the Auctions module.
o​ System displays all ongoing and upcoming auctions.
o​ Auction list displays:
▪​
Auction Title
▪​
Start Date & Time
▪​
End Date & Time
▪​
Item Count
▪​
Reserve Price
▪​
Auction Status
* Part B – View Auction Details
o​ Garage user selects an auction.
o​ System displays complete auction details including:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Item information
▪​
Auction rules
▪​
Reserve price
▪​
Start / End time
* Part C – View Bid Details
o​ Garage user views:
▪​
Current highest bid
▪​
Bid history
▪​
Bid timestamps
* Part D – Deposit Fee Payment
o​ Garage user selects auction item(s) to bid on.
o​ System prompts for deposit fee payment.
o​ Garage user completes deposit payment through online payment
gateway.
o​ System confirms successful deposit payment.
o​ Deposit amount is marked as refundable to the original payment
source.
* Part E – Place Bid
o​ Garage user enters bid amount.
o​ System validates that:
▪​
Deposit payment is successful
▪​
Bid amount is greater than current highest bid
o​ System accepts bid and updates bid history.
o​ System notifies other bidders (if applicable).
* Part F – Auction Closure & Order Creation
o​ Auction reaches end date & time.
o​ System determines highest bidder.
o​ System automatically creates an Auction Order for winning
bidder.
o​ Garage user receives winning confirmation notification.
* Part G – Order List & Details
o​ Garage user navigates to Auction Orders.
o​ System displays all auction orders won by the garage with:
▪​
Order ID
▪​
Auction ID
▪​
Item Name
▪​
Winning Price
▪​
Order Status
▪​
Payment Status
o​ Garage user selects an order to view details including:
▪​
Item information
▪​
Delivery details
▪​
Invoice
▪​
Payment requirements
* Part H – Order Payment
o​ Garage user completes payment for the auction order via online
payment modes.
o​ System updates payment and order status accordingly.
* Part I – Notifications
o​ System sends notifications to garage for:
▪​
Deposit confirmation
▪​
Bid updates
▪​
Auction win confirmation
▪​
Payment reminders
* A1 – Bid Lower Than Current Highest Bid
o​ Garage user places a bid lower than or equal to current highest bid.
o​ System rejects the bid and displays validation message.
Alternative
Flows:
* A2 – Deposit Payment Failure
o​ Deposit payment fails.
o​ System blocks bidding and prompts retry.
* A3 – Auction Lost
o​ Garage does not win the auction.
o​ Deposit refund is initiated to original payment source (as per refund
rules).
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Garage successfully participates in auctions.
Winning bids generate orders automatically.
Payments and deposits are processed correctly.
Notifications keep garage informed at every stage.
* EX-1: Unauthorized Auction Access​
Exceptional
Flow:
if garage is not approved or logged in, auction participation is blocked.
* EX-2: System Failure During Bidding​
Bid is not recorded and garage is notified to retry.
Assumptions:
* Deposit fee refund timelines are governed by platform policy.
* Auction rules are visible to all participants.
* One auction order is generated per winning item.
Notes &
Comments
* Deposit mechanism ensures serious bidders only.
* This use case integrates tightly with Orders and Payments modules.
* Can be extended to support auto-bidding in future phases.
* Garage Hot Deal Purchase & Order Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-GRG-004 — Garage Hot Deal Purchase & Order Management

```
Description
This use case defines how garage users view active hot deals published by insurers,
filter and review item details, purchase hot deal items at fixed prices, complete
payments, view orders, and how the system maintains audit logs for compliance and
tracking.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Garage User
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
*
*
*
*
Triggers:
* Garage user navigates to the Hot Deals module.
Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.
Hot deals must be active and published by insurers.
Inventory must have sufficient available quantity.
Approved payment modes must be configured.
* Part A – View Hot Deals
o​ Garage user opens the Hot Deals section.
o​ System displays all active hot deals.
o​ Hot deal listing shows:
▪​
Item Name
▪​
Deal Price
▪​
Available Quantity
▪​
Start & End Date/Time
▪​
Status (to be confirmed by client)
* Part B – Apply Advanced Filters
o​ Garage user applies filters using one or more of the following:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Part Name
▪​
Part Category
▪​
Vehicle Compatibility
▪​
Price Range
▪​
Expiry Time
▪​
Hot Deal ID
▪​
Status
o​ System refreshes the hot deal list based on applied filters.
* Part C – View Hot Deal Item Details
o​ Garage user selects a hot deal item.
o​ System displays complete item details including:
▪​
Part Name
▪​
Part ID
▪​
Deal Price
▪​
Compatibility (vehicle models)
▪​
Condition
▪​
Available Quantity
* Part D – Purchase Hot Deal Item
o​ Garage user selects the required Hot Deal.
o​ Garage user proceeds to purchase the hot deal.
o​ System will show the total hot deal items, their quantity and total
price.
o​ Garage user confirms the order.
* Part E – Payment Workflow
o​ System redirects the garage user to the payment gateway.
o​ Garage user completes payment using approved payment modes.
o​ System validates payment success.
o​ System automatically generates a Hot Deal Order.
* Part F – Order Listing & Order Details
o​ Garage user navigates to Hot Deal Orders.
o​ System displays all hot deal orders with:
▪​
Order ID
▪​
Item Name
▪​
Deal Price
▪​
Order Date
▪​
Payment Status
▪​
Delivery Status
o​ Garage user selects an order to view detailed information
including:
▪​
Invoice
▪​
Payment information
▪​
Delivery information
* Part G – Audit Trail
o​ System logs all actions related to hot deals including:
▪​
Viewing hot deals
▪​
Purchase actions
▪​
Payment completion
▪​
Order creation
o​ Audit logs are stored for compliance and tracking purposes.
Alternative
Flows:
* A1 – Insufficient Quantity
o​ Garage user selects quantity exceeding availability.
o​ System displays validation error and prevents checkout.
* A2 – Payment Failure
o​ Payment fails or is cancelled.
o​ Order is not created.
o​ System prompts garage user to retry payment.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Successful hot deal purchase results in order creation.
Inventory quantity is reduced accordingly.
Order appears in garage hot deal order list.
Audit trail is maintained.
* EX1 – Unauthorized Access​
Exceptional
Flow:
If garage user is not approved or logged in, system blocks access to hot
deals.
* EX2 – System Error During Transaction​
Transaction is rolled back and no order is created.
Assumptions:
* Hot deal prices are fixed and non-negotiable.
* Delivery fulfilment is handled separately.
* Hot deal status values will be finalized by the client.
Notes &
Comments
* Hot deals enable fast procurement of parts and vehicles at fixed prices.
* This use case integrates with Inventory, Orders, and Payments modules.
* Future enhancements may include bulk purchase limits and expiry
reminders.
* Garage Inventory Management (Parts & Vehicles)
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-GRG-005 — Garage Inventory Management (Parts & Vehicles)

```
Description
This use case defines how garage users manage their internal inventory by adding
parts and vehicles, maintaining quantity and pricing, viewing inventory listings,
editing inventory details, and removing items that are no longer available, with
strict access limited to garage users only.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Garage User
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
* Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.
* Garage user must have inventory management access enabled.
Triggers:
* Garage user navigates to the Inventory module.
* Part A – Add Inventory Item
o​ Garage user clicks Add Inventory Item.
o​ System prompts user to select Item Type:
▪​
Parts
▪​
Vehicle
o​ If Parts is selected:
▪​
System displays item details in read-only mode.
▪​
Garage user enters:
​ Available Quantity
​ Unit Price
o​ If Vehicle is selected:
▪​
System displays vehicle details as configured.
o​ Garage user saves the inventory item.
o​ System adds the item to the garage inventory with an active
Basic Workflow:
status.
* Part B – Inventory Listing
o​ Garage user opens Inventory List.
o​ System displays inventory items with the following fields:
▪​
Item Name
▪​
Item ID
▪​
Item Type (Parts / Vehicle)
▪​
Quantity Available (static/manual entry)
▪​
Status
* Part C – Edit Inventory Item
o​ Garage user selects an inventory item.
o​ Garage user edits permitted fields such as:
▪​
Quantity Available
▪​
Unit Price
▪​
Status
o​ Garage user saves changes.
o​ System updates the inventory record.
* Part D – Delete / Remove Inventory Item
o​ Garage user selects an inventory item that is no longer available.
o​ Garage user clicks Delete / Remove.
o​ System removes the item or marks it as inactive (as per
configuration).
* Access Control
o​ System ensures that only garage users can add, edit, or delete
inventory items of their own except the inventory master details.
o​ Any unauthorized user action is blocked.
Alternative
Flows:
* A1 – Invalid Quantity or Price
o​ Garage user enters invalid or negative quantity/price.
o​ System displays validation error and prevents saving.
* A2 – Duplicate Inventory Item
o​ Garage user attempts to add an item already existing in inventory.
o​ System displays duplication warning (handling as per configuration).
Postconditions:
* Garage inventory is updated accurately.
* Inventory listing reflects current quantity and status.
* Removed items are no longer available for use.
* EX1 – Unauthorized Access​
Exceptional
Flow:
If a non-garage user attempts inventory modification, system denies access.
* EX2 – System Error During Save​
Inventory changes are not saved and user is prompted to retry.
Assumptions:
* Inventory quantity is manually maintained by garage users.
* Pricing entered is exclusive of taxes unless defined otherwise.
* Inventory is independent of insurance inventory unless integrated later.
Notes &
Comments
* This inventory is intended for garage operational use.
* Can be extended to support auto-deduction on job completion in future
phases.
* Access control ensures data ownership and security.
* Purchase Parts for Garage Inventory
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-GRG-006 — Purchase Parts for Garage Inventory

```
Description
This use case defines how garage users purchase parts for their own inventory,
including inventory validation before purchase, order placement, order tracking,
replacement requests, reviews & ratings, and audit logging.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Garage User
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
* Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.
* Required part must already exist in the garage inventory master.
* Garage user must have purchase permissions enabled.
Triggers:
* Garage user initiates Purchase Parts action from inventory or parts catalog.
* Part A – Inventory Validation & Purchase Initiation
o​ Garage user selects a part to purchase.
o​ System validates whether the selected item exists in the garage
inventory.
o​ If item exists, system allows the user to proceed with purchase.
o​ Garage user enters purchase details:
▪​
Quantity
▪​
Supplier / Insurer
o​ Garage user confirms the order.
* Part B – Order Creation & Processing
o​ System creates a purchase order.
o​ System assigns a unique Order ID.
o​ System records order details including:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Item Name
▪​
Supplier / Insurer
▪​
Order Date
▪​
Price
▪​
Quantity
▪​
Delivery Status
▪​
Payment Status
* Part C – Order Listing & Tracking
o​ Garage user navigates to Purchase Order Listing.
o​ System displays all raised and closed orders with required order
details.
o​ Garage user selects an order to view full order summary.
* Part D – Replacement Request
o​ Garage user identifies an issue with delivered parts (damaged,
incorrect, or defective).
o​ Garage user raises a Replacement Request by providing a reason.
o​ System submits the replacement request for processing.
* Part E – Review & Rating
o​ After order completion, garage user submits ratings & reviews
based on:
▪​
Delivery Time
▪​
Quality
▪​
Accuracy
▪​
Communication
o​ System updates aggregated ratings for the part and supplier.
* Part F – Audit Logging
o​ System logs all purchase-related activities including:
▪​
Order creation
▪​
Order updates
▪​
Replacement requests
▪​
Reviews & ratings
* A1 – Item Not Available in Inventory
o​ System detects item does not exist in inventory.
o​ System restricts order placement.
o​ System displays notification instructing user to add the item to
Alternative
Flows:
Postconditions:
inventory first.
* A2 – Payment Failure
o​ Payment attempt fails.
o​ System updates payment status as failed.
o​ User is notified to retry payment.
* A3 – Invalid Quantity
o​ Garage user enters invalid or zero quantity.
o​ System displays validation error and blocks order submission.
* Purchase order is successfully created and tracked.
* Inventory quantity can be updated post-delivery (manual or automated).
* Reviews and ratings contribute to supplier/part reputation.
* E1 – Unauthorized Access​
Exceptional
Flow:
Non-garage users attempting to place purchase orders are denied access.
* E2 – System Error during Order Creation​
Order is not created and user is prompted to retry.
Assumptions:
* Inventory validation is mandatory before purchase.
* Replacement handling workflow is managed separately by supplier/insurer.
* Ratings are immutable once submitted.
Notes &
Comments
* Purchase orders are strictly for garage internal inventory usage.
* This use case supports traceability and compliance through audit logs.
* Can be extended to auto-update inventory on successful delivery in future
phases.
* Garage – Insurance Management & Claim Interaction
Use Case ID:
```

```
Supplier).

account.

*

profile changes.
```
