### PH-UC-GRG-008 — Garage – Subscription Management

```
Description
This use case defines how a Garage views, purchases, upgrades, downgrades,
renews, and manages subscription plans, including payment handling, access
control based on counters, notifications, and audit logging.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Garage User
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
* Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.
* Admin must have configured and published subscription plans.
* Payment gateway must be active.
Triggers:
* Garage navigates to the Subscription section from the dashboard.
* Part A – View Subscription Plans
o​ Garage opens the Active Plans Listing page.
o​ System displays only Active & Published subscription plans.
o​ Each plan displays:
▪​
Feature Group
▪​
Plan Name
▪​
Price
▪​
Billing Cycle
▪​
Plan Benefits
▪​
Counters
▪​
Validity
o​ Inactive or unpublished plans are not shown.
Basic Workflow:
* Part B – Purchase Subscription
o​ Garage selects a subscription plan.
o​ Garage proceeds to payment.
o​ System redirects supplier to the payment gateway.
o​ Garage completes payment using available payment options.
o​ Upon successful payment:
▪​
System activates the subscription
▪​
System displays payment receipt, transaction ID, and
invoice
o​ Garage receives subscription activation notification.
* Part C – Upgrade Subscription
o​ Garage chooses Upgrade Plan from current subscription details.
o​ System displays eligible higher plans.
o​ Garage selects a higher plan.
o​ System calculates price adjustment by deducting unused value of
the current plan from the new plan price.
o​ Garage completes the differential payment.
o​ System activates the upgraded plan immediately.
o​ Garage receives upgrade confirmation notification.
* Part D – Downgrade Subscription
o​ Garage selects Downgrade Plan option.
o​ Garage chooses a lower plan.
o​ System schedules downgrade to activate after expiry of the
current plan.
o​ System confirms downgrade scheduling to the supplier.
* Part E – Renewal
o​
o​
o​
o​
Garage selects Renew Subscription before plan expiry.
Garage completes payment.
Renewed plan is scheduled to activate after current plan expiry.
Garage receives renewal confirmation notification.
* Part F – Current Plan & History
o​ Garage views Current Plan Details, including:
▪​
Plan Name
▪​
Start Date
▪​
End Date
▪​
Counters
▪​
Status
o​ Garage views Subscription History, including:
▪​
Past plans
▪​
Payment status
▪​
Receipts
▪​
Usage logs
* Part G – Access Control Enforcement
o​ System tracks feature usage against plan counters.
o​ If counters are exhausted:
▪​
System restricts access to related features
▪​
System displays usage limit message
* Part H – Notifications & Audit Trail
o​ System sends notifications for:
▪​
Plan activation
▪​
Expiry reminders
▪​
Renewals
▪​
Upgrades / Downgrades
▪​
Payment failures
o​ System logs all subscription actions for audit and compliance.
Alternative
Flows:
* A1 – Payment Failure
o​ Payment fails at gateway.
o​ System displays failure message.
o​ Subscription is not activated.
o​ Garage receives payment failure notification.
* A2 – No Active Plans Available
o​ Admin has not published any active plans.
o​ System displays “No subscription plans available”.
* A3 – Upgrade Not Eligible
o​ Garage selects a plan lower or equal to current plan.
o​ System restricts upgrade and shows validation message.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Garage has an active, upgraded, downgraded, or renewed subscription.
Subscription usage is enforced based on counters.
Payment and subscription history is recorded.
Audit logs are maintained.
* EX1 – Access Violation​
Exceptional
Flow:
Garage attempts to use features beyond allowed counters → System blocks
access.
* EX2 – System Error during Subscription Update​
System rolls back partial updates and notifies the user.
Assumptions:
* Subscription pricing and counter logic are defined by Admin.
* No unused counters are carried forward to the next cycle.
* All monetary calculations follow platform-defined proration rule
Notes &
Comments
* Upgrade pricing adjustment logic must be clearly documented.
* Downgrades are deferred to avoid feature disruption.
* This use case integrates with Payments, Notifications, and Access Control
modules.
* My Account (Garage) – Profile
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-GRG-009 — My Account (Garage) – Profile

```
Description
This use case describes how a logged-in user can view and update their account
profile details, upload or update registration documents, and manage profile
changes while ensuring that restricted and unique identifiers remain protected and
approval workflows are enforced where required.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Actors:
Preconditions:
Garage User
* User must be registered, approved, and logged in.
* User account must be active.
* Admin approval workflow must be enabled for profile updates impacting
compliance.
Triggers:
* User navigates to My Account from the system menu.
* Part A – View Profile Details
o​ User opens My Account section.
o​ System displays all profile details captured during registration,
including:
▪​
Personal details
▪​
Company details
▪​
Contact information
▪​
Uploaded documents
* Part B – Edit Profile Details
o​ User clicks Edit Profile.
o​ System enables editing for allowed fields only.
o​ User updates permitted profile details.
o​ System restricts editing of the following fields:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Email ID
▪​
License Number
▪​
Any other system-defined unique identifiers
o​ User saves profile changes.
* Part C – Company Type Restriction
o​ User attempts to change Company Type (Insurance / Garage /
Supplier).
o​ System blocks direct modification of Company Type.
o​ System displays a message stating that company type cannot be
changed once the account is created.
o​ If business-critical profile details are updated that impact
compliance, system flags the profile for Admin Review and
Re-Approval before changes take effect.
* Part D – Admin Approval (If Applicable)
o​ If uploaded or edited details require re-verification:
▪​
System marks profile as Pending Approval.
▪​
Admin receives notification.
o​ Admin reviews updated profile or documents.
o​ Admin approves or rejects the changes.
o​ System updates profile status accordingly and notifies user.
* A1: Edit Restricted Fields
o​ User attempts to edit Email ID or License number for an approved
Alternative
Flows:
Postconditions:
account.
o​ System disables the field and prevents saving changes.
*
*
*
*
User profile is updated with valid, permitted information.
Restricted identifiers remain unchanged.
Updated documents are stored securely.
Admin approval is enforced where required.
* EX-1: Unauthorized Profile Access​
Exceptional
Flow:
If an unauthenticated user attempts to access My Account, system redirects
to login.
* EX-2: Admin Rejection​
If Admin rejects updated details, system restores previous approved profile
data and notifies user with rejection reason.
* Email ID and License Number are system-wide unique identifiers.
* Certain profile updates may require regulatory compliance review outside
Assumptions:
the system.
* Admin approval workflow is configurable.
* This use case ensures data integrity, regulatory compliance, and controlled
Notes &
Comments
profile changes.
* Future enhancements may include change request tracking and profile
version history.
6.4​ Supplier
* Supplier Onboarding & Registration
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-SUP-001 — Supplier Onboarding & Registration

```
Description
This use case defines the end-to-end process for Supplier users to register on the
platform, complete profile and compliance details, upload mandatory documents,
and receive admin approval before gaining access to system functionalities.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Supplier User
Date Created:
17/12/2025
* Supplier user is not already registered with the same unique identifiers
(Email, Mobile, License).
Preconditions:
* Platform registration is active.
* Admin approval workflow is enabled.
Triggers:
* Supplier user clicks Sign Up from the website Supplier login page.
* Actor opens the registration link (via sales invitation or website
self-registration).
* Actor enters Personal Details: Name, Email, and Contact Number.
* Actor enters Additional Details:
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
Personal details
Company details
Supplier speciality
Registration and licensing details
Bank details
Uploads Trade License Copy
Uploads VAT Certificate
* System validates mandatory fields & document formats and sizes.
* Actor submits the registration form.
* System sends web and email notifications to Admin about new
Basic Workflow:
*
*
*
*
registration.
Admin reviews the submitted details and documents.
Admin manually verifies the license using the Licensing Authority
website.
Admin chooses to Approve or Reject the registration.
If approved:
o​ System activates the Supplier’s account and grants access to relevant
modules.
* If rejected:
o​ Admin enters a rejection reason.
o​ System notifies the Supplier through email notification with the reason
and allows them to edit details and resubmit.
* Until approval is granted, Supplier user:
o​ Can log in
o​ Cannot access operational modules of the platform.
* A1 – Duplicate Registration Attempt
o​ User attempts to register with an Email ID or Mobile Number
already existing in the system.
o​ System blocks registration and displays validation error.
* A2 – Weak Password
Alternative
Flows:
o​ User enters a password not meeting security standards.
o​ System displays password policy message and prevents submission.
* A3 – Incomplete Profile Submission
o​ User submits registration without completing mandatory additional
details.
o​ System prompts user to complete required fields before
submission.
* Supplier user profile is either approved or rejected with reason.
Postconditions:
* Approved Supplier users can access the platform.
* Rejected users can correct details and resubmit.
* EX-1: System Failure during Submission​
Exceptional
Flow:
If system error occurs, registration is not saved and user is prompted to
retry.
* EX-2: Admin Action Timeout​
If admin does not act, registration remains in Pending Approval state.
* Email ID and Mobile Number are unique identifiers system-wide.
Assumptions:
* Admin approval is mandatory for all Supplier registrations.
* Bank details are required for future payment processing.
* Supplier users cannot transact or participate in claims until approved.
Notes &
Comments
* This use case aligns with regulatory and compliance requirements.
* Future enhancements may include auto-verification of licenses.
* Supplier – Request for Quotes (RFQ) Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-SUP-002 — Supplier – Request for Quotes (RFQ) Management

```
Description
This use case describes how a Supplier views RFQs received from insurance
companies, submits quotations with pricing, taxes, discounts, delivery and payment
terms, manages quotation status, handles invoice creation, and processes
replacement or refund requests.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Supplier User
Date Created:
17/12/2025
* Supplier must be registered, approved, and logged in.
Preconditions:
* Insurance company must have sent an RFQ to the supplier.
* Supplier must have an active subscription (if applicable).
* Supplier logs into the system and navigates to the RFQ section
Triggers:
* System receives a new RFQ from an insurance company
* Part A – View & Search RFQs
o​ Supplier opens the RFQ Listing page.
o​ System displays all RFQs received with the following details:
▪​
RFQ Number
▪​
RFQ Date
▪​
Insurance Company Name
▪​
Item Names
▪​
Quantities
▪​
RFQ Status
o​ Supplier searches or filters RFQs using RFQ Number, Insurance
Company, Date Range, or Status.
* Part B – View RFQ Details
o​ Supplier selects an RFQ from the list.
o​ System displays complete RFQ details including:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
RFQ Number and Date
▪​
Insurance Company Name and Contact Details
▪​
Item list with quantity, requested specifications, and
delivery timeline
▪​
Additional RFQ details as provided by the client
* Part C – Prepare Quotation
o​ Supplier enters item-level unit price for each requested part.
o​ Supplier adds applicable taxes, fees, and surcharges.
o​ Supplier applies discounts (percentage or fixed), if applicable.
o​ Supplier specifies payment terms (advance %, credit period, or
full payment).
o​ Supplier specifies delivery terms, including delivery timeline,
shipping method, and charges.
o​ Supplier adds additional notes/comments, if required.
* Part D – Submit Quotation
o​ Supplier submits the completed quotation.
o​ System updates quotation status to Pending.
o​ Insurance company receives notification of quotation submission.
* Part E – Quotation Decision & Invoice Creation
o​ Insurance company reviews the quotation.
o​ Based on insurer action, quotation status is updated to:
▪​
Approved or
▪​
Rejected
o​ If quotation is Approved and payment is completed by insurance
company:
▪​
Supplier generates invoice
▪​
Invoice displays item summary, final price, tax, and
delivery charges
* Part F – Replacement & Refund Management
o​ Supplier receives replacement request within the allowed
replacement tenure.
o​ Supplier reviews the replacement request and:
▪​
Approves or
▪​
Rejects the request with comments/documents
o​ If replacement is not possible and item is refundable:
▪​
Supplier initiates refund processing
o​ Supplier tracks refund status as Pending, Settled, or Rejected.
* Part G – Notifications & Logging
o​ System sends notifications to supplier for:
▪​
New RFQ received
▪​
RFQ updates
▪​
Invoice payment confirmation
▪​
Replacement or refund requests
o​ System logs all RFQ, quotation, invoice, replacement, and refund
actions.
* A1 – RFQ Rejected
o​ Insurance company rejects quotation.
o​ System updates status to Rejected.
o​ Supplier receives rejection notification.
* A2 – Partial RFQ Response
Alternative
Flows:
o​ Supplier cannot quote for one or more items.
o​ Supplier leaves price blank or marks item as unavailable (as per
system rules).
o​ System validates and allows submission if permitted by
configuration.
* A3 – Replacement Rejected
o​ Supplier rejects replacement request with justification.
o​ System notifies insurance company.
* Quotation is submitted and tracked with defined statuses.
* Approved quotations result in invoice generation after payment.
Postconditions:
* Replacement/refund requests are processed and logged.
* RFQ lifecycle is auditable.
* EX1 – System Error During Quotation Submission​
System saves draft quotation (if enabled) or prompts supplier to retry.
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX2 – Unauthorized Access​
Supplier attempts to access RFQ not assigned to them → system blocks
access.
* RFQ statuses and delivery timelines are configurable by the client.
Assumptions:
* Replacement and refund tenure rules are defined by business policy.
* Invoice generation is allowed only after confirmed payment.
* RFQ evaluation and selection remain manual on insurer side.
Notes &
Comments
* This use case integrates with Orders, Payments, Notifications, and Audit
Trail modules.
* Exact RFQ detail fields will be finalized during FSD phase.
* Supplier – Inventory Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-SUP-003 — Supplier – Inventory Management

```
Description
This use case describes how a Supplier manages their inventory by viewing,
searching, adding, editing, deleting, and reviewing inventory items, including access
to deleted inventory records.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Supplier User
Date Created:
17/12/2025
* Supplier must be registered, approved, and logged in.
Preconditions:
Triggers:
* Supplier must have access rights to the Inventory module.
* Supplier navigates to the Inventory module from the dashboard.
* Part A – Inventory Listing
o​ Supplier opens the Inventory Listing page.
o​ System displays all inventory items added by the supplier.
o​ Each inventory item shows:
▪​
Item Name
▪​
Item SKU
▪​
Unit Price
▪​
Available Quantity
▪​
Status
* Part B – Search & Filter
o​ Supplier searches or filters inventory items using:
▪​
Item Name
▪​
SKU
▪​
Category
▪​
Basic Workflow:
Status
* Part C – Add Inventory
o​ Supplier selects Add Inventory.
o​ System displays item master information as read-only.
o​ Supplier enters:
▪​
Available Quantity
▪​
Unit Price
o​ Supplier saves the inventory item.
o​ System adds the item to the active inventory list.
* Part D – Edit Inventory
o​ Supplier selects an existing inventory item.
o​ Supplier edits allowed fields such as:
▪​
Unit Price
▪​
Available Quantity
▪​
Other editable details (as configured)
o​ Supplier saves changes.
o​ System updates the inventory item.
* Part E – Delete Inventory
o​ Supplier selects Delete for an inventory item.
o​ System removes the item from the active inventory list.
o​ System moves the item to the Deleted Inventory list (Recycle Bin).
* Part F – Deleted Inventory View
o​ Supplier navigates to Deleted Inventory.
o​ System displays all deleted inventory items for reference or audit.
* Part G – Inventory Detail Page
o​ Supplier opens an inventory item.
o​ System displays complete item details including all client-defined
fields.
* A1 – Invalid Data Entry
o​ Supplier enters invalid quantity or price.
o​ System shows validation error and prevents save.
Alternative
Flows:
* A2 – Delete Restriction
o​ Supplier attempts to delete an inventory item linked to an active
order.
o​ System restricts deletion and displays an error message.
* Inventory data is updated accurately.
Postconditions:
* Deleted inventory items are retained for audit/reference.
* System maintains current and historical inventory records.
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX1 – System Failure during Save/Delete​
System rolls back changes and displays an error message.
* Item master data is predefined and controlled by the system/client.
Assumptions:
* Deletion is logical (soft delete) and not permanent.
* Only supplier users can manage their own inventory.
* Restore functionality from Deleted Inventory may be added later if required.
Notes &
Comments
* Inventory changes may impact RFQs, Orders, and Availability calculations.
* This use case integrates with RFQ and Order modules.
* Supplier Auction Participation & Order Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-SUP-004 — Supplier Auction Participation & Order Management

```
Description
This use case describes how Supplier users view ongoing and upcoming auctions,
review auction and item details, pay the required deposit, participate in bidding,
track bids, and manage auction orders won through the platform, including
payment, refund and notifications.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Actors:
Preconditions:
Supplier User
*
*
*
*
Supplier user must be registered, approved, and logged in.
Auction must be active or scheduled.
Deposit fee rules must be configured for auction items.
Payment gateway must be available.
* Supplier user navigates to the Auctions module or receives auction
Triggers:
notification.
* Part A – View Auctions
o​ Supplier user opens the Auctions module.
o​ System displays all ongoing and upcoming auctions.
o​ Auction list displays:
▪​
Auction Title
▪​
Start Date & Time
▪​
End Date & Time
▪​
Item Count
▪​
Reserve Price
▪​
Auction Status
* Part B – View Auction Details
o​ Supplier user selects an auction.
o​ System displays complete auction details including:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Item information
▪​
Auction rules
▪​
Reserve price
▪​
Start / End time
* Part C – View Bid Details
o​ Supplier user views:
▪​
Current highest bid
▪​
Bid history
▪​
Bid timestamps
* Part D – Deposit Fee Payment
o​ Supplier user selects auction item(s) to bid on.
o​ System prompts for deposit fee payment.
o​ Supplier user completes deposit payment through online
payment gateway.
o​ System confirms successful deposit payment.
o​ Deposit amount is marked as refundable to the original payment
source.
* Part E – Place Bid
o​ Supplier user enters bid amount.
o​ System validates that:
▪​
Deposit payment is successful
▪​
Bid amount is greater than current highest bid
o​ System accepts bid and updates bid history.
o​ System notifies other bidders (if applicable).
* Part F – Auction Closure & Order Creation
o​ Auction reaches end date & time.
o​ System determines highest bidder.
o​ System automatically creates an Auction Order for winning
bidder.
o​ Supplier user receives winning confirmation notification.
* Part G – Order List & Details
o​ Supplier user navigates to Auction Orders.
o​ System displays all auction orders won by the Supplier with:
▪​
Order ID
▪​
Auction ID
▪​
Item Name
▪​
Winning Price
▪​
Order Status
▪​
Payment Status
o​ Supplier user selects an order to view details including:
▪​
Item information
▪​
Delivery details
▪​
Invoice
▪​
Payment requirements
* Part H – Order Payment
o​ Supplier user completes payment for the auction order via online
payment modes.
o​ System updates payment and order status accordingly.
* Part I – Notifications
o​ System sends notifications to Supplier for:
▪​
Deposit confirmation
▪​
Bid updates
▪​
Auction win confirmation
▪​
Payment reminders
* A1 – Bid Lower Than Current Highest Bid
o​ Supplier user places a bid lower than or equal to current highest
bid.
o​ System rejects the bid and displays validation message.
Alternative
Flows:
* A2 – Deposit Payment Failure
o​ Deposit payment fails.
o​ System blocks bidding and prompts retry.
* A3 – Auction Lost
o​ Supplier does not win the auction.
o​ Deposit refund is initiated to original payment source (as per refund
rules).
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Supplier successfully participates in auctions.
Winning bids generate orders automatically.
Payments and deposits are processed correctly.
Notifications keep Supplier informed at every stage.
* EX-1: Unauthorized Auction Access​
if Supplier is not approved or logged in, auction participation is blocked.
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX-2: System Failure During Bidding​
Assumptions:
* Deposit fee refund timelines are governed by platform policy.
* Auction rules are visible to all participants.
* One auction order is generated per winning item.
Notes &
Comments
* Deposit mechanism ensures serious bidders only.
* This use case integrates tightly with Orders and Payments modules.
* Can be extended to support auto-bidding in future phases.
Bid is not recorded and Supplier is notified to retry.
* Supplier Hot Deal Purchase & Order Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-SUP-005 — Supplier Hot Deal Purchase & Order Management

```
Description
This use case defines how Supplier users view active hot deals published by
insurers, filter and review item details, purchase hot deal items at fixed prices,
complete payments, view orders, and how the system maintains audit logs for
compliance and tracking.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Supplier User
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
*
*
*
*
Triggers:
* Supplier user navigates to the Hot Deals module.
Supplier user must be registered, approved, and logged in.
Hot deals must be active and published by insurers.
Inventory must have sufficient available quantity.
Approved payment modes must be configured.
* Part A – View Hot Deals
o​ Supplier user opens the Hot Deals section.
o​ System displays all active hot deals.
o​ Hot deal listing shows:
▪​
Item Name
▪​
Deal Price
▪​
Available Quantity
▪​
Start & End Date/Time
▪​
Status (to be confirmed by client)
* Part B – Apply Advanced Filters
o​ Supplier user applies filters using one or more of the following:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Part Name
▪​
Part Category
▪​
Vehicle Compatibility
▪​
Price Range
▪​
Expiry Time
▪​
Hot Deal ID
▪​
Status
o​ System refreshes the hot deal list based on applied filters.
* Part C – View Hot Deal Item Details
o​ Supplier user selects a hot deal item.
o​ System displays complete item details including:
▪​
Part Name
▪​
Part ID
▪​
Deal Price
▪​
Compatibility (vehicle models)
▪​
Condition
▪​
Available Quantity
* Part D – Purchase Hot Deal Item
o​ Supplier user selects the required Hot Deal.
o​ Supplier user proceeds to purchase the hot deal.
o​ System will show the total hot deal items, their quantity and total
price.
o​ Supplier user confirms the order.
* Part E – Payment Workflow
o​ System redirects the Supplier user to the payment gateway.
o​ Supplier user completes payment using approved payment
modes.
o​ System validates payment success.
o​ System automatically generates a Hot Deal Order.
* Part F – Order Listing & Order Details
o​ Supplier user navigates to Hot Deal Orders.
o​ System displays all hot deal orders with:
▪​
Order ID
▪​
Item Name
▪​
Deal Price
▪​
Order Date
▪​
Payment Status
▪​
Delivery Status
o​ Supplier user selects an order to view detailed information
including:
▪​
Invoice
▪​
Payment information
▪​
Delivery information
* Part G – Audit Trail
o​ System logs all actions related to hot deals including:
▪​
Viewing hot deals
▪​
Purchase actions
▪​
Payment completion
▪​
Order creation
o​ Audit logs are stored for compliance and tracking purposes.
Alternative
Flows:
* A1 – Insufficient Quantity
o​ Supplier user selects quantity exceeding availability.
o​ System displays validation error and prevents checkout.
* A2 – Payment Failure
o​ Payment fails or is cancelled.
o​ Order is not created.
o​ System prompts Supplier user to retry payment.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Successful hot deal purchase results in order creation.
Inventory quantity is reduced accordingly.
Order appears in Supplier hot deal order list.
Audit trail is maintained.
* EX1 – Unauthorized Access​
Exceptional
Flow:
If Supplier user is not approved or logged in, system blocks access to hot
deals.
* EX2 – System Error during Transaction​
Transaction is rolled back and no order is created.
Assumptions:
* Hot deal prices are fixed and non-negotiable.
* Delivery fulfilment is handled separately.
* Hot deal status values will be finalized by the client.
Notes &
Comments
* Hot deals enable fast procurement of parts and vehicles at fixed prices.
* This use case integrates with Inventory, Orders, and Payments modules.
* Future enhancements may include bulk purchase limits and expiry
reminders.
* Supplier – Subscription Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-SUP-006 — Supplier – Subscription Management

```
Description
This use case defines how a Supplier views, purchases, upgrades, downgrades,
renews, and manages subscription plans, including payment handling, access
control based on counters, notifications, and audit logging.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Supplier User
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
* Supplier user must be registered, approved, and logged in.
* Admin must have configured and published subscription plans.
* Payment gateway must be active.
Triggers:
* Supplier navigates to the Subscription section from the dashboard.
* Part A – View Subscription Plans
o​ Supplier opens the Active Plans Listing page.
o​ System displays only Active & Published subscription plans.
o​ Each plan displays:
▪​
Feature Group
▪​
Plan Name
▪​
Price
▪​
Billing Cycle
▪​
Plan Benefits
▪​
Counters
▪​
Validity
o​ Inactive or unpublished plans are not shown.
Basic Workflow:
* Part B – Purchase Subscription
o​ Supplier selects a subscription plan.
o​ Supplier proceeds to payment.
o​ System redirects supplier to the payment gateway.
o​ Supplier completes payment using available payment options.
o​ Upon successful payment:
▪​
System activates the subscription
▪​
System displays payment receipt, transaction ID, and
invoice
o​ Supplier receives subscription activation notification.
* Part C – Upgrade Subscription
o​ Supplier chooses Upgrade Plan from current subscription details.
o​ System displays eligible higher plans.
o​ Supplier selects a higher plan.
o​ System calculates price adjustment by deducting unused value of
the current plan from the new plan price.
o​ Supplier completes the differential payment.
o​ System activates the upgraded plan immediately.
o​ Supplier receives upgrade confirmation notification.
* Part D – Downgrade Subscription
o​ Supplier selects Downgrade Plan option.
o​ Supplier chooses a lower plan.
o​ System schedules downgrade to activate after expiry of the
current plan.
o​ System confirms downgrade scheduling to the supplier.
* Part E – Renewal
o​
o​
o​
o​
Supplier selects Renew Subscription before plan expiry.
Supplier completes payment.
Renewed plan is scheduled to activate after current plan expiry.
Supplier receives renewal confirmation notification.
* Part F – Current Plan & History
o​ Supplier views Current Plan Details, including:
▪​
Plan Name
▪​
Start Date
▪​
End Date
▪​
Counters
▪​
Status
o​ Supplier views Subscription History, including:
▪​
Past plans
▪​
Payment status
▪​
Receipts
▪​
Usage logs
* Part G – Access Control Enforcement
o​ System tracks feature usage against plan counters.
o​ If counters are exhausted:
▪​
System restricts access to related features
▪​
System displays usage limit message
* Part H – Notifications & Audit Trail
o​ System sends notifications for:
▪​
Plan activation
▪​
Expiry reminders
▪​
Renewals
▪​
Upgrades / Downgrades
▪​
Payment failures
o​ System logs all subscription actions for audit and compliance.
Alternative
Flows:
* A1 – Payment Failure
o​ Payment fails at gateway.
o​ System displays failure message.
o​ Subscription is not activated.
o​ Supplier receives payment failure notification.
* A2 – No Active Plans Available
o​ Admin has not published any active plans.
o​ System displays “No subscription plans available”.
* A3 – Upgrade Not Eligible
o​ Supplier selects a plan lower or equal to current plan.
o​ System restricts upgrade and shows validation message.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Supplier has an active, upgraded, downgraded, or renewed subscription.
Subscription usage is enforced based on counters.
Payment and subscription history is recorded.
Audit logs are maintained.
* EX1 – Access Violation​
Exceptional
Flow:
Supplier attempts to use features beyond allowed counters → System
blocks access.
* EX2 – System Error during Subscription Update​
System rolls back partial updates and notifies the user.
Assumptions:
* Subscription pricing and counter logic are defined by Admin.
* No unused counters are carried forward to the next cycle.
* All monetary calculations follow platform-defined proration rule
Notes &
Comments
* Upgrade pricing adjustment logic must be clearly documented.
* Downgrades are deferred to avoid feature disruption.
* This use case integrates with Payments, Notifications, and Access Control
modules.
* My Account (Supplier) – Profile
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-001 — Platform Administrator Onboarding

```
Description
This use case defines how a Platform Administrator registers on the system, verifies
their email, logs in securely, and manages password recovery following platform
security standards.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
* Platform Administrator registration is enabled by the system.
* Admin has access to a valid email address and mobile number.
Triggers:
* Admin navigates to the Admin Registration or Admin Login page.
* Part A – Admin Registration
o​ Platform Administrator opens the Registration page.
o​ Admin enters required registration details, including:
▪​
Name
▪​
Email ID
▪​
Mobile Number
▪​
Password
▪​
Other client-defined fields
o​ System validates the entered information.
o​ System creates the admin account in Pending Verification status.
Basic Workflow:
* Part B – Email Verification
o​ System sends an email verification link to the registered email ID.
o​ Admin verifies the email by clicking the link.
o​ System marks the email as verified.
o​ Admin account becomes eligible for first login.
* Part C – Admin Login
o​ Admin navigates to the Login page.
o​ Admin logs in using registered email ID and password.
o​ System authenticates credentials.
o​ On successful authentication, admin is redirected to the Admin
Dashboard.
* Part D – Forgot Password
o​ Admin selects Forgot Password on the login screen.
o​ Admin enters registered email ID.
o​ System sends a password reset link to the email ID.
o​ Admin verifies by opening reset link.
* Part E – Reset Password
o​ Admin enters a new password.
o​ System validates password against security rules:
▪​
Minimum length
▪​
Special characters
▪​
Upper/lower case rules
o​ System updates the password successfully.
o​ Admin receives confirmation and can log in with the new password.
Alternative
Flows:
* A1 – Email Verification Failure
o​ Admin is prompted to resend verification email.
* A2 – Invalid Login Credentials
o​ Admin enters incorrect email or password.
o​ System denies access and displays validation error.
* A3 – Password Rule Violation
o​ Admin enters a password that does not meet security rules.
o​ System displays password policy message and blocks reset.
Postconditions:
* Platform Administrator account is successfully created and verified.
* Admin can securely access the Admin Dashboard.
* Password reset actions are completed as per security policies.
* EX-1: System or Email Service Failure​
Exceptional
Flow:
Verification or reset emails are not delivered → system logs error and
allows retry.
Assumptions:
* Admin registration fields and validation rules are configurable by the client.
* Email verification is mandatory before first login.
Notes &
Comments
* Multi-factor authentication (MFA) can be added later if required.
* All admin login and password activities should be logged for security audit.
* This use case integrates with User Management, Security, and Audit Trail
modules.
* Admin – Manage Insurance Companies
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-002 — Admin – Manage Insurance Companies

```
Description
This use case describes how a Platform Administrator manages registered insurance
companies, including viewing and searching listings, reviewing detailed profiles,
monitoring analytics, approving onboarding and profile edits, activating or
deactivating accounts, exporting data, and maintaining audit logs.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Preconditions:
Date Created:
17/12/2025
* Platform Administrator must be registered, verified, and logged in.
* Insurance companies must exist in the system (registered or pending).
* Admin navigates to the Manage Insurance section from the Admin
Triggers:
Dashboard.
* Part A – Insurance Listing, Search & Filter
o​ Admin opens the Insurance Listing page.
o​ System displays a list of all registered insurance companies with:
▪​
Insurance Company Name
▪​
Date of Joining
▪​
Number of Branches
▪​
Subscription Plan Name
▪​
Account Status
o​ Admin searches insurance companies by Name, City, or Country.
o​ Admin applies filters using City, Country, Subscription Plan Name,
and Status.
* Part B – View Insurance Company Details
o​ Admin selects an insurance company from the list.
o​ System displays complete profile details, segmented into
sections:
o​ Company Information
▪​
System shows:
​ Company Name
Basic Workflow:
​ Date Joined
​ Email ID
​ Mobile Number
​ Account Status
o​ Subscription Information
▪​
System displays:
​ Active Plan Name
​ Plan Start Date
​ Plan End Date
​ Plan Type
​ Plan Amount
o​ Branch Information
▪​
System displays branch details including:
​ Branch Name
​ Branch ID
​ Address
​ Contact Person
* Part C – Analytics & Metrics
o​ Admin views Claim Analytics, including total number of claim
requests.
o​ Admin views Auction Analytics, including total auctions created.
o​ Admin views Hot Deals Analytics, including total hot deals
created.
o​ Admin views Garage Metrics, including:
▪​
Network garages
▪​
Number of claim requests per garage
o​ Admin views Inventory Summary, including:
▪​
Item Name
▪​
Unit Price
▪​
Quantity Available
* Part D – Verification & Account Control
o​ Admin reviews new insurance onboarding requests, including:
▪​
Uploaded documents
▪​
Registration details
o​
o​
o​
o​
Admin approves or rejects the onboarding request.
Admin reviews profile edit requests involving important details.
Admin verifies and approves or rejects profile updates.
Admin activates or deactivates insurance company accounts as
required.
* Part E – Export
o​ Admin exports insurance company data into Excel or CSV format.
o​ System exports data based on applied filters.
* Part F – Audit Logging
o​ System logs all admin actions including:
▪​
Review
▪​
Approval / Rejection
▪​
Activation / Deactivation
▪​
Data export
Alternative
Flows:
* A1 – No Records Found
o​ Admin applies filters resulting in no data.
o​ System displays “No insurance companies found”.
* A2 – Export Failure
o​ Export process fails due to system error.
o​ System displays error and allows retry.
* A3 – Rejection with Reason
o​ Admin rejects onboarding or profile edit request.
o​ Admin provides rejection reason.
o​ System notifies insurance company with reason.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Insurance company records are reviewed, updated, and controlled.
Account status and subscription visibility are updated correctly.
Analytics and reports reflect latest data.
All admin actions are auditable.
* EX1 – Unauthorized Action​
Exceptional
Flow:
Assumptions:
Notes &
Comments
Admin attempts an action without permission → system blocks and logs the
attempt.
* Analytics data is near real-time or refreshed at defined intervals.
* Subscription details are fetched from the Subscription module.
* Admin permissions are role-based.
* Deactivation may restrict login and system usage for insurance users.
* Exported data should respect data privacy and compliance rules.
* This use case integrates with Claims, Subscription, Auction, Hot Deals, and
Inventory modules.
* Admin – Manage Garages
Use Case ID:
```

```
View

Export

Exceptional
Flow:

and logs the attempt.

Comments
```

```
including listing and searching suppliers, viewing detailed supplier profiles,
monitoring subscription and RFQ activity, verifying registrations and profile edits,
activating or deactivating supplier accounts, exporting supplier data, and
maintaining a complete audit trail.

Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator

Date Created:
17/12/2025

* Admin navigates to the Manage Suppliers section from the Admin

Dashboard.

o​ System displays all registered suppliers with the following
information:
▪​
Supplier Name
▪​
Contact Information
▪​
Date of Joining
▪​
Subscription Plan Name
▪​
Number of RFQs
▪​
Account Status
o​ Admin searches suppliers by Name, City, or Country.
o​ Admin applies filters using City, Country, Plan Name, and Status
(Active, Inactive, Pending, Rejected).
* Part B – View Supplier Profile Details
o​ Admin selects a supplier from the list.

sections:
o​ Supplier Information
Basic Workflow:

​ Supplier Name
​ Date Joined
​ Email ID
​ Mobile Number
​ Account Status

System displays:

name, email changes, etc.).

Verification

Approval / Rejection

Export actions

Flow:

Admin attempts an action without proper authorization → system blocks
the action and logs the attempt.

* Admin permissions are role-based.
* Exported data follows platform data privacy policies.

Comments
* Deactivated suppliers cannot participate in RFQs or submit quotations.
* Profile changes involving critical identifiers require admin approval.

Use Case ID:
```

```
This use case defines how a Platform Administrator views, searches, filters, and
reviews all claim requests submitted on the platform. It includes accessing detailed
claim information, exporting claim data, and ensuring transparency and control over
claim lifecycle monitoring.

Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator

Date Created:
17/12/2025

* Suppliers must exist in the system (registered, pending, or inactive).
* Admin navigates to the Manage Claim Requests section from the Admin

Dashboard.

Insurance Company Name

Customer Name

Insurance Company
▪​
City
▪​
Country

Completed)

Flows:

Exceptional
Flow:
Assumptions:
criteria.”

* Admin successfully reviews and monitors claim requests.
* Exported claim data is available for reporting or audit purposes.
* No changes are made to claim data during view-only operations.

If admin session expires or permission is invalid, system redirects admin to
login and logs the event.
* Claim data is read-only for admin (no modification).
* RFQ linkage is visible only if initiated by insurer or system.
* Uploaded documents follow platform security and access rules.
* Admin role is limited to monitoring, verification, and reporting, not claim
Notes &

execution.
```

### PH-UC-ADM-006 — Admin – Manage Subscription Plans

```
Description
This use case defines how the Platform Administrator creates, views, searches,
edits, activates/deactivates, and deletes subscription plans, and controls their
visibility on the frontend.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Date Created:
Preconditions:
* Admin is logged into the platform.
* Feature Groups exist (for plan creation).
Triggers:
* Admin navigates to Manage Subscription → Plans.
17/12/2025
* Plan Listing & Search
o​ System displays all existing subscription plans with:
▪​
Feature Group
▪​
Plan Name
▪​
Pricing Type
▪​
Price
▪​
Billing Cycle
▪​
Frequency
▪​
Status
o​ Admin searches plans by Plan Name, Plan Type, or Billing Cycle.
* Add New Plan
o​ Admin clicks Add New Plan.
o​ Admin enters: Feature Group, Plan Name, Pricing Type, Price,
Billing Cycle, Frequency, Status.
Basic Workflow:
o​ System validates all mandatory fields.
o​ Plan is saved as Draft or Published.
* Edit Plan
o​ Admin edits a plan only if:
▪​
Plan is in Draft, OR
▪​
Plan is Published but not purchased by any user.
o​ System revalidates updated fields before saving.
* Delete Plan
o​ Admin deletes a plan only if:
▪​
Plan is in Draft state.
o​ Published plans cannot be deleted.
* Activate / Deactivate Plan
o​ Admin activates or deactivates a plan.
o​ Inactive plans:
▪​
Do not appear on frontend purchase screens.
▪​
Remain usable for already subscribed users until expiry.
▪​
Cannot be renewed.
* A1 – Edit Restricted Plan
o​ If Admin attempts to edit a plan that has already been purchased by
one or more users:
▪​
System displays validation message: “Plan cannot be edited
as it is already subscribed by users.”
▪​
Alternative
Flows:
Edit action is blocked.
* A2 – Delete Restricted Plan
o​ If Admin attempts to delete a published plan:
▪​
System prevents deletion.
▪​
Admin is prompted to deactivate the plan instead.
* A3 – Deactivate Active Plan
o​ If Admin deactivates an active plan:
Postconditions:
Exceptional
Flow:
▪​
Plan becomes unavailable for new purchases.
▪​
Existing subscribers continue until plan expiry.
* Subscription plan configuration is updated successfully.
* Frontend visibility is controlled as per plan status.
* Existing subscribers retain access until subscription expiry.
* EX01 - Mandatory Field Missing
o​ If required plan fields are not provided:
o​ System displays field-level validation errors.
o​ Plan is not saved.
* EX02 - Invalid Pricing Configuration
o​ If pricing or billing cycle values are invalid:
o​ System rejects the configuration.
o​ Admin must correct the inputs.
* EX03 - System Failure
o​ If system error occurs during save/update:
o​ Transaction is rolled back.
o​ Admin is notified with an error message.
Assumptions:
* Feature Groups must exist before creating subscription plans.
* Subscription pricing logic is handled by backend billing engine.
* Currency and tax calculations are managed externally or via configuration.
* Deleting published plans is intentionally restricted to avoid subscription
Notes &
Comments
inconsistencies.
* Deactivated plans should be clearly labeled in Admin UI.
o​ Admin – Manage User Subscriptions
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-007 — Admin – Manage User Subscriptions

```
Description
This use case defines how the Admin manages customer subscriptions including
creation, modification, activation/deactivation, renewals, upgrades, downgrades,
and payments.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Date Created:
Preconditions:
* Admin is logged in.
* Subscription plans exist.
* Users are registered on the platform.
Triggers:
* Admin navigates to Manage Subscription → Users.
17/12/2025
* Subscription Listing & Search
o​ System displays active subscriptions with:
▪​
Company Name
▪​
Feature Group
▪​
Plan Name
▪​
Billing Type
▪​
Address, City, State, Country, Pin Code
o​ Admin searches subscriptions by Plan Name, Plan Type, Billing
Cycle.
* Create Subscription
o​ Admin creates a new subscription by entering:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
User Name
▪​
Subscription Plan
▪​
Billing Type
▪​
Start Date
▪​
Status
▪​
Payment Method
▪​
Payment Reference Number
▪​
Payment Status
* Edit / Modify Subscription
o​ Admin edits existing subscription details.
o​ System immediately updates user access based on changes.
* Delete Subscription
o​ Admin deletes a subscription permanently.
o​ System immediately revokes user’s plan-based access.
* Activate / Deactivate Subscription
o​ Admin activates or deactivates a user’s subscription.
o​ Deactivated plans are hidden from frontend for that user.
* Renewal, Upgrade & Downgrade
o​ Admin tracks start date, expiry date, and renewal cycle.
o​ Admin manually upgrades or downgrades user plans.
o​ System adjusts access rules accordingly.
* Payment Handling
o​ Admin records payment via:
▪​
Online system payment, OR
▪​
Offline payment entry.
* A1 – Offline Payment Entry
o​ Admin selects Offline Payment.
o​ System allows manual entry of payment reference number and
Alternative
Flows:
Postconditions:
status.
o​ Subscription becomes active only after payment status is marked
successful.
* A2 – Manual Upgrade / Downgrade
o​ Admin manually upgrades/downgrades user plan.
o​ System updates access permissions immediately or post-expiry
(based on downgrade rules).
* A3 – Subscription Deactivation
o​ If Admin deactivates an active plan:
*
*
*
*
▪​
Plan becomes unavailable for new purchases.
▪​
Existing subscribers continue until plan expiry.
User subscription lifecycle is updated successfully.
User access permissions reflect the latest subscription state.
Payment and billing records are stored.
Notification events are triggered where applicable.
* EX01 - Invalid User or Plan
o​ If selected user or plan does not exist:
Exceptional
Flow:
▪​
System displays error.
▪​
Subscription creation/edit is aborted.
* EX02 - Payment Failure
o​ If payment fails:
▪​
Subscription remains inactive or pending.
▪​
Admin is notified.
* EX03 - Conflicting Subscription Dates
o​ If expiry or start date overlaps existing subscription:
▪​
Assumptions:
System prompts Admin to correct date values.
* Admin has full authority to override subscription lifecycle.
* Manual upgrades/downgrades do not require user confirmation.
* Payment reconciliation is handled externally if offline payments are used.
Notes &
Comments
* Immediate access revocation on delete is critical for security.
* Subscription changes must be reflected in real time.
o​ Admin – Manage Feature Groups
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-008 — Admin – Manage Feature Groups

```
Description
This use case defines how the Admin manages Feature Groups used for subscription
plan configuration.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
* Admin is logged into the platform.
Triggers:
* Admin navigates to Manage Subscription → Feature Groups.
* Feature Group Listing & Search
o​ System displays Feature Groups with:
▪​
ID
▪​
Name
▪​
Description
▪​
Created On
▪​
Status
o​ Admin searches by Name, Description, Status, or keywords.
* Add Feature Group
o​ Admin creates a Feature Group by entering Name, Description,
Basic Workflow:
and Status.
* Edit Feature Group
o​ Admin updates Feature Group details.
* Activate / Deactivate Feature Group
o​ Admin activates or deactivates Feature Group.
o​ Inactive Feature Groups:
▪​
Cannot be used for plan creation.
▪​
Are not visible to users.
* Delete Feature Group
o​ Admin deletes Feature Group only if:
▪​
It is not attached to any existing plan.
o​ System restricts deletion if linked to plans.
* A1 – Feature Group Linked to Plan
o​ If Admin attempts to delete a Feature Group already attached to a
plan:
Alternative
Flows:
Postconditions:
▪​
System blocks deletion.
▪​
Message displayed: “Feature Group is associated with an
existing plan.”
* A2 – Deactivate Feature Group
o​ Admin deactivates Feature Group.
o​ Group becomes unavailable for new plan creation.
* Feature Groups are created, updated, activated, or deactivated successfully.
* Feature Group visibility is updated in plan creation screens.
* Data integrity between Feature Groups and Plans is maintained.
* EX01 - Duplicate Feature Group Name
o​ If Admin enters a duplicate Feature Group name:
Exceptional
Flow:
▪​
System displays validation error.
▪​
Creation is blocked.
* EX02 - System Error
o​ If save/update fails:
▪​
No changes are committed.
▪​
Admin is informed.
Assumptions:
* Feature Groups act as logical containers for plan features.
* Each Feature Group can be associated with multiple plans.
Notes &
Comments
* Feature Group deletion rules are strict to prevent orphaned plans.
* Deactivation is preferred over deletion for audit and reporting.
* Admin – Manage Review & Rating
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-009 — Admin – Manage Review & Rating

```
Description
This use case allows the Platform Administrator to view, manage, moderate, edit,
and delete reviews and ratings submitted by Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users
against each other for claims and orders, ensuring platform transparency, data
accuracy, and misuse prevention.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Preconditions:
Date Created:
17/12/2025
* Admin is logged in with valid credentials.
* Reviews and ratings have been submitted by platform users.
* Admin has permission to manage reviews & ratings.
* Admin navigates to Manage Review & Rating module from Admin
Triggers:
Basic Workflow:
Dashboard
* Admin accesses the Review & Rating Listing screen.
* System displays a list of all reviews with:
o​ Review ID
o​ User Name
o​ User Type
o​ Rating (1–5)
o​ Review Text
o​ Order ID / Claim ID Reference
o​ Date Added
o​ Status (Active/Inactive)
* Admin searches reviews using keywords or IDs.
* Admin applies filters (Rating range, Reviewed Entity type).
* Admin selects a review to view full details.
* System displays:
o​ Reviewer profile
o​ Reviewed entity (Insurance / Garage / Supplier)
o​ Claim / Order reference
o​ Timestamps
* Admin performs one of the following actions:
o​ Edit rating score
o​ Delete review
o​ Activate / Inactivate review
* System updates the review status/details.
* System updates aggregated ratings where applicable.
* System logs all actions in audit trail.
* A1 – Edit Review Rating
o​ Admin edits the rating score due to incorrect submission.
o​ System validates rating range (1–5).
o​ Updated rating is saved.
o​ Aggregated rating is recalculated automatically.
* A2 – Inactivate Review
o​ Admin marks a review as Inactive instead of deleting.
o​ Review no longer appears in public views.
o​ Review remains stored for audit purposes.
Alternative
Flows:
* A2 – Delete Review
o​ Admin deletes a review due to inappropriate or fraudulent content.
o​ Review is permanently removed.
o​ Aggregated rating is recalculated.
* A2 – Aggregated Rating View
o​ Admin views cumulative ratings per entity.
o​ System displays average rating and total review count for:
▪​
Insurance
▪​
Garage
▪​ Supplier
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
*
Reviews and ratings are moderated as per admin action.
Aggregated ratings reflect latest approved reviews.
Inactive or deleted reviews are excluded from frontend display.
All actions are recorded in audit logs.
Data integrity between reviews and claim/order references is maintained.
* EX1 – System Failure
o​ If system error occurs during edit/delete:
Exceptional
Flow:
Assumptions:
▪​
Changes are rolled back.
▪​
Admin is notified with error message.
* Reviews are submitted only after valid claim/order completion.
* Each review is linked to a valid Claim ID or Order ID.
* Aggregated ratings are calculated based on non deleted reviews only.
* Review moderation is critical to prevent misuse or biased ratings.
* Soft delete (Inactive) is preferred over hard delete where compliance
Notes &
Comments
requires record retention.
* Multi-flow review support ensures bidirectional transparency among all
business entities.
* Future enhancement may include auto-flagging abusive language using AI
moderation.
* Admin – Manage Contact Inquiry & Tasks
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-010 — Admin – Manage Contact Inquiry & Tasks

```
Description
This use case enables the Platform Administrator to manage contact inquiries
submitted by platform users, convert inquiries into actionable tasks, manually
create and manage tasks, track task status, and maintain an audit trail for all inquiry
and task-related actions
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Preconditions:
Date Created:
17/12/2025
* User (Insurance/Garage/Supplier) has access to the Contact Inquiry form.
* Admin is logged in with appropriate administrative permissions.
* Notification services (email/system alerts) are active.
* A user submits a Contact Inquiry​
Triggers:
OR
* Admin navigates to Manage Contact Inquiry & Tasks module.
* Contact Inquiry Flow
o​ User submits a contact inquiry via the application form.
o​ System captures inquiry details and generates a unique Inquiry
ID.
o​ System marks inquiry status as Open.
o​ Admin receives system notification for new inquiry.
o​ Admin opens Inquiry Listing screen.
o​ System displays inquiry list with:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Inquiry ID
▪​
Name
▪​
Email
▪​
Mobile
▪​
Subject
▪​
Submitted On
▪​
Status (Open / Assigned / Closed)
o​ Admin selects an inquiry to view full details.
o​ System displays inquiry message and contact information.
* Task Management Flow
o​ For admin Inquiry will be treated as a task or will create a task
manually and will assign to an admin user.
o​ System updates inquiry status to Assigned.
o​ Assigned admin receives task notification.
o​ Admin updates task status and adds completion comments.
o​ Upon task completion, admin closes the task.
o​ System notifies the original user that the inquiry is closed.
* A1 – Manual Task Creation
o​ Admin creates a task without linking to any inquiry.
o​ Task is saved with status Open.
o​ Task appears in Task Listing.
* A2 – Delete Inquiry
o​ Admin deletes an inquiry.
o​ System moves inquiry to Archived state (if enabled).
Alternative
Flows:
o​ Inquiry is removed from active listings.
* A3 – Reassign Task
o​ Admin updates the assigned admin user.
o​ System sends notification to the new assignee.
o​ Task history is updated.
* A4 – Search & Filter
o​ Admin searches inquiries or tasks using keywords.
o​ Admin filters inquiries by status (Open/Assigned/Closed).
o​ System refreshes listing accordingly.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
*
Contact inquiries are properly tracked and managed.
Tasks are created, assigned, updated, and closed successfully.
Inquiry status reflects task lifecycle accurately.
Notifications are sent at key stages.
All actions are logged for audit and compliance.
* EX1 – Task Assignment Failure
o​ If assigned admin user is inactive:
Exceptional
Flow:
▪​
System blocks assignment.
▪​
Admin is prompted to select another user.
* EX1 – System Failure
o​ If system error occurs during save/update:
▪​
Transaction is rolled back.
▪​
Admin is notified with an error message.
Assumptions:
* All inquiries are non-financial and informational in nature.
* Inquiry deletion does not remove audit logs.
* Task closure implies inquiry resolution when linked.
Notes &
Comments
*
*
*
*
Inquiry-to-task conversion ensures accountability and traceability.
Archive mechanism supports compliance without cluttering active data.
Future enhancement may include SLA tracking and priority tagging.
Tasks may later be extended to support attachments and due dates.
* Admin – Manage Auction
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-011 — Admin – Manage Auction

```
Description
This use case enables the Platform Administrator to view, search, filter, and inspect
all auctions created across Insurance companies, Garages, and Suppliers. The Admin
can review auction details, bidding activity, item-level information, and audit logs to
ensure transparency, compliance, and platform governance.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
*
*
*
*
Triggers:
* Admin navigates to Manage Auction module from the Admin Dashboard.
Admin is logged in with valid administrative credentials.
At least one auction exists on the platform.
Auctions have been created by Insurance, Garage, or Supplier users.
System has bid and auction activity data available.
Basic Workflow:
Alternative
Flows:
* Admin accesses the Auction Listing screen.
* System displays a complete list of auctions with the following fields:
o​ Auction Name / Title
o​ Created By (Insurance / Garage / Supplier)
o​ Start Date
o​ End Date
o​ Auction Status (Upcoming / Ongoing / Closed)
o​ Reserve Price
o​ Number of Items
o​ Total Bids Received
* Admin searches auctions using keywords.
* Admin applies filters based on:
o​ Status
o​ Date Range
o​ Created By
* Admin selects an auction from the list.
* System displays full Auction Details, including:
o​ Auction title and schedule
o​ Starting bid and reserve price
o​ Seller information
o​ Auction terms and conditions
* System displays Item-Level Details for the auction:
o​ Item Name
o​ SKU
o​ Item Condition
o​ Item Status
o​ Reserve Price
o​ Highest Bid
* System displays Bid History with timestamps and bidder details.
* Admin navigates to Audit Trail section.
* System displays activity logs:
o​ Auction Created
o​ Auction Edited
o​ Auction Closed
o​ Winner Declared
* A1 – View Closed Auction
o​ Admin selects a closed auction.
o​ System displays final winning bid and winner details.
* A2 – View Auction Without Bids
o​ Admin opens an auction with no bids.
o​ System displays “No bids placed yet” message.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Admin successfully views and audits auction activity.
Auction and bid data remain unchanged (view-only).
Platform transparency and compliance are ensured.
Admin actions (view/search/filter) are logged for tracking.
* EX1 – Data Loading Failure
o​ If bid or item data fails to load:
Exceptional
Flow:
Assumptions:
Notes &
Comments
*
*
*
*
▪​
System displays partial data with warning message.
▪​
Error is logged for technical review.
Admin has read-only access to auctions (no edit/delete).
Auction status is system-driven and cannot be modified by Admin.
Bid history is immutable once auction is live or closed.
All auctions follow a standardized auction lifecycle.
* This module is primarily for monitoring, compliance, and dispute resolution.
* Audit trail visibility supports legal and regulatory requirements.
* Future enhancement may include auction suspension or manual
intervention rights for Admin.
* Reporting and analytics can be extended from this module.
* Admin – Manage Hot Deals
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-012 — Admin – Manage Hot Deals

```
Description
This use case allows the Platform Administrator to view, search, filter, and inspect all
Hot Deals created by Insurance companies, Garages, and Suppliers. The Admin can
monitor deal performance, item details, pricing, availability, lifecycle status, and
audit history for governance and operational oversight.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
*
*
*
*
Triggers:
* Admin navigates to Manage Hot Deals module from the Admin Dashboard.
Admin is logged in with valid administrative credentials.
At least one Hot Deal exists on the platform.
Hot Deals are created by Insurance, Garage, or Supplier users.
Item and purchase data are available in the system.
Basic Workflow:
* Admin accesses the Hot Deals Listing screen.
* System displays a list of all Hot Deals with the following details:
o​ Hot Deal Name
o​ Item Name
o​ Created By (Insurance / Garage / Supplier)
o​ Deal Price
o​ Start Date
o​ End Date
o​ Status (Active / Inactive / Upcoming / Expired)
o​ Quantity
o​ Number of Purchases
* Admin searches Hot Deals using keyword-based search.
* Admin applies filters:
o​ Status
o​ Date Range
o​ Created By
o​ Price Range
* Admin selects a Hot Deal from the list.
* System displays Hot Deal Details, including:
o​ Hot Deal Name
o​ Description
o​ Deal Price
o​ Start & End Date/Time
o​ Status
o​ Created By
* System displays Item-Level Details linked to the Hot Deal:
o​ Item Name
o​ SKU
o​ Quantity Available
o​ Unit Price
o​ Item Images
o​ Item Specifications
* Admin navigates to the Audit Log section.
* System displays activity logs for the Hot Deal:
o​ Created
o​ Edited
o​ Activated
o​ Deactivated
o​ Expired
Alternative
Flows:
* A1 – View Expired Hot Deal
o​ Admin selects an expired Hot Deal.
o​ System displays deal details in read-only mode.
o​ Purchase-related fields are locked.
* A2 – Hot Deal with Zero Purchases
o​ Admin views a Hot Deal with no purchases.
o​ System displays “No purchases yet” message.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Admin successfully views and audits Hot Deal information.
No data modifications are made (view-only access).
System logs admin access actions.
Deal lifecycle remains unchanged.
* EX1 – Item Data Loading Failure
o​ If item details fail to load:
Exceptional
Flow:
Assumptions:
*
*
*
*
▪​
System displays partial deal information.
▪​
Error is logged for technical investigation.
Admin has read-only access to Hot Deals.
Hot Deal lifecycle (Active, Expired, etc.) is system-driven.
Quantity and purchase count are updated in real time.
Hot Deals are always associated with at least one item.
* This module is primarily used for monitoring promotional activity and
compliance.
Notes &
Comments
* Audit logs support dispute handling and operational reviews.
* Future enhancements may include manual deal suspension or override
pricing controls for Admin.
* Analytics (conversion rate, revenue impact) can be layered on top of this
module.
* Admin – Manage Admin Users, Roles, and Groups
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-013 — Admin – Manage Admin Users, Roles, and Groups

```
Description
This use case enables the Super Admin to manage Admin Groups, Admin Roles, and
Admin User Accounts within the platform. It covers creation, viewing, searching,
filtering, editing, activation/inactivation, permission mapping, assignment, deletion
controls, and audit logging to ensure secure access control and governance.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Preconditions:
*
*
*
*
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Super Admin is authenticated and logged in.
Permission management framework is configured.
At least one permission set exists in the system.
Super Admin has access to User, Role, and Group management modules.
* Super Admin navigates to Admin → Users, Roles & Groups from the Admin
Triggers:
Dashboard.
* Manage Admin Groups
o​ Admin opens Admin Groups Listing.
o​ System displays groups with:
▪​
Group Name
▪​
Description
▪​
No. of Users Assigned
▪​
Created On
▪​
Status (Active/Inactive)
o​ Admin searches or filters groups.
o​ Admin creates a new group by entering:
Basic Workflow:
o​
o​
o​
o​
▪​
Group Name
▪​
Description
▪​
Status
▪​
Assigned Users
Admin edits group details and permissions.
System maps permissions → users → groups.
Admin activates/deactivates a group.
Admin views group details including:
▪​
Permissions
▪​
Users
▪​
Created By / Created On
o​ System logs all group-related actions.
* Manage Admin Roles
o​ Admin navigates to Admin Roles Listing.
o​ System displays roles with:
▪​
Role Name
▪​
Description
▪​
Assigned Permissions Count
▪​
Users Assigned
▪​
Created On
▪​
Status
o​ Admin searches or filters roles.
o​ Admin creates a role by defining:
▪​
Role Name
▪​
Description
▪​
Status
▪​
Permissions
o​
o​
o​
o​
o​
Admin edits role permissions.
Admin activates/deactivates roles.
Admin views detailed role information.
System restricts deletion if role is assigned.
All role actions are logged.
* Manage Admin Accounts (Users)
o​ Admin opens Admin Account Listing.
o​ System displays:
▪​
Admin Name
▪​
Email
▪​
Mobile
▪​
Assigned Group
▪​
Assigned Role
▪​
Created On
▪​
Last Login
▪​
Status
o​ Admin searches and filters admin users.
o​ Admin creates a new admin account by entering:
▪​
Name
▪​
Email
▪​
Mobile
▪​
Password
▪​
Group
▪​
Role
▪​
Status
o​ Admin edits admin account details.
o​ Admin assigns or updates:
▪​
Group
▪​
Role
o​ Admin activates/deactivates admin accounts.
o​ Admin views full admin user details.
o​ Admin user can reset password using Forgot Password.
o​ System logs all admin account actions.
* A1 – Delete Group with Assigned Users
o​ Admin attempts to delete a group.
o​ System checks assigned users.
o​ If users exist → deletion blocked with message.
* A2 – Delete Role Assigned to Users
o​ Admin tries to delete a role.
o​ System prevents deletion and prompts reassignment.
Alternative
Flows:
* A3 – Assign Inactive Group or Role
o​ Admin attempts assignment.
o​ System blocks assignment and displays validation message.
* A4 – Deactivate Admin User
o​ Admin deactivates a user.
o​ User is immediately logged out and blocked from login.
* A5 - Password Reset
o​ Admin user selects “Forgot Password”.
o​ System validates email and sends reset link.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX1 – Duplicate Role or Group Name
o​ System rejects creation.
o​ Error message displayed.
* EX2 – Unauthorized Access
o​ User lacks permission.
o​ System blocks action and logs incident.
* EX3 – System Failure During Save
o​ Changes are rolled back.
o​ Error message shown to admin.
* EX4 – Invalid Email or Mobile
o​ Validation error shown.
o​ Admin must correct details.
Admin users, roles, and groups are updated as per actions.
Access permissions are enforced immediately.
Inactive users/groups/roles are restricted.
Audit trail is recorded for all changes.
Assumptions:
Notes &
Comments
*
*
*
*
*
Super Admin has full access across modules.
Permission mapping follows a bottom-up approach.
Group and role assignment affects access immediately.
Email service is configured for password reset.
Deletion restrictions are mandatory for security.
*
*
*
*
This module forms the core security and access control layer.
Audit logs support compliance and internal audits.
Group + Role combination provides flexible permission control.
Future enhancements may include:
o​ MFA for admin login
o​ Role cloning
o​ Temporary access roles
* Admin – Manage Master Attributes
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-014 — Admin – Manage Master Attributes

```
Description
This use case enables the Admin to centrally manage all master attributes required
across the platform. This includes Items, Item Categories & Sub-Categories, Vehicle
Hierarchy (Category → Type → Make → Model → Variant), Manufacture Origin,
Damage Category & Sub-Category, and User Speciality.
All master attributes are owned and controlled by Admin, ensuring data consistency
across Inventory, Claims, RFQs, Auctions, and Hot Deals.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
* Admin is authenticated and logged into the platform.
* Admin has Master Data management permission.
* Attribute dependency hierarchy is configured (Vehicle → Make → Model →
Triggers:
* Admin navigates to Master Attributes from the Admin Dashboard
Variant).
* System supports audit logging.
* Manage Master Items
o​ Admin views Item Listing with:
o​
o​
o​
o​
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Item Name
▪​
SKU / Code
▪​
Category & Sub-Category
▪​
Status
Admin searches and filters items.
Admin views full item details.
Admin activates/deactivates items.
Inactive items are hidden from:
▪​
Inventory
▪​
Claims
▪​
RFQs
▪​
Auctions
▪​
Hot Deals
o​ Admin exports item list.
o​ System logs all actions.
* Manage Item Categories & Sub-Categories
o​ Admin views category and sub-category listings.
o​ Admin adds, edits, activates/deactivates categories.
o​ Admin deletes categories only if:
▪​
No active items are linked.
o​ Inactive categories are hidden from creation forms.
o​ System maintains audit logs.
* Manage Vehicle Master Attributes​
(Category → Type → Make → Model → Variant)
o​ Admin manages:
▪​
Vehicle Category
▪​
Vehicle Type
▪​
Vehicle Make
▪​
Vehicle Model
▪​
Vehicle Variant
o​ Admin can:
▪​
Add
▪​
Edit
▪​
Activate / Deactivate
▪​
View details
o​ Admin deletes attributes only if:
▪​
*
*
*
*
Not linked to active inventory, RFQs, claims, auctions, or
hot deals.
o​ Inactive vehicle attributes are hidden from inventory creation.
o​ All changes are logged.
Manage Manufacture Origin
o​ Admin views and manages manufacture origins.
o​ Admin links origin with vehicle hierarchy.
o​ Inactive origins are excluded from vehicle creation.
o​ Deletion is restricted if used.
o​ Audit trail is maintained.
Manage Damage Categories & Sub-Categories
o​ Admin manages damage categories and sub-categories.
o​ Admin controls activation/deactivation.
o​ Inactive damage attributes are hidden from claim flows.
o​ Deletion restricted if referenced.
o​ System logs all actions.
Manage Speciality
o​ Admin manages user specialities.
o​ Admin assigns or removes speciality from users.
o​ Speciality updates reflect immediately in system workflows.
User-Initiated Attribute Request
o​ Insurance / Garage / Supplier requests a missing item/attribute.
o​ System creates a pending request.
o​ Admin reviews request.
o​ Admin approves or rejects.
o​ Approved attributes become created and activated.
o​ Action is logged.
* A1 – Delete Attribute in Use
o​ Admin attempts deletion.
o​ System blocks action and displays dependency message.
* A2 – Deactivate Attribute
o​ Attribute becomes unavailable in creation forms.
Alternative
Flows:
o​ Existing references remain intact.
* A3 – Duplicate Attribute Code
o​ System prevents save.
o​ Validation error shown.
* A4 – User Attribute Request Rejected
o​ Request marked as rejected.
o​ User notified with reason.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
*
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX1 – Unauthorized Action
o​ Admin lacks permission.
o​ System blocks action and logs attempt.
* EX2 – System Error During Save
o​ Transaction rolled back.
o​ Error notification shown.
Assumptions:
*
*
*
*
*
Notes &
Comments
* This module is the foundation of all transactional workflows.
* Attribute integrity directly impacts:
o​ Claims
o​ RFQs
o​ Auctions
o​ Inventory
o​ Hot Deals
Master attributes are updated centrally.
Attribute visibility reflects status immediately.
Dependency integrity is preserved.
Approved attributes are available platform-wide.
Complete audit trail is maintained.
Only Admin can manage master attributes.
Deactivation is preferred over deletion.
Attribute hierarchy is mandatory.
User-raised requests do not auto-approve.
Audit trail is mandatory for compliance.
* Admin – Manage Order
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-015 — Admin – Manage Orders

```
Description
This use case allows the Admin to centrally view, monitor, and audit all orders
placed across the platform between Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users,
including Auction Orders and Hot Deal Orders. The Admin has full visibility into
order lifecycle, payment status, fulfillment progress, replacements, and refunds for
governance and dispute handling.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Preconditions:
*
*
*
*
Triggers:
* Admin navigates to Orders from the Admin Dashboard
Admin is logged into the platform.
Admin has Order Management permission.
Orders exist in the system.
Payment and shipment statuses are already updated by respective
workflows.
* Audit logging is enabled.
* Order Listing & Classification
o​ Admin views a consolidated Order Listing.
o​ System displays orders with classification by type:
▪​
Insurance → Supplier
▪​
Garage → Supplier
▪​
Insurance → Garage
▪​
Auction Orders
▪​
Hot Deal Orders
o​ Each order record shows high-level details (Order ID, Buyer, Seller,
Order Type, Date, Status).
* Search & Filter Orders
o​ Admin searches orders using standard search parameters.
o​ Admin applies filters (order type, status, date range, buyer, seller,
etc.).
o​ System refreshes the listing based on applied criteria.
* View Order Details
o​ Admin selects an order.
o​ System displays full order details including:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Buyer information
▪​
Seller information
▪​
Item details (name, SKU, quantity, unit price)
▪​
Shipping information
▪​
Billing information
▪​
Payment method & payment status
▪​
Taxes, discounts, and final amount
▪​
Order creation and fulfillment dates
* Order Activity Timeline
o​ Admin views the Order Activity Timeline, showing:
▪​
Order Placed
▪​
Payment Received
▪​
Order Processed
▪​
Dispatched
▪​
Delivered
▪​
Replacement Requested / Approved / Completed
▪​
Refund Initiated / Completed (if applicable)
* Export Orders
o​ Admin exports:
▪​
Order listing
▪​
Individual order details
o​ System generates Excel/CSV files respecting applied filters.
* Audit & Traceability
o​ System logs all admin views and exports for audit compliance.
* A1 – Order with Replacement or Refund
o​ Order contains replacement or refund requests.
o​ Admin can view:
▪​
Reason for replacement/refund
▪​
Approval status
▪​
Associated documents
o​ Timeline reflects additional steps.
Alternative
Flows:
* A2 – Auction or Hot Deal Order
o​ Order type is Auction or Hot Deal.
o​ System displays:
▪​
Auction ID / Hot Deal ID
▪​
Winning bid price or deal price
▪​
Auction/Deal validity timeline
* A3 – Partial Fulfillment
o​ Order contains multiple items.
o​ Some items are delivered while others are pending.
o​ Timeline reflects item-level fulfillment.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Admin gains complete visibility into order lifecycle.
Order data remains unchanged (read-only).
Exported files are generated successfully.
Audit logs capture admin access and actions.
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX1 – Order Data Not Found
o​ Admin opens an invalid or deleted order.
o​ System displays error message and redirects to listing.
* EX2 – Export Failure
o​ System fails to generate export file.
o​ Error message shown and logged.
* EX3 – Unauthorized Access
o​ Admin lacks permission.
o​ System blocks access and logs the attempt.
Assumptions:
*
*
*
*
*
Notes &
Comments
* This module is critical for:
o​ Dispute resolution
o​ Compliance & audit
o​ Performance monitoring
* Recommended future enhancements:
o​ Order-level annotations/comments for Admin
o​ SLA breach indicators
o​ Advanced analytics dashboard (order value, delays, disputes)
Admin has read-only access to orders.
Order modification is handled only via transactional workflows.
Replacement and refund approvals are managed in respective modules.
Order timeline is system-generated and immutable.
Export functionality respects data access rules.
* Admin – Manage Payments
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-ADM-016 — Admin – Manage Payments

```
Description
This use case enables the Admin to centrally view, monitor, search, filter, and export
all payment and refund transactions occurring across the platform between
Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users. The Admin can differentiate payments based
on purpose (orders, subscriptions, auctions, hot deals, etc.) and track refunds for
audit, compliance, and dispute resolution.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Preconditions:
*
*
*
*
*
Date Created:
17/12/2025
Admin is authenticated and logged into the platform.
Admin has Payment Management access rights.
Payments and refunds exist in the system.
Payment gateway integration is active.
Audit logging is enabled.
Triggers:
* Admin navigates to Payments from the Admin Dashboard
* Payment Listing
o​ Admin views a consolidated Payment Listing.
o​ System displays all payments made across the platform.
o​ Each payment record displays:
▪​
Transaction ID
▪​
Payer (Insurance / Garage / Supplier)
▪​
Payee
▪​
Payment Amount
▪​
Payment Date
▪​
Payment Status
* Payment Type Differentiation
o​ System categorizes each payment by purpose, such as:
Basic Workflow:
▪​
Order Payments
▪​
Auction Payments
▪​
Hot Deal Payments
▪​
Subscription Payments
▪​
Claim-related Payments
o​ Admin can clearly identify why a payment was made.
* View Payment Details
o​ Admin selects a payment record.
o​ System displays full payment details including:
▪​
Transaction ID
▪​
Order Reference ID
▪​
Invoice Number(s)
▪​
Payment Gateway Reference Number
▪​
Payment Method
▪​
Payer and Payee details
▪​
Taxes, discounts, and final amount
▪​
Payment status and timestamps
* Search & Filter Payments
o​ Admin searches payments using standard search parameters.
o​ Admin applies filters such as:
▪​
Date range
▪​
Payment status
▪​
Payment type
▪​
Payer / Payee
o​ System refreshes listing accordingly.
* Refund Listing
o​ Admin navigates to Refund Listing.
o​ System displays all refund transactions with:
▪​
Refund ID
▪​
Original Payment ID
▪​
Order ID
▪​
Refund Date
▪​
Refund Amount
▪​
Refund Reason
▪​
Refund Status
* Export Payments & Refunds
o​ Admin exports:
▪​
Payment listing
▪​
Payment details
▪​
Refund listing
o​ System generates CSV/Excel files based on applied filters.
* Audit Logging
o​ System logs all admin access, views, and export actions.
Alternative
Flows:
* A1 – Partial Refund
o​ Refund is issued for part of the payment.
o​ System displays refunded amount and remaining balance.
* A2 – Failed or Reversed Payment
o​ Payment status shows Failed / Reversed.
o​ Admin can view gateway failure reason and timestamps
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Admin gains complete visibility into all payment and refund transactions.
No payment data is modified (read-only).
Exported files are successfully generated.
Audit logs capture admin activity.
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX1 – Export Failure
o​ Export process fails.
o​ System displays error message and logs incident.
* EX2 – Unauthorized Access
o​ Admin without permission attempts access.
o​ System blocks action and logs attempt.
Assumptions:
*
*
*
*
*
Notes &
Comments
* This module is critical for:
o​ Financial audits
o​ Regulatory compliance
o​ Dispute and reconciliation
* Future enhancements may include:
o​ Payment reconciliation reports
o​ Settlement cycle tracking
o​ Automated anomaly detection
Admin has read-only access to payment data.
Payment initiation and refunds are managed by transactional workflows.
Payment gateway is the single source of truth for transaction status.
Refunds follow configured refund policies and tenure.
Currency and tax calculations are handled at transaction time.
6.6​ Considered Requirements
* Credit / Payment Terms Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-CR-001 — Credit / Payment Terms Management

```
Description
This use case defines how the system manages claim-wise credit and payment
terms, including configurable payment cycles, credit tracking, partial payments, and
automated notifications for overdue payments.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Preconditions:
Date Created:
09/02/2026
* A claim exists and is approved.
* Buyer and Seller accounts are active.
* Credit/payment time terms are configured for the claim.
* An order is generated under the claim.
* Order is generated for a claim.
Triggers:
* Payment term cycle (30/60/90 days) starts.
* A payment or partial payment may or may not recorded.
Basic Workflow:
* System allows the insurer to define payment terms at a claim level (not
global).
* System assigns a payment cycle (e.g., 30, 60, or 90 days) to the invoice.
* System tracks the due date and remaining credit period automatically.
* Buyer makes payment (full or partial) within or without the allowed credit
cycle.
* System updates payment status and outstanding balance accordingly.
* If there is any outstanding payments are there and payment cycle is
about to end then buyer and supplier both will get a payment reminder
notification.
* Once full payment is received, the invoice is marked as Paid and the claim
financials are updated.
* A1 – Partial Payment Flow:
Alternative
Flows:
o​ Buyer makes a partial payment before due date.
```
