```
o​ System records partial payment and recalculates the outstanding
amount.
o​ Remaining balance continues to be tracked until full settlement.
* A2 – Early Payment Flow
o​ Buyer pays full amount before due date.
o​ System immediately marks invoice as Paid and closes credit cycle.
Postconditions:
* Payment status is updated (Paid / Partially Paid / Overdue).
* Payment history is recorded.
* Notifications are sent where outstanding payment is there and payment
cycle is over.
* EX1 – Payment Overdue
Exceptional
Flow:
o​ Payment cycle expires without full payment.
o​ System marks invoice as Overdue.
o​ System sends notifications to both Buyer and Seller.
o​ Overdue amount is displayed in pending payments dashboard.
* EX2 – Payment Failure
o​ Payment attempt fails due to technical or financial reasons.
o​ System logs the failure and notifies both parties.
o​ Buyer is prompted to retry payment within allowed credit window.
Assumptions:
* Payment terms are configurable per claim.
* System supports multiple payment cycles (30/60/90 days).
* Partial payments are allowed and tracked.
* Notifications are enabled for overdue payments.
Notes &
Comments
* This use case supports financial compliance and cash-flow management.
* Overdue payments will not restrict future transactions.
* Revenue Management
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-CR-002 — Revenue Management

```
Description
This use case defines how the system captures, categorizes, filters, and reports all
payments received by PartsHub, enabling administrators to view revenue by date
range and payment type such as Platform Fee, Subscription Fee, Auction Fee, and
others received by PartsHub.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Preconditions:
Date Created:
09/02/2026
* Payments have been successfully received and recorded in the system.
* Admin user is authenticated and authorized to access financial reports.
* Admin navigates to the Revenue Management module.
Triggers:
Basic Workflow:
* Admin applies filters (date range, payment type).
* Admin accesses the Revenue Management dashboard.
* System displays a consolidated list of all payments received by PartsHub.
* Admin applies filters such as:
o​ From Date / To Date
o​ Payment Type (Platform Fee, Subscription Fee, Auction Fee, Hot
Deal Fee, etc.)
* System refreshes the report based on applied filters.
* Admin views total revenue, category-wise breakdown, and individual
transaction details.
* Admin exports the revenue report if required.
* A1 – No Filters Applied
Alternative
Flows:
o​ System displays all revenue transactions by default.
o​ Admin may sort by date, amount, or payment type.
* A2 – Export Report Flow
o​ Admin clicks on Export.
o​ System generates report in Excel/CSV/PDF format.
o​ Admin downloads the report.
Postconditions:
* Revenue report is generated and displayed successfully.
* Exported files are stored/downloaded.
* EX1 – No Data Found
o​ System shows “No records found” when no transactions match the
Exceptional
Flow:
selected filters.
* EX2 – System Error
o​ System logs error and displays an appropriate message if report
generation fails.
Assumptions:
* All revenue types are correctly categorized at the time of payment.
* Admin has permission to view and export financial data.
* Date filters operate based on transaction date.
Notes &
Comments
* This module supports financial reporting, auditing, and business
decision-making.
* Additional filters such as can be added in future phases.
* Subscription Management (Advanced Logic & Custom Plans)
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-CR-003 — Subscription Management (Advanced Logic & Custom Plans)

```
Description
This use case defines advanced subscription management capabilities including
admin-defined discounts, prorated billing during upgrades, grace period handling,
and creation and assignment of custom subscription plans that are not visible on
the frontend.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Platform Administrator
Preconditions:
Date Created:
09/02/2026
* Admin and subscriber accounts exist and are active.
* Subscription plans are configured in the system.
* Subscriber has an active or recently expired subscription.
* Admin creates or updates subscription plans.
Triggers:
* Subscriber requests upgrade, renewal, or admin assigns a custom plan.
* Subscription reaches expiry.
* Admin configures discounts for 12-month subscription plans.
* Subscriber initiates an upgrade request.
* System calculates:
o​ Remaining tenure on current plan.
o​ Monetary value of unused portion.
* System automatically applies the remaining value as a discount to the
new plan (prorated billing).
* Admin configures grace period rules (e.g., 10 days post-expiry).
* Upon subscription expiry:
o​ System grants limited access or full access based on grace period
configuration.
o​ System sends renewal reminders to subscriber.
* Admin creates a custom subscription plan for a specific customer.
* Admin marks the custom plan as “Hidden from Frontend”.
* Admin assigns the custom plan to the selected user.
* System activates the assigned plan and enforces access based on its
configuration.
Basic Workflow:
* A1 – Downgrade Scenario
Alternative
Flows:
o​ Subscriber requests downgrade.
o​ System schedules downgrade to take effect after current plan
expiry.
* A2 – Manual Discount Application
o​ Admin manually applies an additional discount to a specific user.
o​ System recalculates final payable amount.
* A3 – Grace Period Extension
o​ Admin extends grace period for a specific user.
o​ System updates expiry access accordingly.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
Subscription is upgraded, renewed, downgraded, or assigned successfully.
Prorated discounts and grace periods are applied correctly.
Custom plans are active only for assigned users.
All actions are logged for audit purposes.
* EX1 – Upgrade Calculation Error
Exceptional
Flow:
o​ System fails to calculate prorated amount.
o​ System displays error and blocks upgrade until resolved.
* EX2 – Custom Plan Assignment Failure
o​ System fails to assign custom plan.
o​ Admin receives error notification and retries.
* EX3 – Expired Beyond Grace Period
o​ System restricts all paid features.
o​ User is prompted to renew before regaining access.
Assumptions:
* Pricing rules and proration logic are predefined and configurable.
* Grace period duration is configurable by admin.
* Custom plans are excluded from public subscription listings.
* Admin has permission to create, modify, and assign subscription plans.
Notes &
Comments
* This use case enhances subscription flexibility and enterprise customer
handling.
* Supports business negotiations, retention strategies, and contract-based
pricing models.
* Can be extended to include bulk custom plan assignment and SLA-based
pricing in future.
* Insurance User Management & Per-User Subscription Model
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-CR-004 — Insurance User Management & Per-User Subscription Model

```
Description
This use case covers onboarding of insurance companies and their users, role
assignment, claim ownership and reassignment, and managing per-user
subscription plans with slab-based pricing and custom plan support.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company
Preconditions:
Date Created:
09/02/2026
* Insurance company has successfully registered and verified.
* Company admin account is active.
* Subscription plans are configured with user-based pricing slabs.
* Insurance company registers on the platform.
* Company admin adds or invites new users.
Triggers:
* Company purchases or updates a subscription plan.
* Claims are reassigned between users.
* Insurance company self-registers and gets onboarded on the platform.
* Company admin logs in and adds insurance users (claim officers)
manually or via email invitation.
* Company admin assigns roles to users using the basic role structure.
* Company admin activates or deactivates user accounts as needed.
* System designates claim ownership to specific users within the insurance
company.
* Company admin reassigns claims between users when required.
* During subscription purchase:
o​ Insurance company selects the number of users.
o​ System applies pricing based on configured slabs (0–10, 10–25,
25–50, >50).
* All users under the company are linked to the same subscription plan.
* System enforces access limits and feature usage based on the
subscription and user count.
Basic Workflow:
* A1 – Invite Acceptance Flow
Alternative
Flows:
o​ Invited user receives email invitation.
o​ User completes registration and becomes active after verification.
* A2 – User Count Increase
o​ Company admin adds additional users beyond current slab.
o​ System prompts for subscription upgrade or custom plan selection.
* A3 – Custom Subscription Plan
o​ Company admin requests a custom plan for specific user counts.
o​ Platform admin creates and assigns a custom plan.
o​ System activates the custom plan for the company.
Postconditions:
*
*
*
*
*
Insurance company and users are successfully onboarded.
Users are assigned roles and reflect correct access.
Claims are owned and reassigned correctly.
Subscription is applied to all users under the company.
System logs all user, role, claim, and subscription actions.
* EX1 – Subscription Limit Exceeded
Exceptional
Flow:
o​ Company admin attempts to add users beyond subscribed limit.
o​ System blocks user creation and prompts for upgrade.
* EX2 – Invitation Expiry or Failure
o​ User invitation link expires or is invalid.
o​ System prompts admin to resend the invitation.
* EX3 – Claim Reassignment Failure
o​ System fails to reassign claim due to permission or system error.
o​ Admin is notified and can retry.
Assumptions:
* One Standard Role structure is predefined and manageable by insurance
company admin.
* Subscription pricing slabs are configurable by platform admin.
* All users under a company must share the same subscription plan.
* Custom plans are supported for non-standard user counts.
Notes &
Comments
* This use case ensures scalable user management for insurance companies.
* Supports organizational growth with slab-based pricing.
* Aligns claim accountability through ownership and reassignment features.
* Can be extended to include departmental roles, approval hierarchies, and
SSO in future.
* Comment Box / Chat Box (Insurance ↔ Garage)
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-CR-005 — Comment Box / Chat Box (Insurance ↔ Garage)

```
Description
This use case enables structured communication between Insurance and Garage
users through a comment/chat box linked to a specific RFQ, allowing clarification
before approval and maintaining a complete conversation history within the RFQ
timeline.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Insurance Company / Garage User
Preconditions:
Date Created:
09/02/2026
* An RFQ has been created and is active.
* Both Insurance and Garage users have access to the RFQ.
* Users are authenticated and authorized.
* Insurance user needs clarification from garage before approving an RFQ.
Triggers:
Basic Workflow:
* Garage user wants to respond or provide additional information.
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
Insurance user opens an RFQ.
Insurance user enters a question in the structured comment/chat box.
System logs the comment and timestamps it.
Garage user receives a notification.
Garage user replies via the same comment/chat box.
System stores the reply and displays the full conversation thread.
All comments appear in chronological order within the RFQ timeline.
* Insurance user reviews the clarification and proceeds with approval or
further queries.
Alternative
Flows:
Postconditions:
* A1 – Multiple Clarification
o​ Insurance user and Garage user continue exchanging messages until
the RFQ is resolved.
* Clarification messages are saved and linked to the RFQ.
* Complete communication history is visible to authorized users.
* RFQ timeline reflects all chat activity.
* EX1 – Comment Save Failure:
Exceptional
Flow:
o​ System fails to save a comment.
o​ User is notified and can retry.
* EX2 – Unauthorized Access
o​ User without RFQ access attempts to post a comment.
o​ System blocks the action and logs the attempt.
* EX3 – RFQ Closed
o​ If RFQ is already closed, system prevents new comments and
displays a read-only conversation.
Assumptions:
* Only Insurance and Garage users associated with the RFQ can access the
chat.
* Chat history is immutable once posted.
* Notifications are enabled for new messages.
Notes &
Comments
* This feature improves decision accuracy and reduces RFQ approval delays.
* Supports auditability by logging all communications.
* Can be extended to include read receipts, tagging, and escalation in future
phases.
* Independent Garage & Supplier Operations
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-CR-006 — Independent Garage & Supplier Operations

```
Description
This use case covers the end-to-end procurement workflow between Garage and
Supplier users, including RFQ creation, quote exchange, order processing, and
payment, without any insurance claim linkage.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Garage User / Supplier User
Preconditions:
Date Created:
09/02/2026
* Garage and Supplier accounts are active and approved.
* Users are authenticated and authorized.
* Required products/services exist in the system.
* Garage needs to procure parts/services from a supplier.
Triggers:
Basic Workflow:
Alternative
Flows:
Postconditions:
* Supplier receives an RFQ and wants to respond with a quote.
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
*
Garage creates and sends an RFQ directly to one or more suppliers.
System logs the RFQ and notifies the selected suppliers.
Supplier views the RFQ from their dashboard.
Supplier submits a quote against the RFQ.
Garage reviews received quotes.
Garage selects a quote and confirms the order.
System creates an order linked to the RFQ.
Garage completes payment to the supplier.
Supplier updates order status (Processed → Dispatched → Delivered).
* System updates the order timeline and marks payment status accordingly.
* A1 – Multiple Suppliers
o​ Garage sends RFQ to multiple suppliers and selects the best quote.
* A2 – Partial Fulfilment
o​ Supplier delivers part of the order and completes remaining items
later.
* A3 – Deferred Payment
o​ Garage pays after delivery based on agreed payment terms.
* Order and payment records are successfully created.
* RFQ lifecycle is completed (Closed/Converted to Order).
* Order timeline reflects all activities.
* EX1 – No Quote Received:
Exceptional
Flow:
o​ System fails to save a comment.
o​ User is notified and can retry.
* EX2 – Payment Failure:
o​ If payment fails, system notifies both parties and order remains in
pending status.
* EX3 – Supplier Declines RFQ
o​ Supplier declines RFQ; system notifies garage.
Assumptions:
* This process mirrors the Insurance → Supplier workflow except for the
absence of claim linkage and RFQ will get created directly.
* Both parties comply with platform payment and fulfilment policies.
* Payment gateways are configured and operational.
Notes &
Comments
* This use case enables independent procurement from Garage side without
insurance involvement.
* Audit logs should capture all RFQ, quote, order, and payment actions.
​
* Inventory Stock Management (Insurance & Garage)
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-CR-007 — Inventory Stock Management (Insurance & Garage)

```
Description
This use case covers how Insurance companies and Garages manage their own
inventory stock, use stock in claims or jobs, decide whether to use existing stock or
procure from suppliers, and update stock levels based on purchases and
consumption.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Inventory User / Garage User
Preconditions:
Date Created:
09/02/2026
* Insurance/Garage accounts are active and approved.
* Inventory module is enabled for the user.
* Items/products are created in the system.
* User has permission to manage stock.
* New stock is purchased.
* Stock is used in a claim/job.
Triggers:
* User reviews stock levels.
* User decides whether to use existing stock or buy from supplier.
*
*
*
*
Basic Workflow:
Insurance/Garage maintains their own inventory stock in the system.
System displays current stock levels and availability.
When processing a claim or job, user selects required parts.
System prompts user to choose:
o​ Use existing stock, or
o​ Buy from supplier.
* If user selects Use Existing Stock:
o​ Stock quantity is reduced (stock down).
o​ System logs stock usage against the claim/job.
* If user selects Buy From Supplier:
o​ RFQ/order process is initiated.
o​ On successful purchase and delivery, stock is increased (stock up).
* System updates inventory in real time and maintains audit logs.
* A1 – Partial Stock Usage
o​ User uses part of available stock and purchases remaining quantity
Alternative
Flows:
Postconditions:
from supplier.
* A2 – Reorder Threshold Alert
o​ System alerts user when stock falls below predefined minimum
levels.
* A3 – Inventory Traking
o​ User tracks stocks by part umber defined for each and every parts.
* Inventory stock levels are accurately updated.
* Stock usage or procurement is logged and auditable.
* Claim/job reflects correct source of parts (stock vs supplier).
* EX1 – Insufficient Stock:
o​ If stock is unavailable or insufficient, system forces supplier
procurement or update.
Exceptional
Flow:
* EX2 – Stock Mismatch/Error:
o​ If system stock does not match physical stock, user performs
manual stock adjustment with justification.
* EX3 – Purchase Cancellation:
o​ If supplier order is cancelled, stock is not updated and system
reverts pending stock changes.
Assumptions:
* Insurance and Garage users are responsible for maintaining accurate stock
records.
* Stock updates occur automatically through system transactions (purchase,
usage, adjustments).
Notes &
Comments
* This use case supports cost optimization by prioritizing existing stock usage.
* Can be extended to support multi-location warehouses, barcode scanning,
and stock valuation methods (FIFO/LIFO/Weighted Average).
* Stock audit and reconciliation features should be available for compliance
and reporting.
​
* Favourite Garage & Supplier
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-CR-008 — Favourite Garage & Supplier

```
Description
This use case allows an Insurance company to mark specific Garages and Suppliers
as favourites, ensuring they appear at the top of the recommendation list during
claim RFQ creation.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Inventory User / Garage User
Date Created:
09/02/2026
Preconditions:
* Insurance company account is active and approved.
* Garage and Supplier accounts are active and verified.
* User has permission to manage favourites.
* Insurance user chooses to mark or unmark a Garage or Supplier as
favourite.
Triggers:
* Garage user chooses to mark or unmark a Supplier as favourite.
* Insurance user initiates a Claim RFQ.
* Garage user initiates a RFQ.
*
*
*
*
Insurance user views a list of Garages and Suppliers.
User selects one or more Garages/Suppliers and marks them as Favourite.
System saves favourites at the Insurance company level.
When Insurance user creates a Claim RFQ:
o​ System retrieves the favourite list.
o​ Favourite Garages and Suppliers are displayed at the top of the
RFQ recipient list.
* Insurance user selects recipients and proceeds with RFQ submission.
Basic Workflow:
*
*
*
*
Garage user views a list of Suppliers.
User selects one or more Suppliers and marks them as Favourite.
System saves favourites at the Garage user level.
When Garage user creates RFQ to send to the supplier:
o​ System retrieves the favourite list.
o​ Favourite Suppliers are displayed at the top of the RFQ recipient
list.
* Garage user selects recipients and proceeds with RFQ submission.
* A1 – Remove from Favourites
o​ User unmarks a Garage/Supplier, and system removes them from
Alternative
Flows:
their favourite list.
* A2 – Multiple Users, Same Company(For Insurance)
o​ All users within the same Insurance company see and share the
same favourite list.
Postconditions:
* Favourite Garages and Suppliers are saved and available for future RFQs.
* RFQ recipient lists are prioritised with favourites on top.
* EX1 – Favourite Vendor Deactivated:
o​ If a favourite Garage or Supplier becomes inactive, system hides
Exceptional
Flow:
them from RFQ selection and notifies the Insurance company.
* EX2 – Duplicate Favourite Attempt:
o​ If user attempts to favourite an already favourited vendor, system
prevents duplication.
Assumptions:
* Favourites are Insurance-company specific, not user-specific.
* Favourites bypass standard recommendation ranking but do not bypass
eligibility or compliance rules unless explicitly configured.
Notes &
Comments
* This feature improves operational efficiency by prioritising trusted vendors.
* Can be extended to support tagging (e.g., “Fast”, “Premium”, “Low Cost”) or
favourite limits per company
​
* Eligibility vs Favourite Separation
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-CR-009 — Eligibility vs Favourite Separation

```
Description
This use case defines how the system separates system-based eligibility logic from
insurance-specific favourites while recommending Garages and Suppliers during
RFQ creation, ensuring favourites are prioritised without overriding manual
selection.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Inventory User / Garage User
Preconditions:
Date Created:
09/02/2026
* Insurance & Garage company account is active.
* Garages and Suppliers exist in the system.
* Eligibility rules and favourite lists are configured.
* Insurance user initiates an RFQ from a claim.
Triggers:
* System loads recommended Garages/Suppliers.
* Garage user initiates RFQ to the supplier.
Basic Workflow:
* Insurance user initiates RFQ creation.
* System applies defined eligibility rules for garages and for suppliers
* System retrieves the Insurance Company’s favourite Garages and
Suppliers.
* For Garage, system retrieves favourite suppliers.
* System displays recommendations in the following order:
o​ Favourite Garages / Suppliers
o​ System-Recommended (based on eligibility)
o​ All Eligible Garages / Suppliers
* Insurance / Garage user manually selects one or more Garages/Suppliers
and sends RFQ.
Alternative
Flows:
* A1 – No Favourites Configured:
o​ System displays only System-Recommended and All Eligible lists.
* A2 – No Eligible Vendors Found:
o​ System still displays favourites (if any) even if they do not meet
eligibility rules.
Postconditions:
* RFQ is sent to the selected Garages/Suppliers.
* Eligibility and favourite separation is maintained without auto-selection.
* EX1 – Favourite Vendor Inactive:
o​ If a favourite Garage or Supplier becomes inactive, system hides
Exceptional
Flow:
them and does not allow selection.
* EX2 – Eligibility Engine Failure:
o​ If eligibility rules fail to execute, system defaults to showing
favourites and a warning message.
Assumptions:
* Favourites bypass eligibility rules but never bypass platform compliance or
legal restrictions.
* Manual selection is mandatory; system will not auto-select favourites.
* Favourites persist across all claims and users under the same Insurance
Company.
Notes &
Comments
* This logic ensures operational flexibility while maintaining system
governance.
* Can be extended with configurable weightage or scoring models in future.
​
* Quotation Of Alternate / Substitute Parts
Use Case ID:
```



### PH-UC-CR-010 — Quotation Of Alternate / Substitute Parts

```
Description
This use case enables suppliers to propose alternate/substitute parts when the
originally requested OEM part is unavailable, and allows Insurance or Garage users
to review and approve or reject the proposed substitutes within the RFQ workflow.
Creator:
Anubhab Ganguly
Actors:
Inventory User / Garage User
Preconditions:
Date Created:
09/02/2026
* An RFQ has been created by Insurance or Garage.
* RFQ contains one or more requested part items.
* Supplier has access to the RFQ and inventory catalog.
* Supplier opens an RFQ to submit a quotation.
Triggers:
* Requested part is unavailable in supplier’s inventory.
Basic Workflow:
* Supplier opens the RFQ and selects an RFQ line item.
* System first displays the exact matching part from supplier inventory at
the top, if available.
* If the exact part is unavailable, system displays a list of alternate parts
based on matching criteria:
o​ Category
o​ Number of cylinders
o​ Brand / Company name
o​ Model name
o​ Make year
* Supplier selects an alternate part and submits the quotation.
* System marks the quoted item as “Alternate Part Proposed.”
* A1 – Exact Match Available:
o​ Supplier selects the exact part and submits quotation without
Alternative
Flows:
Postconditions:
triggering alternate approval flow.
* A2 – Multiple Alternates Available:
o​ Supplier can select one alternate part per RFQ line item
* A3 – Buyer Requests New Alternate:
o​ Insurance/Garage rejects and requests a different substitute part.
* Quotation is received by the RFQ sender (Insurance / Garage).
* They will be able to compare and select the quote item and followed by the
order processing.
* EX1 – No Alternate Parts Found:
o​ System displays “No suitable alternates found” and allows supplier
Exceptional
Flow:
to skip the rfq item to quote.
* EX2 – Compatibility Validation Failure:
o​ System blocks submission and prompts supplier to select a
compatible part.
Assumptions:
* System has access to accurate part master data and vehicle compatibility
mappings.
* Insurance/Garage approval is mandatory for any alternate part.
* One alternate is mapped per RFQ line item at a time.
* Supplier cannot bypass approval for substitute parts.
Notes &
Comments
* This use case ensures business continuity when OEM parts are unavailable.
* Can be extended to include:
o​ Warranty comparison
o​ OEM vs Aftermarket flag
o​ Pricing tolerance thresholds for auto-approval in future.
​
7.​ Non-Functional Requirements
7.1.​Overview​
Non-Functional Requirements define the quality attributes, constraints, and operational standards
of the platform. These requirements ensure that the system is secure, scalable, reliable,
user-friendly, and maintainable while supporting multiple stakeholders such as Insurance
Companies, Garages, Suppliers, and Platform Administrators. They also define performance
expectations, data protection measures, system availability, and integration capabilities necessary
for smooth business operations and future scalability.
7.2.​Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)
1.​ Scalability
* The system shall support horizontal and vertical scaling to handle increased users,
claims, auctions, and orders.
* The platform shall scale automatically during peak periods such as high claim
volumes or auction events.
* Future modules and integrations shall be added without impacting existing
functionality.
2.​ Scalability:
* The system shall support the current and anticipated growth of BCTC, including:
o​Additional showrooms, warehouses, and branches.
o​New product lines and business units.
o​Increased transaction volumes over time.
* The architecture shall allow for easy scaling of users, storage, and business
processes.
3.​ Availability & Reliability:
* The platform shall maintain a minimum uptime of 99.5%, excluding scheduled
maintenance.
* The system shall recover gracefully from failures without data loss.
* Users shall receive clear notifications in case of system downtime or errors.
* Scheduled maintenance shall be communicated to users in advance.
4.​ Security:
* The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC) for all users.
* All data transmitted between users and the system shall be encrypted.
* Sensitive information such as passwords, documents, and images shall be encrypted
at rest.
* All admin and financial actions shall be logged for audit purposes.
* Inactive or suspended accounts shall be prevented from accessing the platform.
5.​ Data Privacy:
* User data shall be accessible only to authorized users based on role and
permissions.
* Uploaded images, documents, and claim details shall not be visible to unauthorized
users.
* Data retention and deletion policies shall comply with regulatory requirements.
* The system shall prevent unauthorized data sharing between insurance, garage, and
supplier users.
6.​ Usability:
* The system shall provide a consistent and intuitive user interface across all modules.
* Search, filter, and export functionalities shall behave consistently across the
platform.
* Error messages and system notifications shall be user-friendly and actionable.
```



---

## 17. User Stories — All 132 with Full Acceptance Criteria

Source: `PartsHub_Complete_UserStories_Compiled.xlsx` (authored by Navsoft BA Anubhab Ganguly).

Stories grouped by Use Case. Each story has full verbatim Acceptance Criteria text.


### PH-UC-AUTH-001 — Website Login & Signup Access (5 stories)

**US-AUTH-001-001**

*User Story*: As a Visitor, I want to land on the PartsHub homepage showing Login and Sign Up options, so that I can enter the platform.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Homepage displays the PartsHub logo, tagline, and two prominent buttons: 'Login' and 'Sign Up'. 2. Sign Up button opens a role-selection screen with three cards: Insurance Company, Garage, and Supplier — each with an icon and short description. 3. Page is fully responsive; on mobile, cards stack vertically. 4. If user is already logged in (session active), homepage auto-redirects to their role dashboard. 5. Footer shows 'Contact Us', 'Terms & Conditions', and 'Privacy Policy' links.

---

**US-AUTH-001-002**

*User Story*: As a User, I want to log in with email and password, so that I reach my role-specific dashboard.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Login screen shows Email and Password fields with a 'Login' button and 'Forgot Password?' link. 2. Validation: Email must be in valid format (inline red error 'Enter a valid email'); Password is required (error 'Password is required'). 3. On invalid credentials, screen shows a toast/banner 'Invalid email or password' without specifying which field is wrong. 4. After 5 failed attempts, account is locked for 30 minutes; screen shows 'Account temporarily locked. Try again later or reset your password.' 5. On success, user is redirected to their role-based dashboard (Insurance Dashboard / Garage Dashboard / Supplier Dashboard / Admin Panel). 6. 'Remember Me' checkbox keeps session active for 7 days.

---

**US-AUTH-001-003**

*User Story*: As a Visitor, I want to select my role (Insurance / Garage / Supplier) during sign-up, so that I am taken to the correct registration form.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Role selection screen shows three clickable cards with icons: Insurance Company, Garage, Supplier. 2. On card click, system navigates to the role-specific multi-step registration form. 3. A breadcrumb at the top shows 'Sign Up > Select Role > Registration'. 4. 'Back' link returns to role selection without losing any previously entered data. 5. Selected role is highlighted with a coloured border.

---

**US-AUTH-001-004**

*User Story*: As a registered User, I want to reset my password via 'Forgot Password', so that I can regain account access.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Clicking 'Forgot Password' opens a screen with a single Email field and 'Send OTP' button. 2. Validation: email must exist in the system; if not, show 'No account found with this email.' 3. On valid email, system sends a 6-digit OTP to the email; screen transitions to OTP entry (6 separate digit boxes with auto-focus advance). 4. Countdown timer shows '09:45' (10 minutes); after expiry, 'Resend OTP' link appears. 5. After OTP verification, 'Set New Password' screen shows: New Password, Confirm Password fields with live strength indicator (Weak / Medium / Strong). 6. Password rules displayed below field: minimum 8 characters, 1 uppercase, 1 number, 1 special character. 7. On success, confirmation screen shows 'Password reset successfully' with a 'Go to Login' button.

---

**US-AUTH-001-005**

*User Story*: As the System, I want to manage session timeouts and concurrent logins, so that security is maintained.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. After 30 minutes of inactivity, a modal appears: 'Your session is about to expire. Continue?' with a 60-second countdown and 'Stay Logged In' / 'Logout' buttons. 2. If no action, user is auto-logged out and redirected to login screen with message 'Session expired. Please log in again.' 3. If user logs in from a new device, the previous session is invalidated; previous device shows 'You have been logged out because your account was accessed from another device.' 4. Logout button in top-right user menu clears session and returns to login screen.

---

### PH-UC-INS-001 — Insurance Company & Branch Registration & Approval (8 stories)

**US-INS-001-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance Company User, I want to register via invitation link or self-registration, so that I can create my account.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Registration form opens as a multi-step wizard: Step 1 — Personal Details, Step 2 — Company Details, Step 3 — Documents, Step 4 — Review & Submit. 2. Step 1 fields: Full Name (required), Email (required, validated inline for format and uniqueness — shows green tick or red 'Email already registered'), Contact Number (required, numeric only, country code dropdown). 3. If accessed via invitation link, email is pre-filled and read-only. 4. Progress bar at top shows current step (1 of 4). 5. 'Next' button disabled until all required fields pass validation. 6. 'Back' button navigates to previous step preserving all entered data.

---

**US-INS-001-002**

*User Story*: As an Insurance Company User, I want to enter company details and upload Trade License and VAT Certificate, so that my registration is complete.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Step 2 — Company Details: Company Name (required text), Speciality (dropdown from master list), Trade License Number (required, unique — inline validation), Licensing Authority (dropdown — list provided by client), Entity Type toggle: 'Head Quarter' or 'Branch' (radio buttons). 2. If 'Branch' selected: a searchable dropdown appears showing approved HQ companies; if HQ not found, a info banner says 'You can link to your HQ after approval.' 3. Step 3 — Documents: Trade License Copy upload (required, accepts PDF/JPG/PNG, max 5MB — shows file name and size after upload, with remove button), VAT Certificate upload (same rules). 4. Drag-and-drop area with 'or click to browse' text. 5. File validation: if wrong format or size exceeded, show inline red error 'Only PDF, JPG, PNG files up to 5MB are allowed.'

---

**US-INS-001-003**

*User Story*: As an Insurance Company User, I want to review all entered details before submitting, so that I can correct any mistakes.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Step 4 — Review & Submit screen shows all entered data in a read-only summary layout grouped by section (Personal, Company, Documents). 2. Each section has an 'Edit' pencil icon that navigates back to that specific step. 3. Uploaded documents shown as thumbnails with file names. 4. Checkbox: 'I agree to the Terms & Conditions' (mandatory, with link to T&C page). 5. 'Submit Registration' button enabled only when T&C is checked. 6. On submit, a success screen shows: 'Registration submitted successfully! Your application is under review. You will receive an email once approved.' with a reference number. 7. User cannot log in until admin approves.

---

**US-INS-001-004**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to receive a notification and review new insurance registrations, so that I can approve or reject them.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Admin receives: (a) an email with subject 'New Insurance Registration — [Company Name]' containing a summary and 'Review Now' link, (b) a web notification badge on 'Manage Insurance' nav item showing pending count (e.g., red badge '3'). 2. Admin opens Manage Insurance > Pending tab; sees a table with columns: Company Name, Applicant Name, Email, Date Submitted, Status. 3. Clicking a row opens a detail panel showing: all personal details, company details, uploaded documents (viewable inline — PDF viewer / image preview), and a verification checklist. 4. Admin can download documents for offline verification. 5. Two action buttons: 'Approve' (green) and 'Reject' (red).

---

**US-INS-001-005**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to approve or reject a registration with a reason, so that only verified companies access the platform.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. On clicking 'Approve': confirmation dialog 'Are you sure you want to approve [Company Name]?' with 'Confirm' / 'Cancel'. On confirm: status changes to 'Active', row moves to 'Active' tab, user receives welcome email with login link. 2. On clicking 'Reject': a modal opens with a mandatory 'Rejection Reason' text area (min 10 characters) and 'Confirm Rejection' button. On confirm: status changes to 'Rejected', user receives email with reason and a 'Resubmit' link. 3. Both actions update the timestamp and admin name in the audit trail visible on the detail panel. 4. Toast notification confirms 'Registration approved successfully' or 'Registration rejected.'

---

**US-INS-001-006**

*User Story*: As a rejected Insurance User, I want to edit my details and resubmit, so that I can fix the issues flagged by the admin.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. On login after rejection, user sees a banner: 'Your registration was rejected. Reason: [reason text]. Please update your details and resubmit.' 2. All registration fields are editable in the same wizard format; previously uploaded documents are shown with an option to replace. 3. Changed fields are highlighted with a yellow background. 4. 'Resubmit' button at the end. 5. On resubmit, status returns to 'Pending'; admin is re-notified. 6. Resubmission history counter shown: 'Attempt 2 of 3' (max resubmissions configurable).

---

**US-INS-001-007**

*User Story*: As an approved Insurance User, I want to access all relevant modules on first login, so that I can start operations.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. On first login, a welcome modal shows: 'Welcome to PartsHub, [Name]! Your account is active.' with a 'Get Started' button. 2. Dashboard displays module cards: Claims, RFQ, Orders, Garages, Branches (HQ only), Inventory, Auctions, Hot Deals, Subscription, My Account. 3. Sidebar navigation mirrors these modules. 4. If subscription is not active, modules show a lock icon with tooltip 'Subscribe to access this feature' and link to Subscription page. 5. Profile completion indicator: 'Your profile is 70% complete — Complete Now' banner if optional fields are missing.

---

**US-INS-001-008**

*User Story*: As an Insurance Company User (HQ or Branch), I want to edit my own company profile from the My Account section -- including personal details, company information, licensing and compliance documents, branch-HQ mapping, and password/security settings -- so that I can keep my profile accurate, update expiring documents, and manage account security, while compliance-sensitive changes are routed through admin approval.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. EDIT ACCESS & NAVIGATION: The 'Edit Profile' button is accessible from the 'My Account' section in the Insurance Company user's sidebar menu. Only approved and active insurance company users can edit their profile; users with status 'Pending' see a read-only profile with a banner: 'Your registration is under review. You cannot edit your profile until approved.' Users with status 'Rejected' see their rejection reason and an 'Edit & Resubmit' button instead of 'Edit Profile' (which follows the resubmission workflow from registration). The My Account page opens in read-only view by default; clicking 'Edit Profile' activates edit mode.

2. EDIT MODE ACTIVATION: Clicking 'Edit Profile' transforms the read-only My Account page into an editable form. The page header updates to show 'Edit Profile' with an amber 'Editing' indicator. Two sticky buttons appear at the bottom: 'Save Changes' (green, primary) and 'Cancel' (outlined grey). Clicking 'Cancel' shows a confirmation dialog: 'Discard all unsaved changes?' with 'Discard' and 'Continue Editing' buttons. The form is organized into collapsible sections: Personal Details, Company Information, Licensing & Compliance Documents, Branch Mapping (Branch users only), and Password & Security. A banner at the top reads: 'Fields marked with a lock icon cannot be edited. Changes to compliance fields require admin approval before taking effect.'

3. SECTION A — PERSONAL DETAILS (Editable): User can edit: Full Name (text input, required, min 2 characters), Contact Number (text input with country code dropdown, required, numeric-only validation — format: '+971 555-XXX-XXX'), Alternative Contact Number (optional, same format rules), Designation / Job Title (text input, optional — e.g., 'Claims Manager', 'Head of Operations'). Read-only fields (greyed with lock icon): Email Address (displayed with a lock icon and tooltip: 'Email cannot be changed. Contact Platform Administrator to update your email address.'), User ID (system-generated)

---

### PH-UC-INS-002 — Insurance Claim Request Creation & Management (8 stories)

**US-INS-002-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to create a new claim request, so that I can initiate the parts sourcing process.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. User navigates to Claims from sidebar; clicks 'Create New Claim' button (top-right). 2. System opens a multi-section claim form: Section A — Policyholder Details (Name, Policy Number, Contact Info, Date of Claim, Reason — all required), Section B — Vehicle Details (Make, Model, Variant — cascading dropdowns from master data; Year, VIN, Registration Number), Section C — Damage Details (Damage Category/Sub-category dropdowns, Description text area, Severity selector: Minor/Moderate/Severe). 3. All dropdowns show 'Search...' placeholder with type-ahead filtering. 4. Date picker restricts future dates for Date of Claim. 5. Mandatory fields marked with red asterisk (*); 'Save as Draft' and 'Next: Upload Documents' buttons at bottom.

---

**US-INS-002-001-A**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User (HQ or Branch), I want to edit a submitted claim request from the Claim Detail page -- including policyholder details, accident and damage information, vehicle details, uploaded documents and evidence, finalized parts, and workflow settings -- so that I can correct errors, add missing information, update damage assessments, replace documents, and amend parts before the RFQ is sent to garages, while the system enforces status-based access rules, tracks all changes with version history, warns about downstream impacts on pending RFQs, and routes compliance-sensitive changes through admin approval.

*Acceptance Criteria*:



---

**US-INS-002-002**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to upload documents (police report, photos, videos) for the claim, so that documentation is complete.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Document upload screen shows categorized upload zones: Police Report (required), Accident Photos (required, multi-file), Videos (optional, max 50MB each), Additional Documents (optional). 2. Each zone has drag-and-drop area with 'or click to browse' text. 3. Accepted formats shown below each zone (e.g., 'PDF, JPG, PNG — Max 5MB' / 'MP4 — Max 50MB'). 4. Upload progress bar per file; completed files show thumbnail, filename, size, and a red 'X' remove button. 5. Invalid file shows inline red error: 'File type not supported' or 'File exceeds maximum size.' 6. 'Add More Documents' button allows additional uploads at any time. 7. Buttons: 'Back: Claim Details' and 'Next: Preview'.

---

**US-INS-002-003**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to preview the claim before publishing, so that I can review and correct information.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Preview screen shows all claim data in read-only card layout: Policyholder Info, Vehicle Details, Damage Details, Uploaded Documents (thumbnails). 2. Each section has 'Edit' pencil icon navigating back to that section. 3. Documents are clickable for full-screen preview (image lightbox / PDF inline viewer). 4. Buttons: 'Save as Draft' (saves without submitting), 'Publish Claim' (submits the claim). 5. On Publish: confirmation dialog 'Submit this claim? This will generate a Claim ID and notify the team.' 6. Success screen: 'Claim [CLM-2025-0042] created successfully!' with options: 'View Claim', 'Send RFQ to Garages', 'Go to Claims List'.

---

**US-INS-002-004**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to track claim statuses on a visual timeline, so that I have clear visibility into progress.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Claim detail page has a horizontal status timeline at the top showing all statuses as connected nodes: Created → RFQ Sent → Quotation Received → Under Review → Approved → Order Placed → In Progress → Completed → Closed. 2. Current status node is highlighted (filled colour); past statuses show green checkmarks; future statuses are greyed out. 3. Hovering over a completed status shows timestamp and user who triggered the change. 4. Below the timeline, a 'Status History' expandable section shows a table: Status, Changed By, Date/Time, Notes.

---

**US-INS-002-005**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to search, filter, sort, and paginate my claims list, so that I can find claims quickly.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Claims list page shows a data table with columns: Claim ID (link), Policyholder Name, Vehicle, Status (colour-coded badge), Date Created, Last Updated. 2. Filter panel (collapsible) above table: Status dropdown (multi-select), Date Range picker (From–To), Policyholder Name text search, Claim ID search. 3. Column headers are clickable for sorting (ascending/descending arrow indicator). 4. Pagination: 20 rows per page; page selector at bottom (1, 2, 3... Last); row count shown ('Showing 1–20 of 147'). 5. 'Export' button downloads filtered results as CSV/Excel. 6. Quick-action buttons per row: 'View', 'Send RFQ' (if status allows). 7. HQ users see an additional 'Branch' column; Branch users see only their own claims.

---

**US-INS-002-006**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to save a claim as draft and return to edit it later, so that I am not forced to complete everything in one session.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. 'Save as Draft' button available on every step of the claim form. 2. Draft claims appear in the Claims list with status badge 'Draft' (grey). 3. Clicking a draft reopens the form at the last edited step with all data preserved. 4. Drafts are auto-saved every 60 seconds (subtle 'Auto-saved' indicator near the save button). 5. Drafts older than 30 days show a warning: 'This draft will be deleted in X days.'

---

**US-INS-002-007**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to view the complete detail page of a submitted claim with tabbed navigation across Overview, RFQ To Garage, Garage Quotations, RFQ To Supplier, Supplier Quotations, and Orders — so that I can review all claim information, manage RFQs, compare quotations, track orders, and view uploaded evidence from a single consolidated screen.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Page header shows Claim # with status badge, Back button, Prev/Next navigation. 2. Policyholder summary strip with 8 icon-cards (sticky on scroll). 3. Six tabs: Overview, RFQ To Garage, Garage Quotations, RFQ To Supplier, Supplier Quotations, Orders. 4. Overview tab: vehicle/report details, Police Report download link. 5. RFQ To Garage tab: RFQ # header, Send RFQ button, garage table with Status badges (Accepted/Rejected) and Response Notes. 6. Garage Quotations tab: checkboxes, Compare button, Quote ID links, Labor/Parts Cost, Repair Time, Star Ratings. 7. RFQ To Supplier tab: Send RFQ button, supplier table with Submission Date & Time. 8. Supplier Quotations tab: checkboxes, Compare button, Parts Count, Total Cost, Delivery Time, Warranty. 9. Orders tab: Order ID links, colour-coded status badges, Order Type, Total Cost. 10. Photos & Videos panel (persistent): witness details, 4-column thumbnail gallery, lightbox with navigation arrows and counter. 11. Workflow Settings panel (persistent): Default/Custom time and quotes, Venue, Date, Time Slot. 12. Finalized Parts Details table (persistent): Part Name, Condition, Resolution, Qty, Unit Cost, Total Cost, Notes. 13. Global toolbar and sidebar navigation.

---

### PH-UC-INS-003 — Send RFQ to Garages After Claim Creation (5 stories)

**US-INS-003-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to select garages and send an RFQ for a claim, so that garages can provide repair quotations.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. From Claim Detail page, user clicks 'Send RFQ to Garage(s)' button (enabled only when claim status is 'Created' or 'Submitted'). 2. System opens the Garage Selection screen showing a table of eligible garages: Name, Location, Speciality, Rating (stars), Type (Network/Independent), Distance from claim location. 3. AI-recommended garages appear at the top with a 'Recommended' badge and a tooltip explaining why (e.g., 'High rating, speciality match, fast response history'). 4. User can search by name/location and filter by: Speciality, Rating (min stars slider), Type, Distance range. 5. Checkbox selection for each garage; selected count shown: 'X garages selected'. 6. 'Send RFQ' button at bottom; confirmation dialog: 'Send RFQ to X garages for Claim [ID]?' with 'Confirm' / 'Cancel'. 7. On confirm: success toast 'RFQ sent to X garages'; claim status updates to 'RFQ Sent'.

---

**US-INS-003-002**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to track which garages have responded to the RFQ, so that I can monitor quotation progress.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Claim Detail page shows an 'RFQ Status' tab with a table: Garage Name, Sent Date, Response Status (Pending/Accepted/Rejected/Quotation Submitted — colour badges), Response Date. 2. Progress bar at the top: 'X of Y garages responded'. 3. If a garage submits a quotation, the row shows a 'View Quotation' link. 4. SLA indicator: if a garage hasn't responded within 48 hours, row shows amber warning icon with tooltip 'Response overdue'. 5. Real-time updates: when a garage responds, the table row updates without page refresh and a subtle notification bell rings.

---

**US-INS-003-003**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to receive a notification when a new RFQ arrives, so that I can respond promptly.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Garage receives: (a) email with subject 'New RFQ Received — Claim [ID]' containing claim summary, vehicle info, and 'View RFQ' link, (b) web notification — bell icon badge count increments, dropdown shows 'New RFQ from [Insurance Company] for [Vehicle Make Model]'. 2. Clicking the notification navigates to the RFQ detail page. 3. Unread RFQs in the list are shown in bold with a blue dot indicator. 4. Notification preferences: user can configure email/web preferences in My Account > Notification Settings.

---

**US-INS-003-003A**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to compare garage quotes side-by-side, so that I can pick the best offer.

*Acceptance Criteria*:



---

**US-INS-003-004**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want AI to recommend the best garages for my claim, so that I get optimal quotations without manual research.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. On the Garage Selection screen, a highlighted 'AI Recommendations' section appears above the full list showing top 3–5 garages with: confidence score percentage, match reasons (icons + text: 'Location match', 'Speciality match', 'High rating', 'Fast responder'). 2. Each recommendation card shows garage name, rating, distance, and a 'Select' checkbox. 3. A 'Why this recommendation?' info icon opens a tooltip with details. 4. User can dismiss recommendations and choose manually from the full list below. 5. If no recommendations are available (new claim area), system shows: 'No AI recommendations available — please select manually.'

---

### PH-UC-INS-004 — Send RFQ to Suppliers After Parts Finalisation (3 stories)

**US-INS-004-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to send an RFQ to suppliers after parts are finalized, so that I can procure the required parts.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. From the Claim Detail page (after garage quotation is approved), user clicks 'Send RFQ to Suppliers'. 2. System shows a parts list auto-populated from the approved garage quotation (Part Name, Quantity, Specification — read-only). 3. Below the parts list, a Supplier Selection table shows eligible suppliers with: Name, Location, Rating, Parts Availability indicator (green check / red cross per part). 4. User selects suppliers via checkboxes; 'Send RFQ' button shows count 'Send to X suppliers'. 5. Confirmation dialog and success toast on send. 6. Claim status updates to 'RFQ Sent to Suppliers'.

---

**US-INS-004-002**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to compare supplier quotes side-by-side, so that I can pick the best offer.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Quote Comparison screen opens from Claim Detail > 'Compare Supplier Quotes' tab. 2. Comparison table: rows = parts, columns = suppliers; cells show Unit Price, Delivery Days. 3. Totals row at bottom: Total Price, Avg Delivery Time per supplier. 4. Best price per part highlighted in green; fastest delivery highlighted in blue. 5. Sorting toggle: 'Sort by Lowest Price' / 'Sort by Fastest Delivery'. 6. Part type badges: 'OEM' (blue) / 'Aftermarket' (orange) / 'Refurbished' (grey). 7. Each supplier column has a 'Select' checkbox at the top for order creation.

---

**US-INS-004-003**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to select quotes and create purchase orders, so that procurement is initiated.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. User checks one or more supplier columns on the comparison screen and clicks 'Create Orders'. 2. Order preview modal shows: selected suppliers, items per supplier, total amount per order, combined total. 3. 'Confirm Orders' button; confirmation dialog 'Create X orders totalling [Amount]?' 4. On confirm: orders are created; success screen shows order numbers as clickable links. 5. Claim status updates to 'Order Placed'. 6. Email notification sent to each selected supplier with order details.

---

### PH-UC-INS-005 — Order Management for Insurance (4 stories)

**US-INS-005-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to view all orders with filtering, so that I can manage procurement efficiently.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Orders page shows a data table with columns: Order ID (link), Date, Supplier/Garage Name, Order Type (Supplier/Garage/Auction/Hot Deal — badge), Amount, Status (colour badge), Claim ID (link). 2. Filter panel: Status dropdown, Date Range picker, Order Type multi-select, Supplier/Garage name search. 3. HQ users see a 'Branch' column and can filter by branch; Branch users see only their branch's orders. 4. Pagination: 20 per page with page controls. 5. 'Export' button for CSV/Excel download. 6. Clicking an order row opens the order detail page.

---

**US-INS-005-002**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to view order details including billing, timeline, and documents, so that I can track progress.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Order detail page has tabs: Overview, Items, Billing, Status Timeline, Documents, Review. 2. Overview: Order ID, Date, Supplier/Garage info, Total Amount, Current Status badge. 3. Items tab: table of line items (Part Name, Qty, Unit Price, Total, Part Type). 4. Billing tab: billing address, payment status, invoice number (clickable to download PDF). 5. Status Timeline: visual horizontal timeline — Placed → Confirmed → Shipped → Delivered → Completed (same pattern as claim timeline). 6. Documents tab: uploaded invoices, delivery notes (viewable inline).

---

**US-INS-005-003**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to submit a review and rating for a completed order, so that supplier performance is tracked.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Review tab is enabled only when order status = 'Completed'. 2. Star rating component (1–5 stars, hover to preview, click to select). 3. Review comment text area (optional, max 500 characters with counter '123/500'). 4. 'Submit Review' button; confirmation: 'Submit this review? It cannot be edited later.' 5. After submission, review appears on the order detail as a read-only card with stars, comment, date. 6. A 'Reviews' badge appears on the supplier's profile visible to other users.

---

**US-INS-005-004**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to request a replacement or refund for a defective order item, so that issues are resolved.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. On order detail page, a 'Request Replacement/Refund' button appears for Delivered/Completed orders. 2. Modal form: Request Type toggle (Replacement / Refund), Reason dropdown (Defective, Wrong Item, Damaged in Transit, Other), Description text area (required), Evidence Upload (photos, max 3 files). 3. 'Submit Request' button; success toast 'Request submitted. You will be notified of the decision.' 4. Request status visible on order detail as a sub-section: Status (Pending/Approved/Rejected), Submitted Date, Response Date, Admin/Supplier Notes.

---

### PH-UC-INS-007 — Garage Management, Claim Invitation & Assignment (4 stories)

**US-INS-007-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to view and search registered garages, so that I can select them for claims.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Garages page shows a data table: Garage Name (link), Location, Speciality, Rating (stars), Type (Network/Independent badge), Status (Active/Inactive). 2. Search bar: search by name or location. 3. Filters: Speciality dropdown, Rating slider (min stars), Type toggle. 4. Clicking a garage opens a profile panel: full details, documents, claim history, average rating.

---

**US-INS-007-002**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to invite a garage to a claim for quotation, so that I get repair estimates.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. From garage profile or from claim detail, user clicks 'Invite to Claim'. 2. If from garage profile: a modal lists open claims (Claim ID, Vehicle, Status) for selection. 3. If from claim detail: garage is auto-linked to that claim. 4. Confirmation: 'Invite [Garage] to Claim [ID]?' 5. Garage receives notification. 6. Invited garages appear in the claim's 'Invited Garages' tab with status 'Invited'.

---

**US-INS-007-003**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to assign a claim to a garage based on their quotation, so that repair work can begin.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. On claim detail > Quotations tab, each garage quotation shows: Parts Total, Labour Total, Tax, Grand Total, Delivery Timeline. 2. 'Approve & Assign' button on the preferred quotation. 3. Confirmation dialog: 'Assign this claim to [Garage] for [Amount]? This action cannot be undone.' 4. On confirm: claim status changes to 'Assigned', garage receives notification, other garages notified their quotes were not selected.

---

**US-INS-007-004**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to process garage payments after repair, so that completed work is settled.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. After garage marks repair complete, the claim shows 'Payment Due' section with: Invoice amount, Garage bank details (masked), Invoice download link. 2. 'Process Payment' button opens payment confirmation: amount, garage name, invoice reference. 3. On confirm: payment is processed; receipt generated and downloadable. 4. Claim status updates to 'Payment Completed'. 5. Garage receives payment confirmation notification.

---

### PH-UC-INS-008 — Branch Management for Insurance Company (HQ Only) (3 stories)

**US-INS-008-001**

*User Story*: As an HQ Insurance User, I want to view all branches with stats, so that I can monitor branch operations.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Branches page (visible only to HQ users) shows: Branch Name, Location, Status, Active Claims count, Orders count, Subscription Status. 2. Search by name/location. 3. Clicking a branch opens full detail view: profile, subscription, claims, orders — all read-only. 4. HQ cannot create branches (branches self-register).

---

**US-INS-008-002**

*User Story*: As an HQ Insurance User, I want to edit branch details, so that information stays current.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Branch detail has 'Edit' button; opens editable form for: Contact Info, Location, Operational Details. 2. Read-only fields (greyed out): Trade License Number, Licensing Authority. 3. 'Save Changes' button; success toast on save. 4. Changes logged in audit trail visible to admin.

---

**US-INS-008-003**

*User Story*: As the System, I want each branch to have independent subscription billing, so that branches manage their own plans.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Each branch has its own Subscription section; HQ Subscription page shows 'Branch subscriptions are managed independently.' 2. HQ branch detail shows subscription status as read-only badge (Active/Expired/None). 3. Branches access their own Subscription page to manage plans and payments.

---

### PH-UC-INS-010 — Inventory Management for Insurance (3 stories)

**US-INS-010-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to add items to my inventory, so that I can use them for auctions and hot deals.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Inventory page > 'Add Item' button opens a form: Item Name, Category (dropdown), Sub-category (cascading dropdown), Vehicle Compatibility (multi-select), Condition (New/Used radio), Quantity (number, min 1), Price (currency input), Images (multi-upload, max 5, thumbnail preview). 2. 'Save Item' button; success toast; item appears in inventory list.

---

**US-INS-010-002**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to edit and delete inventory items, so that my stock is accurate.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Each inventory row has 'Edit' (pencil) and 'Delete' (trash) icons. 2. Edit opens pre-filled form; 'Save Changes' updates the item. 3. Delete shows confirmation: 'Delete [Item Name]?' — if item is in an active auction/deal, error: 'Cannot delete — item is currently listed in an active auction/hot deal.' 4. Deleted items move to a 'Deleted Items' tab for audit.

---

**US-INS-010-003**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to create an Auction or Hot Deal from an inventory item, so that I can sell salvage parts.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Each inventory row has a 'Actions' dropdown: 'Create Auction', 'Create Hot Deal'. 2. Selecting an action navigates to the respective creation form with item pre-selected. 3. Item status changes to 'Listed' with a badge on the inventory row. 4. If item quantity is 0, actions are disabled with tooltip 'Out of stock'.

---

### PH-UC-INS-011 — Auction Creation, Bidding & Order Generation (4 stories)

**US-INS-011-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to create an auction with start/end dates and pricing, so that I can sell parts competitively.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Auction module > 'Create Auction' button opens form: Auction Title, Item Selection (dropdown from inventory), Start Date/Time, End Date/Time (date-time pickers, Start must be future, End must be after Start), Reserve Price (currency), Starting Bid (currency, must be ≤ Reserve). 2. 'Save as Draft' saves without activating. 3. Draft auctions listed with 'Draft' badge; editable until start time. 4. Validation: all fields required; inline errors if rules violated.

---

**US-INS-011-002**

*User Story*: As a Garage/Supplier User, I want to place bids on active auctions, so that I can acquire parts competitively.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Auction listing page shows active auctions: Title, Item Photo, Current Highest Bid, Time Remaining (live countdown), Number of Bids. 2. Clicking an auction opens detail page: item details, photos, bid history table (Bidder anonymized as 'Bidder 1', 'Bidder 2', Amount, Time). 3. Bid input field with validation: must be higher than current highest bid (inline error 'Bid must be higher than [Amount]'). 4. 'Place Bid' button with confirmation dialog: 'Bid [Amount] on [Auction Title]?' 5. After bidding, user sees 'You are the highest bidder' banner (green) or 'You have been outbid!' notification (red) in real-time.

---

**US-INS-011-003**

*User Story*: As the System, I want to auto-generate an order when an auction ends, so that the winning bid is fulfilled.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. At auction end time, system determines winner (highest bid ≥ reserve price). 2. Winner receives: email 'Congratulations! You won [Auction Title]' + web notification with 'Pay Now' link. 3. Seller receives: 'Auction [Title] sold for [Amount]' notification. 4. If no bids met reserve: auction status shows 'Unsold'; seller sees 'No qualifying bids received' on the auction detail. 5. Winner's order appears in their Orders list with status 'Payment Pending'.

---

**US-INS-011-004**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to view auction history and bid analytics, so that I can evaluate auction performance.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Auction detail page > 'Bid History' tab: table showing all bids (time, amount, anonymized bidder). 2. 'Analytics' card showing: Total Bids, Average Bid, Winning Bid, Reserve Price, Spread (Winning - Starting). 3. Visual chart: bid progression over time (line graph with dots for each bid). 4. Auction status badge: Draft / Active / Completed / Unsold.

---

### PH-UC-INS-012 — Hot Deal Management & Order Creation (3 stories)

**US-INS-012-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to create a Hot Deal from inventory, so that I can offer discounted parts.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Hot Deals > 'Create Hot Deal' form: Item (dropdown from inventory), Original Price (auto-filled, read-only), Discounted Price (currency input), Discount % (auto-calculated or manually entered — synced), Start Date, End Date, Quantity Available. 2. 'Preview' shows the deal card as buyers will see it: item photo, original price struck through, discounted price, savings badge. 3. 'Save as Draft' / 'Publish' buttons.

---

**US-INS-012-002**

*User Story*: As a Garage/Supplier User, I want to browse and purchase Hot Deal items, so that I can get discounted parts.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Hot Deals page shows a grid of deal cards: Item Photo, Title, Original Price (struck), Discounted Price (bold), Discount Badge ('30% OFF'), Stock Remaining, Seller. 2. Filters: Category, Price Range (slider), Discount % (min). 3. Clicking a deal opens detail page: full description, photos (gallery), seller info, quantity selector (max = available stock). 4. 'Buy Now' button → payment screen → confirmation → order created. 5. If sold out, button shows 'Sold Out' (greyed, disabled).

---

**US-INS-012-003**

*User Story*: As the System, I want to auto-manage Hot Deal lifecycle and visibility, so that deals are governed properly.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Deals auto-activate on Start Date and deactivate on End Date or when quantity reaches 0 (status: 'Sold Out'). 2. HQ users see all branch deals; Branch users see only their own. 3. Audit log records: creation, edits, purchases, status changes — visible in admin panel. 4. Expired deals move to 'Expired' tab in the listing.

---

### PH-UC-INS-013 — Insurance Subscription Management (4 stories)

**US-INS-013-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to view subscription plans and subscribe, so that I can access platform features.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Subscription page shows pricing cards side by side: Plan Name, Price (monthly/annual toggle), Feature list (checkmarks), Counter limits (e.g., '50 Claims/month', '100 RFQs/month'). 2. Current plan highlighted with 'Current Plan' badge. 3. 'Subscribe' button on each card → payment checkout page. 4. Plan comparison table toggle for detailed feature-by-feature comparison.

---

**US-INS-013-002**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to upgrade, downgrade, or renew my subscription, so that my plan fits my needs.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Current plan card shows: Plan Name, Renewal Date, Usage (progress bars: 'Claims: 32/50 used'), 'Change Plan' button. 2. Upgrade: shows prorated amount preview ('Pay [Amount] today for immediate upgrade'); on confirm, features unlock immediately. 3. Downgrade: warning 'New plan effective at end of current billing period on [Date].' 4. Renewal: Auto-renew toggle; if off, banner shows 'Your plan expires on [Date]'. 5. Expiry reminders: banner at 7, 3, 1 day(s) before: 'Your subscription expires in X days — Renew Now'.

---

**US-INS-013-003**

*User Story*: As the System, I want to enforce subscription counter limits, so that users operate within plan boundaries.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. When a user reaches a limit (e.g., claims_used = max_claims), a modal appears: 'You have reached your claim limit for this month. Upgrade your plan to continue.' with 'Upgrade Now' / 'Close' buttons. 2. The blocked action button (e.g., 'Create Claim') becomes disabled with a tooltip: 'Limit reached — Upgrade plan'. 3. Usage progress bars on dashboard and subscription page turn red at 90%+. 4. Counters reset on billing cycle renewal; user sees 'Counters reset' notification.

---

**US-INS-013-004**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to view subscription history and download receipts, so that I have billing records.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Subscription page > 'Billing History' tab shows a table: Date, Description (e.g., 'Monthly — Premium Plan'), Amount, Status (Paid/Failed), Receipt (download icon). 2. Clicking download opens a PDF receipt in a new tab. 3. Failed payments show a 'Retry Payment' button. 4. Plan change history: 'Plan History' section showing: Date, From Plan, To Plan, Changed By.

---

### PH-UC-INS-014 — My Account – Profile (3 stories)

**US-INS-014-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to view and edit my profile, so that my account details are up to date.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. My Account page shows: Profile Photo (upload/change), Company Name, Email (read-only, greyed), Contact Number, Address, Speciality, Trade License Number (read-only, greyed). 2. Editable fields have blue 'Edit' pencil icon; clicking enters inline edit mode. 3. 'Save Changes' button appears when any field is modified; 'Discard' reverts. 4. Changes to Company Name trigger a yellow banner: 'This change requires admin approval and will not take effect immediately.' 5. Success toast: 'Profile updated successfully' or 'Change submitted for admin approval.'

---

**US-INS-014-002**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to re-upload compliance documents, so that my records are current.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Documents section shows current files: Trade License (filename, upload date, 'Expires: [Date]'), VAT Certificate. 2. 'Replace' button next to each document; opens file picker (same validation: PDF/JPG/PNG, max 5MB). 3. After upload, status changes to 'Pending Verification' (amber badge) and admin is notified. 4. Previous document version remains viewable ('Previous Version' expandable). 5. If document is within 30 days of expiry, a warning banner: 'Your Trade License expires on [Date] — Please update.'

---

**US-INS-014-003**

*User Story*: As the System, I want to enforce approval workflows on sensitive profile changes, so that data integrity is maintained.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Restricted fields (Company Name, Trade License changes, Document re-uploads) create a pending change request visible in: (a) User's profile as 'Pending Approval' amber badge next to the field, (b) Admin panel > Profile Changes queue. 2. Admin sees before/after values side by side with 'Approve' / 'Reject' buttons. 3. On approval: field updates and user receives notification. 4. On rejection: user sees rejection reason under the field; can re-submit.

---

### PH-UC-GRG-001 — Garage Onboarding & Registration (2 stories)

**US-GRG-001-001**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to register with my business details and documents, so that I can join the platform.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Registration wizard (same pattern as Insurance): Step 1 — Personal (Name, Email, Phone), Step 2 — Business (Business Name, Location with address autocomplete, Speciality multi-select, Workshop Type), Step 3 — Documents (Trade License, VAT Certificate, Workshop Photos — up to 5 images), Step 4 — Review & Submit. 2. All fields validated inline; file uploads show thumbnails with remove option. 3. On submit: success screen 'Registration submitted for review' with reference number.

---

**US-GRG-001-002**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to receive approval and access platform features, so that I can start accepting RFQs.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. On approval: email with login link + web notification. 2. On first login: welcome modal + dashboard with modules: RFQs, Auctions, Hot Deals, Inventory, Purchase Parts, Subscription, My Account. 3. On rejection: login shows rejection banner with reason + 'Edit & Resubmit' button; same resubmission flow as Insurance.

---

### PH-UC-GRG-002 — Garage Claim Request for Quotation (RFQ) Management (4 stories)

**US-GRG-002-001**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to view incoming claim RFQs, so that I can decide which to accept.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. RFQ List page shows: Claim ID, Vehicle (Make/Model), Insurance Company, Received Date, Deadline, Status (New/Accepted/Rejected/Quotation Sent — badges). 2. New (unread) RFQs shown in bold with blue dot indicator. 3. Filters: Status, Insurance Company, Date Range. 4. Clicking a row opens RFQ detail page.

---

**US-GRG-002-002**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to accept or reject an RFQ with a reason, so that my response is recorded.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. RFQ detail shows: claim summary, vehicle details, damage photos (gallery), insurer notes, uploaded documents (viewable inline). 2. Two action buttons: 'Accept RFQ' (green) / 'Reject RFQ' (red). 3. Accept: confirmation dialog → status changes to 'Accepted' → insurer notified → quotation form becomes available. 4. Reject: modal with mandatory Reason dropdown (Not in speciality, Too far, Capacity full, Other) + optional notes textarea → status 'Rejected' → insurer notified.

---

**US-GRG-002-003**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to submit a repair quotation with parts and labour breakdown, so that the insurer can evaluate my estimate.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Quotation form (opens after accepting RFQ): Parts section — dynamic table rows (Add Part button): Part Name, Quantity, Unit Price, Part Type (OEM/Aftermarket dropdown), Line Total (auto-calculated, read-only). 2. Labour section: Hours, Rate per Hour, Labour Total (auto-calculated). 3. Summary card: Parts Total + Labour Total + Tax (configurable %) = Grand Total — all auto-calculated. 4. Supporting Documents upload area. 5. 'Submit Quotation' button; confirmation dialog. 6. On submit: insurer notified; quotation appears in claim's Quotations tab.

---

**US-GRG-002-004**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to receive notifications throughout the RFQ lifecycle, so that I stay updated.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Notification events: New RFQ Received, Quotation Approved/Rejected, Claim Assigned to You, Payment Received. 2. Bell icon in header shows unread count badge; dropdown lists recent notifications with timestamp and action link. 3. Email notifications for each event (configurable in My Account > Notifications). 4. Notification settings page: toggle switches per event type for Email and Web.

---

### PH-UC-GRG-003 — Garage Auction Participation & Order Management (2 stories)

**US-GRG-003-001**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to browse and bid on active auctions, so that I can acquire parts competitively.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Auction listing: cards showing Item Photo, Title, Current Bid, Time Remaining (countdown), Bid Count. 2. Filters: Category, Vehicle Type, Price Range, Time Remaining. 3. Detail page: item gallery, description, bid history, bid input with validation (> current highest). 4. 'Place Bid' → confirmation → real-time update of bid status. 5. 'You are highest bidder' (green) or 'Outbid!' (red) real-time banners.

---

**US-GRG-003-002**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to manage my won auction orders, so that I can pay and receive items.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Won auction → notification 'You won [Auction Title]!' with Pay Now link. 2. My Auction Orders page: table with Order ID, Auction Title, Amount, Status, Payment Deadline. 3. 'Pay Now' navigates to payment checkout. 4. After payment: status 'Paid' → 'Shipped' → 'Delivered'. 5. If payment not made within deadline, order cancelled with notification.

---

### PH-UC-GRG-004 — Garage Hot Deal Purchase & Order Management (2 stories)

**US-GRG-004-001**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to browse and purchase Hot Deal items, so that I can get discounted parts.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Hot Deals grid: same layout as insurance seller view — photo, prices, discount badge, stock. 2. Detail page: gallery, description, seller info, quantity selector, 'Buy Now' button. 3. Payment checkout → order confirmation → order appears in My Orders.

---

**US-GRG-004-002**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to manage my Hot Deal orders and request returns, so that my purchases are tracked.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. My Hot Deal Orders table: Order ID, Item, Amount, Status, Date. 2. For delivered orders: 'Request Return' button opens modal (Reason, Description, Evidence upload). 3. Return status visible on order detail: Pending / Approved / Rejected.

---

### PH-UC-GRG-005 — Garage Inventory Management (1 stories)

**US-GRG-005-001**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to manage my parts inventory, so that my stock is accurately listed.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Inventory page with data table: Item Name, Category, Quantity, Condition, Price, Status. 2. 'Add Item' button → form (same fields as insurance inventory). 3. Inline edit via pencil icon; delete via trash icon with confirmation. 4. Deleted items: 'Deleted' tab for audit. 5. Search and column filters.

---

### PH-UC-GRG-006 — Purchase Parts for Garage Inventory (2 stories)

**US-GRG-006-001**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to purchase parts from suppliers, so that I can replenish my workshop stock.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Parts Marketplace page: search bar + category/vehicle filters → supplier parts catalog. 2. Each item card: Photo, Name, Supplier, Price, Stock Available, 'Add to Cart' button. 3. Cart page: item list, quantities (editable), totals, delivery address, 'Place Order' button. 4. Order tracking: Placed → Confirmed → Shipped → Delivered (status steps component).

---

**US-GRG-006-002**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to review suppliers and request replacements, so that I can manage relationships.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. After delivery: 'Leave Review' button on order (star rating + comment). 2. 'Request Replacement' button → modal (reason, evidence photos). 3. Both actions visible on order detail page. 4. Review visible on supplier profile to other buyers.

---

### PH-UC-GRG-007 — Garage – Insurance Management & Claim Interaction (1 stories)

**US-GRG-007-001**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to view and manage my profile with restrictions on sensitive fields, so that my data is correct.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Same My Account pattern as Insurance: editable fields (Name, Phone, Address, Photos, Speciality), read-only fields (Email, Trade License). 2. Document re-upload triggers admin approval workflow. 3. Pending changes shown with amber badges.

---

### PH-UC-GRG-008 — Garage – Subscription Management (2 stories)

**US-GRG-008-001**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to manage my subscription, so that I maintain platform access.

*Acceptance Criteria*:
