1. Same subscription pattern as Insurance: plan cards, subscribe/upgrade/downgrade, auto-renew toggle, counter progress bars. 2. Expiry warnings and limit-reached modals.

---

**US-GRG-008-002**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to view billing history and download receipts, so that I have records.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Billing History table + downloadable PDF receipts. Same pattern as Insurance.

---

### PH-UC-GRG-009 — My Account (Garage) – Profile (1 stories)

**US-GRG-009-001**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to manage my account profile, upload documents, and update settings, so that everything is current.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Same My Account page as PH-UC-GRG-007 pattern with additional: Notification Settings tab (toggle switches per event for Email/Web), Change Password section (Current Password, New Password with strength indicator, Confirm Password).

---

### PH-UC-SUP-001 — Supplier Onboarding & Registration (1 stories)

**US-SUP-001-001**

*User Story*: As a Supplier User, I want to register with business details and documents, so that I can sell on the platform.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Same registration wizard pattern as Garage: Personal → Business → Documents → Review & Submit. 2. Speciality field = types of parts supplied (multi-select). 3. On approval: access to RFQs, Inventory, Auctions, Hot Deals, Subscription, My Account. 4. Rejection resubmission flow same as other roles.

---

### PH-UC-SUP-002 — Supplier – RFQ Management (2 stories)

**US-SUP-002-001**

*User Story*: As a Supplier User, I want to view incoming RFQs and submit quotations, so that I can win orders.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. RFQ List page: RFQ Number, Date, Insurance Company, Items (comma-list), Qty, Status. 2. Filters: Status, Insurance Company, Date Range, RFQ Number search. 3. RFQ Detail: item list with requested specs, quantities, delivery timeline. 4. Quotation form: per-item rows — Unit Price, Delivery Days, Warranty, Part Type (OEM/Aftermarket), Discount %, Tax %. 5. Auto-calculated totals: Subtotal, Discount, Tax, Grand Total. 6. 'Submit Quotation' → confirmation → insurer notified.

---

**US-SUP-002-002**

*User Story*: As a Supplier User, I want to manage quotation status and handle replacements/refunds, so that orders are fulfilled properly.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Quotation status badges: Submitted → Selected → Order Created → Invoice Generated → Completed. 2. On order confirmation, 'Generate Invoice' button appears; invoice form: auto-filled details + bank info + custom notes. 3. Replacement requests: notification with details; 'Approve' / 'Reject' buttons with reason field. 4. Refund processing: status tracking on order detail page.

---

### PH-UC-SUP-003 — Supplier – Inventory Management (1 stories)

**US-SUP-003-001**

*User Story*: As a Supplier User, I want to manage my parts inventory, so that buyers see accurate availability.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Same inventory CRUD pattern as garage: data table, Add/Edit/Delete, search/filter. 2. Additional: 'Deleted Items' tab shows soft-deleted records with restore option. 3. Low stock warning icon on items where quantity < 5.

---

### PH-UC-SUP-004 — Supplier Auction Participation & Order Management (1 stories)

**US-SUP-004-001**

*User Story*: As a Supplier User, I want to participate in auctions, so that I can acquire salvage inventory.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Same auction browsing/bidding experience as Garage. 2. Won auctions → orders → payment flow. 3. Refund request available for issues.

---

### PH-UC-SUP-005 — Supplier Hot Deal Purchase & Order Management (1 stories)

**US-SUP-005-001**

*User Story*: As a Supplier User, I want to buy Hot Deal items and manage orders, so that I can get discounted parts.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Same Hot Deals browsing/purchase experience as Garage. 2. Order tracking and return requests available.

---

### PH-UC-SUP-006 — Supplier – Subscription Management (1 stories)

**US-SUP-006-001**

*User Story*: As a Supplier User, I want to manage my subscription, so that I maintain platform access.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Same subscription management pattern as Insurance/Garage: plan cards, checkout, upgrade/downgrade, auto-renew, counter limits, billing history, receipt downloads.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-001 — Platform Administrator Onboarding (1 stories)

**US-ADM-001-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to log in securely, so that only authorized personnel access the admin panel.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Admin login page: Email + Password fields, 'Login' button. 2. After credentials validated, a second screen shows 'Enter the OTP sent to your email' with 6-digit input. 3. OTP valid for 5 minutes; 'Resend OTP' after countdown. 4. On success: redirect to Admin Dashboard. 5. Forgot Password: same reset flow as regular users. 6. All login attempts (success/fail) logged in admin audit trail with IP address.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-002 — Admin – Manage Insurance Companies (3 stories)

**US-ADM-002-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage all insurance registrations, so that I govern platform access.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Manage Insurance page: data table — Company Name, Date Joined, Branches count, Subscription Plan, Account Status (colour badge). 2. Tabs: All, Pending, Active, Rejected, Deactivated. 3. Search by Name/City/Country; Filters: City, Country, Plan, Status. 4. Row click opens detail panel (same content as registration review). 5. Action buttons: Approve/Reject (pending), Activate/Deactivate (toggle switch), Export CSV. 6. 'Pending' tab badge shows count.

---

**US-ADM-002-002**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to approve or reject profile edit requests, so that changes are verified.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Profile Changes queue: table — User, Field Changed, Old Value, New Value, Requested Date. 2. Detail view shows before/after side-by-side with changed field highlighted. 3. 'Approve' / 'Reject' buttons (reject requires reason). 4. User notified of decision.

---

**US-ADM-002-003**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to edit registered insurance company profiles from the Admin Manage Insurance section -- including company information, contact details, licensing and compliance data, uploaded documents, account status, and branch mappings -- so that I can correct registration errors, update compliance details, manage account lifecycle, and reassign branch-HQ relationships.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. EDIT ACCESS & PERMISSIONS: Only Platform Administrators with 'Manage Insurance' permission can edit insurance company profiles; the 'Edit Company' button (pencil icon) appears on the insurance company detail page within Admin > Manage Insurance. All account statuses (Active, Pending, Rejected, Deactivated) are editable by admin except: accounts in 'Deactivated' status show a warning banner 'This account is deactivated. Reactivate the account before making profile changes.' with the edit form still accessible but the 'Save Changes' button disabled until the account is reactivated. A yellow info banner at the top of the edit screen reads: 'You are editing this insurance company profile as an Administrator. All changes will be logged in the audit trail and the insurance company will be notified.'

2. EDIT MODE ACTIVATION: Clicking 'Edit Company' transforms the read-only company detail page into an editable form. The page header updates to show 'Editing: [Company Name]' with an amber 'Editing' badge. Two sticky action buttons appear at the bottom: 'Save Changes' (green) and 'Cancel' (outlined grey). Clicking 'Cancel' shows a confirmation dialog: 'Discard all unsaved changes?' with 'Discard' and 'Continue Editing' buttons. The form is organized into collapsible sections: Company Information, Personal & Contact Details, Licensing & Compliance, Branch Mapping, Documents, Account & Status Control, and Subscription Information (read-only).

3. SECTION A — COMPANY INFORMATION (Editable): Admin can edit: Company Name (text input, required, min 2 characters — inline warning: 'Changing the company name will trigger a re-verification notification to the insurance company'), Speciality (multi-select dropdown from master Speciality list, required — at least one must be selected), Entity Type display showing 'Head Quarter' or 'Branch' (READ-ONLY, greyed — entity type cannot be changed post-registration; tooltip: 'Entity type is set during registration and cannot be modified'). Fo

---

### PH-UC-ADM-003 — Admin – Manage Garages (1 stories)

**US-ADM-003-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage garages, so that garage participation is governed.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Same management pattern as insurance: listing, search, filter, tabs (Pending/Active/Rejected/Deactivated). 2. Detail view: profile, subscription, claims, orders tabs. 3. Actions: Approve, Reject, Activate, Deactivate, Export.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-004 — Admin – Manage Suppliers (1 stories)

**US-ADM-004-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage suppliers, so that supplier quality is maintained.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Same management pattern: listing, search, filter, tabs. 2. Detail: profile, subscription, RFQs, inventory stats. 3. Actions: Approve, Reject, Activate, Deactivate, Export.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-005 — Admin – Manage Claim Requests (2 stories)

**US-ADM-005-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to monitor all claims, so that I ensure transparency.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Claims listing: all claims across all insurers; filters: Status, Insurance Company, Garage, Date. 2. Claim detail: tabs — Details, Documents, RFQs, Quotations, Orders, Timeline, Audit Log. 3. Admin view is read-only (no edit actions). 4. Export filtered results.

---

**US-ADM-005-002**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to edit submitted claim requests from the Admin Manage Claims section -- including policyholder details, vehicle information, damage details, uploaded documents, finalized parts, and workflow settings -- so that I can correct data inaccuracies, update claim status, amend parts information, and manage evidence on behalf of insurance companies.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. EDIT ACCESS & PERMISSIONS: Only Platform Administrators with 'Manage Claims' permission can edit claims; the 'Edit Claim' button (pencil icon or 'Edit' text button) appears on the claim detail page in the Admin > Manage Claim Requests section. Claims in any status (Submitted, Under Review, RFQ Sent, Approved, etc.) are editable by admin; however, claims in 'Closed' or 'Cancelled' status show the edit button as disabled with a tooltip: 'Closed/Cancelled claims cannot be edited.' A yellow info banner at the top of the edit screen reads: 'You are editing this claim as an Administrator. All changes will be logged in the audit trail.'

2. EDIT MODE ACTIVATION: Clicking 'Edit Claim' on the claim detail page transforms the read-only view into an editable form. The page header updates to show 'Editing Claim # [CLAIM_ID]' with an amber 'Editing' badge replacing the previous status badge. Two sticky action buttons appear at the bottom of the page: 'Save Changes' (green, primary) and 'Cancel' (outlined grey). Clicking 'Cancel' shows a confirmation dialog: 'Discard all unsaved changes?' with 'Discard' and 'Continue Editing' buttons. The form is organized into collapsible sections matching the claim detail view layout.

3. SECTION A — POLICYHOLDER DETAILS (Editable): Admin can edit: Policyholder Name (text input, required, min 2 characters), Policy Number (text input, required, unique validation — inline warning if changed: 'Modifying the policy number may affect linked records'), Contact Number (text input with country code dropdown, required, numeric validation), Date of Accident (date picker, required, cannot be a future date), Time of Accident (time picker, required, HH:MM AM/PM format). Read-only fields (greyed out, not editable): Claim ID, Date of Claim Creation (system-generated), Created By (insurance user name). Modified fields are highlighted with a yellow left-border indicator to visually distinguish changed values from original values.

4. SECTION B — ACCIDENT & D

---

### PH-UC-ADM-006 — Admin – Manage Subscription Plans (1 stories)

**US-ADM-006-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage subscription plans, so that pricing and features are configured.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Plans page: table — Plan Name, Price, Billing Cycle, Status (Active/Inactive toggle), Subscribers count. 2. 'Create Plan' button → form: Name, Price, Cycle (Monthly/Annual), Feature Checklist, Counter Limits. 3. Edit via row click; Delete requires confirmation and shows warning if plan has active subscribers. 4. Activate/Deactivate toggle controls whether plan appears on frontend.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-007 — Admin – Manage User Subscriptions (1 stories)

**US-ADM-007-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage individual user subscriptions, so that billing issues are resolved.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. User Subscriptions table: User, Plan, Status, Expiry, Usage Counters. 2. Admin actions: manually create subscription, override counters, force-renew, change plan. 3. Each action logged in audit trail.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-008 — Admin – Manage Feature Groups (1 stories)

**US-ADM-008-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage feature groups linked to plans, so that plan capabilities are flexible.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Feature Groups page: table — Group Name, Features count, Linked Plans count. 2. Create/Edit form: Name, Description, Feature checklist (checkboxes from master feature list). 3. Link to plans via plan edit form (multi-select feature groups).

---

### PH-UC-ADM-009 — Admin – Manage Review & Rating (1 stories)

**US-ADM-009-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to moderate reviews, so that platform ratings are fair and accurate.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Reviews page: table — Reviewer, Reviewed Entity, Rating (stars), Comment, Date, Flagged (icon). 2. Filters: Rating range, Entity type, Flagged only. 3. Actions per review: Flag (mark for review), Remove (soft-delete with reason). 4. Removed reviews hidden from public view but retained in audit log.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-010 — Admin – Manage Contact Inquiry & Tasks (1 stories)

**US-ADM-010-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage contact inquiries and tasks, so that customer issues are resolved.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Inquiries tab: table — Name, Email, Subject, Status (Open/Assigned/Resolved/Closed), Date. 2. Detail: full message, 'Create Task' button. 3. Task form: Assignee (dropdown of admins), Priority (Low/Medium/High/Urgent — colour-coded), Due Date. 4. Tasks tab: kanban-style board (Open → In Progress → Resolved → Closed) or table view toggle. 5. Task status changes logged with timestamp.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-011 — Admin – Manage Auction (1 stories)

**US-ADM-011-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to monitor auctions, so that governance is maintained.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Auctions page: all auctions with Status, Seller, Dates, Bid Count, Winning Bid. 2. Detail: item info, full bid history, audit log. 3. Admin actions: suspend auction (with reason), flag suspicious activity.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-012 — Admin – Manage Hot Deals (1 stories)

**US-ADM-012-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to monitor Hot Deals, so that deal governance is enforced.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Hot Deals page: all deals with Status, Seller, Sales Count, Revenue. 2. Detail: item info, purchase history, audit log. 3. Lifecycle tracking: Draft → Active → Sold Out → Expired.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-013 — Admin – Manage Admin Users, Roles, and Groups (3 stories)

**US-ADM-013-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage admin groups with permissions, so that access control is organized.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Groups page: table — Group Name, Members count, Permissions count. 2. Create/Edit: Name, Description, Permission tree (hierarchical checkboxes by module). 3. Members tab: add/remove users from group.

---

**US-ADM-013-002**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage admin roles, so that access is granular.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Roles page: table — Role Name, Assigned Users count, Status (Active/Inactive). 2. Create/Edit: Name, Permission checkboxes. 3. Assign to users via user edit form.

---

**US-ADM-013-003**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage admin user accounts, so that admin access is secure.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Admin Users page: table — Name, Email, Role, Status, Last Login. 2. 'Invite Admin' button → email input → system sends invitation email with temporary password. 3. First login forces password change. 4. Activate/Deactivate toggle; Delete with confirmation.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-014 — Admin – Manage Master Attributes (20 stories)

**US-ADM-014-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage all master data (Items, Vehicles, Damage categories), so that platform dropdowns are maintained.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Master Data section with sub-navigation: Item Categories, Vehicle Makes, Damage Categories, etc. 2. Each master page: data table (Name, Status toggle, Date Modified). 3. 'Add New' button → simple form (Name, optional parent for sub-categories). 4. Edit inline; Deactivate toggle (cannot delete if in use). 5. Used across the platform in all dropdown fields.

---

**US-ADM-014-002**

*User Story*: As the System, I want to prevent deletion of in-use master data, so that referential integrity is maintained.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. If admin tries to deactivate/delete a master record linked to active items: error dialog 'Cannot deactivate — [X] active records depend on this entry.' 2. Deactivated items hidden from dropdowns but existing records retain the value. 3. Cascade check: shows which entities reference this master record.

---

**US-ADM-014-003**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Master Items (Parts) centrally, so that every transactional screen across the platform uses a clean, verified parts catalog.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Master Items.
2. LISTING: Table displays Item Name, SKU/Code, Category, Sub-Category, Status (Active/Inactive badge), Created On, Last Updated. Default sort: Created On descending.
3. SEARCH & FILTER: Search by Item Name or SKU; filter by Category, Sub-Category, Status (All/Active/Inactive).
4. ADD ITEM: 'Add Item' button opens a form with: Item Name (required, max 100 chars), SKU/Code (required, unique — system rejects duplicates with inline error), Category (required, dropdown from active Item Categories), Sub-Category (required, dependent dropdown based on Category), Status (Active by default). On Save: item appears in listing immediately.
5. VIEW DETAILS: Clicking any row opens a read-only detail panel showing all fields plus Created By, Created On, Updated By, Updated On.
6. EDIT: 'Edit' button on detail panel opens the same form pre-filled. SKU/Code is editable only if the item is not linked to any live Inventory, RFQ, Claim, Auction, or Hot Deal — otherwise the field is greyed with tooltip 'SKU cannot be changed while item is in active use.'
7. ACTIVATE/DEACTIVATE: Toggle switch per row. Deactivating hides the item from all creation dropdowns (Inventory, Claims, RFQs, Auctions, Hot Deals). Existing records that already reference it retain the value (read-only display). System shows confirmation: 'Deactivating this item will hide it from all new records. Existing records are unaffected.'
8. DELETE: Permanently delete only if the item has ZERO references across Inventory, Claims, RFQs, Auctions, and Hot Deals. If references exist, system blocks deletion and displays: 'This item is referenced in [N] record(s). Deactivate instead of deleting.'
9. EXPORT: 'Export CSV/Excel' button exports the current filtered listing with all visible columns.
10. AUDIT TRAIL: All create, edit, activate, deactivate, and delete actions are logged with Admin name, timestamp, and changed field values.
11. USER-INITIATED REQUEST: Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users can submit a 'Request Missing Item' form. Pending requests appear in a 'Requests' tab. Admin can Approve (item created and activated), Reject (with mandatory reason), or Merge with an existing item. Requester is notified of the outcome.

---

**US-ADM-014-004**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Item Categories and Sub-Categories, so that the parts catalog is logically structured and consistently grouped across the platform.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Item Categories.
2. CATEGORY LISTING: Table — Category Name, Code, Sub-Category Count, Item Count, Status, Created On. Search by Name or Code; filter by Status.
3. ADD CATEGORY: Fields: Category Name (required, max 80 chars), Code (required, unique), Status (Active by default). Duplicate Code blocked with inline error.
4. EDIT CATEGORY: Name and Code editable. Editing the code on a category that has active items generates warning: 'Changing the code may affect integrations. Confirm?' Status toggle available.
5. DELETE CATEGORY: Allowed only if no items (active or inactive) and no sub-categories are linked. System blocks with 'Category has [N] linked item(s). Remove or reassign items before deleting.'
6. SUB-CATEGORY LISTING: 'Sub-Categories' tab or drill-in from Category row. Displays: Sub-Category Name, Code, Parent Category, Item Count, Status.
7. ADD SUB-CATEGORY: Fields: Sub-Category Name (required), Code (required, unique within parent Category), Parent Category (required, dropdown of active categories), Status (Active by default).
8. EDIT / DEACTIVATE / DELETE SUB-CATEGORY: Same rules as Category — deletion blocked if items are linked.
9. INACTIVE BEHAVIOUR: Inactive Categories and Sub-Categories are hidden from all creation dropdowns across Inventory, RFQs, Claims, Auctions, and Hot Deals. Existing linked records retain the value.
10. AUDIT TRAIL: All actions (create, edit, activate, deactivate, delete) for both Category and Sub-Category are logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-005**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Vehicle Categories and Vehicle Types, so that the vehicle hierarchy is correctly structured as the foundation for Makes, Models, and Variants.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Vehicle Master → Vehicle Categories / Vehicle Types.
2. VEHICLE CATEGORY LISTING: Table — Category Name, Code, Status, Linked Types Count. Search by Name/Code; filter by Status.
3. ADD VEHICLE CATEGORY: Fields: Category Name (required), Code (required, unique), Status (Active by default). Examples: Passenger Car, Commercial Vehicle, Motorcycle, Heavy Equipment.
4. EDIT VEHICLE CATEGORY: Name and Code editable. Status toggleable.
5. DELETE VEHICLE CATEGORY: Blocked if any Vehicle Types, Makes, Models, Variants, Inventory records, RFQs, Claims, Auctions, or Hot Deals reference it. Error: 'Vehicle Category is in use and cannot be deleted. Deactivate instead.'
6. VEHICLE TYPE LISTING: Table — Type Name, Code, Parent Category, Status, Linked Makes Count. Search by Name/Code; filter by Category, Status.
7. ADD VEHICLE TYPE: Fields: Type Name (required), Code (required, unique within parent Category), Parent Vehicle Category (required, dropdown of active categories), Status (Active by default). Examples: Sedan, SUV, Pick-up Truck, Minibus.
8. EDIT VEHICLE TYPE: Parent Category can be changed only if no linked Makes exist yet.
9. DELETE VEHICLE TYPE: Blocked if linked Makes exist or Type is referenced in Inventory/Claims/RFQs.
10. INACTIVE BEHAVIOUR: Inactive Categories and Types are excluded from all vehicle creation dropdowns. Existing records retain the value.
11. AUDIT TRAIL: All create/edit/deactivate/delete actions logged for both Category and Type.

---

**US-ADM-014-006**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Vehicle Makes, so that each brand is correctly linked to its Category and Type within the vehicle hierarchy.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Vehicle Master → Vehicle Makes.
2. LISTING: Table — Make Name, Code, Parent Category, Parent Type, Status, Linked Models Count. Search by Name/Code; filter by Category, Type, Status.
3. ADD MAKE: Fields: Make Name (required, e.g., Toyota, BMW, Mercedes-Benz), Code (required, unique system-wide), Vehicle Category (required), Vehicle Type (required, filtered dropdown based on selected Category), Status (Active by default).
4. EDIT MAKE: All fields editable. If Category or Type is changed and linked Models exist, system warns: 'Changing Category/Type will affect [N] linked Model(s). Confirm?' — change proceeds only on confirmation.
5. DELETE MAKE: Blocked if linked Models exist or Make is referenced in any active Inventory vehicle record.
6. DEACTIVATE: Inactive Makes are hidden from Inventory vehicle creation. Existing Inventory records that reference the Make retain the value.
7. EXPORT: Make listing exportable as CSV/Excel.
8. AUDIT TRAIL: All create/edit/deactivate/delete actions logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-007**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Vehicle Models, so that each model is correctly linked to its Make and available for inventory and claims matching.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Vehicle Master → Vehicle Models.
2. LISTING: Table — Model Name, Code, Parent Make, Parent Category, Parent Type, Status, Linked Variants Count. Search by Name/Code/Make; filter by Category, Type, Make, Status.
3. ADD MODEL: Fields: Model Name (required, e.g., Camry, X5, C-Class), Code (required, unique within parent Make), Vehicle Category (required), Vehicle Type (required, filtered), Vehicle Make (required, filtered based on Category + Type), Status (Active by default).
4. EDIT MODEL: All fields editable. If Make is changed and linked Variants exist, system warns and requires confirmation.
5. DELETE MODEL: Blocked if linked Variants exist or Model is referenced in any Inventory, RFQ, Claim, Auction, or Hot Deal.
6. DEACTIVATE: Inactive Models hidden from new Inventory/Claims vehicle selection. Existing records retain the value.
7. AUDIT TRAIL: All actions logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-008**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Vehicle Variants, so that the most granular level of the vehicle hierarchy is maintained for precise inventory and claim mapping.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Vehicle Master → Vehicle Variants.
2. LISTING: Table — Variant Name, Code, Parent Model, Parent Make, Status, Inventory Usage Count. Search by Name/Code/Model/Make; filter by Category, Type, Make, Model, Status.
3. ADD VARIANT: Fields: Variant Name (required, e.g., '2.5L Petrol Automatic', 'xDrive30i', '1.6T Sport'), Code (required, unique within parent Model), Vehicle Category, Vehicle Type, Vehicle Make, Vehicle Model (all required, cascading filtered dropdowns), Status (Active by default).
4. EDIT VARIANT: All fields editable with same warnings if references exist.
5. DELETE VARIANT: Blocked if Variant is referenced in any active Inventory, Claim, RFQ, Auction, or Hot Deal record.
6. DEACTIVATE: Inactive Variants excluded from Inventory vehicle creation forms. Existing records retain the value.
7. NOTE: Variant is the leaf node of the vehicle hierarchy. All vehicle inventory records must resolve to a Variant.
8. AUDIT TRAIL: All create/edit/deactivate/delete actions logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-009**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Manufacture Origins, so that vehicles and parts can be tagged with their country or region of manufacture for inventory accuracy and compliance.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Manufacture Origin.
2. LISTING: Table — Origin Name, Code, Status, Inventory Usage Count. Search by Name/Code; filter by Status.
3. ADD ORIGIN: Fields: Origin Name (required, e.g., Japan, Germany, UAE, South Korea), Code (required, unique, e.g., JP, DE, AE, KR), Status (Active by default). Duplicate Code blocked with inline error.
4. EDIT ORIGIN: Name and Code editable. Code change on an Origin in active use generates warning: 'Changing the code will affect [N] inventory record(s). Confirm?'
5. LINK TO VEHICLE HIERARCHY: Origin is a flat reference list — it is NOT hierarchically tied to a specific Make or Model. Used as a standalone field in vehicle inventory records.
6. DELETE: Blocked if any Inventory records reference the Origin. System shows: 'Manufacture Origin is used in [N] inventory record(s). Cannot delete.'
7. DEACTIVATE: Inactive Origins excluded from Inventory creation dropdowns. Existing records retain the value.
8. AUDIT TRAIL: All create/edit/deactivate/delete actions logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-010**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Damage Categories, so that Insurance users can classify the type of damage on a claim from a consistent, controlled list.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Damage Master → Damage Categories.
2. LISTING: Table — Category Name, Code, Sub-Category Count, Claims Usage Count, Status. Search by Name/Code; filter by Status.
3. ADD DAMAGE CATEGORY: Fields: Category Name (required, e.g., Collision Damage, Flood Damage, Fire Damage, Theft/Vandalism, Mechanical Failure), Code (required, unique), Status (Active by default).
4. EDIT DAMAGE CATEGORY: Name and Code editable. System warns if Claims reference this Category.
5. DELETE: Blocked if any active or historical Claims reference this Category. Error: 'Damage Category is referenced in [N] Claim(s). Cannot delete. Deactivate instead.'
6. DEACTIVATE: Inactive Damage Categories hidden from Claim creation's Damage Category dropdown. Existing Claims retain the value.
7. AUDIT TRAIL: All create/edit/deactivate/delete actions logged.
8. NOTE: Damage Categories are used exclusively in the Claims workflow.

---

**US-ADM-014-011**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Damage Sub-Categories, so that each claim's damage can be described at a granular level for better RFQ targeting and garage matching.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Damage Master → Damage Sub-Categories.
2. LISTING: Table — Sub-Category Name, Code, Parent Damage Category, Claims Usage Count, Status. Search by Name/Code; filter by Parent Category, Status.
3. ADD DAMAGE SUB-CATEGORY: Fields: Sub-Category Name (required, e.g., Front Bumper, Left Rear Door, Windshield, Engine Bay), Code (required, unique within parent Category), Parent Damage Category (required, dropdown of active Damage Categories), Status (Active by default).
4. EDIT: All fields editable. Code change on an in-use Sub-Category generates a warning.
5. DELETE: Blocked if any Claim records reference this Sub-Category. Error: 'Damage Sub-Category is referenced in [N] Claim(s). Cannot delete.'
6. DEACTIVATE: Inactive Sub-Categories hidden from Claim creation's Damage Sub-Category dropdown. Existing Claims retain the value.
7. CASCADING DEACTIVATION: If a parent Damage Category is deactivated, all its Sub-Categories are automatically deactivated. Admin is shown warning: 'Deactivating this Category will also deactivate [N] Sub-Category(ies). Confirm?'
8. AUDIT TRAIL: All actions logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-012**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Specialities, so that Insurance Companies, Garages, and Suppliers are tagged with their areas of expertise for accurate eligibility filtering during claim RFQ dispatch.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Speciality.
2. LISTING: Table — Speciality Name, Code, Applicable User Types (Insurance/Garage/Supplier — multi-tag), Usage Count (number of registered entities using this speciality), Status.
3. ADD SPECIALITY: Fields: Speciality Name (required, e.g., Accident Repairs, Mechanical, Electrical, Windscreen, Luxury Vehicles, Motorcycles), Code (required, unique), Applicable To (multi-checkbox: Insurance / Garage / Supplier), Status (Active by default).
4. EDIT SPECIALITY: All fields editable. If 'Applicable To' is narrowed while entities are using it, system warns: 'This change will affect [N] profile(s). Confirm?'
5. DELETE: Blocked if any registered entity has this Speciality assigned. Error: 'Speciality is assigned to [N] entity(ies). Remove assignments before deleting.'
6. DEACTIVATE: Inactive Specialities hidden from registration and profile dropdowns. Existing entity profiles that carry this Speciality retain the value with an 'Inactive' label shown to Admin only.
7. ASSIGN/REMOVE FROM USER: Admin can directly assign or remove a Speciality from an entity's profile from within Manage Insurance, Manage Garage, or Manage Supplier.
8. IMPACT ON RFQ ELIGIBILITY: Speciality changes reflect immediately in the next RFQ dispatch eligibility filter cycle.
9. AUDIT TRAIL: All actions logged. Speciality assignment/removal at entity level also logged in the entity's own audit trail.

---

**US-ADM-014-013**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage the Licensing Authority master list, so that Insurance, Garage, and Supplier registrations reference a validated, consistent list of regulatory bodies during onboarding.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Licensing Authority.
2. LISTING: Table — Authority Name, Code, Jurisdiction (e.g., UAE Federal, Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Sharjah), Applicable User Types, Status, Usage Count.
3. ADD LICENSING AUTHORITY: Fields: Authority Name (required, e.g., UAE Insurance Authority, Department of Economic Development – Dubai, DIFC Authority), Code (required, unique), Jurisdiction (required), Applicable To (multi-checkbox: Insurance / Garage / Supplier), Status (Active by default).
4. EDIT: All fields editable. If Status is changed on an authority in use, system warns: 'This authority is selected by [N] registered entity(ies). Deactivating will not affect existing registrations but will hide it from new ones.'
5. DELETE: Blocked if any entity's registration references this authority.
6. DEACTIVATE: Inactive Licensing Authorities hidden from the 'Licensing Authority' dropdown on registration forms. Existing registrations retain the value.
7. CLIENT DATA DEPENDENCY: Initial list seeded from client-provided data before platform go-live. Admin can add/edit/deactivate at any time post-launch.
8. ADMIN REVIEW INTEGRATION: Admin registration review panel displays the selected Licensing Authority alongside the registrant's license number for manual verification.
9. AUDIT TRAIL: All create/edit/deactivate/delete actions logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-014**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Feature Groups as master configuration records, so that subscription plans can be logically organized around named capability bundles.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Feature Groups (also accessible via Manage Subscription → Feature Groups per PH-UC-ADM-008).
2. LISTING: Table — Feature Group Name, Code, Description, Linked Plans Count, Status. Search by Name; filter by Status.
3. ADD FEATURE GROUP: Fields: Name (required, unique, e.g., 'Basic Claims', 'Premium RFQ', 'Full Platform Access'), Code (optional, unique), Description (optional, max 250 chars), Status (Active by default). Duplicate Name blocked.
4. EDIT FEATURE GROUP: Name, Code, and Description editable. Status toggleable. If linked to Plans, warning shown: 'This Feature Group is linked to [N] Plan(s). Editing it will affect plan visibility.'
5. DELETE: Blocked if any Plan references this Feature Group. Error: 'Feature Group is associated with an existing plan. Cannot delete. Deactivate or reassign plans first.'
6. DEACTIVATE: Inactive Feature Groups cannot be selected when creating new Subscription Plans. Plans already using an Inactive Feature Group continue to function for existing subscribers.
7. AUDIT TRAIL: All create/edit/deactivate/delete actions logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-015**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage the Claim Status master list, so that all claim lifecycle states are centrally defined and consistently applied across Insurance, Garage, and Admin views.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Claim Statuses.
2. LISTING: Table — Status Name, Code, Display Colour (hex), Sequence Order, Applicable Actors (Insurance/Garage/Admin), System-Generated (Y/N), Status (Active/Inactive).
3. ADD CLAIM STATUS: Fields: Status Name (required, e.g., Draft, Submitted, Under Review, RFQ Sent, Garage Accepted, Quotation Received, Parts Finalised, Approved, In Repair, Completed, Closed, Cancelled), Code (required, unique), Display Colour (hex colour picker), Sequence Order (integer), Applicable Actors (multi-checkbox), System-Generated (toggle), Status (Active by default).
4. EDIT: All fields editable. Code changes on in-use statuses require confirmation.
5. DELETE: Blocked if any Claim records reference this status.
6. DEACTIVATE: No new Claims can be transitioned to the deactivated status. Existing claims carrying it retain it and can still be progressed forward.
7. CLIENT SEED DATA: Initial list and exact status names to be provided by the client prior to platform go-live.
8. SYSTEM INTEGRATION: All claim status transitions reference this master. Changes propagate to Claims Pipeline visual, filter dropdowns, and notification triggers.
9. AUDIT TRAIL: All actions logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-016**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage the Order Status master list, so that all order lifecycle states across all order types are consistently defined and tracked.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Order Statuses.
2. LISTING: Table — Status Name, Code, Applicable Order Types (Insurance Order/Garage Order/Auction Order/Hot Deal Order — multi-tag), Display Colour, Sequence Order, System-Generated (Y/N), Status (Active/Inactive).
3. ADD ORDER STATUS: Fields: Status Name (required, e.g., Placed, Payment Pending, Payment Confirmed, Processing, Packed, Dispatched, Delivered, Replacement Requested, Refund Initiated, Refund Settled, Cancelled), Code (required, unique), Applicable Order Types (multi-checkbox), Display Colour (hex), Sequence Order (integer), System-Generated (toggle), Status (Active by default).
4. EDIT: All fields editable with confirmation required if Orders reference the status.
5. DELETE: Blocked if any Order records reference this status.
6. DEACTIVATE: Deactivating prevents future transitions to it. Existing orders carrying the status are unaffected.
7. ORDER TYPE SPECIFICITY: 'Applicable Order Types' ensures Auction-specific statuses (e.g., 'Bid Won') do not appear in standard Garage→Supplier order flows.
8. CLIENT SEED DATA: Initial list to be provided by client. Standard milestone statuses (Packed, Dispatched, Delivered, Refund Initiated) are pre-proposed by Navsoft for client confirmation.
9. AUDIT TRAIL: All create/edit/deactivate/delete actions logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-017**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage Approved Payment Modes, so that only validated and configured payment methods are presented to users across Order, Subscription, Auction, and Hot Deal payment flows.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Payment Modes.
2. LISTING: Table — Payment Mode Name, Code, Provider/Gateway, Applicable Flows (Order/Subscription/Auction/Hot Deal — multi-tag), Applicable User Types (Insurance/Garage/Supplier), Status (Active/Inactive).
3. ADD PAYMENT MODE: Fields: Mode Name (required, e.g., Credit Card – Visa/Mastercard, Bank Transfer, PayTabs Hosted, Cash on Delivery), Code (required, unique), Provider/Gateway (free text or dropdown of configured gateways), Applicable Flows (multi-checkbox), Applicable User Types (multi-checkbox), Processing Fee % (optional numeric), Status (Active by default).
4. EDIT: All fields editable. Deactivating a Payment Mode in use by live subscriptions or pending orders prompts a warning.
5. DELETE: Blocked if any historical Payment records reference this mode.
6. DEACTIVATE: Inactive Payment Modes are not displayed on payment screens. Users cannot select them for new transactions.
7. GATEWAY DEPENDENCY NOTE: Admin can add a display-only note indicating which modes require gateway integration before activation.
8. AUDIT TRAIL: All create/edit/deactivate/delete actions logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-018**

*User Story*: As an Insurance, Garage, or Supplier user, I want to request a missing master attribute from within a transactional screen, so that I am not blocked from completing my work when a needed value is absent from the master list.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. TRIGGER POINTS: 'Request Missing Attribute' option appears as a link/button at the bottom of any master-data dropdown (Item, Category, Vehicle Make/Model/Variant, Damage Category, Speciality) when the user cannot find the needed value. Available from: Inventory creation, Claim creation, RFQ creation, Auction creation, Hot Deal creation.
2. REQUEST FORM: Modal appears with: Attribute Type (pre-filled based on which dropdown triggered it), Requested Value (required, free text), Reason/Description (required), Reference (optional — e.g., Claim ID, RFQ ID). User clicks 'Submit Request'.
3. SYSTEM BEHAVIOUR: System creates a pending attribute request record. User sees toast: 'Your request has been submitted. Our team will review and add it shortly.' User can continue with the rest of their form — other fields are NOT blocked.
4. ADMIN REVIEW: Pending requests appear in Admin → Master Attributes → Pending Requests tab with count badge. Each request shows: Attribute Type, Requested Value, Requester Name, User Type, Submission Date, Reference.
5. ADMIN APPROVE: Admin clicks 'Approve' — attribute is created and activated immediately. Requester receives in-app and email notification: 'Your request for [Value] has been approved. It is now available in the system.'
6. ADMIN REJECT: Admin clicks 'Reject' — must provide a reason. Requester notified with reason.
7. ADMIN MERGE: If the requested value is a near-duplicate, Admin can 'Merge with Existing' — selects the existing record and requester is notified it maps to that value.
8. REQUEST HISTORY: Users can view past requests and statuses from their My Account section.
9. AUDIT TRAIL: All requests, approvals, rejections, and merges are logged.

---

**US-ADM-014-019**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want a unified Master Attributes Audit Log, so that every change to any master record is traceable for compliance, debugging, and dispute resolution.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. NAVIGATION: Admin Dashboard → Master Attributes → Audit Log.
2. LOG LISTING: Table — Timestamp, Admin User, Action (Created/Edited/Activated/Deactivated/Deleted/Approved Request/Rejected Request), Master Type (Item/Category/Sub-Category/Vehicle Category/Vehicle Type/Make/Model/Variant/Manufacture Origin/Damage Category/Damage Sub-Category/Speciality/Licensing Authority/Feature Group/Claim Status/Order Status/Payment Mode), Record Name/Code, Old Value (for edits), New Value (for edits).
3. SEARCH & FILTER: Search by Admin User name or Record Name; filter by Master Type, Action, Date Range.
4. DETAIL VIEW: Clicking a log entry opens full detail with before/after values in a side-by-side view.
5. EXPORT: Export filtered audit log as CSV/Excel.
6. RETENTION: Audit logs retained indefinitely — never auto-deleted. Deletion of a master record does NOT erase its audit history.
7. READ-ONLY: Audit logs are immutable — no Admin can edit or delete log entries.

---

**US-ADM-014-020**

*User Story*: As the System, I want to enforce cascading dependency checks and provide a dependency impact preview before any deactivation or deletion, so that Admins can make informed decisions without causing data integrity issues.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. DEPENDENCY CHECK TRIGGER: Whenever Admin initiates Delete or Deactivate on any master record, the system performs a real-time dependency scan before showing the confirmation dialog.
2. DEPENDENCY IMPACT PREVIEW: Confirmation dialog shows: Record Name, Action Requested, Dependency Summary table with columns: Module | Record Type | Count | Sample IDs. Example row: Inventory | Vehicle Record | 15 | INV-001, INV-002...
3. DELETE BLOCKING: If any active transactional record references the master record, Delete is blocked outright. The dialog shows 'Cannot Delete' and offers 'Deactivate Instead' as the only action.
4. DEACTIVATE WITH DEPENDENCIES: Deactivation always allowed even with references. Dialog clearly states: 'Deactivating this record will hide it from all new creation forms. [N] existing record(s) will retain the value but it will be labelled Inactive in admin views.' Admin must click 'Confirm Deactivation'.
5. CASCADING PARENT-CHILD DEACTIVATION: Deactivating a parent master shows cascade warning: 'Deactivating this will also deactivate [N] child record(s): [list]. Confirm?' Admin must explicitly confirm. Cascade is applied atomically.
6. NO ORPHAN CREATION: The system ensures no child record can reference an inactive parent in any creation flow.
7. AUDIT: Every confirmed deactivation/deletion — including cascades — is fully logged in the Audit Trail as a batch entry.

---

### PH-UC-ADM-015 — Admin – Manage Orders (1 stories)

**US-ADM-015-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage all orders, so that I have governance over transactions.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Orders page: all orders with Type (badge), Status, User, Amount, Date. 2. Filters: Order Type (Supplier/Garage/Auction/Hot Deal), Status, Date, User. 3. Detail: items, payment, fulfillment, timeline. 4. Admin actions: resolve disputes, approve refunds, override status (with reason logged).

---

### PH-UC-ADM-016 — Admin – Manage Payments (1 stories)

**US-ADM-016-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage all payments, so that financial compliance is maintained.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Payments page: table — Date, User, Amount, Purpose (Order/Subscription/Auction/Hot Deal — badge), Status (Completed/Pending/Failed/Refunded), Transaction ID. 2. Filters: User Type, Purpose, Status, Date Range. 3. Actions: View Details, Process Refund (with reason). 4. Export for accounting (CSV). 5. Reconciliation dashboard: summary cards showing Total Revenue, Pending, Refunded.

---

### PH-UC-CR-001 — Credit / Payment Terms Management (1 stories)

**US-CR-001-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to configure payment terms, so that flexible billing is supported.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Payment Terms page: configurable terms — Credit Period (days), Credit Limit (amount), Partial Payment toggle, Overdue Grace Period. 2. Assign terms to users via user detail page. 3. Outstanding Credits dashboard: table of users with overdue amounts, days overdue, last payment date. 4. Automated email reminders at 3, 7, 14 days overdue. 5. User sees payment due banner on their dashboard.

---

### PH-UC-CR-002 — Revenue Management (1 stories)

**US-CR-002-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to track platform revenue, so that financial performance is visible.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Revenue Dashboard: summary cards (Total Revenue, Subscriptions, Orders, Auctions, Hot Deals). 2. Breakdown by time period (daily/weekly/monthly — toggle) and user type (Insurance/Garage/Supplier). 3. Charts: revenue trend line, revenue by category pie chart. 4. Export reports. 5. Date range selector.

---

### PH-UC-CR-003 — Subscription Management (Advanced Logic & Custom Plans) (1 stories)

**US-CR-003-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to create custom subscription plans and assign them, so that special pricing is handled.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. 'Create Custom Plan' button on Plans page → form same as regular plan but with 'Custom' flag and 'Hidden from Frontend' toggle on. 2. Assign: search user → 'Assign Custom Plan' → select plan → confirm. 3. Custom plans listed in a 'Custom Plans' tab visible only to admin.

---

### PH-UC-CR-004 — Insurance User Management & Per-User Subscription Model (1 stories)

**US-CR-004-001**

*User Story*: As a Platform Administrator, I want to manage insurance sub-users with claim ownership and per-user subscriptions, so that granular control is available.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Insurance Company detail > 'Users' tab: list of sub-users with Name, Role, Claims Assigned, Subscription. 2. 'Add User' button → invite form. 3. Claim reassignment: drag-and-drop or 'Reassign' button on claim → select new owner. 4. Per-user subscription: plan assigned per user with slab pricing visible in detail.

---

### PH-UC-CR-005 — Comment Box / Chat Box (Insurance ↔ Garage) (1 stories)

**US-CR-005-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance/Garage User, I want a comment box within the RFQ timeline, so that I can discuss quotation details.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. RFQ detail page has a 'Messages' section below the timeline: chat-style interface with message bubbles (sender name, timestamp, text). 2. Text input at bottom with 'Send' button and file attachment icon. 3. New messages appear in real-time without page refresh. 4. Unread message count shown on the RFQ row in the list. 5. Notification on new message (web + email per preferences).

---

### PH-UC-CR-006 — Independent Garage & Supplier Operations (1 stories)

**US-CR-006-001**

*User Story*: As a Garage User, I want to create direct RFQs to suppliers without a claim, so that B2B parts procurement is supported.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. 'Direct RFQ' module in Garage sidebar. 2. Create form: Part Name, Quantity, Specifications, Preferred Delivery Date; select suppliers. 3. Quote exchange, order creation, and payment — same flow as claim-linked RFQ but without claim dependency. 4. Separate 'Direct Orders' section in Orders page.

---

### PH-UC-CR-007 — Inventory Stock Management (Insurance & Garage) (1 stories)

**US-CR-007-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance/Garage User, I want to track inventory stock levels based on purchases and consumption, so that stock accuracy is maintained.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Inventory page adds 'Stock' column showing current balance. 2. Stock-in events auto-created when purchase orders are delivered. 3. Stock-out events on parts consumption or sale. 4. Colour indicators: Green (>10), Amber (5–10), Red (<5). 5. 'Low Stock' filter shows items below reorder level. 6. Stock movement history: expandable row showing In/Out entries with reference.

---

### PH-UC-CR-008 — Favourite Garage & Supplier (1 stories)

**US-CR-008-001**

*User Story*: As an Insurance User, I want to mark garages and suppliers as favourites, so that they appear first in RFQ selection.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. Heart/star icon on garage/supplier profile and list rows; click to toggle favourite (filled = favourited). 2. 'My Favourites' tab in Garages/Suppliers page. 3. In RFQ garage selection, favourites appear in a highlighted 'Favourites' section at top. 4. Favourite count badge on Garages nav item.

---

### PH-UC-CR-009 — Eligibility vs Favourite Separation (1 stories)

**US-CR-009-001**

*User Story*: As the System, I want to separate eligible from favourite entities in RFQ selection, so that favourites are prioritized without overriding rules.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. RFQ garage/supplier selection screen has two sections: 'Your Favourites' (top, highlighted) and 'All Eligible' (below). 2. Non-eligible favourites shown greyed with tooltip 'Not eligible for this claim — Reason: [e.g., speciality mismatch]'. 3. User can still select non-eligible favourites with a confirmation: 'This garage is not in the recommended list. Continue anyway?'

---

### PH-UC-CR-010 — Quotation Of Alternate / Substitute Parts (1 stories)

**US-CR-010-001**

*User Story*: As a Supplier User, I want to propose substitute parts when OEM is unavailable, so that the order can still be fulfilled.

*Acceptance Criteria*:

1. In quotation form, when a part is unavailable, supplier clicks 'Propose Substitute' on the line item. 2. Substitute form: Alternate Part Name, Compatibility Notes, Price, Reason (dropdown: Discontinued, Out of Stock, Better Alternative). 3. On the insurer's/garage's side: RFQ line item shows 'Substitute Proposed' amber badge with a 'Review' button. 4. Review modal: original vs substitute comparison (name, price, compatibility), 'Approve' / 'Reject' buttons. 5. Approved substitute replaces original in the quotation with a 'Substituted' tag.

---


*Total: 131 user stories across 54 use cases.*
