## 18. SRS — Non-Functional Requirements

### 18.1 Overview

Non-Functional Requirements define the quality attributes, constraints, and operational standards of the platform. These requirements ensure that the system is secure, scalable, reliable, user-friendly, and maintainable while supporting multiple stakeholders such as Insurance Companies, Garages, Suppliers, and Platform Administrators. They also define performance expectations, data protection measures, system availability, and integration capabilities necessary for smooth business operations and future scalability.

### 18.2 Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)

**1. Scalability**
- The system shall support horizontal and vertical scaling to handle increased users, claims, auctions, and orders.
- The platform shall scale automatically during peak periods such as high claim volumes or auction events.
- Future modules and integrations shall be added without impacting existing functionality.

**2. Scalability (continued)**
- The system shall support the current and anticipated growth of BCTC, including:
  - Additional showrooms, warehouses, and branches.
  - New product lines and business units.
  - Increased transaction volumes over time.
- The architecture shall allow for easy scaling of users, storage, and business processes.

**3. Availability & Reliability**
- The platform shall maintain a minimum uptime of **99.5%**, excluding scheduled maintenance.
- The system shall recover gracefully from failures without data loss.
- Users shall receive clear notifications in case of system downtime or errors.
- Scheduled maintenance shall be communicated to users in advance.

**4. Security**
- The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC) for all users.
- All data transmitted between users and the system shall be encrypted.
- Sensitive information such as passwords, documents, and images shall be encrypted at rest.
- All admin and financial actions shall be logged for audit purposes.
- Inactive or suspended accounts shall be prevented from accessing the platform.

**5. Data Privacy**
- User data shall be accessible only to authorized users based on role and permissions.
- Uploaded images, documents, and claim details shall not be visible to unauthorized users.
- Data retention and deletion policies shall comply with regulatory requirements.
- The system shall prevent unauthorized data sharing between insurance, garage, and supplier users.

**6. Usability**
- The system shall provide a consistent and intuitive user interface across all modules.
- Search, filter, and export functionalities shall behave consistently across the platform.
- Error messages and system notifications shall be user-friendly and actionable.
- The platform shall support responsive design for desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.

**7. Maintainability**
- The system shall be modular to allow easy maintenance and enhancement.
- Configuration changes such as statuses, thresholds, and feature toggles shall not require code changes.
- AI models shall support version control and rollback.
- Fixes or enhancements in one module shall not negatively impact other modules.

**8. Audit & Logging**
- All critical user actions shall be logged with user ID, timestamp, and action details.
- Financial transactions shall maintain immutable audit trails.
- AI assessment results shall record confidence scores and model versions.
- Logs shall be available for compliance audits and troubleshooting.

**9. Integration**
- The platform shall support secure API-based integration with third-party systems.
- Integration failures shall be handled with retry and error-handling mechanisms.
- Changes in external systems shall not disrupt core platform functionality.

**10. Reporting and Data Accuracy**
- Reports shall reflect near real-time system data.
- Exported data shall match the information displayed on the user interface.
- Access to reports shall be restricted based on user roles.

**11. Compliance and Constraints**
- The platform shall comply with applicable data protection and security standards.
- Wallet management for refunds and payments is explicitly out of scope.
- AI-generated assessments shall act as decision-support and may require human validation in low-confidence cases.

---

## 19. SRS — Payment Gateway Integration

### 19.1 Overview

The platform will integrate with a third-party Payment Gateway system to enable secure, reliable, and seamless payment processing across Insurance Companies, Garages, Suppliers, and Platform Administrators. The integration will support payment initiation, confirmation, refunds, and transaction status tracking while ensuring compliance with security standards and audit requirements. **No other third-party system integrations are included in the current scope.**

### 19.2 Integration Requirements

**1. Payment Gateway Integration Scope**
- The platform shall integrate ONLY with a third-party Payment Gateway system.
- **No other external systems** (ERP, accounting, wallet systems, or CRMs) shall be integrated as part of this scope.
- The payment gateway shall be used for all monetary transactions across the platform.

**2. Supported Payment Transactions**
- The system shall allow users to make payments for:
  - Orders (parts, services, hot deals, auctions)
  - Subscription plans
  - Auction deposit fees
- The system shall support partial and full refunds via the payment gateway.
- **Wallet-based payments and internal balance management are out of scope.**

**3. Payment Flow**
- The system shall redirect users securely to the payment gateway for payment processing.
- On successful payment, the gateway shall return a confirmation response to the platform.
- On failed or cancelled payments, the system shall update the transaction status accordingly.
- Users shall receive clear success or failure notifications after payment completion.

**4. Payment Status Synchronization**
- The system shall store payment transaction details including: Transaction ID, Payment status, Amount paid, Date and time of transaction.
- Payment status shall be updated in near real-time based on gateway responses.
- In case of delayed responses, the system shall reconcile payment status using gateway callbacks or APIs.

**5. Refund Handling**
- Refunds shall be initiated from the platform and processed through the payment gateway.
- Refund status shall be tracked and displayed to authorized users.
- The system shall handle partial and full refunds as per business rules.
- All refund transactions shall be logged for audit purposes.

**6. Security and Compliance**
- All payment-related communication shall be encrypted.
- The platform shall NOT store sensitive payment information such as card details.
- The payment gateway integration shall comply with applicable security standards.
- Access to initiate or manage payments shall be restricted based on user roles.

**7. Error Handling and Reliability**
- The system shall handle gateway timeouts and failures gracefully.
- Failed transactions shall not result in duplicate charges.
- Retry mechanisms shall be implemented for non-critical failures.
- Clear error messages shall be displayed to users in case of payment issues.

**8. Audit and Reporting**
- All payment and refund actions shall be logged with user details and timestamps.
- Admin users shall be able to view payment transaction history.
- Export of payment reports shall be available for reconciliation and auditing.

**9. Configuration and Extensibility**
- Payment gateway credentials and settings shall be configurable without code changes.
- The system shall support future replacement or addition of payment gateways with minimal impact.
- Environment-specific configurations (sandbox, production) shall be supported.

### 19.3 Payment Gateway Status (per May 2026 transcripts)

Vinoth in active evaluation of ~12 payment gateway providers over 2-week period (started May 8). Use cases that selected gateway must support:
- Subscription fees
- Auction deposits + refunds
- Hot deal purchases
- Vehicle valuation portal credits
- Monthly invoicing
- Garage / supplier payouts (Insurance pays platform, platform deducts commission, disburses to seller)

---

## 20. SRS — Assumptions & Constraints

### 20.1 Assumptions

- All stakeholders (Insurance, Garage, Supplier, and Admin users) will have stable internet connectivity to access the platform.
- Users will provide accurate and complete information during registration, claims, orders, and payments.
- The selected payment gateway will provide stable APIs, documentation, and sandbox environments for integration and testing.
- Payment gateway services will handle all sensitive payment data and compliance requirements.
- **Refunds will be processed only through the payment gateway; no internal wallet or credit balance will be maintained.**
- **AI-based damage assessment will act as decision support and may require human validation in low-confidence cases.**
- System roles, permissions, and access levels are finalized and approved before development.
- All uploaded images, documents, and data will meet required quality standards for processing.
- Admin approvals are required for registrations, master data creation, and critical updates.
- Reports and analytics are based on data available within the platform and may not reflect external systems.
- Time zones and currency configurations will be predefined and consistent across the platform.
- Any future integrations beyond the payment gateway will be handled as separate phases.

### 20.2 Constraints

- **Integration is limited strictly to a payment gateway system only; no other third-party integrations are in scope.**
- **Refund will be done to the original payment method** depending on the payment gateway.
- AI models are dependent on the quality and availability of training data.
- Regulatory, compliance, or gateway-specific limitations may affect payment features and workflows.
- Platform performance may vary based on user load and network conditions.
- Changes to business rules after sign-off may impact timelines and cost.
- The platform will operate within the constraints of the selected technology stack.
- System availability may be affected during scheduled maintenance windows.
- Any customization beyond documented requirements will require separate approval.
- Historical data migration is limited to what is explicitly agreed and documented.
- User actions are logged and auditable, and logs cannot be modified by users.
- Feature availability may vary based on subscription plans and roles.

---

## 21. SRS — User Acceptance Criteria

### 21.1 Overview

User Acceptance Criteria define the conditions under which the platform will be considered acceptable by business users. These criteria ensure that the system meets functional expectations, supports defined business workflows, enforces role-based access, and delivers a stable, secure, and usable experience for Insurance Companies, Garages, Suppliers, and Platform Administrators before go-live.

### 21.2 Acceptance Criteria

**1. General Platform Acceptance**
- Users shall be able to access the platform using supported browsers without errors.
- The system shall display role-specific dashboards and menus after login.
- Unauthorized users shall not be able to access restricted features.
- All critical actions shall display confirmation or error messages.

**2. User Registration & Login**
- Users shall be able to register with mandatory details as per their role.
- Login shall be successful with valid credentials.
- Forgot password functionality shall reset passwords securely.
- Admin approval shall be mandatory where applicable before account activation.

**3. Role-Based Functionality**
- Insurance users shall only access insurance-specific modules.
- Garage users shall only access garage-specific features.
- Supplier users shall only access supplier-specific features.
- Admin users shall have access to all management and master modules.
- Subscription plan restrictions shall be enforced correctly.

**4. Claims & Orders**
- Insurance users shall be able to create and track claim requests.
- Garages shall be able to view, accept, reject, and quote claim requests.
- Orders shall display correct status, details, and history.
- Replacement and refund requests shall follow defined workflows.
- Claim and order statuses shall update correctly after actions.

**5. Auctions & Hot Deals**
- Users shall be able to view active auctions and hot deals based on eligibility.
- Auctions shall accept bids only after deposit payment.
- Auction and hot deal items shall display accurate details.
- Order details generated from auctions or hot deals shall be correct.

**6. Inventory & Master Data**
- Admin shall be able to manage master items and categories.
- Insurance, garage, and supplier users shall request missing master items.
- Inventory listings shall reflect accurate quantities and status.
- Deleted or inactive items shall not be selectable in transactions.

**7. Payments & Refunds**
- Payments shall redirect securely to the payment gateway.
- Successful payments shall update system records immediately.
- Failed payments shall not result in partial or duplicate records.
- Refunds shall be processed through the payment gateway only to the original payment method.

**8. Subscription Management**
- Users shall be able to view available subscription plans.
- Subscription upgrades and downgrades shall reflect correctly after payment.
- Subscription history shall be visible to users and admins.
- Feature access shall update based on the active subscription.

**9. Search, Filter & Export**
- Search and filter options shall return accurate results.
- Exported reports shall match on-screen data.
- Only authorized users shall be able to export reports.

**10. Admin Management Modules**
- Admin shall be able to manage users, roles, groups, subscriptions, and masters.
- Status changes (active/inactive) shall reflect immediately.
- Admin actions shall be logged for audit purposes.
- Deleted entities shall follow defined system rules.

**11. Error Handling & Messages**
- System errors shall display clear, user-friendly messages.
- Validation errors shall guide users to correct inputs.
- **No critical action shall fail silently.**

**12. Performance & Stability**
- Pages shall load within acceptable time limits.
- The system shall remain stable during concurrent user activity.
- No data loss shall occur during normal or failed operations.

**13. Final Acceptance**
- All agreed functional and non-functional requirements shall be met.
- **No critical or high-severity defects shall remain open.**
- Business stakeholders shall validate end-to-end workflows.
- System shall be approved for production deployment.



---

## 22. Vehicle Hierarchy — 10 Levels with Concrete Data

Source: `The Vehicle and Parts Hierachy Structure.docx`

The Parts Hub platform uses a 10-level vehicle hierarchy that allows precise vehicle identification down to chassis code level. This hierarchy was approved by Vinoth on May 11, 2026 for BOTH Garage and Supplier onboarding.

### Full Vehicle & Parts Hierarchy Document (verbatim)


THE VEHICLE HIERARCHY — 10 Levels

LEVEL 1 — Vehicle Type

| ID | Vehicle Type |
|---|---|
| VT-01 | Passenger Car |
| VT-02 | SUV / Crossover |
| VT-03 | 4x4 / Off-Road |
| VT-04 | Pickup Truck |
| VT-05 | Van |
| VT-06 | Minibus |
| VT-07 | Bus |
| VT-08 | Light Commercial Vehicle (LCV) |
| VT-09 | Heavy Commercial Truck (HCV) |
| VT-10 | Trailer / Semi-Trailer |
| VT-11 | Motorcycle |
| VT-12 | Electric Vehicle (EV) |
| VT-13 | Construction Equipment |
| VT-14 | Agricultural Equipment |
| VT-15 | Industrial / Warehouse Equipment |
| VT-16 | Special Purpose Vehicle |

LEVEL 2 — Vehicle Origin / Country

Filtered by Level 1 selection. Same list applies across all vehicle types.

| ID | Origin |
|---|---|
| VO-01 | Japanese |
| VO-02 | German |
| VO-03 | Korean |
| VO-04 | American |
| VO-05 | British |
| VO-06 | French |
| VO-07 | Italian |
| VO-08 | Swedish |
| VO-09 | Spanish |
| VO-10 | Chinese |
| VO-11 | Indian |
| VO-12 | Czech / Eastern European |
| VO-13 | Middle Eastern |
| VO-14 | Other / Multi-national |

LEVEL 3 — Brand Segment / Category

Type-aware — segment options change based on Vehicle Type. A Brand Segment record always belongs to one Vehicle Type.

Examples:

| Vehicle Type | Brand Segment |
|---|---|
| Passenger Car | Economy / Mass Market |
| Passenger Car | Compact / Minicar |
| Passenger Car | Luxury / Premium |
| Passenger Car | Performance / Sports |
| Passenger Car | Supercar / Exotic |
| SUV / Crossover | Compact Crossover |
| SUV / Crossover | Mid-Size SUV |
| SUV / Crossover | Full-Size SUV |
| SUV / Crossover | Luxury SUV |
| SUV / Crossover | Off-Road / Expedition |
| HCV Truck | Light Truck (3.5–7.5T) |
| HCV Truck | Medium Truck (7.5–18T) |
| HCV Truck | Heavy Truck (18T+) |
| HCV Truck | Semi / Tractor Unit |
| Construction Equipment | Earthmoving |
| Construction Equipment | Lifting / Cranes |
| Construction Equipment | Concrete & Roadwork |
| Construction Equipment | Material Handling |
| EV | EV Passenger |
| EV | EV SUV / Crossover |
| EV | EV Commercial |
| EV | EV Motorcycle |
| EV | EV Bus |

LEVEL 4 — Brand

Filtered by Vehicle Type + Origin + Brand Segment. A brand belongs to one primary Vehicle Type and one Origin. Cross-type brands (e.g., Mercedes Cars vs Mercedes Trucks) are registered as separate brand records.

Examples:

| Brand | Vehicle Type | Origin | Segment |
|---|---|---|---|
| Toyota | Passenger Car | Japanese | Economy / Mass Market |
| Lexus | Passenger Car | Japanese | Luxury / Premium |
| BMW | Passenger Car | German | Luxury / Premium |
| Mercedes-Benz | Passenger Car | German | Luxury / Premium |
| Mercedes-Benz Trucks | HCV Truck | German | Heavy Truck |
| Caterpillar | Construction Equipment | American | Earthmoving |
| Komatsu | Construction Equipment | Japanese | Earthmoving |
| BYD | EV | Chinese | EV Passenger |
| Volvo Cars | Passenger Car | Swedish | Luxury / Premium |
| Volvo Trucks | HCV Truck | Swedish | Heavy Truck |
| GMC | SUV / Crossover | American | Full-Size SUV |
| Haval | SUV / Crossover | Chinese | Mid-Size SUV |

LEVEL 5 — Model Family

Applies to all brands without exception. Brands with one product line get a single "General" family. This level exists in every record for schema consistency.

Examples:

| Brand | Model Family |
|---|---|
| Toyota | Corolla Family |
| Toyota | Camry Family |
| Toyota | Land Cruiser Family |
| Toyota | Hilux Family |
| Toyota | RAV4 Family |
| Toyota | HiAce Family |
| Toyota | GR Performance |
| BMW | Standard Series (1–7) |
| BMW | X Series |
| BMW | M Series |
| BMW | i Series (EV) |
| Caterpillar | Track Excavators |
| Caterpillar | Wheel Loaders |
| Caterpillar | Motor Graders |
| Caterpillar | Bulldozers |
| Komatsu | PC Series (Excavators) |
| Komatsu | WA Series (Wheel Loaders) |
| Scania | R Series |
| Scania | S Series |
| BYD | Han Series |
| BYD | Seal Series |
| Ferrari | General (single family) |

LEVEL 6 — Model

The specific named model, filtered from Model Family.

Examples:

| Brand | Model Family | Model |
|---|---|---|
| Toyota | Land Cruiser Family | Land Cruiser 300 |
| Toyota | Land Cruiser Family | Land Cruiser Prado |
| Toyota | Land Cruiser Family | Land Cruiser 70 Series |
| Toyota | Camry Family | Camry |
| BMW | X Series | X5 |
| BMW | M Series | M3 |
| Caterpillar | Track Excavators | 320 |
| Caterpillar | Track Excavators | 336 |
| Caterpillar | Track Excavators | 349 |
| Nissan | Patrol Family | Patrol |
| Mercedes-Benz | E-Class Family | E-Class |

LEVEL 7 — Generation / Production Series

Every model has one or more generations. This is the structural break — parts compatibility resets between generations.

| Field | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Generation Name | Human-readable name | "F30 Generation", "XV70 Series", "J300 Series" |
| Production Year Start | Year production began | 2018 |
| Production Year End | Year production ended (blank = current) | 2023 |
| Chassis / Platform Code | Manufacturer platform code | G20, XV70, FJA300W |
| Generation Notes | Key structural changes vs previous gen | "New TNGA platform, different engine mounts" |

Examples:

| Model | Generation Name | Years | Chassis Code |
|---|---|---|---|
| BMW 3 Series | E46 | 1998–2006 | E46 |
| BMW 3 Series | E90 | 2005–2013 | E90 |
| BMW 3 Series | F30 | 2012–2018 | F30 |
| BMW 3 Series | G20 | 2019–2025 | G20 |
| Toyota Camry | XV50 | 2012–2017 | XV50 |
| Toyota Camry | XV70 | 2018–2024 | XV70 |
| Toyota Land Cruiser | J200 | 2008–2021 | UZJ200W |
| Toyota Land Cruiser | J300 | 2022–Present | FJA300W |
| Caterpillar 320 | D Series | 2008–2015 | 320D |
| Caterpillar 320 | Next Gen | 2019–Present | 320-07 |
| Mercedes E-Class | W212 | 2009–2016 | W212 |
| Mercedes E-Class | W213 | 2016–Present | W213 |

LEVEL 8 — Variant / Trim + GCC Spec

Two things live here together — the variant/trim (what the manufacturer sells) and the market spec (which market it was built for). Both directly affect parts fitment.

Variant fields:

| Field | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Variant Name | Full trim/configuration name | "3.5L VXR", "M-Sport 330i", "Long Wheelbase" |
| Body Style | Physical body format | Sedan, Coupe, Wagon, Hatchback, LWB, SWB, Crew Cab, Double Cab |
| Doors | Number of doors | 2, 4, 5 |
| Seating Capacity | Number of seats | 5, 7, 3 (cab) |

GCC Spec field — mandatory on every variant:

| Spec Type | Description |
|---|---|
| GCC Spec | Built for Gulf / Middle East — larger cooling, upgraded AC |
| Euro Spec | European market |
| US Spec | North American market |
| JDM | Japan domestic market |
| ADM | Australian / Right-hand drive market |
| Grey Market | Non-GCC spec registered in UAE |
| Universal | Spec-neutral (applies to most equipment) |

Examples:

| Model | Variant | Body Style | Spec |
|---|---|---|---|
| Toyota Camry XV70 | LE 2.5L | Sedan | GCC Spec |
| Toyota Camry XV70 | XSE V6 3.5L | Sedan | GCC Spec |
| Toyota Camry XV70 | Hybrid LE | Sedan | GCC Spec |
| BMW 3 Series G20 | 320i M-Sport | Sedan | Euro Spec |
| BMW 3 Series G20 | 330e Plug-in Hybrid | Sedan | Euro Spec |
| BMW 3 Series G20 | M340i | Sedan | GCC Spec |
| Nissan Patrol Y62 | Platinum V8 | SUV | GCC Spec |
| GMC Yukon | Denali | SUV | US Spec |
| Land Cruiser 300 | GR Sport 3.5T | SUV | GCC Spec |
| Caterpillar 320 | 320 Standard | N/A | Universal |

LEVEL 9 — Engine & Drivetrain Specification

The most precise technical identifier. One variant can have multiple engine specs if the same trim was sold with different engines in different years or markets.

| Field | Applies To | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| Engine Code | All ICE / Hybrid | 2GR-FKS, B48B20, D13K, C7.1 ACERT |
| Displacement | ICE / Hybrid | 1.5L, 2.5L, 3.5L, 13.0L |
| Configuration | ICE / Hybrid | 3-Cyl, 4-Cyl Inline, V6, V8, V12, Inline-6 |
| Induction Type | ICE / Hybrid | Naturally Aspirated, Turbo, Twin Turbo, Supercharged |
| Fuel Type | All | Petrol, Diesel, LPG, CNG, Full EV, Hybrid (Full), PHEV, Dual Fuel, Hydrogen |
| Electric Motor Output | EV / Hybrid | 150kW, 300kW, Dual Motor 400kW |
| Battery Capacity | EV / Hybrid | 60kWh, 100kWh, 8.8kWh (PHEV) |
| Drive Type | All | FWD, RWD, AWD, 4WD Part-Time, 4WD Full-Time, 6x4, 8x4 |
| Transmission Type | All | Auto 8-Speed, Manual 6-Speed, CVT, DCT, e-CVT, Allison Auto |
| Gearbox Code | All | GA8HP45Z, U760E, UE700E |
| Power Output (HP / kW) | All | 150hp / 110kW, 420hp / 309kW |
| Torque (Nm) | All | 350Nm, 2200Nm |
| Emissions Standard | ICE / Hybrid | Euro 4, Euro 6, Euro 6d, Tier 4 Final (equipment) |

Examples:

| Variant | Engine Code | Fuel Type | Drive Type | Transmission |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Camry XV70 LE | A25A-FKS | Petrol | FWD | Auto 8-Speed |
| Camry XV70 Hybrid LE | A25A-FXS | Full Hybrid | FWD | e-CVT |
| Land Cruiser 300 GCC | F33A-FTV | Diesel Twin Turbo | 4WD Full-Time | Auto 10-Speed |
| Land Cruiser 300 GCC | V35A-FTS | Petrol Twin Turbo | 4WD Full-Time | Auto 10-Speed |
| BMW G20 M340i | B58B30O1 | Petrol Turbo | AWD (xDrive) | Auto 8-Speed |
| BMW G20 330e | B46B20B | PHEV | RWD | Auto 8-Speed |
| Scania R580 | DC13 | Diesel | 6x4 | Scania Opticruise |
| Caterpillar 320 | C7.1 ACERT | Diesel | N/A | Hydraulic Drive |
| BYD Han EV | — | Full EV | AWD | Single Speed |
| Nissan Patrol V8 | VK56VD | Petrol | 4WD Part-Time | Auto 7-Speed |

LEVEL 10 — Chassis / Body Code

The final, most precise identifier. Links directly to OEM parts catalogues. For equipment, this is the machine series/revision code.

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| Chassis Code | Manufacturer's official chassis/body designation |
| Body Code | Specific body variant code where different from chassis |
| VIN Prefix Pattern | First 8–11 digits of VIN that identify this exact configuration |
| OEM Catalogue Reference | Reference ID in TecDoc or equivalent catalogue |

Examples:

| Model | Variant | Chassis Code | VIN Prefix Pattern |
|---|---|---|---|
| BMW 3 Series 2019 Sedan | All G20 Sedan | G20 | WBA5R |
| BMW 3 Series 2019 Touring | All G20 Wagon | G21 | WBA5T |
| Toyota Camry 2018+ | All XV70 | XV70 | 4T1B11 |
| Mercedes C-Class 2015 Sedan | W205 | W205 | WDD205 |
| Mercedes C-Class 2015 Coupe | C205 | C205 | WDD205C |
| Land Cruiser 300 Petrol | FJA300W | FJA300W | JTMGA |
| Land Cruiser 300 Diesel | VJA300W | VJA300W | JTMGB |
| Nissan Patrol Y62 | All | Y62 | JN8AY |
| Caterpillar 320 Next Gen | Standard | 320-07 | CAT0320 |
| Komatsu PC360 | LC-11 | PC360LC-11 | KOM360 |

THE PARTS DATABASE — Parallel Structure

PART CATEGORY TREE

Universal — covers all vehicle types. Three levels deep.

Level A — Part Category:

| ID | Part Category | Primary Vehicle Types |
|---|---|---|
| PC-01 | Engine & Components | All ICE / Hybrid |
| PC-02 | Electric Powertrain | EV / Hybrid |
| PC-03 | Transmission & Drivetrain | All |
| PC-04 | Suspension & Steering | All road vehicles |
| PC-05 | Braking System | All |
| PC-06 | Hydraulic System | Equipment / Trucks |
| PC-07 | Undercarriage | Tracked Equipment |
| PC-08 | Axle & Wheel Ends | Trucks / Equipment |
| PC-09 | Body & Exterior | All |
| PC-10 | Glass | All |
| PC-11 | Electrical & Electronics | All |
| PC-12 | HVAC & Cabin Comfort | All with cabs |
| PC-13 | Exhaust & Emissions | ICE / Hybrid |
| PC-14 | Fuel System | ICE / Hybrid |
| PC-15 | Chassis & Frame | Trucks / Equipment |
| PC-16 | Attachments & Implements | Equipment |
| PC-17 | Interior | Passenger / Cab |
| PC-18 | Wheels, Tyres & Tracks | All |
| PC-19 | Filters & Fluids | All |
| PC-20 | Seals, Gaskets & Fasteners | All |
| PC-21 | Accessories & Modifications | All |

Level B — Part Sub-Category (examples under key categories):

| Category | Sub-Category |
|---|---|
| Engine | Timing System, Cooling System, Lubrication, Air Intake, Engine Mounts, Short Block, Long Block |
| Electric Powertrain | HV Battery Pack, Battery Management System (BMS), Electric Motor, Inverter / Controller, Onboard Charger, DC-DC Converter |
| Transmission | Gearbox Assembly, Clutch Kit, Torque Converter, Transfer Case, Prop Shaft, CV Joints |
| Suspension | Shock Absorbers / Struts, Control Arms, Tie Rods, Sway Bar, Bushings, Wheel Bearings |
| Braking | Brake Pads, Brake Rotors / Discs, Brake Calipers, Brake Lines / Hoses, Master Cylinder, ABS Module |
| Body & Exterior | Bumpers, Doors, Bonnet / Hood, Fenders, Mirrors, Grilles, Pillars, Trunk / Tailgate |
| Electrical | ECU / Control Modules, Sensors, Wiring Harness, Alternator, Starter Motor |
| Lighting | Headlight Assembly, Tail Light Assembly, Fog Lights, DRL, Indicators |
| Hydraulic | Hydraulic Pump, Hydraulic Cylinders, Control Valves, Hoses & Fittings, Hydraulic Filters |
| Undercarriage | Track Chain Assembly, Sprockets, Idlers, Top / Bottom Rollers, Track Shoes |
| Filters & Fluids | Engine Oil Filter, Air Filter, Fuel Filter, Cabin Filter, Hydraulic Filter, Coolant, Engine Oil, AdBlue |
| Attachments | Buckets, Blades, Forks, Augers, Hydraulic Hammers, Quick Couplers |

Level C — Part Name / Type: The specific named part within a sub-category.

Example path: Engine → Cooling System → Water Pump Example path: Braking → Brake Pads → Front Brake Pad Set Example path: Electric Powertrain → HV Battery Pack → High Voltage Battery Module

PART ITEM RECORD — All Fields

Every part in the master database has this record. Admin-controlled. Suppliers and garages cannot add to this — they select from it.

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Part Item ID | System ID | Unique system-generated identifier |
| Part Name | Text | e.g., "Front Brake Pad Set" |
| Part Category | Link → PC Level A |  |
| Part Sub-Category | Link → PC Level B |  |
| Part Type | Link → PC Level C |  |
| Has OEM Part Number | Boolean | Yes / No — controls downstream behavior |
| OEM Part Number | Text / Null | Official manufacturer number — blank if none |
| Aftermarket Part Numbers | List | Cross-reference part numbers from other manufacturers |
| Superseded By | Link → Part Item | If this part number replaced by a newer one |
| Part Description | Long Text | Technical description |
| Unit of Measure | Enum | Each, Set, Pair, Kit, Litre, Metre |
| Compatible Vehicles | Link → Fitment Map | Many-to-many — connects to vehicle records |
| Applicable Vehicle Types | Multi-tag | Which vehicle types this part applies to |
| Part Images | File | Multiple images |
| Weight (kg) | Decimal | For shipping |
| Dimensions | Text | L x W x H |
| Warranty Standard | Text | Default warranty period from OEM |
| Part Status | Enum | Active / Discontinued / Superseded |
| Data Source | Enum | TecDoc / Admin Manual Entry / Supplier Submitted |
| Admin Verified | Boolean | Confirmed by admin — mandatory before part goes live |

THE FITMENT MAP — The Bridge Between Vehicles and Parts

This is the most important table in the entire database. It is a many-to-many relationship table that answers: "Does this part fit this vehicle?"

One part fits many vehicle configurations. One vehicle configuration has many compatible parts.

Fitment Map Record Fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Fitment ID | System ID | Unique ID for each fitment rule |
| Part Item ID | Link → Part Item | Which part |
| Vehicle Type | Link → Level 1 |  |
| Brand | Link → Level 4 |  |
| Model | Link → Level 6 |  |
| Generation | Link → Level 7 |  |
| Variant | Link → Level 8 | Specific trim — or "All Variants" flag |
| Engine Spec | Link → Level 9 | Specific engine — or "All Engines" flag |
| Chassis Code | Link → Level 10 | Most precise match |
| GCC Spec | Enum | GCC / Euro / US / JDM / ADM / Universal |
| Production Year From | Year | Start of fitment range |
| Production Year To | Year | End of fitment range (blank = current) |
| Fitment Notes | Text | e.g., "Applies only to vehicles with Sport Package" |
| Fitment Type | Enum | OEM Direct / OEM Alternative / Aftermarket Compatible |
| Position | Enum | Front / Rear / Left / Right / Upper / Lower / All |
| Verified | Boolean | Admin-confirmed fitment rule |

Example fitment records:

| Part | Fits | Year Range | Spec | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Front Brake Pad Set (Brembo OEM) | BMW G20 330i, All Variants, B48 Engine | 2019–2025 | GCC + Euro | Standard fit, not M-Sport |
| Front Brake Pad Set (Brembo M-Sport) | BMW G20 M340i, M-Sport Variant | 2019–2025 | GCC + Euro | M-Sport brake package only |
| Water Pump | Toyota Camry XV70, All Variants, A25A-FKS (2.5L) | 2018–2024 | GCC Spec | Not applicable to Hybrid variant |
| Water Pump | Toyota Camry XV70, Hybrid LE, A25A-FXS | 2018–2024 | GCC Spec | Different pump — electric assist |
| Hydraulic Pump | Caterpillar 320, Next Gen Series | 2019–Present | Universal | Main hydraulic circuit only |
| Track Chain Assembly | Komatsu PC360LC-11 | 2015–Present | Universal | 600mm shoe width standard |
| HV Battery Module | BYD Han EV, Long Range AWD | 2021–Present | Universal | 100kWh pack configuration |

HOW SUPPLIERS & GARAGES USE THE MASTER DATABASE

MASTER DATABASE (Admin-owned, locked, read-only for others)

↓

Supplier / Garage searches for a Part Item

↓

They select the Part Item record

↓

They create an INVENTORY LISTING by adding only:

→ Quantity available

→ Condition (New / Aftermarket / Used / Remanufactured / Salvage)

→ Quality Grade (OEM / OES / Grade A / Grade B — for used & aftermarket)

→ Their price (ex-VAT)

→ Lead time / availability

→ Warranty they offer

→ Which branch / location holds this stock

↓

If part has NO part number (manual entry needed):

→ Supplier fills description, images, category, vehicle fitment manually

→ Flagged as "Pending Admin Review"

→ Admin reviews → approves → promotes to Master Database

→ Now available for all suppliers to select

COMPLETE HIERARCHY IN ONE VIEW


---

## 23. Parts Database Structure

The Parts Hub maintains an Admin-controlled Parts Master Database. Suppliers and garages cannot add to this — they select from it.

### Part Category Tree (Universal, 3 levels deep)

| Level | Name |
|---|---|
| Level A | Part Category (e.g., Engine, Body & Exterior, Electrical) |
| Level B | Part Sub-Category (e.g., under Engine: Timing System, Cooling System, Lubrication, Air Intake, Engine Mounts, Short Block) |
| Level C | Part Name / Type (e.g., Water Pump, Front Brake Pad Set, High Voltage Battery Module) |

### Part Categories with Primary Vehicle Types

| ID | Part Category | Primary Vehicle Types |
|---|---|---|
| PC-01 | Engine & Components | All ICE / Hybrid |
| PC-02 | Electric Powertrain | EV / Hybrid |
| PC-03 | Transmission & Drivetrain | All |
| PC-04 | Suspension & Steering | All road vehicles |
| PC-05 | Braking System | All |
| PC-06 | Hydraulic System | Equipment / Trucks |
| PC-07 | Undercarriage | Tracked Equipment |
| (+ more) | Body & Exterior, Electrical, etc. | All |

### Part Item Record Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Part Item ID | System ID | Unique system-generated identifier |
| Part Name | Text | e.g., "Front Brake Pad Set" |
| Part Category | Link → PC Level A | |
| Part Sub-Category | Link → PC Level B | |
| Part Type | Link → PC Level C | |
| Has OEM Part Number | Boolean | Yes / No — controls downstream behavior |
| OEM Part Number | Text / Null | Official manufacturer number — blank if none |

---

## 24. Fitment Map

The most important table in the entire database. Many-to-many relationship answering: "Does this part fit this vehicle?"

### Fitment Map Record Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Fitment ID | System ID | Unique ID for each fitment rule |
| Part Item ID | Link → Part Item | Which part |
| Vehicle Type | Link → Level 1 | |
| Brand | Link → Level 4 | |
| Model | Link → Level 6 | |
| Generation | Link → Level 7 | |
| Variant | Link → Level 8 | Specific trim — or "All Variants" flag |
| (additional levels) | | Spec, Engine, Chassis Code as needed |

### How Suppliers & Garages Use the Master

```
MASTER DATABASE (Admin-owned, locked, read-only for others)
          ↓
Supplier / Garage searches for a Part Item
          ↓
They select the Part Item record
          ↓
They create an INVENTORY LISTING by adding only:
  → Quantity available
  → Condition (New / Aftermarket / Used / Remanufactured / Salvage)
  → Quality Grade (OEM / OES / Grade A / Grade B — for used & aftermarket)
  → Their price (ex-VAT)
  → Lead time / availability
  → Warranty they offer
  → Which branch / location holds this stock
          ↓
If part has NO part number (manual entry needed):
  → Supplier fills description, images, category, vehicle fitment manually
  → Flagged as "Pending Admin Review"
  → Admin reviews → approves → promotes to Master Database
  → Now available for all suppliers to select
```

---

## 25. Phase-1 Automation Philosophy — Full Doc

Source: `Automation_phase1.md` provided by Vinoth.

> Reproduced verbatim below.


## End-to-End Insurance → Garage → Supplier Automation (Phase-1, Rule-Based)

This document describes a **fully controlled workflow automation system** for managing vehicle insurance claims, garage quotations, and supplier sourcing — without using AI decisions in Phase-1.

The main objective is:

* Reduce manual effort
* Speed up claim processing
* Avoid duplicate work
* Maintain full human control
* Build a strong base for future AI automation

---

# Overall Workflow Summary

```text
Insurance Creates Claim
        ↓
System Filters Eligible Garages
        ↓
Insurance Selects Garages
        ↓
RFQ Sent to Multiple Garages
        ↓
Garages Submit Quotations
        ↓
Insurance Selects One Garage
        ↓
System Creates Supplier RFQs
        ↓
Suppliers Submit Quotes
        ↓
Insurance Selects Supplier(s)
        ↓
Order / Repair Process Starts
```

---

# Detailed Automation Breakdown

# 1. Claim Creation & Validation

### Purpose

Ensure the claim is complete before entering the workflow.

### Automation

The system validates:

* Vehicle details
* Damage details
* Inspection info
* Photos/videos/documents

### Outcome

* Incomplete claims are blocked
* One central claim record is created
* Same data reused everywhere later

✅ Eliminates duplicate data entry

---

# 2. Garage Eligibility Filtering (Rule-Based)

### Purpose

Find garages capable of handling the claim.

### Automation Rules

System checks:

* Vehicle brand/model compatibility
* Garage speciality
* Authorized vs independent type
* Location proximity
* Active status

### Outcome

* Only valid garages are shown
* Invalid garages automatically removed

⚠️ Important:
System only filters — it does NOT select garages automatically.

---

# 3. Favorites-First Recommendation

### Purpose

Prioritize trusted garages.

### Automation

Insurance companies can maintain:

* Favorite garages
* Preferred partners

### Recommendation Order

1. Favorite Garages
2. System Recommended Garages
3. All Eligible Garages

### Outcome

* Favorites appear first
* Still fully manual selection

⚠️ Favorites bypass some recommendation logic but NOT business control.

---

# 4. Insurance Control Before RFQ

### Purpose

Keep final decision with insurance users.

### Features

Insurance users can:

* Add/remove garages
* Search/filter garages
* View ratings
* Choose network/out-of-network garages

### Outcome

Manual confirmation required before RFQ dispatch.

✅ No forced automation

---

# 5. Multi-Garage RFQ Dispatch

### Purpose

Send requests simultaneously to multiple garages.

### Automation

System:

* Sends RFQs in parallel
* Tracks response deadlines
* Sends reminders
* Marks “No Response” automatically

### Outcome

* Faster response collection
* Full audit trail maintained

---

# 6. Structured Garage Quotation Submission

### Purpose

Standardize quotations.

### Mandatory Inputs

Garages must provide:

* Labor cost
* Parts cost
* Taxes
* Estimated repair time

### Allowed Actions

* Submit quote
* Reject with reason
* Request clarification

### Outcome

* Clean comparison data
* No informal communication chaos

---

# 7. Garage Quote Comparison & Selection

### Purpose

Help insurers compare garage offers.

### Automation Support

System highlights:

* Lowest price
* Fastest repair time

### Outcome

Insurance manually selects ONE garage.

⚠️ System may recommend — never auto-select.

---

# 8. Automatic Supplier RFQ Creation

### Purpose

Start parts sourcing immediately after garage approval.

### Automation

System reuses:

* Approved parts list
* Quantities
* Claim data

### Outcome

* Supplier RFQs generated automatically
* No retyping required

✅ Major operational speed improvement

---

# 9. Supplier Eligibility & Favorites

### Purpose

Find suitable suppliers.

### Rules Used

* Part category compatibility
* Vehicle compatibility
* Delivery capability
* Active supplier status

### Favorites Logic

Favorite suppliers appear first.

### Outcome

Still manually controlled by insurance users.

---

# 10. Supplier Quote Auto-Fill Using Inventory

### Purpose

Speed up supplier responses.

### Automation

If supplier inventory exists:

* Prices auto-filled
* Availability auto-filled

### Outcome

Supplier only confirms the data.

✅ Faster quotation turnaround

---

# 11. Delivery Time Enforcement

### Purpose

Prevent unrealistic sourcing delays.

### Rule

Supplier MUST provide:

* Delivery timeline per part

### Outcome

Quotes without delivery times are rejected automatically.

---

# 12. Flexible Supplier Selection

### Purpose

Allow smart sourcing strategies.

### Options

Insurance can:

* Choose one supplier
* Split parts across multiple suppliers

### Outcome

Flexible procurement control.

⚠️ No forced consolidation.

---

# 13. Partial Availability Handling

### Purpose

Avoid claim delays.

### Automation

If some parts are unavailable:

* Available parts can proceed
* Remaining parts stay open

### Outcome

Workflow never blocks completely.

✅ Continuous progress model

---

# 14. Quote Comparison Dashboard

### Purpose

Support decision-making.

### System Displays

* Price comparison
* Delivery timelines
* Availability
* Side-by-side comparison

### Outcome

Purely informational support.

⚠️ No AI decisions in Phase-1.

---

# 15. Manual Confirmation Rule (Critical Principle)

This is the MOST IMPORTANT business rule.

## System NEVER:

* Auto-selects garages
* Auto-selects suppliers
* Auto-confirms orders
* Auto-commits financial decisions

## Human confirmation required for:

* Garage selection
* Supplier selection
* Split sourcing
* Order creation

✅ Full accountability maintained

---

# Core Philosophy of This Automation

## What is Automated

* Validation
* Eligibility filtering
* Recommendations
* RFQ generation
* Notifications/reminders
* Comparisons
* Tracking

## What Remains Human-Controlled

* Commercial decisions
* Vendor selection
* Approvals
* Final confirmations

---

# Final System Architecture Vision

## This is a:

### “Human-Controlled Intelligent Workflow Automation System”

NOT:

* Fully autonomous AI system
* Auto-decision engine

The automation focuses on:

* Speed
* Accuracy
* Operational efficiency
* Auditability
* Standardization

while preserving:

* Human authority
* Business control
* Compliance

---

# Future AI Readiness (Phase-2 Vision)

This Phase-1 model creates clean structured data for future AI features like:

* Smart garage ranking
* Predictive supplier recommendations
* Fraud detection
* Repair cost prediction
* SLA risk prediction
* Automated negotiation insights
* AI-assisted claim routing

But currently:
✅ AI is NOT making decisions.



---

#### EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

#### Customer Objectives

The client aims to extend the PartsHub platform into real-world operations through dedicated mobile applications for insurance companies, garages, and suppliers. The objective is to enable faster decision-making, real-time collaboration, and on-the-ground execution across the claims, parts, and repair lifecycle. The mobile apps will support quick approvals and monitoring for insurance users, while empowering garages and suppliers with mobile-first workflows for damage capture, quotations, fulfilment, and payment tracking. Overall, the client seeks to reduce turnaround time, improve SLA compliance, enhance cost transparency, without shifting control or governance away from the web platform.

#### Navsoft’s Solution Summary

Navsoft proposes the design and development of a secure, scalable mobile application suite for PartsHub, tailored separately for insurance companies, garages, and suppliers. The solution extends the existing web platform into mobile-first operational workflows, enabling real-time approvals, quotation management, order fulfilment, and payment tracking. Each mobile app is purpose-built around user roles, execution speed, and SLA-driven actions, while core configuration, analytics, and governance remain on the web platform. By combining intuitive mobile UX with different workflow engines and backend services, Navsoft’s solution accelerates decision-making, improves operational efficiency, and drives consistent adoption across the ecosystem.

#### CUSTOMER BUSINESS & REQUIREMENTS

#### Customer Organization

The customer is a Dubai-based organization developing PartsHub, a digital broker platform designed to connect insurance companies, garages, and spare-parts suppliers within a single, integrated ecosystem. The organization operates at the intersection of insurance claims management, automotive repair services, and parts procurement, with a strong focus on reducing repair cycle time, improving cost transparency, and enabling data-driven decision-making. Through PartsHub, the customer aims to streamline multi-party collaboration, enforce SLA compliance, and bring efficiency, consistency, and scalability to vehicle claims and repair operations.

#### Project Goals

Extend the PartsHub platform into mobile-first execution to support real-time, on-the-ground operations.

Enable faster insurance approvals and exception handling through secure, decision-focused mobile workflows.

Empower garages to capture damage evidence, submit quotations, and execute repairs directly from mobile.

Enable suppliers to respond to RFQs, manage fulfilment, and track payments with minimal turnaround time.

Improve SLA adherence and reduce overall claim and repair cycle time across the ecosystem.

Increase adoption of digital workflows while maintaining governance and control through the web platform.

Ensure scalability, security, and role-based access across all mobile applications.

#### Current Systems

The customer is a new start-up currently in the product development phase and does not yet operate a live production system.

#### Stakeholders

#### Customer Operating Model

The customer operates a multi-stakeholder marketplace model that brings together insurance companies, garages, and spare-parts suppliers on a single digital platform. Insurance companies initiate and govern claim workflows, garages execute vehicle inspections and repairs, and suppliers provide parts through competitive and SLA-driven processes. The platform is designed to orchestrate collaboration, approvals, and transactions across these parties. Operational execution is expected to occur primarily in the field and on the shop floor, making mobile-first workflows critical, while configuration, analytics, and governance remain centralized within the core platform.

#### PROPOSED SOLUTION

#### Functional Requirements

The mobile apps shall provide secure authentication, role-based access control, and real-time notifications to support time-critical decision-making. Functional capabilities will include claim monitoring and approvals for insurance users; damage capture, quotation submission, and job execution for garages; and RFQ response, pricing, fulfilment, and payment tracking for suppliers. The solution shall integrate seamlessly with the core PartsHub platform, ensuring data consistency, auditability across all mobile workflows.

The PartsHub mobile solution is designed to support multiple user groups, each with clearly defined roles, responsibilities, and access privileges. User access is strictly role-based to ensure operational efficiency, data security, and auditability across the platform.

#### Insurance Company Users

These users are responsible for overseeing and governing claim workflows. Their interaction with the mobile app is decision-oriented and focused on monitoring, approvals, and exception handling.

#### Garage Users

Garage users execute inspection, quotation, and repair activities. Their workflows are operational, time-sensitive, and mobile-first, often performed on the shop floor or at inspection sites.

#### Supplier Users

Supplier users manage parts pricing, availability, fulfilment, and invoicing. Their interaction is highly SLA-driven and commercially focused.

#### Platform Administrators (Web-Only)

Administrative users manage system configuration, user onboarding, permissions, and governance. These users access the platform exclusively through the web application.

#### Insurance Mobile App Features

The Insurance Mobile App will be designed as a decision-oriented, approval-focused application that enables insurance users to monitor, review, and act on claims in real time. The app will deliberately avoid configuration-heavy or analytical workflows, ensuring governance, audit control, and system integrity remain within the web platform.

#### Authentication & Access Management

The application will leverage the same authentication and authorization framework as the PartsHub web platform to ensure consistency, security, and centralized governance.

#### User Login

#### Secure login using registered email ID and password

#### Uses the same credentials as the web application

Adheres to the existing role-based access and permission model defined on the web platform

#### Password Recovery

#### OTP or email-based password reset mechanism

Follows the same recovery flow as the web platform to maintain uniform security policies

#### Biometric Authentication

#### Support for device-native biometric authentication (Fingerprint / Face ID)

Biometric login can be enabled only after a successful first login using credentials

#### Session & Security Controls

#### Automatic logout after defined inactivity period

#### Device-level session tracking for enhanced security

Ability for administrators to remotely log users out from all devices via the web platform

#### Supplier Mobile App Features

The Supplier Mobile App will be designed as the primary commercial execution tool for suppliers within the PartsHub ecosystem. It will enable suppliers to respond to RFQs, price parts competitively, manage fulfilment, submit invoices, and track payments entirely through mobile.

#### Product Intent & Design Principles

#### The Supplier Mobile App enables suppliers to:

#### Receive RFQs in real time

#### Price parts competitively using supplier-defined pricing and RFQ information

#### Confirm availability and delivery commitments

#### Fulfil orders and submit invoices

#### Track payment status

Note: All workflows are designed to be executed entirely from mobile. Any delay in supplier response directly impacts insurance timelines, making mobile-first execution critical.

#### Authentication & Access Control

#### User Login

#### Login using email ID or mobile number with password

#### Uses the same credentials as the web platform

#### Password Recovery

#### OTP or email-based password reset

#### Biometric Login

#### Biometric authentication (Fingerprint / Face ID) is strongly recommended

#### Designed to save time during high-volume RFQ periods

#### Role-Based Access

#### Owner / Manager: Full access to all mobile features

#### Staff: Limited access to RFQs and order updates only (optional sub-scope)

#### Scope of Work / Services

The scope of work outlined below may vary based on the nature, scale, and complexity of the engagement. Navsoft’s services for the PartsHub Mobile Applications initiative shall include, but not be limited to, the following activities:

#### Discovery & Solution Design

Review and validation of client-provided product vision, business objectives, and mobile feature strategy documents

#### Functional requirement elaboration and validation for:

#### Insurance Mobile Application

#### Garage Mobile Application

#### Supplier Mobile Application

Incremental Platform Administrator (Web-Based) features required for mobile enablement

#### Identification of mobile-specific workflows, dependencies, and constraints

#### Preparation of key design and scope artefacts, including:

#### Software Requirements Specification (SRS)

User journey mapping and workflow definition across Insurance, Garage, and Supplier personas

#### Platform UX/UI Design

#### User experience (UX) design for:

#### Insurance decision-oriented mobile workflows

#### Garage operational and camera-first mobile workflows

#### Supplier RFQ, pricing, and fulfilment mobile workflows

#### Web-based administrator screens required for mobile governance

#### Creation of UI wireframes, interaction flows, and visual designs

#### Responsive design principles applied where applicable

#### Design walkthroughs and formal design sign-off prior to development

#### Mobile Application Development

#### Development of native mobile applications for:

#### Insurance users

#### Garages

#### Suppliers

Implementation of role-based authentication, authorization, and session management

Development of mobile dashboards, operational workflows, and execution features as defined in the approved functional scope

#### Implementation of push notifications

#### Integrations & Backend Services

#### Integration of mobile applications with the existing PartsHub backend and APIs

Integration with notification services and other third-party services as defined in the approved scope

#### Testing, Deployment & Support

#### Functional testing across Insurance, Garage, and Supplier mobile applications

#### Role-based access, workflow, and SLA scenario testing

#### Basic security and integration testing

Deployment support for mobile applications on client-owned cloud infrastructure (AWS or client-preferred).

#### Exclusions

The following is a list of activities that are clearly excluded the scope of this proposal.

Enhancements, modifications, or redevelopment of the existing PartsHub web platform that are not explicitly required for mobile enablement are excluded from the scope of this project.

Development of any additional mobile applications beyond the Insurance, Garage, and Supplier mobile apps defined in this proposal is excluded.

Provisioning, setup, management, or maintenance of server infrastructure, cloud environments, networking components, firewalls, or related infrastructure is excluded and shall remain the client’s responsibility.

Procurement, licensing, subscription, or renewal of any software, hardware, mobile devices, cloud services, App Store or Play Store developer accounts, or third-party tools is excluded from this engagement.

The mobile applications proposed under this engagement are workflow-driven operational applications and do not include intelligent automation, AI-assisted decision-making, or machine learning-based processing capabilities.

Any Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), computer vision, image recognition, predictive analytics, recommendation engines, automated decision-making, smart estimation, or AI-assisted processing capabilities are explicitly excluded from this project scope.

Any future requirement related to AI enablement, AI integrations, intelligent automation, or AI-driven workflows shall be treated as a separate phase and commercial engagement under a formal Change Request process.

Advanced analytics, dashboards, business intelligence reports, and historical trend analysis are excluded from the mobile applications and remain web-only where applicable.

Integration with third-party systems, including payment gateways, ERP, CRM, accounting, logistics, or external data providers, is excluded unless explicitly defined in the approved integration scope.

Data migration activities, including extraction, cleansing, validation, transformation, or bulk upload of historical or legacy data from existing systems, spreadsheets, or external platforms, are excluded unless separately agreed in writing.

Configuration or redesign of auctions, pricing rules, hot deals, inventory catalogs, or commercial policies beyond mobile participation and execution is excluded.

Changes to existing backend services, APIs, or database schemas that are not directly required to support the defined mobile functionality are excluded.

Support for additional languages, localization requirements, or country-specific regulatory compliance beyond what is explicitly stated in this proposal is excluded.

Ongoing platform monitoring, managed services, 24×7 support, or long-term application maintenance beyond the agreed post go-live support period are excluded.

Performance guarantees related to internet connectivity, device hardware limitations, operating system upgrades, or third-party service availability are excluded from Navsoft’s responsibility.

Any functionality, enhancement, infrastructure component, or service not explicitly documented in the approved Scope of Work or Functional Scope Definition shall be treated as out of scope and addressed through a formal change management process.

#### Project Deliverables

For a fixed cost project, our deliverables would be limited to the Development only, where in, we will develop the proposed system in the Navsoft’s designated development server. To once again clarify, Development does not include UAT, Data Migration, and Go-Live.

We would also be conducting the basic testing and the client needs to perform UAT within the stipulated time frame as per the project plan. However, as the time for UAT acceptance is mostly client dependant and difficult to predict at this stage, the same is not included in our Time Estimation.

During the course of the project, Navsoft will make relevant deliverables available to the client. Navsoft expects these deliverables to be reviewed, accepted and signed-off by the client within the timeframe as per the project plan. If the client finds any defects in the deliverable, he needs to inform within five days from when the deliverable has been submitted. Below are some reference deliverables depending on your project type and contract:

Project plan - This document is a structured approach to project execution, ensuring alignment with business goals, and facilitating effective project management and communication.

Requirement Specification - Detailed documentation of the project's functional and non-functional requirements, often in the form of user stories, use cases, or a requirements document.

Codes / Builds – All program files, database and APIs will be transferred to the client’s designated hosting server,

Use Cases and Test Cases – all use cases and test cases will be transferred to the client’s server,

Meeting Recordings - Video recordings or documentation capturing meeting sessions conducted for end-users and stakeholders.

#### Acceptance Criteria

#### Acceptance Criteria shall be as follows:

For the Development, Changes, and Enhancements set of scope of work, the Navsoft resource(s) shall submit the deliverables. The progress of the tasks shall be maintained using the project tracking tool JIRA. The client shall approve the deliverables and verify the progress of tasks on JIRA.

As per standard practices, the client-side User Acceptance Testing (UAT) should be completed within five working days of Navsoft's submission of the deliverable. A project deliverable will be considered fully delivered upon the occurrence of any one of the following:

Completion of the billing cycle.

Receipt of acceptance or sign-off from the client.

Expiry of a calendar month from delivery without further communication from the client regarding acceptance.

Transfer or upload of the project deliverables to the client’s designated server.

Please note that the full contract value/payment must be cleared before the upload or transfer of files/code to the client’s production server.

For Support Services, Navsoft resource(s) shall resolve the service issues and submit the service tickets. The client shall verify the submitted service tickets and approve the ticket within two working days of submission.

For Change requests and business consultant services, the Navsoft resource(s) shall submit the deliverables along with details of the work performed. The client shall review and approve the deliverables within two working days of submission.

In case of delays, deemed acceptance shall be considered after five working days of submission of deliverables.

All the submissions shall be soft copies in English.

Approval shall be in the form of Email.

#### Assumptions and Dependencies

#### General Assumptions

The client shall be responsible for procuring, provisioning, and maintaining all required server infrastructure, cloud environments, network components, and software or hardware licenses necessary for development, testing, and production environments.

All project deliverables shall be reviewed by the client and consolidated feedback shall be provided within five (5) working days from the date of submission. If no feedback is received within this period, the deliverable shall be deemed accepted for the purpose of project continuity.

Navsoft may provide technical guidance on third-party tools or services where relevant; however, the final selection, licensing, commercial arrangements, and vendor engagement decisions shall remain the client’s responsibility.

Navsoft shall not be held responsible for delays or schedule slippage arising from non-availability of client personnel, delays in providing inputs, approvals, or feedback, or dependencies on third-party vendors or systems.

Any delays caused by third-party applications, services, cloud providers, app stores, or external vendors shall not be attributable to Navsoft.

Once requirements and scope are formally signed off, any new or modified requirements shall be addressed only through the agreed Change Management process.

All project communication, documentation, and deliverables shall be prepared and maintained in the English language.

#### Team-Related Assumptions

It is assumed that the client will appoint a dedicated Project Manager or Project Coordinator with sufficient authority to make timely decisions and approvals required to keep the project on schedule.

The client’s designated stakeholders shall be responsible for reviewing deliverables, providing timely clarifications, and making decisions necessary for successful execution of the engagement.

#### Scope Related Assumptions

The functional scope of the mobile applications and incremental web administrator features shall be limited to what is explicitly documented in the approved Scope of Work and Functional Scope Definition.

A maximum of three (3) UI/UX design iterations per mobile application shall be included within the scope. Any additional iterations shall be treated as additional effort and may require a Change Request.

The mobile applications are intended for execution, approvals, and real-time actions only; all configuration, analytics, and administrative controls shall remain web-based as defined in the proposal.

#### Mobile App–Specific Assumptions & Dependencies

It is assumed that the mobile applications will be developed and supported for the agreed versions of Android and iOS only. Support for additional operating system versions, device types, or form factors shall require separate evaluation and approval.

The client shall provide or procure valid Apple App Store and Google Play Store developer accounts and shall be responsible for compliance with all store policies, guidelines, and review requirements.

Mobile application release timelines are dependent on third-party app store review and approval processes, which are outside Navsoft’s control.

It is assumed that end users will have access to supported mobile devices with functional cameras, sufficient storage, and stable internet connectivity for normal application usage.

Push notifications, real-time alerts, and deep-linking features are dependent on third-party notification services and device-level permissions granted by end users.

Mobile application performance, responsiveness, and user experience may vary based on device hardware capabilities, operating system limitations, and network conditions.

The client shall be responsible for providing timely branding assets, app naming, store descriptions, screenshots, and policy documents required for app store submissions.

The current project scope is based entirely on standard rule-based mobile workflows and operational process execution. No AI-based processing, AI-generated outputs, automated recommendations, computer vision, or machine learning services are assumed to exist within the platform scope under this engagement.

Mobile-captured images, videos, and documents shall be subject to size, format, and quality constraints defined by the platform administrator to ensure performance and storage efficiency.

Any changes to mobile app scope, workflows, supported devices, or security requirements identified after design sign-off shall be handled through the formal Change Management process.

#### Testing, Training & Handholding Assumptions

Navsoft shall perform functional and integration testing of the solution in the development and test environments made available by the client.

The client shall be responsible for planning, executing, and signing off User Acceptance Testing (UAT) based on the approved functional scope.

UAT shall be limited to validating the delivered features against the agreed requirements. Any new requirements identified during UAT shall be managed through the Change Management process.

This engagement does not include infrastructure-level load testing, or performance benchmarking involving simulated concurrent user traffic unless explicitly agreed.

Navsoft shall provide remote training and knowledge transfer sessions through online meetings or webinars. On-site training is excluded unless separately agreed.

#### Third-Party Interface & Dependency Assumptions

Integration with third-party systems or services shall be limited strictly to those explicitly defined in the approved Scope of Work.

The client shall ensure availability of necessary APIs, credentials, documentation, and vendor-side support required for third-party integrations.

In the event of delays, incompatibilities, policy changes, version upgrades, or discontinuation of third-party systems, the recommended approach shall be to proceed with Go-Live without the impacted integration and address it later through Change Management, subject to mutual agreement.

#### Scope Change Management Assumptions

Any changes to scope, features, timelines, locations, or non-functional requirements shall be handled through a formal Scope Change Procedure.

Navsoft shall submit a Change Request detailing the impact on effort, timeline, and commercials, and shall commence additional work only after receiving formal written approval from the client.

#### Client Responsibilities & Dependencies

The client shall provide timely access to accurate, complete, and reliable information, documentation, and stakeholders necessary for Navsoft to perform its obligations.

The client shall designate appropriate personnel with relevant domain and operational knowledge to support requirement clarification, validation, and acceptance activities.

Navsoft shall rely on information, confirmations, and representations provided by the client while performing the work, and shall not be responsible for issues arising from inaccurate or incomplete inputs.

#### Project Cut-over and Go-live

#### If Go-Live is delayed due to:

#### Delays in UAT execution or user training

#### Non-availability of client resources

#### Client-initiated changes to Go-Live timelines

#### App Store or Play Store approval delays

#### Any reasons not attributable to Navsoft

Navsoft may, in the interest of project continuity, retain project resources for an additional period on a chargeable basis. Applicable rates shall be as per the agreed commercial terms.

| File storage for photos/videos: | AWS S3 (or Azure Blob) + CDN |
| Notifications & Messaging |  |
| Push notifications: | Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) + Apple Push Notification Service (APNs) |

#### Suggested Tools

The Navsoft team shall use the tools that are being used by the client. If the tool is very specific to the client's environment, then the client shall conduct training for the Navsoft team on it. Following are standard set of tools that are suggested for use.

Project Planning – We are using the following tools for creating an integrated project plan, project schedule, and work break-down structure:

#### Microsoft Project Plan

#### MS Office Suite

Project-Level Communication – For project-level daily communication, we are use the mix of following tools/software depending on the client’s preference and nature of the project:

#### Slack

#### Zoom

#### Phone

#### WhatsApp

Project Tracking – For project-level task management, we are using the following tools/software depending on the client’s preference and the nature of the project.

#### Atlassian Jira

#### Code Repository

#### GitHub

#### CI/CD Tools

#### Jenkins

#### Docker Containers

#### PROPOSED MODEL

#### Fixed Price Model

In a Fixed Price Model; a fixed price is charged based on the mutually finalised and defined scope.

Payments in a Fixed Price Model are made on completion of the clearly defined milestones/phases or specified payment schedule as per the executed contract.

In this model the company internally decides on the no. of resources it has to allocate to enable try and complete the project as per the defined time and schedule.

Generally, and depending on the size of the project milestone based / phase wise delivery is provided.

Even if the time frame exceeds what has been specified in the proposal; development will still continue till the agreed scope is completed without any extra charge.

Weekly status reports and weekly demos (as and when required) is provided as part of the updates and project monitoring system. Various 3rd party tools are also used to monitor progress and track the project.

However, any new change or addition outside the finalised scope is chargeable extra as per the ongoing rates and ideally a Change Request Form will be raised for pre-approval of the same.

This model is ideal when the scope is clearly defined / known and there is very limited probability of any scope creep / changes.

Its most significant advantage is its predictability of cost as it rarely exceeds the budget while its biggest downside is that there is very limited scope for change. Thus if initial requirements are not clearly spelled out the deliverables will not be as expected.

The contract ends once a project is delivered. However, a client based on their specific needs has the option of subsequently going for the various maintenance packages.

#### This Model Is Mostly Suitable For:

Short term projects with limited project scope.

Projects that have really well defined goals, requirements, workflow & scope but with limited chances of changes.

Clients who have fixed budgets which can’t be exceeded.

#### ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

#### Project Team Structure

To ensure the project's success, it requires specific roles and skill sets from both client team and Navsoft.

| NAME | ROLE | RESPONSIBILITIES |
|---|---|---|
| Project Manager (PM) | Owns overall project planning, execution, and delivery governance. | Acts as the single point of contact for the engagement.Manages timelines, milestones, risks, and dependencies.Coordinates between mobile, backend, integration, and admin workstreams. |
| Business Analyst | Leads requirement elaboration for Insurance, Garage, and Supplier mobile applications. | Translates business requirements into functional specifications and user stories.Validates scope alignment and prevents scope creep.Supports UAT planning, defect triage, and requirement clarifications.. |
| UX/UI Designer | Designs mobile-first user experiences for Insurance, Garage, and Supplier apps. | Creates user journeys, wireframes, and visual designs optimized for real-world usage.Ensures consistency across apps while respecting role-specific needs. |
| Mobile App Developer | Develop mobile applications based on approved designs and specifications. | Implement role-based workflows, camera integration, and notifications.Integrate mobile apps with backend APIs and operational services.Address defects and performance issues during testing cycles. |
| Backend / API Developer | Develop and enhance backend APIs required for mobile enablement. | Implement mobile-specific security, session management, and audit logging.Integrate notification services and third-party components as defined in scope. |
| Testing & QA | Plans and executes functional, integration, and role-based testing. | Validates mobile workflows, permissions, and SLA scenarios.Supports UAT defect management and regression testing. |
| Client/Stakeholder Representatives | Appoint a Project Sponsor or Steering Committee to provide strategic direction, governance, and timely decision-making. | Own overall business vision and success criteriaApprove scope, budget, and timelinesProvide strategic direction and priority decisionsSign off on major milestones and go-live readinessResolve escalations beyond project team level |

#### Project Governance Structure

|  | NAVSOFT TEAM | FREQUENCY | CLIENT TEAM | RESPONSIBILITY |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE | Top Management | Monthly Connect | Project Owner | Review high-level project status and strategic alignment.Resolve escalated issues and conflicts. Approve major project decisions and funding. |
| PROJECT MANAGEMENT | Project Manager | Weekly Connect | Client Stakeholders | Ensure delivery timeline and project progressDevelop & maintain project plans & schedulesCommunicate project status to stakeholdersManage risks, issues and changes. |
| PROJECT TEAM | Project Team Members | Daily Connect | Client Stakeholders | Clarify doubts, business processes, flows Resolve queries by client stakeholders Share updates of daily work progressEnsure the project meets quality standards |

#### Scope Change Management

Change requests if and when generated will be discussed and managed by the Project Manager.

Proper analysis of the change will be carried out and a time, cost and impact analysis will be prepared and forwarded to the client by the Project Head /  Lead Developer.

Project Manager will get the approval of the additional efforts and cost from the client.

#### On the client’s approval of the Change Request will be:

#### Invoiced as per the rates finalized under the commercial contract

The requested changes will be incorporated into the project schedule and subsequently executed and implemented.

#### The Change Request could include but would not be limited to:

Additional functionalities to be developed like reports, functional modules etc.

Deviation in functional flow of the application or a particular module.

Changes in reporting, screens, design, number of pages etc.

The Change Request will affect the delivery date and increase the project costs.

There may be other Change Request processes introduced during the planning and design phase.

#### The Change Request could include but would not be limited to:

Additional functionalities to be developed like reports, functional modules etc.

Deviation in the functional flow of the application or a particular module.

Changes in reporting, screens, design, number of pages, etc.

The Navsoft project manager shall record the change requests and present them to the client. On approval, the Navsoft project manager shall perform an analysis and present the options including the costing and timelines to the client.

Navsoft team shall perform the required changes on the approval by the Change management team consisting of Navsoft project manager and the client.

#### Project Communication Plan

Project Manager will provide a communication plan to the client as soon as the project is started. Depending on the size, nature and complexity of the project, the project communication plan will include some/most of the meeting activities as deemed necessary and detailed below:

| TYPE | OWNER | ATTENDEES | FREQUENCY | PROPOSED AGENDA |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Scrum Meeting | PM | PM & Project Team (Navsoft) | Daily | Update from everyone on – What was done yesterday?What is the plan for today?What are the obstracles? |
| Project Team Meeting | PM | Navsoft Project Team and Client | Weekly Twice | Discuss the project progressDiscuss upcoming milestonesReview the risk and issues log.Discuss upcoming leave plans. |
| Project Status Review Meeting | PM | Navsoft Project PM & Client | Monthly | Status update on the key project parameters, Schedules, Budget, Risks, Issues and Benefits. |
| Project Strategic Plan Meeting | Navsoft | Navsoft Top Management, PM & Project Owner | Monthly | Review high-level project status and decide the strategic direction of the project. |

#### Escalation Plan

Navsoft generally uses the following defined process for addressing and resolving the issues that cannot be managed at the current level. We ensure timely resolution of problems, minimize project disruption, and maintain alignment with project goals.

| EVENT | REPORTED BY | REPORTED TO | ACTION | CRITERIA FOR ESCALATION |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Issue Identification | Client’s Project Stakeholders | Navsoft Development Team | Identify and report issues as they arise.Use issue tracking tools (e.g., JIRA) to report and monitor issues. | Issues that need attention by development team or that affect project deliverables, timelines, or quality. |
| Initial Issue Handling | Client’s Project Stakeholders | Assigned team member (e.g., developer, designer) | Attempt to resolve the issue based on standard procedures and best practices.Document the resolution attempts and actions taken. | If the issue cannot be resolved within 24-48 hours or if it significantly impacts the project. |
| Escalation to PM | Client’s Project Stakeholders | Navsoft Project Manager | Report to PM if the issue cannot be resolved by development team.Address the issue and explore potential solutions.Perform root-cause analysis of the reported issues | Issues that impact project milestones, require additional resources, or have significant implications for the project’s timeline or deliverables. |
| Escalation to Navsoft Top Management | Project Owner | Navsoft Top Management | Report to top mgt. if the issue is still unresolved after PM intervention. Request high-level guidance or decisions from Navsoft top management. | Issues that threaten the overall success of the project or require strategic intervention. |
| Resolution & Communication | Navsoft Project Team | Project Owner &Stakeholders | Implement the resolution as agreed upon by the relevant governance level.Communicate the resolution, any changes, and their implications to all stakeholders. | The issue is fully resolved, and all stakeholders are informed of the outcome. |

#### Project Commencement Plan

We shall commence work on the project within 7 working days from the date of receipt of the following as minimal turnaround time is needed for project initiation, project planning and other internal formalities.

#### Formal Work / Work Order

#### Advance Payment

All reference materials, photographs etc. required to develop the system.
