(Navsoft) will not be responsible for any delay in the commencement of the project on account of non-receipt of the above-mentioned documents and/or advance payment.

#### TIME ESTIMATION

#### Time Estimation Criteria

We are detailing here under an indicative and tentative timeline based on the high level project scope outlined by the client as well as after briefly analysing the client’s business requirements. This is subject to change and variance.

Further the estimation of the timeline has been done module-wise to enable break it into the smaller milestones.

Also in addition, it is challenging to provide an appropriate timeframe as unforeseen challenges such as scope changes, third-party integration difficulties may arise during the development of a large and complex application. While we will strive to adhere to the timeframe, it is also subject to change due to these potential challenges.

Please note that our below estimated time doesn’t include UAT, Data Migration, DevOps and Go-Live stages. It is difficult to estimate their scope/time-frame at this stage or till the development phase is nearly completed.

The successful and timely completion of the development of your project within the given timeframe depends on the following matters:

Any change request or enhancement of the proposal scope will require additional timeline.

The below is an indicative timeline based on the workflow and basic scope of work that has been discussed between you and our team. Once we discuss the scope in a more granular level and accordingly prepare the SRS document, a more accurate timeline will emerge.

Time-bound submission of deliverables from the client’s end.

Presence in Joint Meetings as and when required.

Timely Approvals of the work in progress / deliverables.

Any delay in approvals, artifacts and project requirements etc. would affect the project execution and result in putting the project on hold after three reminders. Once a project goes on hold the production team will re-schedule and re-estimate the project plan

|---|---|

| UX/UI Design | 3 weeks |
| Development of Insurance Mobile App | 4 weeks |
| Development of Garage Mobile App | 4 weeks |

| Testing & Stabilization | 2 weeks |
| Deployment & Go-Live | 1 week |

Established in 1999 Navigators Software (Navsoft) is an award winning software company based in India, that has provided solutions to over  800 clients in 33 countries across diverse verticals. It is trusted by clients that range from small and medium businesses to Fortune 500 clients like Citibank, BPCL, Drake & Scull, Ametek, McAfee, Cadburys, Dabur, Signature Systems, etc.

Navsoft collaborates with the world’s leading technology platforms to address tomorrow’s challenges, today. We enable our clients to reach their full potential by creating meaningful products, digital assets and deeper customer relationships.

Having a large experienced team of over  450 geeks and offices in USA, India & UAE it deploys the world’s leading technology to address tomorrow’s challenges, today. It is primarily into  Application Development, EntNrise Solutions – Digital Transformation, Emerging Technologies and Outsourcing.

Besides providing services it also has an innovative Product Division that has successfully launched multiple enterprise products   - B2B & B2C Ecommerce ERP, Online Grocery Store & Last Mile Delivery App. and Healthcare & Clinic Management App.

It has in the recent past been selected by Nasscom as one of  the TOP 50 Emerging Companies  as well as the  Top 5 Cloud Companies  by one of the leading publishing houses.

#### Mobile App Feature Strategy (Along with Web App)

#### Core Principle (Very Important to State Clearly)

#### Web App = Full Feature Set (System of Record)

#### Mobile App = Operational Execution + Real-Time Actions

#### No feature duplication unless it is operationally required

#### Garages & Suppliers are mobile-first

#### Insurance is web-first, mobile is approval & monitoring-first

#### 2. Insurance Mobile App – “Monitor, Approve, Act”

#### Purpose

Insurance users will not manage complexity on mobile.They will monitor, approve, review, and intervene quickly.

.

#### 3. Authentication & Access Management

#### 3.1 Login

#### Email + password login

#### Same credentials as web app

#### Uses same role & permission model

#### 3.2 Forgot Password

#### OTP / email-based reset

#### Same flow as web

#### 3.3 Biometric Authentication (Phase-1)

#### Fingerprint / Face ID

#### Enabled after first successful login

#### 3.4 Session & Security

#### Auto logout on inactivity

#### Device-level session tracking

#### Logout from all devices (triggered via web admin)

#### 4. Mobile Dashboard (Decision-Oriented, Not Analytical)

#### 4.1 Dashboard Objective

#### Answer only 4 questions instantly:

#### What needs my attention?

#### What is stuck?

#### What is costing money?

#### What is delayed?

#### 4.2 Dashboard Widgets (Phase-1)

#### Claims awaiting approval

#### Quotes pending review

#### Orders awaiting confirmation

#### Payments pending release

#### SLA alerts (if any breach)

#### No charts, no trends, no analyticsThose remain in Web Advanced Analytics

#### 5. Claims Module (Read + Decide)

#### 5.1 Claim List View

#### Shows:

#### Claim ID

#### Customer name

#### Vehicle (Make / Model)

#### Claim status

#### Submitted date

#### Priority / SLA flag

#### Filters:

#### Pending approval

#### In progress

#### Completed

#### Rejected

#### Search:

#### Claim ID

#### Policy number

#### Policy number

#### Multiple angles

#### Zoom enabled

#### Damage severity

#### Damage categories

#### Confidence score

#### Flag if manual review needed

#### Parts recommended

#### OEM / aftermarket indicator

#### Labor cost summary

#### Total estimated cost

#### Garage name

#### Rating

#### Past performance snapshot

#### SLA adherence indicator

#### 5.3 Claim Actions (Controlled)

#### Available actions:

#### Approve claim

#### Reject claim

#### Request clarification

#### Add internal note

#### Restrictions:

#### Cannot edit claim data

#### Cannot change garage assignment

#### Cannot modify parts list

#### This avoids:

#### Accidental changes

#### Audit risks

#### Scope creep

#### 6. Quotes Review (Garage & Supplier)

#### 6.1 Quote List

#### Claim ID

#### Garage / Supplier name

#### Quoted amount

#### Status

#### Side-by-side total cost

#### Delivery timeline

#### Quality indicators

#### Actions:

#### Approve

#### Reject

#### Ask for revision – submit questions

Full quote breakdown & negotiation remains web-only.

#### 7. Orders & Fulfillment Tracking

#### 7.1 Order List

#### Order ID

#### Linked claim

#### Garage / Supplier

#### Status (Confirmed / Shipped / Delivered)

#### 7.2 Order Details

#### Part list

#### Delivery timeline

#### Shipment updates

#### Invoice summary

#### Actions:

#### Approve order

#### Acknowledge delivery

#### Flag issue

#### 8.1 Payment Overview

#### Payments pending approval

#### Released payments

#### Failed / disputed payments

#### 8.2 Payment Detail

#### Linked claim & order

#### Amount

#### Beneficiary (garage / supplier)

#### Invoice document

#### Actions:

#### Approve payment

#### Put on hold

#### Add internal remark

No payment setup or configuration on mobile.

#### 9. Auctions & Hot Deals (Monitoring Only)

#### View live auctions

#### View bids summary

#### View auction results

#### 9.2 What Insurance Cannot Do

#### Create auction

This avoids operational misuse.

#### Payment pending and all relevant notifications

#### Tap → opens relevant screen

#### Deep linking into claim / order

Notifications are critical for adoption.

#### 11. My Account (Minimal)

#### View profile

#### Change password

#### Enable / disable biometric login

#### Logout

#### No:

#### Plan changes

#### Garage Mobile App

#### Supplier Mobile App

#### RFQ, Pricing, Fulfilment & AI-Assisted Selling App

#### 1. Product Intent (Very Clear)

The Supplier Mobile App is the primary commercial execution tool for suppliers to:

#### Receive RFQs in real time

#### Price parts competitively using AI intelligence

#### Confirm availability and delivery

#### Fulfil orders and submit invoices

#### Track payments— entirely from mobile

If suppliers need the web to respond fast → insurance timelines break.

#### 2. Supplier User Personas

#### Supplier Owner

#### Sales Manager

#### Inventory Manager

#### Dispatch / Logistics Coordinator

#### Design assumptions:

#### High notification dependency

#### Fast response required

#### Competitive pricing pressure

#### Mobile used throughout the day

#### 3. Authentication & Access Control

#### 3.1 Login

#### Email / mobile + password

#### Same credentials as web

#### 3.2 Forgot Password

#### OTP / email-based reset

#### 3.3 Biometric Login

#### Strongly recommended

#### Saves time during RFQ rush

#### 3.4 Role-Based Access (Phase-1)

#### Owner / Manager: Full access

#### Staff: RFQ + order updates only (optional sub-scope)

#### 4. Supplier Dashboard (Action-Driven)

#### 4.1 Dashboard Purpose

#### Immediately answer:

#### What RFQs are waiting?

#### Which quotes are expiring?

#### What orders must be shipped today?

#### What payments are pending?

#### 4.2 Dashboard Widgets

#### New RFQs

#### RFQs expiring soon

#### Confirmed orders

#### Orders pending dispatch

#### Pending payments

No analytics charts.Pure execution.

#### 5. RFQ (Request for Quote) – Core Module

This is the heart of the Supplier App.

#### 5.1 RFQ Notifications

#### Real-time push notifications

#### SLA timer visible

#### Priority indicator (urgent / normal)

#### 5.2 RFQ List

#### Each RFQ shows:

#### RFQ ID

#### Insurance / Garage name

#### Vehicle details

#### Number of parts requested

#### RFQ deadline

#### Status

#### Filters:

#### New

#### Submitted

#### Accepted

#### Rejected

#### Expired

#### 5.3 RFQ Detail Screen (Very Important)

#### A. RFQ Summary

#### Claim reference

#### Vehicle make / model / variant

#### Delivery location

#### Required delivery timeline

#### B. Parts Requested

#### For each part:

#### Part name

#### OEM number (if available)

#### Quantity

#### Condition type:

#### OEM

#### Aftermarket

#### Used

#### Repair vs replace indicator

#### 7. Quote Creation & Submission (Mobile First)

#### 7.1 Quote Builder

#### Supplier can:

#### Enter unit price per part

#### Enter available quantity

#### Enter delivery time

#### Add taxes / fees

#### Add remarks

#### 7.3 Quote Submission

#### Submit quote

#### Track status:

#### Submitted

#### Under review

#### Accepted

#### Rejected

#### Revision requested

#### 8. Inventory Management (Supplier – Mobile)

#### Supplier can:

#### Add new items

#### View item list

#### Update stock quantity

#### Enable / disable items

Bulk uploads and catalog management remain web-only.

#### 9. Order Management & Fulfilment

#### 9.1 Order List

#### Each order shows:

#### Order ID

#### Linked claim

#### Buyer (garage / insurance)

#### Order status

#### Dispatch deadline

#### 9.2 Order Detail

#### Parts list

#### Quantities

#### Delivery address

#### Agreed delivery timeline

#### 9.3 Fulfilment Actions

#### Supplier can:

#### Confirm order

#### Mark “Ready for dispatch”

#### Mark “Dispatched”

#### Mark “Delivered”

#### Optional:

#### Upload delivery proof

#### Add dispatch notes

#### 10. Invoice & Payments

#### 10.1 Invoice Submission

#### Supplier can:

#### Upload invoice

#### Link invoice to order

#### View invoice status

#### 10.2 Payment Tracking

#### Supplier sees:

#### Amount approved

#### Amount paid

#### Pending amount

#### Payment status

No bank detail editing on mobile (web only).

#### 11. Auctions & Hot Deals (Participate)

#### 11.1 Auctions

#### Supplier can:

#### View ongoing auctions

#### View item details

#### Place bids

#### Track bid ranking

#### View won auctions

#### 11.2 Hot Deals

#### Browse hot deals

#### Buy immediately

#### Track order & delivery

#### 11.3 Supplier must be able to participate from mobile:

#### View auctions

#### Place bids

#### Track bid ranking

#### View won auctions

#### Buy and manage hot deals

#### 12. Notifications (High Dependency)

#### Supplier receives:

#### New RFQ

#### RFQ about to expire

#### Quote accepted / rejected

#### Order confirmed

#### Dispatch reminder

#### Payment released

Push notifications are critical for adoption.

#### 13. My Account (Minimal)

#### View profile

#### Change password

#### Enable biometric login

#### Logout

#### No:

#### Subscription changes

#### Plan upgrades

#### Company profile edits

#### 14. Explicit Out-of-Scope (Supplier Mobile – Phase 1)

#### To lock scope with Navsoft:

❌ Subscription & billing setup❌ Advanced pricing analytics❌ Supplier performance reports❌ Bulk inventory creation❌ AI configuration❌ Valuation logic configuration❌ Historical analytics & exports

All remain Web App only.

## 29. Transcript Decisions — Chronological

Source: 69 meeting transcripts (Group 0-3: 60 transcripts Jan-Apr 2026; Group 4: 9 transcripts late-Apr to mid-May 2026, with May 4/5/8-2/11-1/11-2 being the latest additions).

### November 2025 — Project Kickoff
- **10-Nov-2025**: Project kickoff
- BAs (Anubhab Ganguly) and designers (Manojit Das) start gathering requirements
- Initial SRS draft by Anubhab Ganguly, approved by Priya Singh

### January – February 2026
- **09-Feb-2026**: CR-004 created (Insurance multi-user model)
- Detailed BRD analysis begins
- Initial design iterations

### March 2026
- **18-Mar-2026**: Decision — reviews/ratings are platform-wide, not per-insurance
- **25-Mar-2026**: Auction reserve-not-met flow defined (B1 = sell anyway, B2 = cancel)

### April 2026 — Detailed Design
- **06-Apr-2026**: Notifications framework (multi-channel: web/email/Pusher/Firebase) confirmed
- **08-Apr-2026**: Onboarding decisions:
  - Trade license stable across renewals
  - 30-day grace period
  - OCR verification ~80% threshold
  - File upload 10 MB (raised from 3 MB for govt PDFs)
  - Mobile OTP only
- **09-Apr-2026**:
  - Supplier identity hidden from Insurance/Garages until order creation
  - Garage info visible to Insurance from start
- **10-Apr-2026**:
  - ID conventions: CLM prefix (8 digits, expandable), CJ prefix for Quotation
  - Launch markets: UAE first, Middle East simultaneously
  - Pricing AED
  - Subscription: 30,000 RFQs/month per Insurance company subscription
  - Garages and Suppliers onboarded globally for specialty parts
- **13-Apr-2026**:
  - Common filter framework across all listings
  - Address format: Country + City + Address Line 1 + Line 2 + Area
  - Inventory auto-populate from closed claims
  - Claim status flow defined: Draft → Submitted → Awaiting Garage Quote → Quotes Received → Garage Quote Approval Pending → Order Created → Parts Shipping → Parts Delivered → Repair Progress → Completed → Invoiced
  - Sub-claims design (parent_claim_id, photos+reason only required)
  - Pagination 50/100/200/500/1000
  - Default date range: last 30/60 days on big lists
- **14-Apr-2026**: Subscription model (3rd-party SaaS with Navsoft-branded UI)
- **17-Apr-2026**:
  - UAE plate-number aggregator API exploration
  - Damage assessment design (Garage uploads photos/videos, can ADD findings not edit insurance's)
  - File upload 10 MB confirmed
- **24-Apr-2026**:
  - Phase-1 automation philosophy locked
  - Garage participation in auctions confirmed
  - Insurance has multi-user (CR-004); Garage/Supplier still being decided
- **28-Apr-2026**:
  - Vehicle hierarchy structure: Vehicle Type → Origin (filtered by Type) → Brand Segment → Brand
  - Insurance can support certain origins for some types but not others
  - Brand selection grouped, left side menu fixed/floating
  - Section refresh only the changed section (no full page reload)
  - Loaders specific to sections
- **30-Apr-2026**:
  - Branch self-onboarding (BR-004)
  - Branch trade license shows company name with parent in authority
  - Claim form button rules: Cancel = no save + exit; Save & Exit = save draft + exit; Next = save + navigate
  - Confirmation modals REMOVED from claim creation per Vinoth's directive

### May 2026 — Current Build Phase

#### 04-May-2026 (Weekly meeting, 1h 32m)
- **Maps & Address Editability**:
  - Address always from Google Map
  - Address NOT editable when marker placed at a specific known location (existing building/office)
  - Address Lines 1 & 2 EDITABLE only when marker placed at random/non-specific location (no house number)
  - Area field always editable
- **Vehicle Hierarchy Redesign Demanded**:
  - Vinoth wants nested-checkbox UI grouping (not row-additive one-by-one entry)
  - Order: Type → grouped countries (per type) → year-range calendar picker → brand
  - "Select All" supported at each level
  - Grouping data driven from master fitment DB
- **AI Scope Concerns**:
  - Approx 220 working days originally allocated, much already lost
  - Sandeep doing approval-level review before resuming AI engagement
  - Navsoft must review BOTH Vinoth's PRD interpretation AND the proposal pricing/milestones structure
- **Mobile Parallel Dev**:
  - React Native team to start ASAP
  - Don't wait for web completion
- **Email/SMTP Issue**:
  - Zepto SMTP returning error 5:35 (auth failure / no credits / domain not verified)
  - Decision: Implement proper exception handling so frontend knows when email delivery fails
  - During development: support both real OTP and static OTP override for testing
- **My Account Approval Pending**:
  - When user has submitted profile change for approval, they should still be able to edit and update before admin processes
  - Need lock mechanism if both user and admin edit simultaneously
- **Inventory Discussion**:
  - Vinoth promised wireframes for Inventory by next day
  - Manojit had designed detail page from given links

#### 05-May-2026 (Design meeting, 1h 11m)
- **"Site Visit" → "Vehicle Inspection" rename platform-wide**:
  - Order of fields: Inspection Date → Inspection Time → Inspection Venue → Inspection Location
  - All labels/headings changed
- **Send RFQ to Garage from listing page**:
  - All fields empty initially (no claim selected yet)
  - Click "Claim ID" → opens ERP-style **popup modal**
  - Modal: text search box + search icon + filterable list (same filters as Claim listing)
  - Select claim → modal closes → claim ID populates form → rest of data fills from claim
  - Search icon becomes **X button** → clicking X resets entire form
- **Vehicle Inspection Required toggle (Yes/No)**:
  - Appears on BOTH Create Claim form AND Send-RFQ-Garage form
  - RFQ form pre-fills from claim
  - User can override toggle on RFQ (overrides for THIS RFQ only)
  - Override is **audited** in log activity
  - If toggle = "No" on RFQ → all inspection fields disappear, RFQ sends without inspection
- **Premium Garage Use Case** (no inspection):
  - High-end vehicle, customer requests specific garage
  - Insurance bypasses inspection
  - Garage inspects vehicle on arrival at their facility (not at insurance venue)
  - Quotation request sent directly to specific garage
- **Add More Garages Later**:
  - Garages already sent to are NOT disabled in list
  - But indicator shows "previously sent"
  - User can re-send to them (per insurance company's discretion)
- **Garage Capacity Field MISSING**:
  - Vinoth flagged garage onboarding form is missing capacity field
  - Current Workload % is meaningless without Capacity
  - Garage detail page has fields that onboarding form lacks — design inconsistency
- **Garage Diagnosis Field**:
  - Garage assessment must allow diagnosis input BEFORE parts entry
  - Multiple labor cost types supported (per-part labor)
  - Parts added one-by-one (not bulk) with photos/condition/action per part
- **Inventory Discussion**:
  - Vinoth promised import flow walkthrough
  - Garage uploads should support 10 MB
- **AI Approach**:
  - Vinoth's AI PRD already shared
  - Sandeep to review before Navsoft team engages with client AI team
  - No formal BRD for AI — only flow discussions
- **Mobile Parallel**:
  - Insurance/Garage/Supplier mobile designs to start simultaneously with web backend APIs
- **Manojit Allocation Issue**:
  - Soumya negotiating dedicated time with Apurba — Manojit is highly demanded across projects

#### 08-May-2026 Part 2 (Sprint check-in, 53m)
- **Venue Master Time Picker**:
  - 30-min intervals (calendar/Teams-style: 9:00, 9:30, 10:00, 10:30...)
  - NOT a dropdown listing
  - Slot intervals range: 5, 10, 15, 20, 30, 60 minutes
- **Day-of-week Configuration**:
  - Per-day open/close hours
  - Individual day disable (e.g., Saturday off)
- **Slot Blocking** (already decided):
  - Dynamic — only after user confirms (not on draft)
  - Edit affects only future bookings
- **JIRA Workflow CORRECTED**:
  - Internal Review (Chinmay tests) → Client Review (Vinoth tests) → Done (only Vinoth marks)
  - **Navsoft NEVER marks Done** — only Vinoth's team
  - Move to Client Review immediately on Chinmay pass (don't batch at sprint end)
  - If Chinmay finds issues → return to In Progress
- **Test Coverage Concerns**:
  - Vinoth wants tasks moved to Client Review continuously throughout sprint
  - Browser coverage to be confirmed: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (Opera Mini not priority)
  - Test cases must map to use cases in JIRA Xray
- **ARM Technologies Architecture**:
  - ARM Technologies is parent company
  - Parts Hub is one product within ARM portfolio
  - Phase-2+ will introduce API documentation layer for insurance ERPs to integrate
- **API Provider Status**:
  - 7+ scattered providers being engaged:
    - **SignZy** — trade license verification (current report = 30-line detailed; only validation needed)
    - **ScienceSoft** — accident reports by chassis number
    - **Auto Data** — vehicle details by plate number
    - **ShorePass**
    - **ArcBridge**
    - Plus 2+ more
  - Trade license API works with one provider account but fails with another (AWS issue per provider)
- **Payment Gateway Exploration**:
  - Vinoth in contact with ~12 providers over 2 weeks
  - Need single gateway supporting all use cases (subscription, auction deposits/refunds, hot deal purchases, monthly invoicing, garage/supplier payouts)

#### 11-May-2026 Part 1 (Weekly meeting, 1h 3m)
- **10-Level Vehicle Hierarchy APPROVED** for BOTH Garage AND Supplier
- **Suppliers extend hierarchy to PART LEVEL** (key differentiator from garages)
- **Inventory CSV Import + Export REQUIRED**
- **Hot Deals + Auction designs APPROVED** (email approval sent)
- **Testing Issues Found**:
  - iPad scrolling issue in onboarding form
  - Test cases not mapped to use cases in Xray
  - Test execution structure unclear to client
  - Camera upload test case missing
  - Browser coverage gaps
- **MAJOR SECURITY ISSUE flagged**: "Forms are open to accesses" (XSS)
- **CRITICAL DIRECTIVE**: **Navsoft must NOT fix anything until coordinated call with Vinoth + security report**
- Vinoth's team to send detailed security report 12-May

#### 11-May-2026 Part 2 (Security follow-up, 22m)
- **XSS Vulnerability Specifics**:
  - Angular `innerHTML` not sanitized in display
  - Current `<script>` injection won't fire (Angular default protection partial)
  - But `<img onerror=...>` would execute → security exposure
- **Decision**: Navsoft developers must add sanitization on every field value coming into DOM
- Need exception handling for every value going into variables → DOM display
- **iPad Upload Bug**:
  - Chinmay sent video from simulator (Vinoth wanted real device test)
  - Vinoth needs iOS version info — older iOS Safari has known file-picker quirks
  - Last 4-5 iOS versions should be supported (per requirements)

## 32. Glossary

| Term | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **BRD** | Business Requirements Document |
| **SRS** | System Requirements Specification (v1.3 is the master) |
| **PMP** | Project Management Plan |
| **RTM** | Requirements Traceability Matrix |
| **FSD** | Functional Specification Document |
| **SDD** | Software Design Document |
| **RFQ** | Request for Quotation |
| **HQ** | Headquarters (Insurance Company main entity, distinct from branches) |
| **OCR** | Optical Character Recognition (for trade license verification, ~80% threshold) |
| **CR** | Change Request (suffix on use case IDs for cross-cutting features) |
| **MSP** | Microsoft Project (file format for PMP) |
| **WBS** | Work Breakdown Structure |
| **PDPL** | UAE Personal Data Protection Law |
| **GDPR** | General Data Protection Regulation (EU) |
| **GCC** | Gulf Cooperation Council (used in vehicle spec context) |
| **CNN** | Convolutional Neural Network (damage detection model) |
| **VIN** | Vehicle Identification Number |
| **OEM** | Original Equipment Manufacturer |
| **OES** | Original Equipment Supplier |
| **TecDoc** | International parts catalogue standard |
| **RBAC** | Role-Based Access Control |
| **Site Visit** | OLD term — replaced by "Vehicle Inspection" platform-wide on 05-May-2026 |
| **Vehicle Inspection** | Garage's on-site inspection of damaged vehicle at insurance-designated venue |
| **Premium Garage** | Garage chosen by customer for high-end vehicle without inspection (garage inspects on arrival) |
| **B1/B2** | Auction reserve-not-met seller decisions (B1 = sell anyway; B2 = cancel) |
| **Fitment Map** | Database table mapping parts to compatible vehicles (many-to-many) |
| **Assessor** | Insurance-side role that reviews AI damage assessment outputs |
| **Eligibility** | Filter determining which garages/suppliers can be selected for a claim |
| **Favorite** | HQ-marked preferred entity (appears first in RFQ flows but does NOT bypass eligibility) |
| **PARTS HUB** | The platform's product name (capitalized in client legal name) |
| **ARM Technologies** | Parent company of Parts Hub product (per May 8) |
| **NAV/1318/23-24** | Master proposal document number |
| **NAV/1452** | Mobile app proposal document number |
| **NAV/PH/1318/25-26** | SRS document number |
| **CLM** | Claim ID prefix (8 digits, expandable) |
| **CJ** | Quotation ID prefix |
| **VT-** | Vehicle Type ID prefix (e.g., VT-01 Passenger Car) |
| **VO-** | Vehicle Origin ID prefix (e.g., VO-01 Japanese) |
| **PC-** | Part Category ID prefix (e.g., PC-01 Engine & Components) |
| **PAR-** | JIRA Epic/Story prefix |
| **L.L.C.** | Limited Liability Company (UAE legal form) |
| **S.P.C.** | Single Person Company (UAE legal form) |

---

## How to Use This Document with Claude

**At the start of a new conversation**, do one of:

1. **Upload this file** and say: *"This is the Parts Hub knowledge base. Read it before we start so you have full project context."*

2. **Paste relevant sections** if the conversation will focus on a specific area.

3. **Refer to specific sections** during the conversation: *"Check Section 16 of the Parts Hub knowledge base for the PH-UC-INS-003 use case detail"*

**Suggested update cadence**: Update this file at the end of significant working sessions when:
- New transcripts are reviewed → add to Section 29 timeline
- New deliverables are produced → add to Section 30
- Decisions change → update relevant section + add to Section 29 timeline
- Status changes → update Section 31 issues

Source: `__Pre-Project-Meetings/Parts Hub Requirements.txt`

### Verbatim Excerpts from Discovery Transcript


> We have an idea that we think that it's going to work here and UAE engineer will explain about this idea, will give you the whole, like the, the whole. The overview of the idea. Then after that, I will go through the technical requirements after what we did from the discovery phase. I will show you a small presentation and I will share the same document as well. What we want exactly from you, you evaluate the requirements and if there's any suggestions or there's any improvement you think it's gonna benefit us, you can suggest that. But the most important what we're looking for is the quotation of that building, of that platform, as well as the roadmap of the project and how long you need to finish it.

> we want to open or build a platform as a broker between the insurance company, the garage, and the supplier. Our idea is basic, that somebody will come to the insurance company, he will file a claim. Insurance company will call the garages to List all the parts that needed for the repair. After that, this list will go to the supplier. Specialist. Supplier. Supplier. Four wheel, classic, sport car, like this. All this should be powered with AI and machine learning. Every time I am entering more data, machine learning will understand that.

> This type of accident, like by the photo, I have like Lexus, and there is damage from front right. Every time when I am entering the data, which part I need, it will learn that this part is needed always to this damage. And it will be like a broker or platform. No need, the situation now, when there is an accident, the customer will go to insurance company, insurance company will ask him to come on certain day and time, and insurance company will ask all the garages to come to this place to evaluate the car. All these garages will give the insurance company quotation, but with spare part and labor. My main aim for the insurance company to reduce the cost of labor, to reduce the hidden cost, to reduce the cost of supplying the spare part. This is the main goal for this platform? Did you get the idea?

> If it is a small accident, they will tell him, bring your car to this location at this time. Or evaluating by all garages. **Subject**: Subscription Module Discussion & Clarifications on Previous BRD Points
**Date**: 01-12-2025
**Location**: Google Meet
**Time**: Scheduled Start Time: 6:00 PM IST
Scheduled End Time: 7:00 PM IST
**Prepared by**: Priya Singh

| # | Decision | Action | Responsibility | Target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Subscription Module Discussion & Clarifications on Previous BRD Points | In Progress |  | Vinoth |
| 2 | Wireframes development | In Progress |  | Manojit |
| 1 | Subscription model finalised with major structural rules | Three plans (Basic, Silver, Gold/Diamond) with flexible validity (90/180/360 days). No monthly-plan model. | Team | - |
| 2 | Each subscription includes features with counters & unlimited-use features | Admin must configure: available features, counters, reset type (monthly or term-wise). | Developer | During development |
| 3 | Vehicle Value Feature to run on a credit-based system | Subscription includes default credits; additional credits purchasable and never expire. | Dev + BA | - |
| 4 | Credit Reset Logic | Admin will set reset type—monthly OR at end of subscription term. | Developer | During subscription module dev |
| 5 | Add-on credits do not expire | Must persist across renewals and subscription changes. | Developer | - |
| 6 | Subscription Upgrade Flow Finalised | Pro-rata calculation required. Unused amount from old plan must be adjusted against new plan cost. | Developer | - |
| 7 | User Subscription Flow Finalised | On first login, users must select Free plan or a Paid plan. A subscription wall will be shown to clarify plan usage limits. | Developer | - |
| 8 | Usage Tracking & Notifications | Monthly counters, credits, and feature usage must be visible in user subscription settings. Notifications: 30d → 14d → 7d → daily till expiry. | Developer | - |
| 9 | Expiration & Grace Period | Admin can configure grace period days. If grace period = 0, user moves to restricted access immediately. | Developer | - |
| 10 | Refund Logic for Auction Deposit (GAP005) | Deposit fee discussion reopened — refund may be required as credits or fee may be removed entirely. Vinoth to confirm final logic after internal discussion. | Vinoth | 03-12-2025 |
| 11 | BRD Clarifications Required | Items AUC009 & HD001 contain mismatched references; corrections to be shared by Vinoth. | Vinoth | 03-12-2025 |
| 12 | Meeting Reschedule Request | Meeting on 04-Dec to be conducted at 4 PM IST due to half-day leave in team. | Priya | Completed |

## 35. Garage Onboarding — Locked Master Structure

Source: `__Masters/Garage Onboarding Master Structure (1).docx`

This is the canonical, locked structure for the cascading garage onboarding flow with all field definitions and database tables.


**Garage Onboarding — Locked Structure**

**STEP 1 — Personal Details**

| Field | Type | Required |
|---|---|---|
| First Name | Text | Required |
| Last Name | Text | Required |
| Email | Email | Required |
| Mobile Number | Number + Country Code | Required |
| Password | Text (masked) | Required |
| Confirm Password | Text (masked) | Required |

**STEP 2 — Company Details**

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Company / Garage Name | Text | Required | Legal registered name |
| Country | Dropdown | Required | UAE at launch |
| Emirate | Dropdown | Required | Driven by Country |
| City / Area | Dropdown | Required | Driven by Emirate |
| Address Line 1 | Text | Required |  |
| Address Line 2 | Text | Optional |  |
| VAT Number | Text | Required | UAE TRN |
| VAT Certificate | File Upload | Required |  |
| Trade License Number | Text | Required |  |
| Trade License Start Date | Date | Required |  |
| Trade License Expiry Date | Date | Required |  |
| Trade License Copy | File Upload | Required |  |
| Licensing Emirate | Dropdown | Required |  |
| Licensing Authority | Dropdown | Required | Driven by Licensing Emirate |

**STEP 3 — Garage Category (Vehicle Classification)**

This step is a cascading selection flow, not a flat form. Each selection drives the next.

**Stage 3.1 — Vehicle Type (Multi-Select)**

The garage selects all vehicle types they support.

| Vehicle Type |
|---|
| Passenger Car |
| SUV / Crossover |
| 4x4 / Off-Road |
| Pickup Truck |
| Van |
| Minibus |
| Bus |
| Light Commercial Vehicle (LCV) |
| Heavy Commercial Truck (HCV) |
| Motorcycle |
| Electric Vehicle (EV) |
| Special Purpose Vehicle |
| Construction Equipment |
| Agricultural Equipment |

**Stage 3.2 — Vehicle Origin Country (Multi-Select)**

Shown after Vehicle Type is selected.

| Origin |
|---|
| Japanese |
| German |
| Korean |
| American |
| British |
| French |
| Italian |
| Swedish |
| Chinese |
| Indian |
| All Origins |

**Stage 3.3 — Vehicle Group / Brand Segment (Multi-Select)**

Driven by the Vehicle Type + Origin combination selected above. Shows the segments relevant to what the garage picked.

**Example — Passenger Car + Japanese:**

| Brand Segment |
|---|
| Economy / Mass Market |
| Compact / Minicar |
| Luxury / Premium |
| Performance / Sports |

**Example — SUV + American:**

| Brand Segment |
|---|
| Mass Market SUV |
| Full-Size SUV |
| Luxury SUV |
| Performance / Off-Road |

This is the grouping level that makes brand selection manageable. Without it, a garage selecting "All Passenger Cars" would face a list of 100+ brands with no structure.

**Stage 3.4 — Brand Selection (Multi-Select)**

Filtered list of brands based on the segments selected above. Garage picks the exact brands they support.

**Example — Economy/Mass Market Japanese Passenger Car:**

| Brands Shown |
|---|
| Toyota |
| Honda |
| Nissan |
| Mazda |
| Mitsubishi |
| Suzuki |

Option at this stage: "All Brands in selected segments" checkbox — for garages that are fully general and don't want to pick individually.

**STEP 4 — Specialties (Multi-Select)**

All specialties displayed in grouped format so the garage can navigate and select quickly.

Group A — Mechanical General Mechanical Repair, Engine Repair & Overhaul, Gearbox / Transmission Repair, Suspension & Steering, Brake System Repair, Cooling System Repair, Exhaust System Repair, Fuel System Repair, Oil & General Service, 4WD / Off-Road Systems

Group B — Electrical & Diagnostics Electrical Repair, Diagnostics & Fault Scanning, ECU Programming & Coding, Wiring & Harness Repair, Battery & Charging Systems

Group C — Body & Paint Bodywork Repair, Painting (Full / Partial), Dent Removal (PDR), Windshield & Glass Repair, Interior Upholstery, Car Wrapping, Detailing & Polishing

**Group D — Tyres & Wheels Tyre Supply & Fitting, Wheel Alignment, Wheel Balancing, Rim Repair**

Group E — EV & Hybrid Hybrid Vehicle Service, EV Repair & Programming, EV Battery Diagnostics, Charging System Repair

Group F — Specialist Services AC Repair & Service, Performance Tuning, Classic Car Restoration, Off-Road Modifications, On-Road Modifications, On-Demand / Home Service

**STEP 5 — Review & Submit**

Garage sees a summary of everything entered across all steps and confirms submission.

| Field | Type |
|---|---|
| Summary View | Read-only display of all entered data |
| Terms & Conditions Acceptance | Checkbox — Required |
| Submit Application | Action button |
| Submission Confirmation | System — auto email sent to registered email |

**STEP 6 — Admin Review & Approval (Backend)**

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| Registration Status | Pending → Approved / Rejected |
| Reviewed By | Admin user |
| Review Date | System timestamp |
| Rejection Reason | Required if rejected — sent to garage |
| Resubmission | Garage can correct and resubmit if rejected |

**POST-APPROVAL**

Once approved, the garage receives login credentials confirmation and accesses the platform. Everything else — bank details, operating hours, capacity, insurance panel, certifications, labour rates, parts sourcing preferences — gets handled inside the Account / Profile section.

**How the Tables Actually Connect**

The garage onboarding creates one new table and two mapping tables. Nothing else is new.

**Table 1 — Garage Profile Table**

(The only truly new table for garages)

Stores everything unique to the garage itself.

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Garage ID | Primary Key |  |
| First Name | Text |  |
| Last Name | Text |  |
| Email | Text |  |
| Mobile | Text |  |
| Company Name | Text |  |
| Country ID | FK → Countries Table |  |
| Emirate ID | FK → Emirates Table |  |
| City ID | FK → Cities Table |  |
| Address Line 1 | Text |  |
| Address Line 2 | Text |  |
| VAT Number | Text |  |
| VAT Certificate | File |  |
| Trade License Number | Text |  |
| Trade License Start | Date |  |
| Trade License Expiry | Date |  |
| Trade License Copy | File |  |
| Licensing Authority ID | FK → Licensing Authorities Table |  |
| Status | Enum | Pending / Approved / Rejected |
| Created At | Timestamp |  |

**Mapping Table 1 — Garage ↔ Vehicle Master**

(Replaces the need for any vehicle data inside the garage table)

This is a simple many-to-many table. One garage supports many vehicle configurations. One vehicle configuration is supported by many garages.

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| ID | Primary Key |  |
| Garage ID | FK → Garage Profile |  |
| Vehicle Type ID | FK → Vehicle Types (L1) |  |
| Vehicle Origin ID | FK → Vehicle Origins (L2) |  |
| Brand Segment ID | FK → Brand Segments (L3) |  |
| Brand ID | FK → Brands (L4) |  |

**Example rows this table holds:**

| Garage ID | Vehicle Type | Origin | Segment | Brand |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| G-001 | Passenger Car | Japanese | Economy | Toyota |
| G-001 | Passenger Car | Japanese | Economy | Honda |
| G-001 | Passenger Car | German | Luxury | BMW |
| G-001 | SUV / Crossover | American | Full-Size | GMC |
| G-002 | HCV Truck | Swedish | Heavy Truck | Scania |
| G-002 | HCV Truck | German | Heavy Truck | Mercedes-Benz Trucks |

Key point: G-001 selecting "Toyota" here is not storing the word "Toyota". It is storing Brand ID = BRD-0045, which is the Toyota record in the master Brand table. If Toyota's details ever change in the master, every garage linked to it reflects that automatically.

**Mapping Table 2 — Garage ↔ Specialties**

(Specialties are the only truly garage-specific data)

Specialties don't exist in the vehicle master. This is its own reference table.

**Specialty Master Table (admin-controlled, like vehicle master)**

| Specialty ID | Group | Specialty Name |
|---|---|---|
| SP-001 | Mechanical | General Mechanical Repair |
| SP-002 | Mechanical | Engine Repair & Overhaul |
| SP-003 | Mechanical | Gearbox / Transmission Repair |
| SP-010 | Electrical | Diagnostics & Fault Scanning |
| SP-015 | Body & Paint | Bodywork Repair |
| SP-020 | EV & Hybrid | EV Repair & Programming |
| ... | ... | ... |

**Garage ↔ Specialty Mapping Table**

| ID | Garage ID | Specialty ID |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | G-001 | SP-001 |
| 2 | G-001 | SP-010 |
| 3 | G-001 | SP-015 |
| 4 | G-002 | SP-003 |

**The Full Picture — What Queries This Structure Enables**

This design directly powers the job-matching engine. Here is what a real query looks like when an insurance company sends an RFQ:

## 36. Supplier Onboarding — Locked Master Structure

Source: `__Masters/Supplier Onboarding Master Structure (1).docx`

Canonical structure. Same cascading hierarchy as Garage but with **Supplier Type** as first differentiator (controls allowed inventory conditions).


**Supplier Onboarding — Locked Structure**

**STEP 1 — Personal Details**

Identical structure to Garage onboarding.

| Field | Type | Required |
|---|---|---|
| First Name | Text | Required |
| Last Name | Text | Required |
| Email | Email | Required |
| Mobile Number | Number + Country Code | Required |
| Password | Text (masked) | Required |
| Confirm Password | Text (masked) | Required |

**STEP 2 — Company Details**

Same structure as Garage. Only difference is no "Garage Network Type" — replaced by Supplier Type in Step 3.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Company Name | Text | Required | Legal registered name |
| Country | Dropdown | Required | UAE at launch |
| Emirate | Dropdown | Required | Driven by Country |
| City / Area | Dropdown | Required | Driven by Emirate |
| Address Line 1 | Text | Required |  |
| Address Line 2 | Text | Optional |  |
| VAT Number | Text | Required | UAE TRN |
| VAT Certificate | File Upload | Required |  |
| Trade License Number | Text | Required |  |
| Trade License Start Date | Date | Required |  |
| Trade License Expiry Date | Date | Required |  |
| Trade License Copy | File Upload | Required |  |
| Licensing Emirate | Dropdown | Required |  |
| Licensing Authority | Dropdown | Required | Driven by Licensing Emirate |

**STEP 3 — Supplier Type (Multi-Select)**

This is the first differentiator from Garage onboarding. A supplier can be more than one type — for example, a company can be both an OEM Parts Supplier and a Distributor simultaneously.

| Supplier Type ID | Supplier Type |
|---|---|
| ST-01 | OEM Parts Supplier |
| ST-02 | Aftermarket Parts Supplier |
| ST-03 | Used Parts Supplier |
| ST-04 | Salvage / Scrap Yard |
| ST-05 | Parts Distributor / Wholesaler |
| ST-06 | Retail Parts Supplier |
| ST-07 | EV Parts Supplier |
| ST-08 | Accessories Supplier |
| ST-09 | Tyre & Tyre Accessories |

Why Supplier Type matters beyond just a label: It controls what the supplier can list in their inventory. A Salvage / Scrap Yard can list Salvage condition parts. An OEM Parts Supplier can list OEM condition parts. The Supplier Type gates the condition options available when they add inventory — preventing a scrap yard from listing parts as "OEM New."

**STEP 4 — Supplier Category (Vehicle Classification)**

Exact same cascading flow as Garage onboarding — same master tables, same logic.

**Stage 4.1 — Vehicle Type (Multi-Select)**

| Vehicle Type |
|---|
| Passenger Car |
| SUV / Crossover |
| 4x4 / Off-Road |
| Pickup Truck |
| Van |
| Minibus |
| Bus |
| Light Commercial Vehicle (LCV) |
| Heavy Commercial Truck (HCV) |
| Motorcycle |
| Electric Vehicle (EV) |
| Special Purpose Vehicle |
| Construction Equipment |
| Agricultural Equipment |

**Stage 4.2 — Vehicle Origin Country (Multi-Select)**

| Origin |
|---|
| Japanese |
| German |
| Korean |
| American |
| British |
| French |
| Italian |
| Swedish |
| Chinese |
| Indian |
| All Origins |

**Stage 4.3 — Brand Segment / Group (Multi-Select)**

Driven by Vehicle Type + Origin. Same segments from master — no new data.

**Stage 4.4 — Brand Selection (Multi-Select)**

Filtered by segments selected. Same Brand master table — FK references only.

Option: "All Brands in selected segments" checkbox — same as garage.

**STEP 5 — Supplier Specialty (Multi-Select)**

This is the second differentiator. Supplier specialties are about what parts they supply, not what repairs they do. Grouped for easy navigation.

**Group A — Engine & Powertrain**

| Specialty ID | Specialty |
|---|---|
| SS-001 | Engine Parts & Components |
| SS-002 | Transmission / Gearbox Parts |
| SS-003 | Clutch & Torque Converter |
| SS-004 | Transfer Case Components |
| SS-005 | Prop Shaft & CV Joints |
| SS-006 | Fuel System Parts |
| SS-007 | Exhaust System Parts |
| SS-008 | Cooling System Parts |
| SS-009 | Air Intake Components |
| SS-010 | Timing System Parts |

**Group B — Chassis & Suspension**

| Specialty ID | Specialty |
|---|---|
| SS-011 | Suspension Parts |
| SS-012 | Steering Components |
| SS-013 | Brake System Parts |
| SS-014 | Axle & Wheel End Parts |
| SS-015 | Chassis & Frame Components |

**Group C — Body & Exterior**

| Specialty ID | Specialty |
|---|---|
| SS-016 | Body Panels (Doors, Fenders, Bonnets) |
| SS-017 | Bumpers & Grilles |
| SS-018 | Mirrors & Handles |
| SS-019 | Windshield & Glass |
| SS-020 | Lighting Components |
| SS-021 | Seals & Weatherstripping |

**Group D — Electrical & Electronics**

| Specialty ID | Specialty |
|---|---|
| SS-022 | ECU / Control Modules |
| SS-023 | Sensors & Actuators |
| SS-024 | Wiring Harness & Cables |
| SS-025 | Alternators & Starter Motors |
| SS-026 | Batteries & Charging Components |
| SS-027 | Infotainment & Navigation |

**Group E — EV & Hybrid Parts**

| Specialty ID | Specialty |
|---|---|
| SS-028 | High Voltage Battery Packs |
| SS-029 | Battery Management Systems (BMS) |
| SS-030 | Electric Motors & Inverters |
| SS-031 | Onboard Chargers & DC-DC Converters |
| SS-032 | EV Charging Equipment |
| SS-033 | Hybrid System Components |

**Group F — Wheels, Tyres & Interior**

| Specialty ID | Specialty |
|---|---|
| SS-034 | Tyres |
| SS-035 | Alloy Wheels / Rims |
| SS-036 | Wheel Components & TPMS |
| SS-037 | Interior Trim & Upholstery |
| SS-038 | Seats & Seat Components |
| SS-039 | Dashboard & Cabin Parts |
| SS-040 | Airbags & Safety Components |

**Group G — Filters, Fluids & Consumables**

| Specialty ID | Specialty |
|---|---|
| SS-041 | Filters (Oil, Air, Fuel, Cabin) |
| SS-042 | Engine Oils & Lubricants |
| SS-043 | Coolants & Fluids |
| SS-044 | AdBlue / DEF |
| SS-045 | Brake Fluids |

**Group H — Heavy Equipment & Commercial**

| Specialty ID | Specialty |
|---|---|
| SS-046 | Hydraulic System Parts |
| SS-047 | Undercarriage Components |
| SS-048 | Attachments & Implements |
| SS-049 | Heavy Truck Parts |
| SS-050 | Bus & Coach Parts |

**Group I — Accessories**

| Specialty ID | Specialty |
|---|---|
| SS-051 | Exterior Accessories |
| SS-052 | Interior Accessories |
| SS-053 | Performance Parts |
| SS-054 | Off-Road Accessories |
| SS-055 | Protection & Styling |

**STEP 6 — Review & Submit**

| Field | Type |
|---|---|
| Summary View | Read-only display of all entered data |
| Terms & Conditions Acceptance | Checkbox — Required |
| Submit Application | Action button |
| Submission Confirmation | Auto email to registered address |

**STEP 7 — Admin Review & Approval**

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| Registration Status | Pending → Approved / Rejected |
| Reviewed By | Admin user |
| Review Date | System timestamp |
| Rejection Reason | Required if rejected |
| Resubmission | Allowed after rejection with corrections |

**How Supplier Type Controls Inventory Condition**

**This is the critical business rule that connects Supplier Type to the parts master:**

| Supplier Type | Allowed Part Conditions in Inventory |
|---|---|
| OEM Parts Supplier | New OEM only |
| Aftermarket Parts Supplier | New Aftermarket (Grade A, B) |
| Used Parts Supplier | Used (Grade A, B, C) |
| Salvage / Scrap Yard | Salvage / As-Is only |
| Parts Distributor / Wholesaler | New OEM + New Aftermarket |
| Retail Parts Supplier | New OEM + New Aftermarket + Used |
| EV Parts Supplier | New OEM + New Aftermarket (EV parts only) |
| Accessories Supplier | New Aftermarket (Accessories category only) |

This rule is enforced at the inventory listing stage — not just a label. When a supplier adds inventory, the condition dropdown is filtered based on their registered Supplier Type.

**Database Structure — Supplier Tables**

Applying the exact same principle as Garage — no duplication of master data, only FK references.

## 41. Vinoth's Design Questionnaire (Original Design Vision)

Source: `__Design/partshub_questionaire.docx` — Vinoth Martin's submitted questionnaire to Navsoft Design team. Captures design philosophy and brand voice in client's own words.


**Navsoft Design Questionnaire**

Please fill in as many sections as you can to help us understand your requirements.

The respondent's email (vinothmartin@gmail.com) was recorded on submission of this form.

**Project Name***

**Parts Hub**

**Client Name ***

**PARTS HUB E TRANSACTION COMPLEX - L.L.C**

**Contact Person ***

**Vinoth Martin**

**Title**

**Product Manager**

**Address**

**Abu Dhabi - UAE**

**Email ***

vinothmartin@gmail.com

--------------------

**Phone Number**

+971585488661

**Current Website URL (if applicable)**

**Project Overview**

**Please describe your organization in a few sentences ***

Parts Hub is an online platform enabling the sale and purchase of mechanical parts to B2B markets directly from parts suppliers. The core offering of the platform is to be a point of sale for car parts, however, the business will expand its catalogue of parts offered by also selling a variety of mechanical parts in the near future.

What differentiates the platform from other online retailers for industrial parts is that Parts Hub will utilise Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence to identify required parts and the suppliers who stock and sell them at a rapid rate, eliminating the need to search for and select a specific part manually.

**Do You Want to Build a New Website or Redesign an Existing One? ***

**New Website**

**O**

**Redesign Existing One**

**What problems do your clients have that your organization solves? What makes you different?**

Many companies, including insurance companies, overpay for their parts supplies each year due to a lack of visibility into the cheapest options available on the market. We estimate that our platform could help these companies save 10-30% annually by providing access to a range of options and transparent pricing. Insurance companies will be major clients in the beginning, but our platform is not limited to serving only this industry.

—

Our clients - insurance companies, garages, and auto parts suppliers - face several industry-wide challenges that result in inefficiency, cost overruns, and delays. Parts Hub addresses these core problems:

Lack of Transparency in Pricing - Parts Hub uses Al-driven real-time pricing intelligence to ensure fair and transparent quotes across all suppliers.

Manual and Time-Consuming Claim Evaluation -Parts Hub automates the entire process - from image-based damage assessment to automated quotation generation - reducing evaluation time from days to minutes. Fragmented Communication Between Stakeholders - Our platform unifies all three under one digital ecosystem, enabling seamless collaboration and real-time updates.

Inefficient Parts Sourcing and Availability - Our intelligent parts-matching engine and predictive demand analytics identify available OEM, aftermarket, used or salvaged parts instantly.

High Operational Costs and Human Error - Automation and data-driven decision-making significantly reduce administrative overheads and human error.

**What frustrates you about your current website?**

**Who are your direct competitors? ***

1. Audatex Middle East - https://www.audatex.ae/ 2. Addenda - https://www.addenda.ai/ 3. Camcom - https://camcom.ai/

**What Services Do You Offer? ***

Parts Hub offers a complete end to end Al driven ecosystem. We go beyond just estimating repairs we connect insurance companies, garages, and suppliers on one intelligent platform. Our system uses Al for photo based damage detection, automatic parts identification, and real time price comparison across multiple suppliers. We provide live quotation management, instant parts sourcing, and workflow automation from claim to delivery. Garages and suppliers get a simple mobile friendly interface to respond fast, while insurers gain full visibility, cost control, and data insights. With predictive analytics and continuous machine learning, Parts Hub keeps improving accuracy and efficiency - offering a single smart platform that replaces the disconnected tools used by competitors.

―

## 42. ALL 549 BRD Requirements (Verbatim from Cleaner Source)

This section reproduces every single requirement from `Business Requirements Analysis - PartsHub.docx` with FULL descriptive text — the cleaner authoritative source (the SRS PDF version had broken column extraction).


### 42.1 Insurance Requirements


#### ONBOARD & REGISTRATION REQUIREMENTS

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ONB-001 | Registration Flow | Insurance companies can register either through sales team invitation or self-registration via website link. | High |
| ONB-002 | Registration Validation | Registered users cannot access system until admin approves their profile. | High |
| ONB-003 | Mandatory Information | Personal details required: Name, Email, Contact Number. | High |
| ONB-004 | Company Details | Company details required: Company Name, Speciality, Trade License Number, License Copy, Licensing Authority and to mention if they are head quarter or branch. | High |
| ONB-005 | Licensing Authority | Licensing Authority should be a dropdown; list to be provided by client. | Medium |
| ONB-006 | Required Documents | Trade License copy and VAT Certificate must be uploaded. | High |
| ONB-007 | Branch Handling | Each branch registers separately because each branch has its own trade license. | High |
| ONB-008 | Parent–Child Mapping | Branch should be able to select Parent Company during registration if not available then they can update it later. | Medium |
| ONB-009 | Admin Verification | Admin manually verifies license through licensing authority website. | High |
| ONB-010 | Notification to Admin | On new registration, admin receives web and email notification for approval. | High |
| ONB-011 | Rejection Handling | Admin provides rejection reason; insurer can edit details and resubmit. | High |
| ONB-012 | Notification to Insurance Company | Insurance company receives rejection notification via email and web in-app notifications. | High |
| ONB-013 | Admin Platform | Admin will use only web-based portal; responsive UI required. | Medium |
| ONB-014 | Post-Approval Access | Once approved, insurer gains access to all relevant modules. | High |


#### Dashboard

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ID-001 | Dashboard | Static dashboard will be implemented and will be accessed by proper subscription plan | High |


#### CLAIM REQUEST REQUIREMENTS

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CLM-001 | Claim Creation | Insurance creates claim request after receiving accident details and images. | High |
| CLM-002 | Upload Documents | Insurance user must upload police report, photos, videos, witness details, and damage description. | High |
| CLM-003 | Policy Information | Claim form must include Policyholder Name, Policy Number, Contact Information, Date of Claim, Reason for claim. | High |
| CLM-004 | Additional Documents | System should provide option to upload additional supporting documents. | Medium |
| CLM-005 | Claim Status | Multiple claim statuses needed (client will provide exact list). | High |
| CLM-006 | AI Future Scope | In future AI will identify damaged parts automatically from images. | Medium |
| CLM-007 | Manual Override | Insurance must manually add/remove parts if AI misses or wrongly detects items. | High |
| CLM-008 | Claim Request Access | Insurance must preview, publish, edit, search and filter claim requests. | High |


#### RFQ TO GARAGE REQUIREMENTS

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| GAR-001 | RFQ Trigger | After claim creation, RFQ will be sent to one or multiple garages selected by insurer and garage will get notifications. | High |
| GAR-002 | Garage Selection Logic | Selection of garages will be done by insurance company manually. | High |
| GAR-003 | Multi-Garage Option | System should support sending RFQ to one garage or multiple garages simultaneously. | High |
| GAR-004 | RFQ Response | Garages should be able to accept or reject RFQ. | High |
| GAR-005 | Garage Quotation | Garage will send Required Parts List + Labor Cost in the quotation and for review they can attach required Photos & Videos. | High |
| GAR-006 | Quotation Comparison | Garage quotations compared based on pricing (labor cost). | High |
| GAR-007 | Order Creation | Order will be created based on the selected Garage quotation. | High |


#### RFQ TO SUPPLIER REQUIREMENTS

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| SUP-001 | RFQ Trigger | After selecting garage and finalizing parts list, insurer sends RFQ to suppliers. | High |
| SUP-002 | Supplier Quote Structure | Supplier quotation includes: Part Name, Unit Price, Total Cost. | High |
| SUP-003 | Multi-Supplier Assignment | For a particular claim, parts will be sourced from one or multiple suppliers depending on availability. | High |
| SUP-004 | Quotation Comparison | Compare supplier quotes based on lowest part price. | High |
| SUP-005 | Quote Selection | For now, RFQ sending & Quote evaluation is fully manual due to complexity and Multiple quote can be selected for one particular claim. | Medium |
| SUP-006 | Order Creation | Order will be created based on the selected supplier quotations. | High |


#### Orders

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ORD-001 | Orders List | Insurance company must be able to view all orders placed to suppliers and garages.Orders list must show orders created for parts procurement (supplier orders) and repair/labor (garage orders).Orders must be categorized as Garage Order (labor) and Supplier Order (parts).Orders list must show summary fields: Order ID, Order Date, Supplier/Garage Name, Order Status.Insurance can filter orders by date, order type, supplier/garage, and status.Insurance can search orders using Order ID, Supplier/Garage name, and Claim ID. | High |
| ORD-002 | Orders List Visibility | Order visibility: HQ sees all orders; each branch sees only its own orders. | High |
| ORD-003 | Order Details | Order details must show Order ID, Order Date, Supplier/Garage Name, and Contact Details.Order details must show part details: Part Name, Part details, Quantity, Unit Price, Total Price.Address information: Billing Address, Shipping Address | High |
| ORD-004 | Billing Information | Billing details: Cost Breakdown, Tax, Additional Charges (e.g., delivery fee). | High |
| ORD-005 | Order Status | Order details must show a full status/timeline history: (status to be given by client). | High |
| ORD-006 | Reviews & Ratings | Insurance must be able to give reviews and ratings for suppliers/garages for each claim.System must calculate an aggregated rating for each supplier/garage based on past orders. | Medium |
| ORD-007 | Filters | Insurance can filter orders by status: Pending, Confirmed, Dispatched, Delivered, Cancelled. | High |
| ORD-008 | Replacement / Refund | Insurance can raise replacement / refund requests if supplied part is defective/incorrect. | High |
| ORD-009 | Replacement / Refund Workflow | Replacement / Refund requests must require supplier approval and log a reason. | Medium |
| ORD-010 | Status Timeline | Order details must show a chronological activity timeline (status-by-status). | Medium |

### 42.2 Garage Requirements

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| GAR-001 | Add Network Garages | Insurance companies must be able to mention their network garages to the platform. | High |
| GAR-002 | Garage Listings | System must display a combined list of: (1) network garages, (2) independently registered garages. | High |
| GAR-003 | Garage Details | List must show garage name, garage ID, specialization, contact person, contact details, address, and licensing details. | Medium |
| GAR-004 | HQ Garage Access | HQ sees all garages associated with all branches; branch sees only garages tied to its operations. | High |
| GAR-005 | Garage Related Claims | Insurance must be able to view all assigned / invited garages participating in each claim request. | High |
| GAR-006 | Claim Invite | Insurance company can invite one or multiple garages to a claim request for estimates/quotations. | High |
| GAR-007 | Claim Assignment | Based on the Garage quotations, Insurance company can choose to which garage claim will be assigned by choosing quotation. | High |
| GAR-008 | Payment Handling | Insurance must be able to view repair costs submitted by the garage and process payments for completed claims. | High |
| GAR-009 | Claim Search | Insurance can search claim requests based on claim status (Pending, In Progress, Approved, Completed, etc.). | High |
| GAR-010 | Invoice Details | Garage invoice will get generated only when Insurance company will make the payment. | High |
| GAR-011 | Search | Insurance users can search for garages using name, ID, specialization, location, or license number. | Medium |
| GAR-012 | Filter | Insurance can filter garages by network / non-network, specialization, region, or activity status. | Medium |


#### Branches

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| BR-001 | Branch List | HQ must display a list of all branches registered under the insurance companyBranch list must show: Branch Name, Branch ID, Branch Address, Branch Manager Name, Contact Information. | High |
| BR-002 | Statistics | HQ can view operational statistics for each branch (Details to be shared by client). | High |
| BR-003 | Branch Section | Branch user should only see their own data; branch section must not appear for branch users (HQ-only feature). | High |
| BR-004 | Onboarding(Add Branch) | Insurance companies do not manually create branches from their portal. Branches are created only via branch self-onboarding. | High |
| BR-005 | Search & Filter | HQ user must be able to search branches by name, ID,  manager, or phone number. | High |
| BR-006 | Edit Branch | HQ must be able to edit branch details except subscription/licensing fields. | High |
| BR-007 | Subscription | Each branch must purchase its own subscription separately; HQ subscription does not cover branches. | High |


#### Inventory

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| INV-001 | Add Inventory | Insurance company must be able to add new inventory items that includes only Parts and Vehicle. | High |
| INV-002 | Add Inventory | While adding parts, All the item information will be read only but available quantity and unit price user can enter. | High |
| INV-003 | Add Inventory | If a part / vehicle is newly received and directly used for auction/hot deal, it must be added to inventory first. | High |
| INV-004 | Inventory Listing | Inventory list must show: Item Name, Item ID, Item Type(Vehicle or Parts), Quantity Available (Static entry), Status. | High |
| INV-005 | Inventory Edit | Inventory items must be editable by insurance (e.g., update quantity, change status). | High |
| INV-006 | Inventory Delete | Insurance should be able to delete/remove inventory items no longer available. | Medium |
| INV-007 | Auction | Auctions must be created from the Items in Inventory | High |
| INV-008 | Hot Deals | Hot deals must be created from the Items in Inventory | High |
| INV-009 | Stakeholder Access | Only Insurance users can add/edit/delete Items; | High |


#### Auction

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| AUC-001 | Create Auction | Insurance company must be able to create a new auction. Auction must include Name/Title, Start & End Date & Time, Seller Information, Item Information (Mandatory), Reserve Price, Starting Bid. | High |
| AUC-002 | Inventory | Auction Items must be selectable from Inventory only. | High |
| AUC-003 | Add  & Edit Parts | Insurance must be able to Add and edit auction items before auction goes live. | Medium |
| AUC-004 | Delete Parts | Insurance must be able to delete Items from auction before it goes live. | Medium |
| AUC-005 | Auction Details | Auction details page must show all Items linked with the status. | High |
| AUC-006 | Bid Details | System must show all bids placed based on particular auction and Every user from supplier and garages can participate. | High |
| AUC-007 | Bids | System must maintain a full bid history for audit. | Medium |
| AUC-008 | Order Creation | After auction closes, the highest bid must automatically generate an order. | High |
| AUC-009 | Order Details | Order must include: Order Amount, Buyer Details, Item Info, Payment Details and Order status. And auction orders must appear in the Insurance Order module under the Auction category. | High |
| AUC-010 | Order Visibility | Insurance must be able to view all orders.(HQ can see all orders including its branches orders and Branches can see their orders only) | Medium |


#### Hot Deals

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| HD-001 | Create Hot Deals | Insurance company must be able to create a new Hot Deals. Hot Deal must include Hot Deal Name, Start & End Date & Time, Deal Price, Item Information (Mandatory). | High |
| HD-002 | Inventory | Hot Deals Items must be selectable from Inventory only. | High |
| HD-003 | Add & Edit Parts | Insurance must be able to Add and edit Hot Deals items before auction goes live. | Medium |
| HD-004 | Delete Parts | Insurance must be able to delete items from Hot Deal. | Medium |
| HD-005 | Hot Deal Details | Hot Deal details page must show all items linked with the status. | High |
| HD-008 | Order Creation | When a buyer purchases from a Hot Deal, the system must generate an order automatically. | High |
| HD-009 | Order Details | Order must include item details, Item Type(Parts or Vehicle), deal price, buyer details, and Order status. And Hot Deals orders must appear in the Insurance Order module under the Hot Deals category. | High |
| HD-010 | Order Visibility | Insurance must be able to view all orders.(HQ can see all orders including its branches orders and Branches can see their orders only) | Medium |


#### Subscription

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ISUB-001 | Active Plans Listing | Insurance must be able to view all active subscription plans available for purchase. | High |
| ISUB-002 | Plan Visibility | Only Active & Published plans created by Admin must be shown. Inactive plans must not appear. | High |
| ISUB-003 | Plan Information | Each plan must display: Feature Group, Plan Name, Price, Billing Cycle, Plan Benefits, Counters, Validity. | High |
| ISUB-004 | Payment Options | Insurance must be able to pay and to get subscription. | High |
| ISUB-005 | Upgrade Plan | Insurance must be able to upgrade to a higher plan. | High |
| ISUB-006 | Downgrade Plan | Downgrade will be allowed anytime but will be activate after expiry of current plan. | Medium |
| ISUB-007 | Payment Confirmation | System must show successful payment receipt, transaction ID, and invoice. | High |
| ISUB-008 | Current Plan Details | Insurance must be able to view their current subscription details: Plan Name, Start Date, End Date, Counters, and Status. | High |
| ISUB-009 | No Carry Forward | Unused counters must not carry forward to next cycle. | High |
| ISUB-010 | Renewal Option | Insurance must be able to renew the plan before expiry and renewed plan will be active after the current plan expires. | High |
| ISUB-011 | Subscription History | Insurance must be able to view complete subscription history: Past plans, payment status, receipts, usage logs. | High |
| ISUB-012 | Access Control | Insurance cannot use features beyond plan counters (system must enforce). | High |
| ISUB-013 | Notifications | Insurance receives notifications for plan activation, expiry, renewal, upgrades, downgrades and payment failures. | Medium |
| ISUB-014 | Audit Trail | System must store subscription actions for compliance. | Medium |


#### My Account

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ACC-001 | Edit Profile | User must be able to edit all profile details captured during registration. | High |
| ACC-002 | Edit Profile | Editable information must not include: Email Id, Licence number and any other details which is related to unique identifier of the user. | High |
| ACC-003 | Edit Profile | User must not be allowed to change their registered Company Type (Insurer, Garage, Supplier) once account is created if any important details has been updated hence another approval is required to reflect the details. | High |
| ACC-004 | Attachments | User must be able to upload/update registration documents (trade license, ID, certifications and etc.). | Medium |

### 42.2 Garage Requirements


#### ONBOARD & REGISTRATION REQUIREMENTS

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| GON-001 | Registration | Garage user must be able to register on the platform by providing First Name, Last Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, and Password. | High |
| GON-002 | Additional Information | Garage users must be able to add personal details, company details, speciality, license documents, registration details, and bank details after login.System must support uploading trade license, bank proof, registration certificates, and identity proof. |  |
| GON-003 | Unique Identifier Validation | Unique identifiers across users will be unique across the system (no duplicates allowed). | High |
| GON-004 | Registration | Password must follow security standards. | High |
| GON-005 | Login | Registered garage users must be able to log in using email ID and password. | High |
| GON-006 | Forgot Password | Login screen must include Forgot Password with link to reset password via email verification. | High |
| GON-007 | Approval Process | After submission of all details, the admin must review and approve or reject the garage registration. | High |
| GON-008 | Rejection Process | If rejected, system must show rejection reason and provide option for document re-upload. | High |
| GON-009 | System Usage | Garage users must not be allowed to use the platform until their registration is approved. | High |
| GON-010 | Approval Notification | Approved users must receive system notifications via email activation. | Medium |


#### Dashboard

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| GD-001 | Dashboard | Static dashboard will be implemented and will be accessed by proper subscription plan | High |


#### CLAIM REQUEST FOR QUOTATION(RFQ)

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| GCR-001 | Claim List | Garage must be able to view all claim requests sent to them for requesting for quotation. | High |
| GCR-002 | Stage segregation | Claim request list must show status-wise segregation (Input to be given by client). | High |
| GCR-003 | Claim List | Claim request list must show Claim ID, Vehicle Details, Insurance Company, Request Date, and Status. | High |
| GCR-004 | Claim Details | Garage must be able to view full claim details including vehicle information, damage photos, request notes, documents submitted by insurer. | High |
| GCR-005 | Accept & Reject | Garage must have options to Accept or Reject the claim request for quotation. | High |
| GCR-006 | Add Parts | Garage must be able to add required parts with their prices for repair by choosing parts. | High |
| GCR-007 | Labour Cost | Garage must be able to enter Labour Cost for the service. | High |
| GCR-008 | Quotation Submission | Garage must submit a final quotation including part details (Along with Prices) and labour charges. | High |
| GCR-009 | Document Upload | Garage must be able to upload supporting documents (estimate file, additional photos, etc.). | Medium |
| GCR-010 | Activity Log | System must log all actions (Accept/Reject claim, Add parts during sending quotation, Submit quotation). | Medium |
| GCR-011 | Notifications | Garage must receive notifications when a new claim is requested for quotation, quotation actions are taken, or quotation selected. | High |


#### Auction

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| GAP-001 | View Auctions | Garage users must be able to view all ongoing and upcoming auctions on the platform. | High |
| GAP-002 | Auction List | Auctions list must display Title, Start/End Date & Time, Item Count, Reserve Price, Auction Status. | Medium |
| GAP-003 | Auction Details | Garage users must be able to view complete details of the auctioned item(s). | High |
| GAP-004 | Bid Details | Garage must be able to view current highest bid, bid history , and bid timestamps. | High |
| GAP-005 | Deposit Fee | Garage users must pay a deposit fee to participate in bidding for selected auction items and will be refundable to original payment source. | High |
| GAP-006 | Place Bid | Garage users must be able to place a bid only if the deposit payment is successful & Bid must always be greater than the current highest bid. | High |
| GAP-007 | Order List | Garage users must be able to view all auction orders won by them & Order list must show Order ID, Auction ID, Item Name, Winning Price, Order Status, Payment Status. | High |
| GAP-008 | Order Details | Garage must be able to view details for auction orders including item info, delivery info, invoice, and payment requirements. | High |
| GAP-009 | Payment Handling | Garage must be able to complete payment for auction orders through available online payment modes. | Medium |
| GAP-010 | Notifications | System must send notifications to the garage for deposit confirmation, bid updates, winning confirmation, and payment reminders. | High |


#### Hot Deal

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| GHD-001 | Hot Deals | Garage users must be able to view all active hot deals published by insurers. | High |
| GHD-002 | Hot Deals Listing | Hot deals listing must display Item Name, Deal Price, Available Quantity, Start/End Time, Status (will be confirmed by client). | High |
| GHD-003 | Line items | Garage users must be able to view full details of an item available under a hot deal. | High |
| GHD-004 | Item Details | Item details must include Part Name, Part ID, Price, Compatibility (vehicle models), Condition, and Available Quantity. | High |
| GHD-005 | Advanced Filter | Garage users must be able to filter hot deals by: Part Name, Part Category, Vehicle Compatibility, Price Range, Expiry Time, Hot Deals Id, and status. | Medium |
| GHD-006 | Order Listing | Garage users must be able to view all orders placed through hot deals. Order ID, Item Name, Deal Price, Order Date, Payment Status, Delivery Status. | High |
| GHD-007 | Order Page | Garage users must be able to view detailed order information including Invoice, Payment Requirements, Delivery Info. | High |
| GHD-008 | Payment Workflow | Garage must be able to make payment for a hot deal purchase through approved payment modes. | Medium |
| GHD-009 | Audit Trail | System must maintain logs for hot deal purchases and orders for compliance and tracking. | Medium |


#### Inventory

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| INV-001 | Add Inventory | Garage must be able to add new inventory items that includes only Parts and Vehicle. | High |
| INV-002 | Add Inventory | While adding parts, All the item information will be read only but available quantity and unit price user can enter. | High |
| INV-003 | Inventory Listing | Inventory list must show: Item Name, Item ID, Item Type(Vehicle or Parts), Quantity Available (Static entry), Status. | High |
| INV-004 | Inventory Edit | Inventory items must be editable by garage (e.g., update quantity, change status). | High |
| INV-005 | Inventory Delete | Garage should be able to delete/remove inventory items no longer available. | Medium |
| INV-006 | Stakeholder Access | Only garage users can add/edit/delete Items; | High |


#### Purchase Parts

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| GPP-001 | Purchase | Garage users must be able to purchase parts directly for their own inventory. | High |
| GPP-002 | Purchase | System must validate inventory before purchase as Item to be there in inventory which will be purchased. | High |
| GPP-003 | Purchase | If Item is not there in the inventory, system must restrict order placement and notify user to add the item first in the inventory. | High |
| GPP-004 | Order Listing | Garage must be able to view all purchase orders they have Raised and Got Closed. | High |
| GPP-005 | Order Listing | Orders must display Order ID, Item Name, Supplier/Insurer, Order Date, Price, Quantity, Delivery Status, and Payment Status. | High |
| GPP-006 | Order Details | Garage must be able to view full order summary. | High |
| GPP-007 | Replacement | Garage must be able to raise replacement requests for damaged, incorrect, or defective items with some reason. | High |
| GPP-008 | Audit Trail | All purchase actions, order modifications, and reviews must be logged for traceability. | Medium |

| INS-003 | Insurance Profile | Garage must be able to view detailed profile of each insurance company including registered address, communication details and other details. | Medium |

| INS-005 | Claim Requests For Quotation list | Claim request list must display Claim ID, Vehicle Details, Damage Type, Request Date, Status. | High |
| INS-006 | Quote Sending | Clicking a RFQ must take garage to full claim details where they can send quotation. | High |
| INS-007 | Payments & Invoices | Garage must be able to view payments received and pending for completed claims from a selected insurance company. | High |
| INS-008 | Invoices | When payment will be received, then Invoice will be generated from garage side and will be sent to Insurance for payment receiving. |  |
| INS-009 | Payment Summary | Summary must support filtering by date range, branch, claim status, and payment status. | Medium |
| INS-010 | Notification | Garage must receive notifications when Claim Request For Quotation sent from insurers. | High |
| INS-011 | Access Control | Only network garage accounts can view insurance details. | High |
| INS-012 | Audit Trail | System must log all financial transactions, invoice downloads, and communication related to insurer payments. | Medium |

#### ONBOARD & REGISTRATION REQUIREMENTS

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
