| SON-001 | Registration | Supplier’s user must be able to register on the platform by providing First Name, Last Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, and Password. | High |
| SON-002 | Additional Information | Supplier’s users must be able to add personal details, company details,speciality, license documents, registration details, and bank details after login.System must support uploading trade license, bank proof, registration certificates, and identity proof. |  |
| SON-003 | Unique Identifier Validation | Unique identifiers across users will be unique across the system (no duplicates allowed). | High |
| SON-004 | Registration | Password must follow security standards. | High |
| SON-005 | Login | Registered supplier users must be able to log in using email ID and password. | High |
| SON-006 | Forgot Password | Login screen must include Forgot Password with link to reset password via email verification. | High |
| SON-007 | Approval Process | After submission of all details, the admin must review and approve or reject the garage registration. | High |
| SON-008 | Rejection Process | If rejected, system must show rejection reason and provide option for document re-upload. | High |
| SON-009 | System Usage | Supplier’s users must not be allowed to use the platform until their registration is approved. | High |
| SON-010 | Approval Notification | Approved users must receive system notifications via email activation. | Medium |


#### Dashboard

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| SD-001 | Dashboard | Static dashboard will be implemented and will be accessed by proper subscription plan. | High |


#### Request for Quotes

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| SRFQ-01 | RFQ Listing | Supplier must be able to view all RFQs received from insurance companies. | High |
| SRFQ-02 | RFQ List Information | RFQ list must show: RFQ Number, RFQ Date, Insurance Company Name, Item Names, Quantities, Status. | High |
| SRFQ-03 | RFQ Search | Supplier must be able to search and filter RFQs by RFQ No, Insurance Company, Date Range, Status. | Medium |
| SRFQ-04 | RFQ Access | Supplier must be able to open and view complete RFQ details. | High |
| SRFQ-05 | RFQ Details | RFQ details must include: RFQ No, RFQ Date, Insurance Company Name, Contact Details and other details to be shared by client. | High |
| SRFQ-06 | Item Details | Supplier must be able to view full item list with quantity, requested specs, and required delivery timeline. | High |
| SRFQ-07 | Price Entry for Quotation | Supplier must be able to enter item-level unit price for each requested part as quotation. | High |
| SRFQ-08 | Taxes & Fees | Supplier must be able to add taxes, fees, and any additional surcharges. | High |
| SRFQ-09 | Discounts | Supplier must be able to provide discounts (percentage or fixed). | Medium |
| SRFQ-10 | Payment Terms | Supplier must specify payment terms such as advance %, credit period, or full payment. | Medium |
| SRFQ-11 | Delivery Terms | Supplier must specify delivery details including delivery time, shipping method, and charges if applicable. | High |
| SRFQ-12 | Additional Notes | Supplier can add notes/comments before submitting RFQ response in the form of Quotation. | Low |
| SRFQ-13 | Submit Quotation | Supplier must be able to submit completed quotation for insurer’s approval. | High |
| SRFQ-14 | Quotation Status | Status must show: Pending, Approved, Rejected. | High |
| SRFQ-15 | Invoice Creation | Supplier must be able to generate invoices related to approve quotations. | Medium |
| SRFQ-16 | Invoice Details | Invoices must display item summary, final price, tax, delivery charges, and will be created once the payment is done by the insurance company. | Medium |
| SRFQ-17 | Replacement Requests | Supplier must receive and manage replacement requests from insurers for supplied parts within given replacement tenure. | High |
| SRFQ-18 | Replacement Workflow | Supplier must approve/reject replacement requests and upload comments or required documents. | High |
| SRFQ-19 | Refund Processing | Supplier must be able to process refunds for orders where replacement is not possible or the part is refundable within refund acceptable tenure. | High |
| SRFQ-20 | Refund Status | Supplier can track refund status: Pending, Settled, Rejected. | Medium |
| SRFQ-21 | Notifications | Supplier must receive notifications for: New RFQ, RFQ Updates, Invoice Payment, Replacement/Refund requests. | High |


#### Inventory

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| SIN-01 | Inventory Listing | Supplier must be able to view a complete list of all inventory items added by them. Inventory list must show: Item Name, Item SKU, Unit Price, Available Quantity, Status. | High |
| SIN-02 | Search & Filter | Supplier must be able to search items by Item Name, SKU, Category, or Status. | Medium |
| SIN-03 | Add Inventory | While adding parts, All the item information will be read only but available quantity and unit price user can enter. | High |
| SIN-04 | Edit Inventory | Supplier must be able to edit item information such as price, quantity, or other details. | High |
| SIN-05 | Delete Inventory | Supplier must be able to delete an inventory item. | Medium |
| SIN-06 | Deleted Inventory View | Supplier must be able to view deleted items in a separate “Deleted Inventory” list or recycle bin. | Medium |
| SIN-07 | Inventory Detail Page | Supplier must be able to view complete details of each inventory item along with client’s given fields information | High |


#### Auction

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| SAUC-01 | View Auctions | Supplier must be able to view all ongoing auctions created by insurance companies. | High |
| SAUC-02 | Auction Listing | Auction listing must show: Auction Title, Start/End Date & Time, Total Items, Reserve Price, Current Status. | High |
| SAUC-03 | Item Details | Supplier must be able to view detailed item information: Part Name, Part ID, Condition, Quantity, Compatibility, Reserve Price. | High |
| SAUC-04 | Bid Details | Supplier must be able to view current highest bid, bidding history (masked bidder ID), and bid increments. | High |
| SAUC-05 | Deposit Requirement | Supplier must pay a deposit fee to participate in the auction. | High |
| SAUC-06 | Deposit Validation | System must allow bidding only after deposit payment is confirmed. | High |
| SAUC-07 | Place Bid | Supplier must be able to place a bid higher than the current highest bid and according to minimum increment rules. | High |
| SAUC-08 | Notification | Supplier must get real-time notifications when outbid or when auction is ending soon and if auction has won and payment reminders. | Medium |
| SAUC-09 | Order Listing | Supplier must be able to view the list of orders generated from auctions they have won. | High |
| SAUC-10 | Order Listing | Order list must show: Order ID, Auction ID, Item Name, Winning Price, Order Date, Delivery Status, Payment Status. | High |
| SAUC-11 | Order Details | Supplier must be able to view detailed order information: item details, invoice, delivery information, payment instructions. | High |
| SAUC-12 | Payment | Supplier must be able to make the final payment for the won auction. | Medium |


#### Subscription

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| SSUB-001 | Active Plans Listing | Supplier must be able to view all active subscription plans available for purchase. | High |
| SSUB-002 | Plan Visibility | Only Active & Published plans created by Admin must be shown. Inactive plans must not appear. | High |
| SSUB-003 | Plan Information | Each plan must display: Feature Group, Plan Name, Price, Billing Cycle, Plan Benefits, Counters, Validity. | High |
| SSUB-004 | Payment Options | Supplier must be able to pay to get subscription. | High |
| SSUB-005 | Upgrade Plan | Supplier must be able to upgrade to a higher plan. | High |
| SSUB-006 | Downgrade Plan | Downgrade will be allowed anytime but will be activate after expiry of current plan. | Medium |
| SSUB-007 | Payment Confirmation | System must show successful payment receipt, transaction ID, and invoice. | High |
| SSUB-008 | Current Plan Details | Supplier must be able to view their current subscription details: Plan Name, Start Date, End Date, Counters, Status. | High |
| SSUB-009 | No Carry Forward | Unused counters must not carry forward to next cycle. | High |
| SSUB-010 | Renewal Option | Supplier must be able to renew the plan before expiry. | High |
| SSUB-011 | Subscription History | Supplier must be able to view complete subscription history: Past plans, payment status, receipts, usage logs. | High |
| SSUB-012 | Access Control | Supplier cannot use features beyond plan counters (system must enforce). | High |
| SSUB-013 | Notifications | Supplier receives notifications for plan activation, expiry, renewal, upgrades, downgrades, payment failures. | Medium |
| SSUB-014 | Audit Trail | System must store subscription actions for compliance. | Medium |


#### Hot Deal

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| SHD-01 | Hot Deals Listing | Supplier must be able to view all active hot deals created by insurance companies. Listing must show: Hot Deal Name, Item Name, Deal Price, Available Quantity, Start/End Time, Status (Active, Upcoming, Expired). | High |
| SHD-02 | View Item Details | Supplier must be able to view full details of items available under hot deals. Item details must include Part Name, SKU/Part ID, Description, Compatibility, Deal Price, MRP (if available), Quantity Available, Images. | High |
| SHD-03 | Advance Filter | Supplier must be able to filter hot deal items by: Part Name, Category, Price Range, Status, Vehicle Compatibility. | Medium |
| SHD-04 | Search | Supplier must be able to search hot deal items by Item Name or SKU/Part ID. | Medium |
| SHD-05 | Orders Listing | Supplier must be able to view orders placed through hot deals they participated in. Orders list must show: Order ID, Item Name, Deal Price, Order Date, Quantity, Delivery Status, Payment Status. | High |
| SHD-06 | Order Details | Supplier must be able to view complete order details: Invoice, Payment Breakdown, Delivery Info, Buyer Details. | High |
| SHD-07 | Notification | Supplier must receive notifications for: Hot Deal Expiry Alerts, Order Confirmations, Payment Updates. | Medium |


#### My Account

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| SACC-001 | Edit Profile | User must be able to edit all profile details captured during registration. | High |
| SACC-002 | Edit Profile | Editable information must not include: Email Id, Licence number and any other details which is related to unique identifier of the user. | High |
| SACC-003 | Edit Profile | User must not be allowed to change their registered Company Type (Insurer, Garage, Supplier) once account is created if any important details has been updated hence another approval is required to reflect the details. | High |
| SACC-004 | Attachments | User must be able to upload/update registration documents (trade license, ID, certifications and etc.). | Medium |

### 42.4 Platform Administrator Requirements


#### Platform Administrator Onboarding

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-OB-001 | Registration | Platform Administrator must be able to register on the platform using required admin details. | High |
| ADM-OB-002 | Registration Fields | Registration must capture: Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, Password, and other field level information to be shared by client. | High |
| ADM-OB-003 | Email Verification | After registration, admin must verify email before first login. | High |
| ADM-OB-004 | Login | Admin must be able to log in using registered email and password. | High |
| ADM-OB-005 | Forgot Password | Admin must be able to reset password using email. | High |
| ADM-OB-006 | Reset Password Rules | New password must follow platform security rules: min length, special characters, etc. | High |


#### Manage Insurance

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-INS-001 | Insurance Listing | Admin must be able to view a list of all registered insurance companies. Listing must display: Insurance Company Name, Date of Joining, No. of Branches, Subscription Plan Name, and Status. | High |
| ADM-INS-002 | Search | Admin must be able to search insurance companies by Name, City, and Country. | High |
| ADM-INS-003 | Filters | Admin must be able to filter by City, Country, Subscription Plan Name, Status (Active/Inactive/Pending/Rejected). | High |
| ADM-INS-004 | View Details | Admin must be able to view complete profile details for any selected insurance company. | High |
| ADM-INS-005 | View Details – Company Info | Company info must display: Company Name, Date Joined, Email ID, Mobile Number, and Account Status. | High |
| ADM-INS-006 | View Details – Subscription Info | Subscription details must show: Active Plan Name, Start Date, End Date, Plan Type, and Plan Amount. | High |
| ADM-INS-007 | View Details – Branch Info | Branch details must display Branch Name, Branch ID, Address, and Contact Person. | Medium |
| ADM-INS-008 | View Details – Claim Analytics | Claim details must show total No. of Claim Requests related to that insurance company. | High |
| ADM-INS-009 | View Details – Auction Analytics | Admin must be able to view the total number of auctions created by that insurance company. | Medium |
| ADM-INS-010 | View Details – Hot Deals Analytics | Admin must see the total number of Hot Deals created by the insurance company. | Medium |
| ADM-INS-011 | View Details – Garage Metrics | Admin must see list of insurance company’s Network Garages, and their corresponding number of claim requests. | High |
| ADM-INS-012 | View Details – Inventory Info | Admin must be able to view inventory summary: Item Name, Unit Price, and Quantity Available. | High |
| ADM-INS-013 | Activate / Deactivate | Admin must be able to activate or deactivate any insurance company account. | High |
| ADM-INS-014 | Verify Registration | Admin must verify onboarding requests from new insurance companies (document verification + details validation) and admin must verify & approve profile edit requests involving Important details. | High |
| ADM-INS-015 | Export | Admin must be able to export insurance company details into Excel/CSV formats. | Medium |
| ADM-INS-016 | Export Filters | Export must respect applied filters (City, Country, Plan, and Status). | Medium |
| ADM-INS-017 | Audit Log | System must log all admin actions related to insurance company review, approval, activation, deactivation and other all details. | High |
| ADM-INS-018 | Notifications | Admin receives notifications for new registrations, edit requests, subscription renewals. | Medium |


#### Manage Garage

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-GAR-001 | Garage Listing | Admin must be able to view the list of all registered garages. Listing must show: Garage Name, Contact Information, Date of Joining, Subscription Plan Name, No. of Active Claim Requests, and Account Status. | High |
| ADM-GAR-002 | Search | Admin must be able to search garages by Name, City, and Country. | High |
| ADM-GAR-003 | Filter | Admin must be able to filter garages by City, Country, Plan Name, Status (Active / Inactive / Pending / Rejected). | High |
| ADM-GAR-004 | View Details | Admin must be able to view complete garage profile details, registered in Parts Hub. | High |
| ADM-GAR-005 | View Details – Garage Info | Details must include: Garage Name, Contact Person Name, Email, Mobile Number, and Status. | High |
| ADM-GAR-006 | View Details – Subscription Info | Subscription details must show: Active Plan Name, Start Date, End Date, Plan Type, and Plan Amount. | High |
| ADM-GAR-007 | View Details – Claim Requests | Admin must see the number of Accepted and Rejected Claim Requests for that garage. | High |
| ADM-GAR-008 | View Details – Insurance Companies | Admin must see list of associated Insurance Companies + No. of claim requests received from each. | Medium |
| ADM-GAR-009 | View Details – Parts Purchased | Admin must see Purchased Part Name, Quantity Purchased, and Unit Price for purchased items. | Medium |
| ADM-GAR-010 | Verify Profile Edits | Admin must review and approve/reject any profile edit requests (documents, license, contact details). | High |
| ADM-GAR-011 | Activate / Inactivate | Admin must be able to activate or deactivate any garage account. | High |
| ADM-GAR-012 | Export | Admin must be able to export garage listing into Excel/CSV format. | Medium |
| ADM-GAR-013 | Export with Filters | Export must only include data based on applied filters (City, Country, Plan Name, Status). | Medium |
| ADM-GAR-014 | Notifications | Admin must receive notifications for new registration, profile edit requests, subscription renewal alerts. | Medium |
| ADM-GAR-015 | Audit Trail | All admin actions such as view, approve, reject, activate, deactivate must be logged. | High |


#### Manage Suppliers

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-SUP-001 | Supplier Listing | Admin must be able to view a list of all registered suppliers. Listing must show: Supplier Name, Contact Information, Date of Joining, Subscription Plan Name, Number of RFQs, and Account Status. | High |
| ADM-SUP-002 | Supplier Listing | Listing must show: Supplier Name, Contact Information, Date of Joining, Subscription Plan Name, Number of RFQs, and Account Status. | High |
| ADM-SUP-003 | Search | Admin must be able to search suppliers by Name, City, and Country. | High |
| ADM-SUP-004 | Filter | Admin must be able to filter suppliers by City, Country, Plan Name, Status (Active, Inactive, Pending, and Rejected). | High |
| ADM-SUP-005 | View Supplier Details | Admin must be able to view complete supplier profile. | High |
| ADM-SUP-006 | Supplier Info | Supplier account information must include: Supplier Name, Date Joined, Email ID, Mobile Number, and Status. | High |
| ADM-SUP-007 | Subscription Plan Details | Subscription details must include: Active Plan Name, Start Date, End Date, Plan Type, and Plan Amount. | High |
| ADM-SUP-008 | RFQ Details | Admin must see all Request for Quotes information: Quote Number, Quote Date, Insurance Company Name, Item Details with quantity and unit price, Status. | High |
| ADM-SUP-009 | Inventory Details | Admin must see supplier’s inventory including Item Name, SKU, Unit Price, Quantity Available. | High |
| ADM-SUP-010 | Verify Registration | Admin must verify new supplier registrations including business documents and compliance. | High |
| ADM-SUP-011 | Verify Profile Edits | Admin must review and approve or reject profile edit requests (license, company name, email change, etc.). | High |
| ADM-SUP-012 | Activate/Deactivate Supplier | Admin must be able to activate or deactivate any supplier account. | High |
| ADM-SUP-013 | Export Supplier List | Admin must be able to export supplier listing into Excel/CSV. | Medium |
| ADM-SUP-014 | Export Based on Filters | Export must only export filtered results (City, Country, Plan, and Status). | Medium |
| ADM-SUP-015 | Notifications | Admin must receive notifications for new supplier registrations, profile edit requests, subscription renewals. | Medium |
| ADM-SUP-016 | Audit Trail | All admin actions including verify, approve, reject, activate, deactivate must be logged. | High |


#### Manage Claim Requests:

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-CLM-001 | Claim Request Listing | Admin must be able to view a list of all claim requests submitted on the platform. Claim list must show: Insurance Company Name, Customer Name, Policy Number, Date of Claim, Reason for Claim, and Claim Status. | High |
| ADM-CLM-002 | Search | Admin must be able to search claims by Policy Number, Customer Name, Insurance Company Name. | High |
| ADM-CLM-003 | Filter | Admin must be able to filter claims by Insurance Company, City, Country, Status (Pending, Assigned, Accepted, Rejected, and Completed). | High |
| ADM-CLM-004 | Claim Details View | Admin must be able to view full claim request details. | High |
| ADM-CLM-005 | Claim Info | Admin must view: Customer Name, Policy Number, Contact Information, witness details uploaded by the insurance company or claimant, police report details and attached documents/images, detailed description of damages, the list of parts required, quantities, any RFQ linkage, other relevant documents, and additional notes. | High |
| ADM-CLM-006 | Export Claim List | Admin must be able to export claims listing into Excel/CSV. | Medium |
| ADM-CLM-007 | Export with Filters | Only filtered claim data must be included in exported file. | Medium |


#### Manage Subscription

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-SUB-PLAN-001 | Plan Listing | Admin must be able to view all existing subscription plans. Listing must show: Feature Group, Plan Name, Pricing Type, Price, Billing Cycle, Frequency, and Status. | High |
| ADM-SUB-PLAN-002 | Search Plans | Admin must be able to search plans by Plan Name, Plan Type, and Billing Cycle. | High |
| ADM-SUB-PLAN-003 | Add New Plan | Admin must be able to create a new subscription plan by entering: Feature Group, Plan Name, Pricing Type, Price, Billing Cycle, Frequency, and Status. | High |
| ADM-SUB-PLAN-004 | Edit Plan | Admin must be able to edit or update plan details only if plan is draft or published but not purchased by any user. | High |
| ADM-SUB-PLAN-005 | Save Edited Plan | System must re-validate plan fields upon editing. | Medium |
| ADM-SUB-PLAN-006 | Delete Plan | Admin must be able to delete a plan permanently only if plan is in draft stage and if published then plan should not be deleted. | Medium |
| ADM-SUB-PLAN-007 | Activate/Deactivate Plan | Admin must be able to make a plan Active or Inactive. | High |
| ADM-SUB-PLAN-008 | Frontend Visibility | Inactive plans must not appear in frontend subscription purchase screens but already purchased users can avail the features until their term get expired but cannot renew further. | High |
| ADM-SUB-PLAN-009 | Audit Trail | All plan creation, updates, deletions, and status changes must be logged. | High |
| ADM-SUB-USER-001 | Subscription Listing | Admin must be able to view all active user subscription plans. List must show: Company Name, Feature Group, Plan Name, Billing Type, Address, City, State, Country, and Pin Code. | High |
| ADM-SUB-USER-002 | Search Subscription | Admin can search subscriptions by Plan Name, Plan Type, and Billing Cycle. | High |
| ADM-SUB-USER-003 | Create Subscription | Admin must be able to create a new subscription for a user. Must include: User Name, Subscription Plan, Billing Type, Start Date, Status, Payment Method, Payment Ref Number, and Payment Status. | High |
| ADM-SUB-USER-004 | Edit Subscription | Admin must be able to edit an existing customer’s subscription plan and must update customer’s access related to the updates. | High |
| ADM-SUB-USER-005 | Delete Subscription | Admin can delete a customer’s subscription plan permanently. Deleted subscription must remove the customer's access to plan-based features immediately. | Medium |
| ADM-SUB-USER-006 | Activate/Deactivate | Admin can activate or deactivate a user’s subscription and particular plan cannot be seen by that user in the frontend. | High |
| ADM-SUB-USER-007 | Renewal Tracking | Admin must track start date, expiry date, renewal cycle. | High |
| ADM-SUB-USER-008 | Payment | Admin can take payment through system also can take payment offline as well. | High |
| ADM-SUB-USER-009 | Manual Upgrade & Downgrade | Admin can upgrade or downgrade any user from admin’s end. |  |
| ADM-SUB-USER-010 | Notification | Admin will get notification for user renewal, upgrade, downgrade and other standard notification. |  |
| ADM-SUB-USER-011 | Audit Trail | All subscription changes must be logged. | High |
| ADM-SUB-FG-001 | Feature Group Listing | Admin must be able to view the list of all feature groups. Feature Group list must show: ID, Name, Description, Created On, and Status. | High |
| ADM-SUB-FG-002 | Search Feature Group | Admin must be able to search feature groups by Name, Description, Status, or keywords. | High |
| ADM-SUB-FG-003 | Add Feature Group | Admin must be able to create a new Feature Group by entering: Name, Description, and Status. | High |
| ADM-SUB-FG-004 | Edit Feature Group | Admin must be able to edit/update any feature group. | High |
| ADM-SUB-FG-005 | Delete Feature Group | Admin must be able to permanently delete a feature group. | Medium |
| ADM-SUB-FG-006 | Active/Inactive Feature Group | Admin can activate or deactivate a feature group. Inactive feature groups cannot be used for plan creation and not visible to users. | High |
| ADM-SUB-FG-007 | Delete Restrictions | If a feature group is attached to any existing plan, deletion must not be allowed . | High |
| ADM-SUB-FG-008 | Audit Trail | System must log creation, editing, deletion, activation, and deactivation. | High |


#### Manage Review & Rating

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-RR-001 | Review & Rating Listing | Admin should be able to view a complete list of all reviews and ratings submitted by Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users between them related to claims. | High |
| ADM-RR-002 | Review & Rating Listing | Listing should display: Review ID, User Name, User Type, Rating (1–5), Review Text, Order ID / Claim ID Reference, Date Added, Status (Active/Inactive). | High |
| ADM-RR-003 | View Details | Admin should be able to view full review details, including reviewer profile, associated entity (insurance/garage/supplier), order/claim reference, and timestamps. | High |
| ADM-RR-004 | Search | Admin should be able to search reviews by standard approach. | High |
| ADM-RR-005 | Filter | Admin should be able to filter by: Rating Range (1–5),  Reviewed Entity(Insurance/Garage/Supplier) | High |
| ADM-RR-006 | Edit Review | Admin should be able to edit/update review rating score in case of: incorrect rating submitted by mistake. | Medium |
| ADM-RR-007 | Delete Review | Admin should be able to delete a review or rating completely if inappropriate, fraudulent, or irrelevant. | High |
| ADM-RR-008 | Rating Aggregation View | Admin should be able to see cumulative rating for each business entity (Insurance/Garage/Supplier). | Medium |
| ADM-RR-009 | Audit Trail | System must maintain logs for: creation, edits, deletion, activation/inactivation of reviews. | Medium |


#### Manage Contact Inquiry and Tasks

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-CT-001 | Create Contact Inquiry | Users will be able to create inquiry to admin through a form in the app with mentioning details. | High |
| ADM-CT-002 | Contact Inquiry Listing | Admin should be able to view a list of all contact inquiries submitted through the platform. Listing must display: Inquiry ID, Name, Email, Mobile, Subject, and Submitted On, Status (Open/Assigned/Closed). | High |
| ADM-CT-003 | View Inquiry Details | Admin should be able to view the full inquiry details including message, and contact info. | High |
| ADM-CT-004 | Search Inquiries | Admin should be able to search inquiries by standard approach. | Medium |
| ADM-CT-005 | Filter Inquiries | Admin should be able to filter by Status (Open/Assigned/Closed). | Medium |
| ADM-CT-006 | Delete Inquiry | Admin should be able to delete an inquiry if required. Deleted records should move to an archive (optional). | Medium |
| ADM-CT-007 | Convert Inquiry to Task | Admin should be able to assign an inquiry as a task to any admin user. | High |
| ADM-TASK-001 | Task Listing | Admin should be able to view a list of all tasks created manually or inquiries. | High |
| ADM-TASK-002 | Task Listing | Task list must display with the inquiry details including assigned to. | High |
| ADM-TASK-003 | Add Task | Admin should be able to add a new task manually, without an inquiry reference. | High |
| ADM-TASK-004 | Edit Task | Admin should be able to edit/update task details (Completion comments, status). | Medium |
| ADM-TASK-005 | Notifications | System should send notifications to admin users when: an Inquiry is created, assigned and will send notification to users (Insurance/Garage/Suppliers) when Inquiry will be Closed. | Medium |
| ADM-TASK-006 | Audit Trail | System must maintain an audit log for: inquiry actions, manual task creation, assignment, updates, and closure. | Medium |


#### Manage Auction

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-AUC-001 | Auction Listing | System should allow the admin to view a complete list of all auctions created across Insurance, Garages, and Suppliers. Each auction in the list should display key fields such as: Auction Name/Title, Created By, Start Date, End Date, Status, Reserve Price, Number of Items, and Total Bids Received. | High |
| ADM-AUC-002 | Search & Filter | Admin should be able to search auctions using standard approach. Admin should be able to filter auctions by: Status (Ongoing, Upcoming, and Closed), Date Range, and Created By. | High |
| ADM-AUC-003 | View Auction Details | System should allow admin to view full auction details, including: Auction Title, Schedule, Reserve Price, Seller Information, Item Details, Bid History, Starting Bid, and Terms, and all items under an auction including Item Name, SKU, Reserve Price, Status, Bids history, Highest Bid, and Item Condition. | High |
| ADM-AUC-004 | View Audit Trail | Admin should be able to view an activity log/audit trail for each auction (Created, Edited, Closed, and Winner Declared). | Low |


#### Manage Hot Deals

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-HD-001 | Hot Deals Listing | System should allow the admin to view a complete list of all Hot Deals created across Insurance, Garages, and Suppliers. Each Hot Deal should display key fields: Hot Deal Name, Item Name, Created By, Deal Price, Start Date, End Date, Status, Quantity, No. of Purchases. | High |
| ADM-HD-003 | Search | Admin should be able to search hot deals using standard approach. | High |
| ADM-HD-004 | Filter | Admin should be able to filter hot deals by Status (Active, Inactive, Upcoming, and Expired), Date Range, Created By, Price Range. | High |
| ADM-HD-005 | Hot Deal Details | Admin should be able to view full details of the hot deal including: Hot Deal Name, Description, Item Details, Deal Price, Start & End Date/Time, Status, Created By. | High |
| ADM-HD-006 | Item Details | Admin should see item-level details linked with hot deals: Item Name, SKU, Quantity Available, Unit Price, Images, and Specifications. | Medium |
| ADM-HD-007 | Audit Log | Admin should be able to view an audit trail for every hot deal: Created, Edited, Activated, Deactivated, and Expired. | Low |


#### Manage Admin Groups

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-GRP-001 | Group Listing | System should allow the admin to view a list of all admin user groups created in the platform. Each group listing should display: Group Name, Description, No. of Users Assigned, Created On, Status (Active/Inactive). | High |
| ADM-GRP-002 | Search | Admin should be able to search user groups by group name, and keywords. | High |
| ADM-GRP-003 | Filter | Admin should be able to filter groups by Status (Active/Inactive) and Created Date. | Medium |
| ADM-GRP-004 | Add Group | Admin should be able to create a new admin group by entering: Group Name, Description, Status, and assigning User. | High |
| ADM-GRP-005 | Edit Group | Admin should be able to edit/update an existing group, including modifying permissions and updating name/description. | High |
| ADM-GRP-006 | Permission Mapping | System should allow mapping of permissions→ Users → groups (bottom-up approach). | High |
| ADM-GRP-007 | Delete Group | Admin should be able to delete an admin group. Deletion should be restricted if users are assigned to the group. | Medium |
| ADM-GRP-008 | Active/Inactive | Admin should be able to activate or deactivate a user group. Inactive groups should not be assignable. | High |
| ADM-GRP-009 | View Details | Admin should be able to view full details of a group including: Group Name, Permissions Assigned, Users Assigned, Created On, Created By. | Medium |
| ADM-GRP-010 | Audit Trail | System should keep a log of all changes made to admin groups (create/edit/activate/inactivate/delete). | Medium |


#### Manage Admin Roles

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-ROLE-001 | Role Listing | System should display a list of all admin roles created in the platform. Each role should show: Role Name, Description, Assigned Permissions Count, No. of Users Assigned, Created On, Status (Active/Inactive). | High |
| ADM-ROLE-002 | Search | Admin should be able to search roles by Role Name, Keywords, or Description. | High |
| ADM-ROLE-003 | Filter | Admin should be able to filter roles. | Medium |
| ADM-ROLE-004 | Add Role | Admin should be able to create a new role by: Role Name, Description, Status, and selecting Permissions. | High |
| ADM-ROLE-005 | Edit Role | Admin should be able to edit an existing role, including updating permissions mapped to the role. | High |
| ADM-ROLE-006 | Delete Role | Admin should be able to delete a role, but not if it is assigned to any admin user. | Medium |
| ADM-ROLE-007 | Status Management | Admin should be able to activate or deactivate a role. Inactive roles cannot be assigned to users. | High |
| ADM-ROLE-008 | View Role Details | Admin should be able to view complete details: Role Name, Assigned Permissions, Users Assigned, Created On, Updated On. | Medium |
| ADM-ROLE-009 | Audit Log | System should keep an audit trail of role creation, editing, activation, deactivation, and deletion. | Medium |


#### Manage Admin Account(Users)

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-ACC-001 | Admin Account Listing | System should allow the admin to view a list of all admin user accounts. Listing should display: Admin Name, Email, Mobile Number, Assigned Group, Assigned Role, Created On, Last Login, Status (Active/Inactive). | High |
| ADM-ACC-002 | Search | Admin should be able to search admin users by Name, Email, Mobile Number, Group, Role. | High |
| ADM-ACC-003 | Filter | Admin should be able to filter by Status, Group, Role, Created Date. | Medium |
| ADM-ACC-004 | Add Admin Account | Admin should be able to create a new admin user, adding details: Name, Email, Mobile Number, Password, Group, Role, Status. | High |
| ADM-ACC-005 | Edit Admin Account | Admin should be able to edit/update an existing admin account. | High |
| ADM-ACC-006 | Assign Group | Admin should be able to assign a user group to the admin account (as per Manage Groups module). | High |
| ADM-ACC-007 | Assign Role | Admin should be able to assign a role to the admin account (as per Manage Roles module). | High |
| ADM-ACC-008 | View Details | Admin should be able to view full details for each admin: Name, Email, Mobile, Group, Role, Created On, Created By, Last Login Activity, Status. | Medium |
| ADM-ACC-009 | Active/Inactive | Admin should be able to set an admin user account as Active/Inactive. Inactive admins must not be able to log in. | High |
| ADM-ACC-010 | Password Reset | User can reset their password while logging in using forget password link. | Medium |


#### Manage Payments

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-PAY-001 | Payment Listing | Admin should be able to view a complete list of all payments made between Insurance, Garage, and Suppliers across the platform. | High |
| ADM-PAY-002 | Payment Type Differentiation | Admin should be able to distinguish payments for which purpose payment was made. | High |
| ADM-PAY-003 | View Payment Details | Admin should be able to view complete payment details including: Transaction ID, Order ref. details, Invoice numbers, Payment gateway ref. no., and other details. | High |
| ADM-PAY-004 | Search | Admin should be able to search by: Standard Approach. | High |
| ADM-PAY-005 | Filter | Admin should be able to apply filters: Standard Approach. | High |
| ADM-PAY-006 | Export | Admin should be able to export payment listing and payment details to CSV/Excel. | Low |
| ADM-PAY-007 | Refund Listing | Admin should be able to view all refund transactions with fields: Refund ID, Payment ID, Order ID, Refund Date, Refund Amount, Refund Reason, and Refund Status. | High |


#### Manage Orders

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADM-ORD-001 | Order Listing | Admin should be able to view a complete list of all orders placed between Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users on the platform. Admin should be able to differentiate orders by type: Insurance → Supplier, Garage → Supplier, Insurance → Garage, Auction Orders, Hot Deals Orders. | High |
| ADM-ORD-002 | View Order Details | Admin should be able to view full order details including: Buyer details, Seller details, Item details, Quantities, Unit price, Shipping Information, Billing Information, Payment Method, Order Timeline and other details. | High |
| ADM-ORD-003 | Order Activity Timeline | Admin should see an order activity log: Order placed → Payment received → Processed → Dispatched → Delivered → Replacement/Refund actions. | Medium |
| ADM-ORD-004 | Search | Admin should be able to search orders by standard approach. | High |
| ADM-ORD-005 | Filter | Admin should be able to filter orders by: standard approach. | High |
| ADM-ORD-006 | Export | Admin should be able to export order listing and order details in Excel/CSV format. | Low |

| Req ID | Area | Business Requirement | Priority |

| ADM-ITEM-002 | Search | Admin should be able to search items by Item Name, Item Code/SKU, Category, or standard approach. | High |
| ADM-ITEM-003 | Filter | Admin should be able to apply filters such as Category, Status (Active/Inactive), and standard filters. | Medium |
| ADM-ITEM-004 | View Item Details | Admin should be able to view full details of an item including: Name, SKU, Type, Category, Sub category and other details given by client. | Medium |
| ADM-ITEM-005 | Activate/Deactivate | Admin should be able to activate or deactivate an item. Inactive items must not appear in: • Inventory creation • Claims • RFQs • Hot Deals • Auctions. | High |
| ADM-ITEM-006 | Export | Admin should be able to export item list (Excel/CSV) including all relevant details. | Low |
| ADM-ITEM-007 | Admin Ownership | Master items are exclusively managed by Admin — no other user type can add or edit master items directly. | High |
| ADM-ITEM-008 | User Item Addition Request | Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users should be able to request Admin to add a missing item when creating: • Inventory • RFQ • Claim parts • Auction items • Hot deals. | High |
| ADM-ITEM-009 | Audit Trail | System must capture audit logs for: item creation, edition, activation/inactivation, and request approval/rejection. | Medium |

## 43. Final Out of Scope Document

**This is CRITICAL** — the canonical scope boundary doc. Source: `__Proposal/Final Out of Scope.docx`.

Items marked "Status: Out of scope" with "Comments: Included in the Current Phase" are actually IN-scope despite name (renegotiated). The document captures both states for audit purposes.


**1. Mobile Application (Flutter)**

Status: Out of scope

**Details:**

**1.1 Native Mobile Apps**

Original proposal only included web applications

Client requested mobile apps for Insurance, Garage and Supplier modules

Flutter framework preferred by client

Requires separate cost estimation and timeline

iOS and Android app store publishing process

**2. VEHICLE VALUATION FEATURE**

Status: Out of scope

**Details:**

**2.1 Car Value Assessment Tool**

AI-powered vehicle valuation system

Integration with external databases for vehicle pricing

Accident history tracking

Market value analysis

Credit-based system for valuation searches

**3. ADVANCED AI FEATURES (Beyond Phase 1)**

Status: Out of current phase

**Details:**

**3.1 Automated Supplier and Garage Selection**

Automatic RFQ routing based on supplier and garage specialty

AI-based supplier and garage selection

**3.2 Intelligent Pricing Analysis**

AI analysing quotes from multiple suppliers/garages

Automatic selection of best pricing per part

Historical pricing analysis

Price comparison algorithms

**4. Credit / Payment Terms Management - What will be maintained with the payment terms?**

Status: Out of scope

**Details:**

Claim-wise payment terms (not universal)

30/60/90 day payment cycles? - If payment fails after payment cycle then notify both the parties for paying and collecting and pending payments will be shown.

Credit time limit tracking

Partial payment handling

Comments: Included in the Current Phase

**5. NOTIFICATION SYSTEM - ADVANCED**

Status: Out of scope

**Details:**

**6.1 Marketing Notifications**

o     Bulk app notifications with images

o     Promotional push notifications

o     User segmentation for targeted notifications

**6. Credit Acquisition Methods**

Status: Out of scope

**Details:**

o     Complimentary Credits (Subscription-Based)

§  Credits included FREE with subscription plans

**§  Number of free credits varies by subscription tier:**

Ø  Basic Plan: X free valuations per month

Ø  Standard Plan: Y free valuations per month

Ø  Premium Plan: Z free valuations per month

§  Free credits reset monthly (use-it-or-lose-it)

§  Cannot be carried forward to next month

o   Manual Credit Purchase

§  Users can purchase additional credits when free credits exhausted

**§  Purchase options:**

Ø Individual credit purchase (e.g., 1, 5, 10, 50 credits)

Ø  Bulk purchase with discounts

§  Important: Manually purchased credits NEVER expire

§  Payment via platform payment gateway

§ Invoice generated for each credit purchase

7. Revenue Management - What are the revenues to be generated? - All kind of payments received by Partshub need to show in a report with access of filters related to from date and to date and payment type

·         Status: Out of scope

**·         Details:**

**7.1 Revenue Management Fees Including (Platform Fee, Subscription Fee, & Auction Fee)**

o     Revenue Management process is not mentioned in the proposal Document

o     Credit point is also an out of scope requirement.

Comments: Included in the Current Phase

**8. Subscription Management - Okay**

·         Status: Out of scope

**·         Details:**

**8.1 Subscription Discount Logic**

o     Admin-defined discounts for 12-month plans.

o     Custom discount logic for any subscription plan is not mentioned in the scope.

**8.2 Car Valuation & Credit Points**

o     Car valuation feature is out of scope.

o     Credit point system (purchase, spending, admin configuration) is out of scope.

o     Credit-based consumption for valuation check.

o     Credit point expiry management and validity rules.

o     Notifications related to credit balance or purchase prompts for valuation.

o     Alerts for “zero credits while checking valuation”.

o     Automated prompts for credit purchase.

o     Adding or carrying forward credit balance from old plan to upgraded plan.

o     Credit adjustments during plan changes.

**8.3 Analytics Dashboard Based on Subscription Levels**

o     Subscription-based visibility of dashboard components (blurring, masking, partial visibility).

o     Configurable dashboards or component-level access control.

o     Custom analytics widgets per subscription plan.

P.S. Only static dashboard is in the scope and user can access through the subscription plan.

**8.4 Prorated Billing Adjustments During Upgrade**

o     Calculating remaining amount from old plan tenure.

o     Automatically applying remaining value as a discount in the new plan.

**8.5 Subscription Grace Period Handling**

o     Grace period configuration by admin.

o     10-day post-expiry access, renewal reminders, or restricted access logic.

**8.6 Custom Subscription Plans**

o     Admin creating custom subscription plans for individual customers.

o     Assigning custom plans to specific users.

o     Custom plans outside the standard pricing table.

Comments: Included in the current scope

9. Insurance User Management - Need to make the structure - How to register and how to add is -> Insurance company will onboard them self and once they get registered in to the system they will be able to add users

·         Status: Out of scope

**·         Details:**

9.1 Insurance User Management as, Insurance user will register themselves as a claim officer, not as a company

o     Invite user by email

o     Assign role (basic role structure)

o     Activate / deactivate users

o     Claim owner inside insurance

o     Ability to reassign claims between users

**9.2 Per-User Subscription Model**

o     Insurance subscriptions are per user, not per company

**o     During subscription purchase:**

§     Insurance selects number of users

§     Pricing = Plan price × number of users - User count base subscription plan

o     All User will be under same subscription

Comments: This is not mentioned in the current scope but after discussion with Sandeep, we are including this

**10. Comment Box / Chat Box (Insurance<->Garage) Related To Claim - Okay**

·         Status: Out of scope

**·         Details:**

**10.1 Insurance asking clarification questions to garage before approval**

o     Structured clarification chat per RFQ

o     Logged inside RFQ timeline

Comments: This is not mentioned in the proposal but after discussion with Sandeep, we are including this

**11. Independent Operation Between Garage & Supplier - Okay**

·         Status: Out of scope

**·         Details:**

**11.1 Garage To Supplier Operation**

o     Garage to Supplier RFQ

o     Quote Selection and Order Processing from Garage

o     Payment to supplier

**11.1 Supplier To Garage Operation**

o     Garage RFQ Visibility

o     Quote Send based on RFQ

o     Payment update

PS: Process will be same as Insurance and Garage Operation.

Comments: This is not mentioned in the proposal but after discussion with Sandeep, we are including this

**12. Inventory Stock Management - Okay**

·         Status: Out of scope

**·         Details:**

**12.1 Inventory(Insurance & Garage) Stock Management**

o     Maintain own stock

o     Use own stock in claims

o     Decide If Use Existing Stock or Buy From Supplier

o     Stock Update based on Purchase(Stock up) and Sell(Stock Down)

Comments: Included in the current phase.

**13. Alternate / Substitute Parts Approval**

·         Status: Out of scope

**·         Details:**

**13.1 Alternate / Substitute Parts Approval**

o     Supplier proposing alternate parts inside system for the parts RFQ was sent

o     OEM parts may not be available always

Comments: Need to  discuss this with Sandeep

**14. Plan Wise Network Garages - Not needed**

·         Status: Out of scope

**·         Details:**

**14.1 Network Garage Addition Based on Insurance Plan**

o     Network Garages will be added to the system.

o     But the network garages to be added based on the insurance plan.

o     When a Claim request will be created with giving the insurance number, system should automatically fetch the network garages based on the insurance plan.

**15. Favourite Garage & Supplier - Okay**

·         Status: Out of scope

**·         Details:**

**15.1 Mentioning Garage & Supplier As Favourite By Insurance Company**

o     There will be option to make garage and supplier as favourite.

o     When insurance company will send Claim RFQ, favourite list will come at the top

Comments: Included in the current scope

**16. Eligibility vs Preference Separation - Detail requirement required**

·         Status: Out of scope

**·         Details:**

**16.1 Eligibility vs Preference Separation While Sending Claim RFQ**

**o     Clear distinction between:**

§     Eligibility (hard rules)

§     Preference (favorites, ranking)

o     Eligibility rules as hard filters

o     Preferences as soft ordering

o     Favorites do override eligibility

Comments: Included in the current scope

**17. Optional Pre-Purchase Supplier Part Inspection (Paid Service)**

·         Status: Out of scope

**·         Details:**

**17.1 Pre-Purchase Inspection Trigger (Mandatory Option)**

o     Before any supplier order or payment is finalized, the system must present a mandatory decision checkbox to the user (Insurance & Garage) : ☑ Request Part Inspection before Proceeding

**17.2 Who Can Request Inspection?**

o     Insurance company (primary authority)

o     Garage (when purchasing parts directly from suppliers)

This option is user-driven, not automatic, and applies per supplier order.

**17.3 Inspection Scope Selection**

**o     Inspection can be requested:**

§     ✔ For All Parts in the Order

§     ✔ For Selected Items Only (e.g., engine, gearbox, high-value parts)

**17.4 Inspection Execution (PartsHub-Managed)**

o     The system creates an Inspection Task

o     Task is assigned to PartsHub-approved inspectors (mechanic, electrical, specialist, etc.)

o     Inspector physically visits the supplier location

o     Supplier has no control over inspection outcome

Inspection is conducted before payment or order confirmation.

**17.5 Inspection Execution (PartsHub-Managed)**

o     The system creates an Inspection Task

o     Task is assigned to PartsHub-approved inspectors (mechanic, electrical, specialist, etc.)

o     Inspector physically visits the supplier location

o     Supplier has no control over inspection outcome

**17.6 Inspection Responsibility & Trust Model**

o     Inspection is done only by PartsHub team or PartsHub-assigned professionals

o     Supplier cannot self-certify

o     Garage / Insurance does not inspect themselves

o     Inspection findings are recorded directly in the system

**17.7 Inspection Outcome & Status Update**

**Each inspected part (or inspection batch) must have a clear status:**

o     Inspection Requested

o     Inspection In Progress

o     Inspection Passed

o     Inspection Failed

**The inspection result must include:**

o     Pass / Fail

o     Inspector Notes

o     Optional Photos Or Remarks

**17.8 Post Inspection Decision Control**

**After Inspection us completed:**

o     Insurance / Garage reviews inspection result

**o     User can then decide to:**

§     Proceed with purchase

§     Cancel purchase

§     Replace item with alternative part

§     Choose another supplier

**17.9 Inspection Fee Model**

**Fee Characteristics:**

o     Small fixed fee (example: AED 20–25 per inspection)

o     Fee charged: Per Parts Or Per Inspection Visit

**Billing Rules:**

o     Fee applies only if inspection is requested

o     Fee is non-refundable once inspection is completed

o     Fee shown clearly before confirmation

o     Fee added as a separate line item in billing

**17.10 Payment & Order Flow Impact**

**If Inspection Is Requested:**

o     Order/payment is placed in “Pending Inspection” state

o     Payment is blocked until inspection is completed

**o     After inspection:**

§     User explicitly confirms to proceed

§     Payment flow resumes

**If Inspection is not requested:**

o     Order proceeds normally

**17.11 Audit & Compliance Requirements**

o     Who requested inspection

o     Inspection scope (all items / selected items)

o     Inspector assigned

o     Inspection date & time

o     Inspection result

o     Fee charged

o     Final user decision
