## 45. Onboarding Verification Checklist — Full Concept

Source: `Created_Docs/Created Docs/OnboardingVerificationChecklist.docx`

Reference concept for the admin's onboarding verification UI with per-field checklist approach (vs single Approve/Reject decision).


PARTSHUB

AI-Driven Broker Platform

Onboarding Verification Checklist

Concept, UI/UX Reference & User Stories

| Document Version | 1.0 |
|---|---|
| Project | PartsHub |
| Prepared by | Navsoft Developers (Navigators Software Pvt. Ltd.) |
| Stakeholder | Vinoth Martin (Product Owner) |
| Date | April 2026 |
| Status | Draft for Review |

**1. Executive Summary**

The Onboarding Verification Checklist is the field-level review interface presented to the Platform Administrator when a new Insurance Company, Garage, or Supplier submits their registration for approval on PartsHub. Instead of simply displaying the submitted data and offering a single Approve/Reject decision, the checklist allows the admin to review and mark each individual field or document as Verified (✓), Flagged with a query (!), or Rejected (✗) before making the overall application decision.

This concept was introduced during the PartsHub discovery sessions (Jan–Mar 2026) and evolved through multiple weekly meetings. Vinoth Martin (Product Owner) confirmed on 10 April 2026 that the checklist format for the admin-side upload and review process would be defined and shared by him. The UI design visible in the Figma screen (uploaded 17 April 2026) shows the checklist in its implemented form — a sectioned view where each field within Company Information, Trade License, VAT Information, and Contact Information carries its own set of three action icons, and the overall progress is tracked as 'X / 9 validated' at the top.

| 💡  Why a Per-Field Checklist Instead of a Simple Approve/Reject?A single Approve/Reject decision gives the admin no way to communicate which specific field or document triggered the rejection.Registrants would receive vague rejection emails and waste time re-uploading correct documents alongside ones that were already fine.The checklist approach allows the admin to: (a) approve correct fields immediately, (b) flag specific fields for clarification via 'Request More Info', and (c) reject specific fields that are outright invalid — before making the overall application decision.This directly maps to Vinoth's requirement that users know exactly what to fix on resubmission, reducing back-and-forth and accelerating onboarding. |
|---|

**2. UI/UX Reference — Figma Screen Analysis**

The uploaded Figma screen (17 April 2026) shows the Admin-side registration review page for 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC' — a Garage entity submitted on 14 Apr 2026 from Dubai. The screen reveals the following design and functional details:

**2.1 Page Header**

Company avatar (initials 'AG' in a circle) + Company Name + submission metadata: 'Submitted 14 Apr 2026 · Garage · Dubai'.

Progress indicator: '0 / 9 validated' shown as a horizontal progress bar below the header — dynamically updates as the admin marks items.

**2.2 Section Structure**

The page is divided into four collapsible sections, each carrying a 'Pending' badge on the right until all fields within are actioned:

| Section | Fields Contained |
|---|---|
| COMPANY INFORMATION | Company name, Company address |
| TRADE LICENSE | Trade license number, Licensing authority, City / Emirate, Trade license document (PDF link) |
| VAT INFORMATION | VAT number, VAT certificate (PDF link) |
| CONTACT INFORMATION | Contact details (phone + email combined) |

**2.3 Per-Field Action Icons**

**Every field row displays three icon buttons on the right side:**

| Icon | Action | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| ✓ (Tick) | Verify / Approve | Marks this specific field as verified. The field row gains a green visual state. The progress counter increments. |
| ! (Exclamation) | Flag / Query | Flags this field for clarification. Opens an input to type a specific query message that will be sent to the applicant as part of 'Request More Info'. The field row gains an amber visual state. |
| ✗ (Cross) | Reject Field | Marks this specific field as rejected. Opens an input for the rejection reason for this field. The field row gains a red visual state. |

**2.4 Documents**

Document fields (e.g., Trade license document, VAT certificate) display as a clickable PDF link in blue/purple text, allowing the admin to open and review the document inline or in a new tab before marking its action icon.

**2.5 Overall Decision Buttons**

**Three primary action buttons appear at the bottom of the page:**

| Button | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| Approve application | Approves the entire application. Enabled only when all 9 fields have been marked with ✓. On confirm: status → Approved, user receives welcome email, dashboard access granted. |
| Request more info | Sends a structured message to the applicant containing all fields marked with ! and their associated query messages. Status → 'Additional Information Requested'. Admin can send without having resolved ✓ or ✗ items. |
| Reject application | Rejects the entire application. Can be triggered at any point. A mandatory overall rejection reason must be entered. Status → Rejected. Rejection email sent with per-field rejection details. |

**3. Concept & Business Rules**

**3.1 Application Lifecycle States**

A registration application moves through the following states, driven by admin actions on the checklist:

| Status | Description |
|---|---|
| Pending | Application submitted by user. Admin review not yet started. All checklist fields show no action. |
| In Review | Admin has opened the application and started marking checklist items. Some fields may be ✓, !, or ✗. |
| Additional Information Requested | Admin has clicked 'Request more info'. Applicant receives field-specific queries and must respond/resubmit. |
| Approved | All 9 fields marked ✓. Admin clicked 'Approve application'. User granted platform access. |
| Rejected | Admin clicked 'Reject application'. Rejection email sent with field-level reasons. Applicant may resubmit. |

**3.2 Validation Progress Counter**

The '0 / 9 validated' counter at the top of the page tracks how many fields have been marked ✓ (verified).

Fields marked ! or ✗ do not count toward the validated total — they represent unresolved items.

The 'Approve application' button is disabled until the counter reaches its maximum (all fields ✓).

The counter updates in real time as the admin actions each field.

If the admin reverses a ✓ (clicks the tick icon again to un-mark), the count decrements.

**3.3 Section-Level Status Badge**

Each section (Company Information, Trade License, VAT Information, Contact Information) displays a status badge in its header: 'Pending', 'Partial', or 'Verified'.

'Pending' = no fields in the section have been actioned yet.

'Partial' = some but not all fields in the section have been actioned.

'Verified' = all fields in the section have been marked ✓.

**3.4 Document Review**

Document fields (Trade License PDF, VAT Certificate PDF) display as clickable links. The admin must open and review the document before marking its icon.

The system does not enforce that the document was opened before the icon is clicked, but Vinoth noted that admins are expected to manually verify license details against the UAE licensing authority website.

Future phase: SIGNY (signzy.com) API integration will automate trade license verification. OCR confidence score ≥ 80% = auto-pass; < 60% = flagged for manual review. If API is unavailable, application routes to manual admin review.

**3.5 Request More Info Flow**

When the admin marks one or more fields with ! and clicks 'Request more info', the system compiles a structured message listing each flagged field and its associated query.

The applicant receives this as an email and sees it as a banner on their login screen: 'Additional Information Requested. The following items need your attention: [list]'.

The applicant can edit only the flagged fields (✗ fields are locked, ✓ fields remain read-only) and resubmit. The admin is then re-notified.

The status label reads 'Additional Information Requested' (not 'Required') — Vinoth specifically corrected this wording on 10 April 2026.

**3.6 Checklist Applies to All Three Entity Types**

The same checklist structure applies to Insurance Company, Garage, and Supplier registrations.

The field set varies by entity type. Garages and Suppliers include Vehicle Speciality & Brand fields not present for Insurance companies.

The UI layout, icon behaviour, progress counter, section badges, and decision buttons are identical across all three entity types.

**4. Checklist Fields by Entity Type**

| Section / Field | Insurance  \|  Garage  \|  Supplier |
|---|---|
| COMPANY INFORMATION |  |
| Company name | ✓  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| Company address | ✓  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| TRADE LICENSE |  |
| Trade license number | ✓  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| Licensing authority | ✓  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| City / Emirate | ✓  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| Trade license document (PDF) | ✓  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| VAT INFORMATION |  |
| VAT number (15-digit TRN) | ✓  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| VAT certificate (PDF) | ✓  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| CONTACT INFORMATION |  |
| Contact details (Phone + Email) | ✓  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| VEHICLE SPECIALITY & BRANDS (Garage/Supplier only) |  |
| Vehicle types | ✗  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| Specialities | ✗  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| Origin countries | ✗  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |
| Brands | ✗  \|  ✓  \|  ✓ |

**5. Use Case Reference**

| Field | Details |
|---|---|
| Use Case ID | UC-OB-004 |
| Use Case Name | Admin Approve/Reject Onboarding Applications — Verification Checklist |
| Primary Actor | Platform Administrator |
| Secondary Actor | Insurance Company / Garage / Supplier (Applicant) |
| Goal | Review submitted registration fields item by item, communicate specific queries or rejections, and make an overall application decision. |
| Pre-conditions | A user has submitted a registration application with status 'Pending'. Admin is logged in with 'Manage Onboarding' permission. |
| Post-conditions | Application status is set to Approved, Additional Information Requested, or Rejected. Applicant is notified with field-level detail. |
| Related Use Cases | UC-OB-001/002/003 (User Registration), US-INS-001-004 (Admin Receives Notification), US-INS-001-005 (Admin Approve/Reject), US-INS-001-006 (User Resubmits) |
| Discovery Source | Vinoth Martin — PartsHub discovery sessions Jan–Mar 2026. Wording correction ('Additional Information Requested') confirmed 10 April 2026. Figma design confirmed 17 April 2026. |

**6. User Stories**

US-OVC-001 — Admin Reviews Registration Using the Verification Checklist

| 📋  StoryAs a Platform Administrator, I want to review each field of a submitted registration application individually — marking each as Verified, Flagged, or Rejected — so that I can give the applicant specific, field-level feedback before making the overall application decision. |
|---|

Acceptance Criteria

When a new registration is submitted, the admin receives an in-app notification (bell badge increment) and an email with subject 'New [Insurance/Garage/Supplier] Registration — [Company Name]' containing a 'Review Now' link. The admin also sees the pending application in the relevant tab (Insurance / Garage / Supplier) under Admin > Manage [Entity].

Clicking on an application row opens the Verification Checklist page for that application. The page header displays: the company avatar (initials in a circle), Company Name, and submission metadata ('Submitted [Date] · [Entity Type] · [City]'). Below the header, a progress bar shows 'X / [Total] validated', where X starts at 0 and increments as the admin marks fields ✓. The total varies by entity type (9 for Insurance/Garage core fields; more for entities with speciality sections).

The page body is divided into labelled sections (Company Information, Trade License, VAT Information, Contact Information — plus additional sections for Garage/Supplier). Each section header shows the section name on the left and a status badge on the right: 'Pending' (amber), 'Partial' (blue), or 'Verified' (green).

Each field row within a section displays: the field label (left), the submitted value (centre), and three icon buttons (right): ✓ Verify, ! Flag, ✗ Reject. Document fields display the submitted value as a clickable file link (e.g., trade_license_2024.pdf) that opens the document in a new tab.

Clicking ✓ (Verify) on a field: the row background changes to light green, the ✓ icon becomes filled/active, and the progress counter increments by 1. Clicking ✓ again on an already-verified field reverses the action — row returns to default state and counter decrements.

Clicking ! (Flag) on a field: a text input appears inline below the field row with placeholder 'Type your query for this field...'. The admin types a specific query (e.g., 'Trade license number does not match the uploaded document — please clarify'). The row background changes to light amber. The ! icon becomes active. The query text is saved when the admin clicks elsewhere or tabs away. The field can still be actioned again — clicking ✓ after ! replaces the flag with a verification and clears the query.

Clicking ✗ (Reject) on a field: a text input appears inline below the field row with placeholder 'Reason for rejecting this field...'. The admin enters a rejection reason (required, minimum 5 characters). The row background changes to light red. The ✗ icon becomes active. A rejected field cannot be marked ✓ directly — the admin must click ✗ again to un-reject first.

The three overall decision buttons at the bottom of the page behave as follows: 'Approve application' is enabled only when all fields are marked ✓ (progress counter = total). 'Request more info' is enabled at any point when at least one field is marked !. 'Reject application' is enabled at any point.

Gherkin Scenarios — US-OVC-001

Feature: US-OVC-001 — Admin Reviews Registration Verification Checklist

**Background:**

Given the Platform Administrator is logged in with 'Manage Onboarding' permission

And a Garage registration for 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC' has been submitted

And the application status is 'Pending'

Scenario: Admin opens the registration checklist and sees the initial state

When the admin clicks the notification or the application row

**Then the Verification Checklist page opens showing:**

Company avatar 'AG', Company Name 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC',

and metadata 'Submitted 14 Apr 2026 · Garage · Dubai'

And a progress bar shows '0 / 9 validated'

And four sections are visible: COMPANY INFORMATION, TRADE LICENSE,

VAT INFORMATION, CONTACT INFORMATION

And each section header shows a 'Pending' badge

And every field row shows three icon buttons: ✓, !, ✗

And the 'Approve application' button is disabled

Scenario: Admin verifies a field using the tick icon

Given the admin is on the checklist for 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC'

When the admin clicks ✓ on the 'Company name' row

Then the 'Company name' row background changes to light green

And the ✓ icon becomes active (filled)

And the progress bar updates to '1 / 9 validated'

And the COMPANY INFORMATION section badge changes to 'Partial'

Scenario: Admin reverses a verification

Given the 'Company name' row is already marked ✓

And the progress bar shows '1 / 9 validated'

When the admin clicks ✓ again on the 'Company name' row

Then the row returns to its default (unactioned) state

And the progress bar reverts to '0 / 9 validated'

And the COMPANY INFORMATION section badge returns to 'Pending'

Scenario: Admin flags a field with a query

Given the admin suspects the Trade license number may be incorrect

When the admin clicks ! on the 'Trade license number' row

**Then a text input appears below the row with placeholder:**

'Type your query for this field...'

And the row background changes to light amber

And the ! icon becomes active

**When the admin types:**

'Trade license number does not match the uploaded document — please clarify'

And clicks elsewhere to save

Then the query text is saved against the field

And the progress bar does NOT increment (! does not count as validated)

Scenario: Admin opens and reviews the trade license document

Given the 'Trade license document' row shows 'trade_license_2024.pdf' as a link

When the admin clicks the file link

Then the PDF opens in a new browser tab

And the admin can review the document before marking the icon

Scenario: Admin rejects a specific field

Given the admin has reviewed the VAT certificate

And the VAT certificate document appears expired

When the admin clicks ✗ on the 'VAT certificate' row

**Then a text input appears with placeholder:**

'Reason for rejecting this field...'

And the row background changes to light red

When the admin enters 'VAT certificate has expired. Please upload a current certificate.'

and saves

Then the rejection reason is saved against the VAT certificate field

And the ✗ icon becomes active

And the progress bar does NOT increment

Scenario: Admin cannot approve until all fields are verified

Given 8 of 9 fields have been marked ✓

And 1 field is still unactioned

Then the 'Approve application' button remains disabled

And hovering shows a tooltip: 'All fields must be verified before approving'

Scenario: All fields verified — Approve application becomes enabled

Given all 9 fields have been marked ✓

And the progress bar shows '9 / 9 validated'

And all four section badges show 'Verified'

Then the 'Approve application' button becomes enabled (green)

US-OVC-002 — Admin Approves a Registration Application

| ✅  StoryAs a Platform Administrator, I want to approve a fully verified registration application — so that the applicant is granted platform access and notified with a welcome email. |
|---|

Acceptance Criteria

The 'Approve application' button is only active when all checklist fields are marked ✓ and the progress counter equals the total field count. If the admin attempts to click 'Approve application' when fields remain unverified, the button is visually disabled and shows a tooltip: 'All fields must be verified before approving.'

Clicking 'Approve application' when enabled shows a confirmation dialog: 'Approve registration for [Company Name]? The applicant will receive a welcome email and be granted platform access.' with 'Confirm' (green) and 'Cancel' buttons.

On Confirm: the application status changes to 'Approved', the applicant's account status changes to 'Active', the applicant receives a welcome email with their login link and a 'Get Started' button, the application row moves from the Pending tab to the Active tab in the admin's listing, a success toast shows: '[Company Name] has been approved and notified.', and the admin action is logged in the audit trail with Admin name, timestamp, and action 'Approved'.

Gherkin Scenarios — US-OVC-002

Feature: US-OVC-002 — Admin Approves a Fully Verified Application

**Background:**

Given all 9 checklist fields for 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC' are marked ✓

And the progress bar shows '9 / 9 validated'

And all section badges show 'Verified'

Scenario: Admin approves the application

When the admin clicks 'Approve application'

**Then a confirmation dialog appears:**

'Approve registration for Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC?

The applicant will receive a welcome email and be granted

platform access.'

And 'Confirm' (green) and 'Cancel' buttons are shown

When the admin clicks 'Confirm'

Then the application status changes to 'Approved'

And 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC' receives a welcome email with login link

And the application row moves to the Active tab

And a success toast shows: 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC has been approved and notified.'

**And the action is logged in the audit trail:**

Admin: [Admin Name], Action: Approved, Timestamp: [timestamp]

Scenario: Approve button is disabled when fields remain unverified

Given 7 of 9 fields are ✓ and 2 remain unactioned

When the admin views the decision buttons

Then the 'Approve application' button is visually disabled (grey)

And hovering shows tooltip: 'All fields must be verified before approving'

When the admin clicks the disabled button

Then no action occurs and no dialog appears

US-OVC-003 — Admin Requests More Information from Applicant

| ❓  StoryAs a Platform Administrator, I want to request more information from an applicant by flagging specific fields with query messages — so that the applicant knows exactly which fields need clarification and can resubmit targeted corrections. |
|---|

Acceptance Criteria

The 'Request more info' button is enabled whenever at least one field is marked ! (Flagged). It is available regardless of how other fields are marked — the admin can send a partial review while still working through the checklist.

Clicking 'Request more info' shows a pre-send summary dialog listing all fields currently marked !: 'You are requesting clarification on [N] field(s): [Field 1] — [Query], [Field 2] — [Query]...' with 'Send Request' and 'Cancel' buttons.

On Send: the application status changes to 'Additional Information Requested' (not 'Required' — wording confirmed by Vinoth, 10 April 2026). The applicant receives an email titled 'Additional Information Requested — [Company Name]' listing each flagged field and its associated query message. A web notification is also sent to the applicant. The application row reflects the new status badge. A success toast shows: 'Information request sent to [Company Name].' The admin action and the query details are logged in the audit trail.

When the applicant logs in after a 'Request more info' action, a banner appears: 'Additional Information Requested. The following fields need your attention: [list].' Only the flagged fields (!) are editable on resubmission. Fields already marked ✓ are shown as read-only with a green badge. Fields marked ✗ are shown in red with the rejection reason.

On applicant resubmission, the admin is re-notified and the application status reverts to 'Pending' (or 'In Review'). The checklist is re-presented with the original admin markings visible — the admin can review the updated values and change their per-field markings accordingly.

Gherkin Scenarios — US-OVC-003

Feature: US-OVC-003 — Admin Requests More Information on Flagged Fields

**Background:**

Given the admin has opened the checklist for 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC'

**And has marked 'Trade license number' with ! and query:**

'License number does not match the uploaded document — please clarify'

**And has marked 'VAT number' with ! and query:**

'VAT number format is invalid — must be a 15-digit TRN beginning with 100 or 101'

Scenario: Admin clicks 'Request more info' and sees the pre-send summary

When the admin clicks 'Request more info'

**Then a pre-send summary dialog appears showing:**

'You are requesting clarification on 2 field(s):'

'Trade license number — License number does not match the uploaded document'

'VAT number — VAT number format is invalid'

And 'Send Request' and 'Cancel' buttons are shown

Scenario: Admin sends the information request

Given the pre-send summary dialog is open

When the admin clicks 'Send Request'

Then the application status changes to 'Additional Information Requested'

**And 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC' receives an email titled:**

'Additional Information Requested — Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC'

And the email lists both flagged fields and their query messages

And an in-app notification is sent to the applicant

And a success toast shows: 'Information request sent to Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC.'

And the action is logged in the audit trail with all query details

Scenario: 'Request more info' button is disabled when no fields are flagged

Given no fields have been marked with !

Then the 'Request more info' button is disabled

And hovering shows: 'Flag at least one field with ! before requesting more info'

Scenario: Applicant sees flagged fields on resubmission

Given the applicant logs in after receiving the information request

**Then a banner shows:**

'Additional Information Requested. The following fields

need your attention: Trade license number, VAT number'

And only 'Trade license number' and 'VAT number' are editable

And other fields that were marked ✓ are displayed read-only with a green badge

When the applicant corrects the two fields and clicks 'Resubmit'

**Then the admin receives a re-notification:**

'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC has resubmitted their application'

And the checklist reopens with previously marked fields visible

And the two resubmitted fields show the new values for re-review

US-OVC-004 — Admin Rejects a Registration Application

| ❌  StoryAs a Platform Administrator, I want to reject a registration application with field-level and overall rejection reasons — so that the applicant receives a clear, actionable rejection message and can correct and resubmit their application. |
|---|

Acceptance Criteria

The 'Reject application' button is available at any point during the review regardless of how many fields have been actioned. The admin does not need to complete the checklist before rejecting.

Clicking 'Reject application' opens a rejection modal with: (a) a summary of any fields already marked ✗ with their rejection reasons, (b) a mandatory 'Overall rejection reason' text area (minimum 10 characters), (c) 'Confirm Rejection' (red) and 'Cancel' buttons.

If no fields were marked ✗, the summary section shows 'No specific fields have been rejected.' The overall reason is still mandatory.

On Confirm Rejection: application status changes to 'Rejected', the applicant receives a rejection email with subject 'Registration Not Approved — [Company Name]' listing all ✗ fields and their reasons plus the overall rejection reason and a 'Resubmit' link. A success toast shows: '[Company Name] registration has been rejected.' The action is logged in the audit trail with all rejection detail.

When the rejected applicant logs in, a banner shows: 'Your registration was rejected. Reason: [overall reason]. [N] specific field(s) need attention.' An 'Edit & Resubmit' button is shown instead of the normal login flow to dashboard.

Gherkin Scenarios — US-OVC-004

Feature: US-OVC-004 — Admin Rejects a Registration Application

**Background:**

Given the admin has reviewed 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC'

**And has marked 'VAT certificate' ✗ with reason:**

'VAT certificate is expired. Upload a valid current certificate.'

And other fields remain unactioned

Scenario: Admin rejects the application

When the admin clicks 'Reject application'

**Then a rejection modal opens showing:**

Fields rejected: VAT certificate —

'VAT certificate is expired. Upload a valid current certificate.'

And a mandatory 'Overall rejection reason' text area is displayed

When the admin enters 'Application cannot proceed due to invalid compliance documents.'

and clicks 'Confirm Rejection'

Then the application status changes to 'Rejected'

**And 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC' receives a rejection email with:**

subject 'Registration Not Approved — Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC'

overall reason: 'Application cannot proceed due to invalid compliance documents.'

field detail: VAT certificate — 'VAT certificate is expired.'

and a 'Resubmit' link

**And a success toast shows:**

'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC registration has been rejected.'

And the action is fully logged in the audit trail

Scenario: Overall rejection reason is mandatory

Given the rejection modal is open

When the admin clicks 'Confirm Rejection' without entering an overall reason

**Then an inline validation error shows:**

'Please provide an overall rejection reason (minimum 10 characters)'

And the rejection is not submitted

Scenario: Rejected applicant sees rejection banner on next login

Given 'Al Ghurair Auto Garage LLC' has been rejected

When the applicant logs in

**Then a banner shows:**

'Your registration was rejected.

Reason: Application cannot proceed due to invalid compliance documents.

1 specific field needs attention.'

And an 'Edit & Resubmit' button is visible instead of the dashboard

When the applicant clicks 'Edit & Resubmit'

Then the registration form opens with the ✗ field (VAT certificate)

highlighted in red with the rejection reason shown below it

And the applicant can replace the document and resubmit

**7. User Story Summary**

| Story ID | Title / Actor / Scope |
|---|---|
| US-OVC-001 | Review Registration Using the Verification Checklist Actor: Platform Administrator Scope: Per-field ✓/!/✗ actions, progress counter, section badges, document review, real-time UI updates |
| US-OVC-002 | Approve a Registration Application Actor: Platform Administrator Scope: Approval gated on 100% verified fields, confirmation dialog, welcome email, status transition to Approved |
| US-OVC-003 | Request More Information from Applicant Actor: Platform Administrator / Applicant Scope: ! flag with queries, pre-send summary, email + notification, applicant resubmission with targeted field editing |
| US-OVC-004 | Reject a Registration Application Actor: Platform Administrator / Applicant Scope: Overall + per-field rejection reasons, mandatory overall reason, rejection email with Resubmit link, login banner |

**8. Open Items & Decisions Pending**

| Item | Detail & Owner |
|---|---|
| Final checklist content | Vinoth committed on 10 April 2026 to share the definitive list of checklist fields and their exact labels. As of this document, the UI screen shows 9 fields for Garage. Confirmation needed for Insurance and Supplier field counts. — Vinoth Martin |
| KYC API integration timing | SIGNY API for trade license auto-verification is agreed for a future phase. Current implementation requires manual admin review. Timeline for Phase 2 integration not yet defined. — Vinoth Martin / Dev Team |
| Vehicle Speciality checklist fields | It is not yet confirmed whether Vehicle Speciality, Brands, and Origin Countries appear as checklist items in the admin review, or only as profile data. — Vinoth Martin |
| Resubmission limit | The number of resubmission attempts allowed before the application is permanently locked has not been defined. US-INS-001-006 references a configurable max. — Vinoth Martin |
| Admin assignment | Whether multiple admins can review the same application simultaneously (and if there is a locking mechanism) is not yet defined. — Product Decision Pending |
| 'Request more info' with ✗ fields | If a field is marked ✗ and another is marked !, can the admin combine both in a single 'Request more info' action, or must they reject the application outright? — Vinoth Martin |

This document is a living specification. All requirements are subject to change pending confirmation by Vinoth Martin (Product Owner, PartsHub). Prepared by Navsoft Developers (Navigators Software Pvt. Ltd.).

## 46. Venue Master — Concept, Business Rules & User Stories

Source: `Created_Docs/Created Docs/VenueMaster_Concept_UserStories.docx`

Conceptualised by Vinoth Martin (Product Owner) during discovery sessions Jan-Mar 2026, slot-blocking confirmed 17-Apr-2026.


PARTSHUB

AI-Driven Broker Platform

Venue Master

Concept, Business Rules & User Stories

| Document Version | 1.0 |
|---|---|
| Project | PartsHub |
| Prepared by | Navsoft Developers (Navigators Software Pvt. Ltd.) |
| Stakeholder | Vinoth Martin (Product Owner) |
| Date | April 2026 |
| Status | Draft for Review |

**1. Executive Summary**

The Venue Master is a centrally managed reference module within the PartsHub platform that defines and controls physical vehicle inspection locations used during the insurance claims workflow. It was conceptualised by Vinoth Martin (Product Owner) during the discovery sessions conducted between January and March 2026, and formally refined with slot-blocking logic confirmed on 17 April 2026.

In the PartsHub claims lifecycle, after an Insurance Officer creates a claim and before sending an RFQ to garages, the officer must schedule a physical vehicle inspection at a defined location — the inspection venue. This venue is not a free-text field; it is a structured master data record managed by the Insurance Admin. The Venue Master module gives administrators the tools to create, configure, and manage these venues, while the platform enforces capacity limits, slot availability, and time slot blocking in real time.

| Why Venue Master Exists |
|---|
| Vehicle insurance claims in the UAE require a physical inspection of the damaged vehicle before any repair or parts quotation can proceed. |
| Without a managed venue system, inspections would be uncoordinated — double-bookings, capacity overruns, and logistical gaps would occur at scale. |
| The Venue Master solves this by treating inspection locations as first-class data entities with capacity enforcement, operating hours, and real-time slot availability — ensuring every claim is tied to a concrete, bookable inspection appointment. |
|  |

**2. Concept & Background**

**2.1 What Is the Venue Master?**

The Venue Master is an Insurance Admin-managed repository of vehicle inspection venues. Each venue record represents a physical location where a damaged vehicle can be brought for inspection before the claims RFQ process begins. A venue can be one of two types:

Garage-linked venue: A venue tied to a registered garage on the PartsHub platform. The garage's address, contact, and operational details are inherited from the garage's profile.

Custom venue: A venue at any physical location not tied to a registered garage — for example, a company yard, an insurance branch office, or a third-party inspection centre.

**2.2 How It Fits Into the Claims Workflow**

**The Venue Master sits between claim creation and RFQ dispatch. The sequence is:**

| Step | Action |
|---|---|
| 1 | Insurance Officer creates a claim (policyholder, vehicle, damage details). |
| 2 | Officer navigates to 'Send RFQ to Garages' — the system prompts venue selection before the garage list appears. |
| 3 | Officer selects a venue from the Venue Master dropdown and picks an available date and time slot. |
| 4 | The slot is immediately blocked at the point the officer moves past the venue page — not at final submission. |
| 5 | Officer selects garages and sends the RFQ. The venue, date, and time slot are attached to the RFQ. |
| 6 | Garages receiving the RFQ can see the venue details (address, contact, Google Maps link) on their RFQ acceptance screen. |
| 7 | If the claim is deleted while in draft, the blocked slot is released immediately. |

**2.3 Slot Blocking Logic (Formally Agreed — 17 April 2026)**

| Key Business Decision — Venue Slot Blocking |
|---|
| Slots are blocked at the point the user navigates past the venue selection page — not at claim submission. |
| On final claim submission, the system validates that the selected slot is still in the future. |
| If a claim is deleted while in draft, the slot is released immediately back to available. |
| This prevents double-booking while allowing the officer to continue completing the claim form without losing their slot. |
|  |

**2.4 Venue Visibility in the Insurance Portal**

The Venue Master is a dedicated menu item in the Insurance User's sidebar navigation (confirmed visible in Figma screens reviewed April 2026). It is accessible to Insurance Admins for management, and to Insurance Officers in read-only mode during claim creation. The venue, date, and time slot selected for a claim appear in the persistent 'Workflow Settings & Venue Details' right panel on the Claim Detail page — visible across all tabs of the claim.

**3. Venue Master — Data Model**

**Each venue record in the Venue Master contains the following attributes:**

| Attribute | Description |
|---|---|
| Venue Name | A unique, descriptive name for the venue (e.g., 'Dubai HQ Inspection Bay', 'Al Quoz Workshop Yard'). |
| Venue Type | Garage-Linked or Custom. Garage-linked venues inherit address and contact from the garage profile. |
| Linked Garage | If Garage-Linked type: the registered garage selected from the platform's garage directory. Read-only after creation. |
| Full Address | Street address, area, city, emirate, country. Auto-populated for garage-linked venues; manually entered for custom venues. |
| Contact Number | Contact number at the venue. Shown to garages on the RFQ acceptance screen. |
| Google Maps Link | URL to the venue location on Google Maps. Auto-generated from address or manually entered. |
| Capacity | The maximum number of vehicles that can be inspected simultaneously. Used for daily capacity enforcement. |
| Operating Hours | Start time and end time of operations (e.g., 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM). Slots outside these hours are unavailable. |
| Slot Duration | Duration of each inspection slot (e.g., 30 minutes or 1 hour). Determines how many slots are generated per day. |
| Status | Active or Inactive. Inactive venues are hidden from the venue selection dropdown during claim creation. |
| Created By | The Insurance Admin who created the venue record. |
| Created On | System-generated timestamp of creation. |

**4. Business Rules**

**4.1 Capacity Enforcement**

A venue's daily capacity is defined as: (Operating Hours Duration ÷ Slot Duration) × Simultaneous Vehicle Capacity.

Example: A venue operating 9 AM–5 PM (8 hours) with 30-minute slots and capacity of 2 vehicles = 32 slots per day.

Once all slots for a given day are booked, that date becomes unavailable for selection.

The calendar picker on the venue selection screen greys out fully-booked dates.

**4.2 Slot Availability**

Available time slots are shown as a list of time ranges (e.g., 9:00 AM – 9:30 AM, 9:30 AM – 10:00 AM).

Slots already booked by other claims appear greyed out and are not selectable.

The officer can only select a slot that is currently available at the time of selection.

If two officers attempt to book the same slot simultaneously, the first to navigate past the venue page wins. The second sees the slot as unavailable when they arrive.

**4.3 Draft Claim Slot Release**

When a claim is in draft state and the user has selected a venue slot, that slot is held (blocked) immediately upon navigation past the venue page.

If the draft claim is deleted by the officer, the slot is released immediately and becomes available for others.

If the draft expires after 30 days without submission, the slot is released automatically.

**4.4 Venue Management Rules**

Only Insurance Admins with the 'Manage Venues' permission can create, edit, activate, or deactivate venue records.

Insurance Officers (non-admin) can only view and select from the venue dropdown during claim creation.

A venue cannot be deleted if it is linked to any existing claim (Draft, Submitted, or beyond). It can only be deactivated.

Deactivating a venue removes it from the dropdown for new claims but does not affect any existing claims already booked at that venue.

Editing the operating hours or slot duration of a venue only affects future slot generation — existing booked slots are not altered.

**5. Use Case Reference**

| Field | Details |
|---|---|
| Use Case ID | UC-CLM-003 |
| Use Case Name | Schedule Vehicle Inspection Venue (Master) |
| Actor | Insurance Admin (venue management), Insurance Officer (venue selection during claims) |
| Goal | Create and manage vehicle inspection venues; book a venue slot as part of the claims RFQ workflow. |
| Pre-conditions | Insurance company is onboarded and active. At least one venue record exists and is Active. |
| Post-conditions | A venue slot is blocked for the selected claim. Garage receives venue details on RFQ acceptance. |
| Related Use Cases | UC-CLM-002 (Send RFQ to Garages), UC-GAR-001 (Garage Receives RFQ) |
| Discovery Source | Vinoth Martin — PartsHub discovery sessions, Jan–Mar 2026. Slot-blocking logic confirmed 17 April 2026. |

**6. User Stories**

US-VM-001 — Create and Manage Inspection Venues (Admin)

| Story |
|---|
| As an Insurance Admin, I want to create and manage vehicle inspection venues in the Venue Master — including venue type, address, capacity, operating hours, and slot duration — so that Insurance Officers have a structured, validated set of inspection locations to book when creating claims. |
|  |

Acceptance Criteria

The Venue Master is accessible from the Insurance User sidebar under the 'Venue Master' menu item. Only users with the Insurance Admin role and 'Manage Venues' permission can create, edit, activate, or deactivate venue records. Insurance Officers accessing this page see a read-only listing with no management actions.

The Venue Master listing page displays a searchable and filterable data table with columns: Venue Name, Venue Type (Garage-Linked / Custom badge), City, Capacity (vehicles), Slot Duration, Operating Hours, Status (Active / Inactive — toggle switch), Created On. Default sort is Created On descending. A search bar allows filtering by Venue Name or City. A filter panel provides: Venue Type (All / Garage-Linked / Custom), Status (All / Active / Inactive). An 'Add Venue' button is visible in the top-right.

Clicking 'Add Venue' opens a creation form with two steps. Step 1: Venue Type Selection — a choice between 'Garage-Linked' (radio) and 'Custom' (radio). Step 2: Venue Details — fields vary by type selection.

For Garage-Linked venues, the form shows: Venue Name (required, text, max 100 characters), Linked Garage (required, searchable dropdown from the platform's active garage directory — selecting a garage auto-populates Address, Contact Number, and a Google Maps link derived from the garage's registered address), Capacity (required, number, minimum 1), Operating Hours (Start Time picker, End Time picker — End must be after Start), Slot Duration (required, dropdown: 15 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, 2 hours). Address, Contact Number, and Google Maps Link are shown as read-only auto-populated fields below the garage selection.

For Custom venues, the form shows: Venue Name (required, text, max 100 characters), Full Address (required, text), Area (optional, text), City (required, dropdown from city master), Emirate (required, dropdown), Country (required, defaults to 'United Arab Emirates'), Contact Number (required, with country code dropdown), Google Maps Link (optional, URL format). Capacity, Operating Hours, and Slot Duration fields are identical to the Garage-Linked form.

An inline slot preview is shown below the Operating Hours and Slot Duration fields: 'This configuration generates X available slots per day.' The count updates in real time as the admin changes the values. Formula: floor((End Time − Start Time in minutes) / Slot Duration in minutes) × Capacity.

Clicking 'Save' creates the venue and it appears immediately in the listing with Status 'Active'. A success toast shows: 'Venue [Venue Name] created successfully.' An error toast shows if the venue name already exists: 'A venue with this name already exists. Please use a unique name.'

Clicking any venue row in the listing opens a read-only detail panel showing all venue attributes. An 'Edit' button (pencil icon) at the top-right of the panel activates edit mode. All fields except Venue Type (radio) and Linked Garage (if already linked to a claim) are editable. Editing Operating Hours or Slot Duration shows a warning banner: 'Changes to hours or slot duration affect future slot generation only. Existing booked slots are not changed.'

Each venue row in the listing has an Activate/Deactivate toggle switch. Toggling to Inactive shows a confirmation dialog: 'Deactivating this venue will remove it from the venue selection dropdown for new claims. Existing claims at this venue are not affected. Continue?' On confirm, the Status badge changes to Inactive (grey) and the venue disappears from the officer's venue dropdown. Toggling back to Active shows a confirmation: 'Reactivate this venue?' On confirm, the venue reappears in the dropdown.

Attempting to delete a venue that is linked to any claim (in any status) shows an error: 'This venue is linked to [N] claim(s) and cannot be deleted. Deactivate it instead.' Venues with zero claim links can be permanently deleted after a confirmation dialog. Permanent deletion removes all historical slot generation data for that venue.

Gherkin Scenarios — US-VM-001

Feature: US-VM-001 — Insurance Admin Creates and Manages Venue Master Records

**Background:**

Given the Insurance Admin is logged in with 'Manage Venues' permission

And the user navigates to Venue Master from the sidebar

Scenario: Admin views the Venue Master listing page

When the admin opens the Venue Master page

Then a data table is displayed with columns: Venue Name, Venue Type,

City, Capacity, Slot Duration, Operating Hours, Status, Created On

And an 'Add Venue' button is visible in the top-right

And a search bar and filter panel are available above the table

Scenario: Admin creates a Garage-Linked venue

When the admin clicks 'Add Venue'

And selects 'Garage-Linked' as the Venue Type

And enters Venue Name 'Dubai Central Inspection Bay'

And selects garage 'Precision Auto Works' from the Linked Garage dropdown

Then the Address, Contact Number, and Google Maps Link are

auto-populated from 'Precision Auto Works' profile

When the admin sets Capacity to 3, Operating Hours 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM,

and Slot Duration to 30 minutes

Then the slot preview shows: 'This configuration generates 48 available slots per day.'

When the admin clicks 'Save'

Then the venue appears in the listing with Status 'Active'

And a success toast shows: 'Venue Dubai Central Inspection Bay created successfully.'

Scenario: Admin creates a Custom venue

When the admin clicks 'Add Venue' and selects 'Custom'

And enters: Venue Name 'Al Quoz Company Yard', Address '14th Street Al Quoz',

City 'Dubai', Emirate 'Dubai', Contact '+971 4 XXX XXXX'

And sets Capacity to 5, Operating Hours 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Slot Duration 1 hour

Then slot preview shows: 'This configuration generates 50 available slots per day.'

When the admin clicks 'Save'

Then the venue 'Al Quoz Company Yard' appears in the listing with Status 'Active'

Scenario: Admin cannot create a venue with a duplicate name

Given a venue named 'Dubai Central Inspection Bay' already exists

When the admin attempts to save a new venue with the same name

**Then an error toast shows:**

'A venue with this name already exists. Please use a unique name.'

And the venue is not saved

Scenario: Admin deactivates an active venue

Given venue 'Al Quoz Company Yard' has Status 'Active'

And it is linked to 2 existing claims

When the admin toggles the Status switch to Inactive

**Then a confirmation dialog shows:**

'Deactivating this venue will remove it from the venue selection

dropdown for new claims. Existing claims at this venue are not

affected. Continue?'

When the admin clicks 'Confirm'

Then 'Al Quoz Company Yard' Status changes to Inactive (grey badge)

And the venue no longer appears in the officer's venue dropdown

And the 2 linked claims continue to show 'Al Quoz Company Yard' as their venue

Scenario: Admin cannot delete a venue linked to a claim

Given venue 'Dubai Central Inspection Bay' is linked to 5 claims

When the admin attempts to delete the venue

**Then an error message shows:**

'This venue is linked to 5 claim(s) and cannot be deleted.

Deactivate it instead.'

And the venue is NOT deleted

Scenario: Insurance Officer cannot manage venues

Given the logged-in user has the Insurance Officer role (not Admin)

When the officer navigates to Venue Master

Then the listing is visible in read-only mode

And no 'Add Venue', 'Edit', 'Delete', or Status toggle controls are visible

US-VM-002 — Book a Venue Slot During Claim Creation (Officer)

| Story |
|---|
| As an Insurance Officer, I want to select an inspection venue and book a time slot as part of the RFQ workflow — so that the damaged vehicle's inspection is scheduled at a concrete location before the RFQ is sent to garages, and the slot is held immediately to prevent double-booking. |
|  |

Acceptance Criteria

When the Insurance Officer clicks 'Send RFQ to Garages' from the Claim Detail page (available when claim status is 'Submitted'), the system opens the RFQ flow which begins with a Venue Selection screen before presenting the garage list.

The Venue Selection screen shows: a 'Select Venue' dropdown listing all Active venues from the Venue Master (ordered alphabetically, with Venue Name and City shown for each entry), a Date picker (current date and future dates selectable; past dates are greyed out and disabled), and a Time Slot selector which becomes active only after a venue and date are selected.

Upon selecting a venue and date, the Time Slot selector displays all slots generated by that venue's configuration for the chosen date as a list of time ranges (e.g., '09:00 AM – 09:30 AM', '09:30 AM – 10:00 AM'). Slots already booked by other claims appear greyed out and cannot be selected. Available slots appear as selectable buttons. If all slots for the selected date are fully booked, the date is disabled in the picker and a message shows: 'No slots available on this date. Please select another date.'

The moment the officer clicks 'Next' to proceed from the Venue Selection screen to the Garage Selection screen, the selected slot is immediately blocked in the system. No other officer can book the same slot for any other claim from this point forward.

If the officer navigates back from the Garage Selection screen to the Venue Selection screen, the previously selected slot is released and becomes available again. A warning banner shows: 'Navigating back will release your current slot. You will need to re-select a slot.'

The selected venue, date, and time slot are displayed as a read-only summary card on the Venue Selection screen and carried through to the RFQ confirmation step. On the Claim Detail page, they appear permanently in the 'Workflow Settings & Venue Details' right panel.

On final RFQ submission, the system validates that the selected slot date and time are still in the future. If the slot time has already passed (officer took too long on subsequent steps), an error shows: 'Your selected time slot has passed. Please go back and select a new slot.' The slot is released and the officer is returned to the Venue Selection screen.

If the claim draft is deleted by the officer after a slot has been blocked, the slot is released immediately and returned to available for other bookings.

If the claim draft expires (reaches 30 days without submission), the blocked slot is automatically released by the system.

Gherkin Scenarios — US-VM-002

Feature: US-VM-002 — Insurance Officer Books a Venue Slot During Claim RFQ

**Background:**

Given the Insurance Officer is logged in

And a claim with status 'Submitted' exists for Claim # JKLMN56780

And at least one Active venue exists in the Venue Master

Scenario: Officer views the Venue Selection screen when initiating RFQ

When the officer clicks 'Send RFQ to Garages' on Claim # JKLMN56780

Then the system opens the RFQ flow starting with the Venue Selection screen

And a 'Select Venue' dropdown lists all Active venues alphabetically

with Venue Name and City for each entry

And a Date picker shows current and future dates (past dates greyed out)

And the Time Slot selector is disabled until a venue and date are selected

Scenario: Officer selects a venue and views available time slots

Given the officer is on the Venue Selection screen

When the officer selects venue 'Dubai Central Inspection Bay'

And selects the date 22-Apr-2026

Then the Time Slot selector activates and shows all 30-minute slots

from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM as selectable buttons

And slots already booked by other claims appear greyed out and unselectable

And available slots appear as selectable teal buttons

Scenario: Officer books an available time slot

Given the officer has selected venue 'Dubai Central Inspection Bay'

And date 22-Apr-2026 is selected

When the officer clicks the slot '10:00 AM – 10:30 AM'

Then the slot is highlighted as selected

**And a summary card shows:**

Venue: Dubai Central Inspection Bay

Date: 22-Apr-2026

Time Slot: 10:00 AM – 10:30 AM

When the officer clicks 'Next' to proceed to garage selection

Then the slot '10:00 AM – 10:30 AM' on 22-Apr-2026 is immediately blocked

And no other officer can book this slot for any other claim

Scenario: Fully booked date is unavailable for selection

Given all slots for 23-Apr-2026 at 'Dubai Central Inspection Bay' are booked

When the officer selects 'Dubai Central Inspection Bay' as venue

Then 23-Apr-2026 appears greyed out in the date picker

**And hovering over 23-Apr-2026 shows a tooltip:**

'No slots available on this date. Please select another date.'

Scenario: Officer navigates back — slot is released

Given the officer has selected venue, date, and slot

And has clicked 'Next' to proceed to the garage selection screen

When the officer clicks 'Back' to return to the Venue Selection screen

**Then a warning banner appears:**

'Navigating back will release your current slot.

You will need to re-select a slot.'

When the officer confirms navigation

Then the slot '10:00 AM – 10:30 AM' on 22-Apr-2026 is released

And the slot becomes selectable again for other officers

Scenario: Time slot has expired before final RFQ submission

Given the officer selected slot '10:00 AM – 10:30 AM' on 22-Apr-2026

And the officer spent significant time on subsequent steps

And the current time is now 10:45 AM on 22-Apr-2026

When the officer reaches the final RFQ confirmation screen and clicks 'Submit'

Then the system validates the slot and detects it is now in the past

**And an error shows:**

'Your selected time slot has passed.

Please go back and select a new slot.'

And the officer is returned to the Venue Selection screen

And the expired slot is released from the system

Scenario: Draft claim deleted — slot is released immediately

Given a draft claim has a slot '2:00 PM – 2:30 PM' on 24-Apr-2026 blocked

At 'Al Quoz Company Yard'

When the officer deletes the draft claim

**Then a confirmation dialog shows:**

'Permanently delete this draft claim? This action cannot be undone.'

When the officer confirms deletion

Then the draft claim is permanently deleted

And the slot '2:00 PM – 2:30 PM' on 24-Apr-2026 at 'Al Quoz Company Yard'

is immediately released and becomes available for other bookings

Scenario: Draft claim expires after 30 days — slot auto-released

Given a draft claim was created 30 days ago with slot '11:00 AM – 11:30 AM'

blocked at 'Dubai Central Inspection Bay' on a now-past date

When the system's nightly expiry job runs

Then the draft claim is soft-deleted

And any blocked slot associated with it is released

Scenario: Venue and slot details appear on Claim Detail page

Given the officer has completed the venue selection and submitted the RFQ

When the officer or any authorised user opens Claim # JKLMN56780 detail page

**Then the 'Workflow Settings & Venue Details' panel on the right shows:**

Venue: Dubai Central Inspection Bay

Date: 22-Apr-2026

Time Slot: 10:00 AM – 10:30 AM

And this panel is visible across all tabs of the claim detail page

US-VM-003 — Garage Views Venue Details on RFQ Acceptance

| Story |
|---|
| As a Garage User, I want to see the inspection venue address, contact number, and a Google Maps link on the RFQ acceptance screen — so that I know where the vehicle inspection will take place and can navigate to the location. |
|  |

Acceptance Criteria

When a Garage User opens an incoming RFQ from the RFQ list, the RFQ Detail screen includes an 'Inspection Venue' section displaying: Venue Name, Full Address, Contact Number, and a clickable Google Maps link that opens the location in Google Maps in a new browser tab.

The Inspection Venue section appears between the claim summary and the Accept/Reject action buttons. It is always visible for claim-linked RFQs (it does not appear for Direct RFQs which are not claim-linked).

If the venue is a Garage-Linked type, the displayed address and contact match the garage's registered details on the platform. If the venue is a Custom type, the admin-entered address and contact are displayed.

The inspection date and time slot are shown alongside the venue: 'Inspection Date: [DD/MM/YYYY]', 'Time Slot: [HH:MM AM – HH:MM AM]'. These are read-only and cannot be changed by the garage.

After the garage accepts the RFQ, the venue details remain visible on the RFQ detail screen as a reference for the garage's own scheduling.

Gherkin Scenarios — US-VM-003

Feature: US-VM-003 — Garage Views Venue Details on RFQ Acceptance Screen

**Background:**

Given an RFQ has been sent to 'Precision Auto Works' for Claim # JKLMN56780

**And the claim has an inspection venue booked:**

Venue: Dubai Central Inspection Bay

Address: 14th Street, Al Quoz Industrial, Dubai

Contact: +971 4 XXX XXXX

Date: 22-Apr-2026, Time Slot: 10:00 AM – 10:30 AM

Scenario: Garage User views venue details on the RFQ detail screen

Given the Garage User is logged in at 'Precision Auto Works'

When the user opens the incoming RFQ for Claim # JKLMN56780

**Then the RFQ detail screen displays an 'Inspection Venue' section showing:**

Venue Name: Dubai Central Inspection Bay

Address: 14th Street, Al Quoz Industrial, Dubai

Contact: +971 4 XXX XXXX

Inspection Date: 22-Apr-2026

Time Slot: 10:00 AM – 10:30 AM

And a 'View on Google Maps' link is visible and clickable

Scenario: Google Maps link opens in a new tab

Given the garage user is viewing the RFQ detail screen

When the user clicks the 'View on Google Maps' link

Then Google Maps opens in a new browser tab

And the map is centred on the venue address

Scenario: Venue details remain visible after RFQ acceptance

Given the garage user has accepted the RFQ for Claim # JKLMN56780

When the user views the RFQ detail screen post-acceptance

Then the Inspection Venue section is still visible with all venue details

And the details are read-only — no edit option is presented to the garage

Scenario: Inspection Venue section does not appear for Direct RFQs

Given the garage user opens a Direct RFQ (not linked to an insurance claim)

When the user views the Direct RFQ detail screen

Then no 'Inspection Venue' section is displayed

And the screen shows only the parts request and supplier details

**7. User Story Summary**

| Story ID | Title / Scope / Actor |
|---|---|
| US-VM-001 | Create and Manage Inspection Venues Actor: Insurance Admin Scope: Venue Master CRUD, capacity configuration, slot duration, operating hours, activate/deactivate |
| US-VM-002 | Book a Venue Slot During Claim Creation Actor: Insurance Officer Scope: Venue selection in RFQ flow, real-time slot display, slot blocking on navigation, release on draft deletion, expiry validation |
| US-VM-003 | Garage Views Venue Details on RFQ Acceptance Actor: Garage User Scope: Inspection venue display (address, contact, Maps link, date, time slot) on RFQ detail screen |

**8. Open Items & Decisions Pending**

| Item | Detail & Owner |
|---|---|
| Venue capacity per slot vs. per day | Current definition is 'simultaneous vehicles per slot'. Confirm if capacity should also be enforced as a daily maximum separate from per-slot capacity. — Vinoth Martin |
| Slot buffer time | Should a buffer time between consecutive slots be configurable (e.g., 15 min gap after each 30-min slot for cleaning/prep)? — Vinoth Martin / Dev Team |
| Multi-venue per claim | Can a single claim have more than one inspection venue (e.g., a follow-up at a different location)? Current design assumes one venue per claim. — Vinoth Martin |
| Slot cancellation by admin | Can an admin cancel a booked slot from the Venue Master view? If so, what happens to the linked claim? — Product Decision Pending |
| Notification to garage on venue update | If the venue, date, or time slot changes after RFQ is sent, does the garage receive a notification? Behaviour not yet defined. — Vinoth Martin / Dev Team |
| Venue Master access for Branches | Should Branch-level Insurance Users be able to create venues, or is this HQ Admin only? — Vinoth Martin |

This document is a living specification. All requirements are subject to change pending client confirmation by Vinoth Martin (Product Owner, PartsHub). Prepared by Navsoft Developers.

## 47. Insurance Dashboard — Full Specification

Source: `Shared_Docs/Shared Docs/Dashboard.docx`


Insurance Dashboard – Purpose

**1.Top KPI Summary Cards (Always Visible)**

KPIs to show (Subscription Based)

| KPI | What it shows | Why needed |
|---|---|---|
| Total Active Claims | Claims currently in progress | Immediate workload visibility |
| New Claims (Today / This Week) | Fresh incoming claims | Ops monitoring |
| Claims Awaiting Action | Stuck or pending claims | Prevent delays |
| Claims with Garages | Claims under garage assessment | Track bottlenecks |
| Claims with Suppliers | Parts sourcing stage | Cost & time risk |
| Completed Claims | Successfully closed | Throughput health |

**2. Latest / Priority Claims Table – Should show High value & long pending claims**

| Field | Why |
|---|---|
| Claim ID | Unique reference |
| Policy Holder | Identify customer |
| Policy Number | Validation |
| Claim Date | SLA tracking |
| Current Status | Action awareness |
| Assigned Owner | Accountability |
| Next Action Required | Clear guidance |
| Priority / SLA Flag | Escalation |

Smart Logic

**Highlight:**

SLA breached

High-value claims

Long pending claims

Click row → claim details

**3. Quick Actions (CTA Section)**

Buttons

➕ Create New Claim

📤 Assign / Reassign Claim

🏭 Invite Garage

📦 Send RFQ to Supplier

🔍 View All Claims

📊 Go to Analytics (if enabled)

Why

Reduces navigation friction

Speeds up daily operations

**4. Subscription & Usage Awareness (Subtle)**

What to show

Current plan

Users used vs allowed

Claims / RFQs used (if capped)

Feature locks (Upgrade)

Important

Never block silently

Always explain why something is locked

**5. Insurance Dashboard – Auctions & Hot Deals (Sell-Focused)**

What to show

• Active auctions created• Auctions with bids• Auctions sold• Active hot deals• Orders from hot deals

Important

• Read-only snapshot• Clicking any metric redirects to Auction or Hot Deal module• Visibility controlled by role and subscription

Garage App Dashboard – Detailed Specification

**1. Top KPI Summary Cards**

The following KPIs must be displayed at the top of the dashboard. Visibility is controlled by role and subscription plan.- New Claim Requests – Incoming requests from insurance companies- Accepted Claims – Jobs confirmed by the garage- Pending Acceptance – Claims awaiting accept/reject decision- In-Progress Repairs – Active repair jobs- Waiting for Parts – Repairs blocked due to parts unavailability- Completed Jobs – Finished repairsEach KPI must be clickable and redirect to a filtered job list.

**2. Claim / Job Status Pipeline**

A visual pipeline showing the flow of jobs across stages:- New Request- Accepted- Inspection / Assessment- Waiting for Parts- Repair in Progress- Completed- Invoiced / PaidThis helps garages instantly identify bottlenecks and stalled jobs.

**3. Action Required & Alerts**

This section highlights items requiring immediate attention.Examples:- Claims pending acceptance- Inspection required- Jobs waiting for parts- Supplier delivery delayed- Invoice pending submissionAlerts must be actionable and clickable.

**4. Quick Actions**

Frequently used actions must be easily accessible.- Accept / Reject Claim- Submit Parts List- Request Parts from Supplier- Raise Invoice- View All Jobs- Rate Supplier / Insurance (if enabled)

**5. Subscription & Usage Awareness**

Displays current subscription usage information.- Current plan- Active jobs allowed vs used- RFQs used- Locked features with upgrade promptsSystem must never silently block actions due to plan limits.

**6. Auctions & Hot Deals Snapshot (If Enabled-Selling)**

A brief overview of alternative selling channels.• Auctions created• Auctions sold• Revenue from auctions• Hot deals created• Orders from hot dealsThis encourages supplier participation in platform-led sales.

**7. Auctions & Hot Deals Snapshot (If Enabled-Buying)**

• Active auctions participated in• Bids placed• Auctions won• Active hot deals viewed• Orders from hot deals

Behaviour

• Selling and Buying metrics displayed as separate sections or tabs• Read-only snapshot with drill-down navigation• Visibility controlled by role and subscription

Supplier App Dashboard – Detailed Specification

Dashboard Purpose

The Supplier Dashboard is the supplier’s daily operational command center. It provides real-time visibility into incoming RFQs, orders to be fulfilled, delivery progress, payment status, and inventory pressure. The dashboard is action-driven and operational, not analytical.

**1. Top KPI Summary Cards**

The following KPIs must be displayed at the top of the dashboard. Visibility is controlled by role and subscription plan.- New RFQs – Newly received requests from insurers or garages- RFQs Pending Response – Quotes not yet submitted- Quotes Submitted – RFQs already responded to- Orders Confirmed – Orders placed based on accepted quotes- Orders In Progress – Orders being packed or shipped- Orders Delivered – Successfully delivered ordersEach KPI must be clickable and redirect to a filtered RFQ or order list.

**2. RFQ to Order Status Pipeline**

A visual pipeline showing the lifecycle of RFQs and orders:- RFQ Received- Quote Submitted- Quote Accepted- Order Confirmed- Packed / Ready to Ship- Dispatched- Delivered- Invoiced / PaidThis helps suppliers quickly identify bottlenecks and delayed actions.

**3. Financial Snapshot (High-Level)**

Only summary-level financial information should be shown.- Amount invoiced (current period)- Amount received- Amount pending- Average payment cycle timeNo accounting, tax, or ledger-level details should appear here.

**4. Auctions & Hot Deals Snapshot (If Enabled-Selling)**

A brief overview of alternative selling channels.• Auctions created• Auctions sold• Revenue from auctions• Hot deals created• Orders from hot dealsThis encourages supplier participation in platform-led sales.

**5. Auctions & Hot Deals Snapshot (If Enabled-Buying)**

• Active auctions participated in• Bids placed• Auctions won• Active hot deals viewed• Orders from hot deals

Behaviour

• Selling and Buying metrics displayed as separate sections or tabs• Read-only snapshot with drill-down navigation• Visibility controlled by role and subscription

**6. Quick Actions**

Frequently used supplier actions must be easily accessible.- View RFQs- Submit Quote- Update Order Status- Mark Order as Dispatched- Raise Invoice- View Ratings & Reviews

**7. Subscription & Usage Awareness**

Displays current subscription usage and limits.- Active subscription plan- RFQs allowed vs used- Order limits (if applicable)- Locked features with upgrade promptsSystem must never silently block actions due to plan restrictions.

Master matrix of features that are subscription-gated, with counter/limit type per feature.


| User Type | Feature Module | Feature / Capability | Counter / Limit (if any) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Insurance | Dashboard | Dashboard Access | Subscription-dependent |
|  | Claim Request | Create claim request | Number of claims per plan |
|  |  | Upload documents (police report, photos, videos) | Number of uploads per claim / per plan |
|  | RFQ to Garage | Send RFQ to selected garages | RFQ count limit per plan |
|  | RFQ to Supplier | Send RFQ to supplier(s) | RFQ count limit per plan |
|  | Order | Place orders, view order details | Order limits per plan |
|  | Auction | Create auctions, manage items | Auction creation limit per plan |
|  | Hot Deals | Create hot deals | Number of hot deals per plan |
| Garage | Dashboard | Access dashboard based on subscription | Subscription-dependent |
|  | Claim RFQ | View, accept/reject claims | RFQ responses per plan |
|  |  | Submit Quotations | Number of quotations per plan |
|  | Auction | Participate in auctions, place bids | Auction bids per plan |
|  | Hot Deals | View and purchase from hot deals | Hot deals per plan |
|  | Inventory | Add/edit/delete inventory items | Inventory items per plan |
|  | Purchase Parts | Send RFQ to supplier(s) | RFQ count limit per plan |
|  |  | Place orders, view order details | Order limits per plan |
| Supplier | Dashboard | Access dashboard based on subscription | Subscription-dependent |
|  | Claim RFQ | View, accept/reject claims | RFQ responses per plan |
|  |  | Submit Quotations | Number of quotations per plan |
|  | Inventory | Add/edit/delete inventory items | Inventory items per plan |
|  | Auction | Participate in auctions, place bids | Auction bids per plan |
|  | Hot Deals | View hot deals from insurers | Hot deals per plan |

## 50. Use Case Analysis Document

Source: `__Requirement/Use Case Analysis Partshub.docx`

Pre-SRS use case enumeration grouped by stakeholder.


Use Case Analysis

Website Login & Signup Access

Website Login & Signup Access

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-AUTH-001 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how a visitor accesses the system from the website homepage by logging in as an existing user or signing up as a new user (Insurance Company, Garage, or Supplier). It includes homepage access, login, signup role selection, and forgot password flow. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Website and homepage must be live.User must have an existing approved account to log in.Signup feature must be enabled for all three categories (Insurance, Garage, Supplier). |  |  |
| Triggers: | Visitor opens the website and sees the homepage with “Login” and “Sign Up” buttons |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Homepage Access:Visitor navigates to the website.System displays the homepage with Login and Sign Up buttons at the top-right corner.Existing User Login:Visitor clicks Login.System displays Login form with fields: Email ID, Password, and “Forgot Password”.User enters valid Email ID and Password.System authenticates the credentials.If successful and account is approved → User is redirected to their system.New User Signup:Visitor clicks Sign Up.System displays Role Selection Page with three options:Insurance CompanyGarageSupplierVisitor selects one role.System redirects the visitor to the appropriate registration form for that role.User fills the registration form and submits.System stores the data and triggers the onboarding/approval workflow (based on role). |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: Incorrect Login CredentialsUser enters wrong email or password.System displays “Invalid credentials”.User retries.A2: Forgot PasswordUser clicks Forgot Password.System shows “Enter registered email” input.User enters the email.System sends password reset link to email.User clicks the link and creates a new password.User returns to login screen and signs in.A3: User Attempts Login but Not Yet ApprovedUser enters valid email/password but account is pending admin approval.System displays: “Your account is pending approval. You will be notified once approved.”User cannot enter system. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Login Successful:User is redirected to their system according to their role.Signup Successful:User registration is created, awaiting admin reviewForgot Password Completed:User has a valid new password |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Unregistered Email in Login:User enters an email not found in system.System displays: “Email not registered. Please sign up.”EX-2: Email Already Exists During Signup:User enters email already registered.System prevents registration and displays: “This email is already registered. Please login.” |  |  |
| Assumptions: | User type based home page exist for Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users.Admin approval is mandatory for onboarding users (e.g., Insurance Company, Garage, Supplier).Same email cannot be used across users.Password reset emails always reach the registered mailbox. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Homepage design will include a clear CTA for Login/Signup in top navigation.User type selection is mandatory for signup because each user has a different registration details.Authentication should follow secure standards. |  |  |

Insurance

Insurance Company & Branch Registration & Approval

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-001 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes the end-to-end registration and approval process for insurance companies and their individual branches, covering both sales-team-initiated registration and self-registration. It includes document submission, parent–branch mapping, admin verification, approval/rejection, resubmission, and system access enablement. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Registration page and onboarding system must be active.Licensing Authority dropdown list must exist (provided by client).Sales team should have sent invitation links manually.Branches must have valid individual Trade Licenses and VAT certificate. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Insurance company or branch gets a sales team manual invitation to registerORInsurance company or branch starts self-registration using the public onboarding link. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Actor opens the registration link (via sales invitation or website self-registration).Actor enters Personal Details: Name, Email, and Contact Number.Actor enters Company Details:Company NameTrade License NumberLicensing Authority (dropdown)Uploads Trade License CopyUploads VAT CertificateActor specifies whether the entity is Head Quarter (HQ) or Branch.If registered as Branch:System displays a dropdown list of available HQ companies.Actor selects the parent HQ.If HQ not available in the dropdown, actor proceeds and may update it later after approval.Actor submits the registration form.System sends web and email notifications to Admin about new registration.Admin reviews the submitted details and documents.Admin manually verifies the license using the Licensing Authority website.Admin chooses to Approve or Reject the registration.If approved:System activates the insurer’s account and grants access to relevant modules.If rejected:Admin enters a rejection reason.System notifies the insurer through email notification with the reason and allows them to edit details and resubmit. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: Branch cannot find Parent HQ in dropdownActor selects "Branch" but HQ not listed in dropdown.Actor continues registration without selecting parent HQ.After approval, actor can update the parent HQ later from their profile settings.A2: User receives Sales Team InvitationSales team sends onboarding invitation link to insurer.Insurer opens pre-generated registration link.User proceeds with normal registration workflow. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | If Approved:The insurer or branch can log in and use all authorized modules.If Rejected:Insurer profile remains inactive until corrected and resubmitted.System stores complete audit logs of registration, approval, rejection, and resubmission. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Uploaded documents invalid or unreadableAdmin marks document as invalid.System rejects application with instructions to upload correct documents.EX-2: Licensing Authority verification unavailableAdmin unable to validate license due to website downtime.Admin marks verification as pending.System keeps insurer in “Pending Review” state until manual verification completes.EX-3: Duplicate Registration AttemptActor attempts to register using an email or company name already registered.System alerts the user and blocks duplicate registration. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Branch and HQ registrations are treated independently due to separate Trade Licenses.Parent-child mapping does not influence approval decision; each entity follows the same approval workflow.Admin's manual license verification method is outside system automation.Notification service (email + web alerts) is configured correctly.Sales team will be able to send the invitation to the insurance company outside the system only. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Insurance companies cannot create branches from their portal; only branch users self-register.The HQ list must be maintained accurately by Admin to ensure proper parent-branch mapping.Approval workflow is mandatory; no user can access the system without Admin approval. |  |  |

Insurance Claim Request Creation & Management

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-002 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case covers the end-to-end process for insurance users to create, edit, publish, preview, search, and manage claim requests after receiving accident details. It includes uploading required documents, recording policyholder details, providing damage information, and managing claim statuses. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Insurance user must have an approved and active account.Accident details (photos, videos, witness information, etc.) are available to be uploaded.Claim status master list will be provided by client and configured in system. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Insurance user receives accident information from customer or Police and initiates a new claim request based on their inspection. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Insurance user logs into the system.User navigates to Claim Request section.User clicks Create New Claim.System displays the Claim Form containing fields for:Policyholder NamePolicy NumberContact InformationDamage DescriptionWitness detailsDate of ClaimReason for ClaimClaim StatusUser uploads mandatory documents:Police ReportPhotosVideosWitness detailsUser uploads additional supporting documents using “Add More Documents”.User reviews the entered information in Preview/Draft Mode.User clicks Publish / Submit Claim.System saves the claim request and assigns a unique Claim ID.Claim enters the default status (client will define exact status list, e.g., “Submitted”, “Under Review” based on clients status details).System shows confirmation message with Claim ID. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: User Edits Claim Before PublishingUser clicks Edit while reviewing in Preview Mode.System returns user to form.User makes corrections and updates uploads.User saves and publishes again.A2: User Saves Claim as DraftUser decides not to submit immediately.User clicks Save as Draft.System stores the claim in “Draft” status.User can later open the draft, edit, and publish.A3: Search & Filter ClaimsUser navigates to Claim List page.User enters keywords, Claim ID, Policy Number, or Policyholder Name.User applies filters (status, date range, user, policy number, etc.).System displays matching claims. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | A new claim request is successfully created and stored in system.Claim has a unique Claim ID and a valid status.All uploaded documents are stored in secure document storage.Claim becomes available for further processing (internal review, sending RFQ to garages, etc.). |  |  |
