| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Required Documents MissingUser attempts to publish without uploading mandatory documents.System displays error: “Police Report, Photos, and Videos are required to submit the claim.”User must upload missing documents before proceeding.EX-2: Invalid or Corrupted FilesUser uploads an unreadable or unsupported file.System rejects the file and prompts for a valid file format.EX-3: Policy Number Not FoundAfter creating of a new claim, if policy number is seen as not valid, claim will get cancelled. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Insurance companies follow internal guidelines for claim creation; system does not auto-validate accident authenticity.Claim status workflow may include many stages (e.g., Submitted → Reviewing → RFQ Sent → Approved), but final list will be provided by client.Large files (videos/images) must follow platform size limits. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Preview feature ensures user validates all fields before final submission.Claim records should be searchable by multiple parameters for operational efficiency.Claim editing allowed only until certain workflow stage (if required, can be defined later).System should maintain full audit logs for create/edit/publish actions. |  |  |

Send RFQ to Garages After Claim Creation

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-003 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how an insurance user selects one or multiple garages (network or independent) and sends an RFQ (Request for Quotation) for a created claim. Garages receive notifications and can later respond with quotations. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 19/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | A claim must already be created and available in the System.Insurance user must be logged in with required permissions.Network and independent garages must be registered and active in the platform.Claim has necessary information and attachments to request a quote. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Insurance user opens a submitted claim and selects the option to Send RFQ to Garages. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Insurance user opens the Claim Details Page.User clicks Send RFQ to Garage(s).System displays a list of eligible garages (Network + Independent).User manually selects one or multiple garages from the list.User confirms the RFQ send action.System generates RFQ associated with the claim.System sends RFQ notifications to selected garages through:Web notificationsEmail notificationsGarages receive RFQ with claim information and required documents.System updates claim status automatically (e.g., "RFQ Sent to Garages").System logs RFQ actions and stores timestamp and list of garages selected. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: RFQ Sent to Only One GarageInsurance user selects a single garage.System sends RFQ only to that garage.Same flow continues with status update & logging.A2: RFQ Sent to Multiple Garages SimultaneouslyInsurance user selects multiple garages.System sends the same RFQ details and documents to all selected garages.Garages independently receive notifications and respond later.A3: User Wants to Add More Garages LaterUser returns to the claim and opens the Claim.User clicks on the Send RFQ to Garage(s) to send RFQ to more Garages.User selects new garages and sends RFQs and in the list those garages will not be visible to whom RFQ is already sent.System logs new RFQs. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | RFQ is successfully sent to selected garages.Selected garages receive claim details and notification alerts.Claim status is updated to reflect RFQ dispatch.System logs RFQ action for audit and tracking. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: No Garage SelectedUser attempts to send RFQ without selecting a garage.System displays error: “Please select at least one garage to continue.”No RFQ is sent. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | RFQ details include all necessary documents and information associated with the claim.RFQ timestamps and garage selection history must be maintained for audit. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Garage selection is fully manual by insurance user (no AI or auto-suggestion for now).Network & independent garages must be clearly marked in the selection list. |  |  |

Send RFQ to Suppliers After Parts Finalisation

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-004 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how an insurance user sends RFQs to one or multiple suppliers after finalizing the garage and parts list, receives and compares supplier quotations, manually selects one or more supplier quotes for a claim, and creates orders based on the selected quotations. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 19/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Claim must be created and active.Garage must be selected for the claim.Parts list must be finalized for the claim.Suppliers must be on boarded and active in the system. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Insurance user finalizes parts list and selects Send RFQ to Supplier. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Insurance user opens the Claim Details page.User reviews and confirms the finalized Parts List.User clicks Send RFQ to Supplier.System displays list of eligible suppliers.User manually selects one or multiple suppliers.System sends RFQ notifications to selected suppliers with part details.Supplier receives RFQ notification and opens RFQ.Supplier submits quotation with following details for each part:Part NameUnit PriceTotal CostSystem stores supplier quotations against the claim.Insurance user views all received quotations.System provides quotation comparison view, highlighting lowest price per part.Insurance user manually evaluates and selects one or multiple supplier quotes for the claim.User confirms quote selection.System creates Order(s) based on the selected supplier quotations.System updates claim and order statuses accordingly. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: Parts Sourced from Multiple SuppliersInsurance user selects different supplier quotes for different parts.System creates multiple orders, one per supplier.All orders remain linked to the same claim.A2: RFQ Sent to Single SupplierInsurance user selects only one supplier.Supplier submits quotation.User selects quotation and order is created.A3: Partial Quote SelectionSupplier provides quote for only some parts.User selects quoted parts from one supplier and sources remaining parts from another supplier.System allows mixed supplier selection. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | RFQs are successfully sent to selected suppliers.Supplier quotations are recorded and linked to the claim.One or more supplier orders are created based on selected quotations.Orders are traceable to the originating claim and garage. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: No Supplier SelectedUser attempts to send RFQ without selecting any supplier.System displays error:“Please select at least one supplier to proceed.”EX-2: Supplier Quote Missing Required FieldsSupplier submits quote without unit price or total cost.System rejects submission and prompts supplier to complete required fields.EX-3: No Supplier Quotes ReceivedNo suppliers respond within defined timeframe.Insurance user may resend RFQ or select different suppliers. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Quote evaluation and selection is fully manual due to pricing complexity.Multiple supplier quotations can be selected for a single claim.Order creation is directly dependent on selected quotations.System highlights lowest price but does not auto-select.Supplier availability is determined externally or manually. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Future enhancement may include automated quote ranking, SLA-based supplier selection, or partial automation.Each supplier order should contain clear part-to-claim mapping.Audit trail must capture RFQ sending, quote submission, quote selection, and order creation events. |  |  |

Order Management for Insurance (Supplier & Garage Orders)

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-005 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how insurance users view and manage all orders created for a claim, including supplier (parts) and garage (labour) orders. It covers order listing, filtering, visibility rules (HQ vs branch), detailed order view, billing information, status timeline, reviews & ratings, and replacement/refund workflows. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 19/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Orders must already be created from selected supplier quotations or garage approvals.Insurance user must be logged in with appropriate role (HQ or Branch).Order statuses and workflow are configured as per client-provided list. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Insurance user navigates to the Orders module from the system menu. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Orders List ViewInsurance user opens the Orders module.System displays a consolidated Orders List containing:Supplier Orders (Parts)Garage Orders (Labor)Each order row shows summary fields:Order IDOrder DateSupplier / Garage NameOrder Type (Supplier / Garage)Order StatusSystem enforces visibility rules:HQ users see all orders across branches.Branch users see only orders created for their branch.Part B – Search & Filter OrdersInsurance user applies filters by:Date rangeOrder Type (Supplier / Garage)Supplier / GarageOrder Status (Pending, Confirmed, Dispatched, Delivered, Cancelled)Insurance user searches orders using:Order IDSupplier / Garage NameClaim IDSystem refreshes list with matching results.Part C – Order Details ViewInsurance user clicks on an Order ID.System displays Order Details, including:Order ID & Order DateSupplier / Garage NameContact DetailsBilling AddressShipping AddressSystem displays Item Details:Part NamePart DescriptionQuantityUnit PriceTotal PricePart D – Billing & Status TimelineSystem displays Billing Information:Cost BreakdownTaxesAdditional Charges (e.g., delivery fees)System displays Order Status Timeline, showing chronological status changes (as per client-defined statuses).Part E – Reviews & RatingsAfter order completion, insurance user submits Review & Rating for supplier or garage linked to the claim.System stores the rating and recalculates aggregated rating for that supplier/garage.Part F – Replacement / Refund RequestInsurance user raises a Replacement or Refund Request for defective or incorrect parts.User provides reason and supporting details.System sends request to supplier for approval.Supplier reviews and approves or rejects the request.System updates order status and logs decision. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: View Only Garage OrdersUser applies filter: Order Type = Garage.System shows only labor-related orders.A2: View Only Supplier OrdersUser applies filter: Order Type = Supplier.System shows only parts-related orders.A3: Replacement Instead of RefundSupplier approves replacement request.System updates order with replacement shipment details.Status timeline reflects replacement activity. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Insurance user can fully track supplier and garage orders.All order activities are logged with complete audit history.Reviews and ratings impact aggregated supplier/garage score.Replacement/refund actions are traceable and status-driven. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Unauthorized Order AccessBranch user attempts to access HQ-level or other branch orders.System blocks access and displays authorization error.EX-2: Replacement/Refund without ReasonUser submits request without reason.System prompts: “Reason is mandatory for replacement/refund.”EX-3: Supplier Rejects Replacement/RefundSupplier rejects request with justification.System updates order timeline and notifies insurance user. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Status definitions and transitions will be provided by client.Ratings calculation logic is average-based unless specified otherwise.Replacement/refund workflow applies only to supplier (parts) orders.Garage labour disputes are handled outside this workflow. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Orders module is a central operational dashboard for insurance users.Status timeline improves traceability and dispute resolution.Review & rating feature supports long-term supplier and garage performance evaluation. |  |  |

Garage Management, Claim Invitation & Assignment

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-007 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how insurance companies manage garages on the platform, including linking network garages, viewing a combined garage directory, searching and filtering garages, inviting garages to claims for quotations, assigning claims based on garage quotes, handling repair cost review and payment, and generating garage invoices. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 19/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Insurance user must be logged in with an approved and active account.Garages (network and independent) must be registered or available in the platform.Claim must exist to invite garages.Claim and garage quotation statuses are configured as per client-provided list. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Insurance user navigates to the Garage module to add and manage network garages. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Add & View GaragesInsurance user opens the Garage Management module.User adds Network Garages by entering or selecting garage details (as per platform rules).System displays a combined garage list, including:Network garagesIndependently registered garagesFor each garage, system displays:Garage NameGarage IDSpecializationContact Person & Contact DetailsAddressLicensing DetailsSystem enforces visibility rules:HQ users see all garages across all branches.Branch users see only garages tied to their branch operations.Part B – Search & Filter GaragesInsurance user searches garages using:Garage NameGarage IDSpecializationLocationLicense NumberInsurance user filters garages by:Network / Non-NetworkSpecializationRegionActivity StatusSystem refreshes the list with matching garages.Part C – Claim-Garage AssociationInsurance user opens a Claim Request.System displays all garages already invited or participating in that claim.Insurance user clicks Invite Garages to Claim.User selects one or multiple garages from the garage list.System sends claim invitation notifications to selected garages.Part D – Quotation Review & Claim AssignmentGarages submit quotations for repair/labor costs.Insurance user reviews submitted quotations.Based on quotation evaluation, insurance user selects a garage.System assigns the claim to the selected garage.Claim status is updated accordingly (as per client-defined workflow).Part E – Payment & Invoice HandlingAssigned garage completes repair work and submits repair cost details.Insurance user reviews the submitted repair costs.Insurance user processes payment to the garage.Upon successful payment, system generates Garage Invoice.Invoice is linked to the claim and available for future reference.Part F – Claim SearchInsurance user searches claims using claim status, such as:PendingIn ProgressApprovedCompletedSystem displays matching claim records with assigned garages. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: Invite Multiple Garages but Assign Only OneInsurance user invites multiple garages to a claim.Only one garage is selected based on quotation.Other garage invitations are marked as “Not Selected”.A2: Network Garage PreferenceInsurance user filters garages by Network.Only network garages appear for selection and invitation.A3: Payment DelayedGarage submits repair cost.Insurance user delays payment due to internal review.Invoice is not generated until payment is completed. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Garages are successfully managed and categorized (network / independent).Claims are assigned to selected garages based on quotations.Garage payments and invoices are properly generated and logged.Claim and garage activities are fully traceable. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: No Garage Selected for InvitationInsurance user attempts to invite garages without selection. System displays error: “Please select at least one garage to continue.”EX-2: Branch Access Branch user attempts to view or assign garages outside its scope.Every insurance user can see every garage present in the platform.EX-3: Payment FailurePayment to garage fails.System marks payment as failed and does not generate invoice.User is notified to retry payment. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Garage quotation format and approval rules are standardized.Invoice generation is strictly payment-driven.Claim status lifecycle is defined and configurable by the client.Insurance users manually evaluate garage quotations. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Garage module is tightly integrated with Claims, RFQ, Orders, and Payments.Clear HQ vs Branch access control is critical for governance.This use case can be further split into Garage Master Management, Garage RFQ, and Garage Payment use cases if needed. |  |  |

Branch Management for Insurance Company (HQ Only)

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-008 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how the Insurance Company HQ views and manages its registered branches. It covers branch listing, visibility and access control, operational statistics, branch search, Branch complete visibility, editing and filtering, and subscription independence. Branch creation is strictly handled via branch self-onboarding and not through HQ. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 19/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Insurance HQ user must be logged in with an active and approved HQ account.One or more branches must have completed self-onboarding and been approved by Admin.Branch subscription data must be available in the system. |  |  |
| Triggers: | HQ user navigates to the Branches section from the system menu. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Branch List ViewHQ user opens the Branches module.System displays a list of all branches registered under the insurance company.Each branch record displays:Branch NameBranch IDBranch AddressBranch Manager NameContact InformationPart B – Branch StatisticsHQ user selects a branch.System displays operational statistics for the selected branch (details to be provided by client).Part C – Access ControlSystem enforces role-based access:HQ users can access the Branches module.Branch users do not see the Branches section in the UI and can only access their own data.Part D – Search & Filter BranchesHQ user searches branches using:Branch NameBranch IDBranch Manager NamePhone NumberSystem filters and displays matching branch records.Part E – Edit Branch DetailsHQ user selects Edit Branch.System allows editing of permitted fields such as:Branch AddressBranch Manager NameContact InformationSystem restricts editing of:Subscription detailsLicensing detailsHQ user saves changes.System updates branch details.Part F – Subscription AwarenessHQ user views branch subscription status.System indicates that each branch maintains an independent subscription, separate from HQ. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: Branch Has No Active SubscriptionHQ user views a branch with inactive or expired subscription.System displays subscription status warning.Branch functionality remains limited until renewal (as per business rules).A2: Search Returns No ResultsHQ user searches with unmatched criteria.System displays “No branches found”. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | HQ has complete visibility into all registered branches.Branch data is updated where permitted.Access control rules are strictly enforced.Subscription independence per branch is clearly maintained. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Unauthorized Access AttemptBranch user attempts to access Branches module.System blocks access and hides the Branches section.EX-2: Edit Restricted FieldsHQ user attempts to edit subscription or licensing fields. System prevents action and displays permission error. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Branch operational statistics will be defined and finalized by the client.Branch creation and deletion are out of scope for HQ users.Subscription purchase and renewal are handled at branch level. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Branch self-onboarding ensures regulatory and licensing compliance.HQ’s role is limited to governance and oversight, not branch creation.This use case supports scalability for multi-branch insurance companies. |  |  |

Inventory Management for Insurance (Parts & Vehicles)

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-010 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how insurance users manage inventory items (Parts and Vehicles). It covers adding inventory, maintaining quantity and pricing, listing and editing items, deleting unavailable inventory, and using inventory items to create Auctions and Hot Deals. Inventory access and control are restricted to insurance users only. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 19/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Insurance user must be logged in with an active and approved account.Inventory module must be enabled for the insurance company.Parts and Vehicle master data must already exist in the system (for read-only fields). |  |  |
| Triggers: | Insurance user navigates to the Inventory module from the system menu. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Add Inventory ItemInsurance user clicks Add Inventory Item.System asks user to select Item Type:PartsVehicleIf Parts is selected:System displays part details in read-only mode.User enters:Available QuantityUnit PriceIf Vehicle is selected:System displays vehicle details (as configured).User saves the inventory item.System adds the item to inventory with active status.Note: Any part or vehicle intended for Auction or Hot Deal must first be added to inventory.Part B – Inventory ListingInsurance user opens Inventory List.System displays all inventory items with the following fields:Item NameItem IDItem Type (Vehicle / Parts)Quantity Available (static/manual entry)Status (Available, Used, Inactive, etc.)Part C – Edit Inventory ItemInsurance user selects an inventory item.User edits permitted fields such as:Quantity AvailableUnit PriceStatusUser saves changes.System updates inventory details.Part D – Delete / Remove Inventory ItemInsurance user selects an inventory item no longer available.User clicks Delete / Remove.System removes or marks the item as inactive (as per configuration).Part E – Create Auction / Hot DealInsurance user selects an inventory item.User chooses Create Auction or Create Hot Deal.System validates that the item exists in inventory.System redirects user to Auction or Hot Deal creation flow with item details prefilled. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: Inventory Added for Immediate AuctionUser receives a new part/vehicle.User adds item to inventory.User can create an Auction or Hot Deal from the inventory item.A2: Zero Quantity InventoryUser marks item as unavailable or inactive. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Inventory items are accurately maintained and tracked.Only valid inventory items are used for Auctions or Hot Deals.Inventory reflects correct quantity, pricing, and status. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Unauthorized AccessInsurance user can edit, or delete items from their own inventory only.System will not allow them to edit other insurance user’s inventory.EX-2: Invalid Quantity or PriceUser enters negative or invalid values.System displays validation error and prevents saving.EX-3: Attempt to Create Auction/Hot deal Without InventoryUser attempts to create Auction/Hot Deal without adding item to inventory.System blocks action and prompts user to add inventory first. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Inventory quantity is a manual/static entry and not auto-deducted unless defined later.Read-only item details are sourced from master data.Auction and Hot Deal workflows are handled in separate use cases. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Inventory is the single source of truth for Auctions and Hot Deals.Strict access control ensures data integrity.This use case can be extended later to include automated stock movement and reconciliation. |  |  |

Auction Creation, Bidding & Order Generation

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-011 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how insurance companies create and manage auctions using inventory items, allow suppliers and garages to participate in bidding, maintain bid history, and automatically generate orders for winning bids. It also covers auction item management before go-live and auction order visibility in the Insurance Orders module. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 19/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Insurance user must be logged in with an active and approved account.Inventory must contain Parts or Vehicles intended for auction.Auction configuration and bidding rules are enabled in the system.Suppliers and garages must be registered and active to participate in auctions. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Insurance user selects Create Auction from the Auction module. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Create AuctionInsurance user navigates to the Auction module.User clicks Create New Auction.System displays Auction Creation form with mandatory fields:Auction Name / TitleStart Date & TimeEnd Date & TimeSeller Information (Insurance Company / Branch)Item InformationReserve PriceStarting BidUser selects auction items only from Inventory.User saves the auction in Draft status.Part B – Manage Auction Items (Before Go-Live)Before auction start time, insurance user can:Add items from InventoryEdit item detailsDelete items from auctionSystem allows item changes only while auction status is Draft / Scheduled.User publishes the auction.Part C – Auction Details & BiddingAuction goes live at scheduled start time.System displays Auction Details Page showing:Auction informationLinked items with their current statusSuppliers and garages can view auction and place bids.System records every bid with bidder details, bid amount, and timestamp.System maintains full bid history for audit and transparency.Part D – Auction Closure & Order CreationAuction closes automatically at end date/time.System identifies the highest valid bid for each item.System automatically generates an Order for the winning bid.Order includes:Order AmountBuyer Details (Supplier / Garage)Item InformationPayment DetailsOrder StatusSystem categorizes the order as Auction Order.Auction order appears in the Insurance Orders module under Auction category.Part E – Order VisibilitySystem enforces order visibility rules:HQ users can view all auction orders across branches.Branch users can view only their own auction orders. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: No Bids ReceivedAuction closes with no bids.System marks auction as Closed – No Bids.No order is created.A2: Auction Item Removed Before Go-LiveInsurance user deletes item from Inventory before auction starts.System removes item from auction successfully.System will restrict that auction to get published. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Auction is successfully created and completed.Bid history is preserved for audit.Winning bids result in automatically generated orders.Auction orders are visible in Insurance Orders module. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Attempt to Add Non-Inventory ItemUser attempts to add an item not present in Inventory. System blocks action and displays error:“Auction items must be selected from Inventory.”EX-2: Unauthorized Auction ModificationUser attempts to edit auction after it goes live.System blocks modification. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Bidding rules (increment, extension, etc.) are configurable.Auction orders follow standard order lifecycle.Payment handling is managed post order creation.One order is created per winning item unless otherwise defined. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Auction module is tightly coupled with Inventory and Orders.Full bid audit trail is mandatory for regulatory and dispute handling.This use case can be extended to support multi-item bundled auctions. |  |  |

Hot Deal Management & Order Creation

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-012 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how insurance companies create and manage Hot Deals using inventory items, allow buyers to purchase items at fixed deal prices, and automatically generate orders upon purchase. It also covers Hot Deal item management before go-live and order visibility rules for HQ and branch users. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 19/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Insurance user must be logged in with an active and approved account.Inventory must contain Parts or Vehicles intended for Hot Deals.Hot Deal configuration is enabled in the system.Suppliers and garages must be registered and active buyers. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Insurance user selects Create Hot Deal from the Hot Deals module. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Create Hot DealInsurance user navigates to the Hot Deals module.User clicks Create New Hot Deal.System displays Hot Deal creation form with mandatory fields:Hot Deal NameStart Date & TimeEnd Date & TimeDeal PriceItem InformationUser selects Hot Deal items only from Inventory.User saves the Hot Deal in Draft status.Part B – Manage Hot Deal Items (Before Go-Live)Before Hot Deal start time, insurance user can:Add items from InventoryEdit item detailsDelete items from Hot DealSystem allows item changes only while Hot Deal status is Draft / Scheduled.User publishes the Hot Deal.Part C – Hot Deal Details & PurchaseHot Deal goes live at scheduled start time.System displays Hot Deal Details Page showing:Hot Deal informationLinked items with their statusSupplier or Garage selects an item and purchases at the deal price.Part D – Order Creation & VisibilityUpon successful purchase, system automatically generates an Order.Order includes:Item DetailsItem Type (Parts or Vehicle)Deal PriceBuyer DetailsOrder StatusSystem categorizes the order as Hot Deal Order.Hot Deal orders appear in the Insurance Orders module under Hot Deals category.System enforces visibility rules:HQ users can view all Hot Deal orders across branches.Branch users can view only their own Hot Deal orders. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: Item Sold OutItems related to hot deal got purchased.User will mark that item as unavailable or inactive.A2: Hot Deal Ends Without PurchaseHot Deal end time is reached.System marks Hot Deal as Expired.No orders are created.A3: Item Removed Before Go-LiveInsurance user deletes item from Inventory before Hot deal starts.System removes item from hot deal successfully.System will restrict that hot deal to get published. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Hot Deals are successfully created and executed.Orders are automatically generated for purchased items.Hot Deal orders are tracked and visible in Orders module. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Attempt to Add Non-Inventory ItemUser attempts to add an item not present in Inventory. System blocks action and displays error:“Hot Deal items must be selected from Inventory.”EX-2: Unauthorized Auction ModificationUser attempts to edit Hot Deal after it goes live.System blocks modification.EX-3: Purchase FailureBuyer payment or confirmation fails.System cancels transaction and no order is created. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Hot Deals are fixed-price offers (no bidding).Payment handling is managed through order workflow.One order is created per successful purchase. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Hot Deals complement Auctions for quick liquidation of inventory.Tightly coupled with Inventory ensures item availability control.This use case can be extended to include limited-time discounts, bulk purchases, or auto-expiry notifications. |  |  |

Insurance Subscription Management

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-013 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how an insurance company views available subscription plans, purchases a plan, upgrades or downgrades subscriptions, renews plans, views subscription history, and how the system enforces access control, counters, notifications, and audit logging. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 19/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Insurance user must be registered, approved, and logged in.Admin must have created and published subscription plans.Payment gateway must be active.User must have permission to purchase/manage subscription. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Insurance user navigates to the Subscription module. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View Available Subscription PlansInsurance user opens the Subscription Plans page.System displays a list of Active & Published plans only.Each plan displays:Feature GroupPlan NamePriceBilling CyclePlan BenefitsCountersValidityInactive or unpublished plans are not visible.Part B – Purchase SubscriptionInsurance user selects a subscription plan.User clicks Buy / Subscribe.System redirects user to payment flow.User completes payment using available payment options.System validates payment success.System activates the subscription and sets:Start DateEnd DateCountersStatus = ActiveSystem generates:Payment receiptTransaction IDInvoiceUser receives confirmation notification.Part C – View Current SubscriptionInsurance user navigates to Current Subscription section.System displays:Plan NameStart DateEnd DateCounters (used / remaining)Subscription StatusPart D – Upgrade SubscriptionInsurance user selects Upgrade Plan.System displays higher-tier plans only.User selects a new plan and proceeds with payment.Upon successful payment:New plan becomes active immediatelyPrevious plan is closedSystem updates counters based on upgraded plan.System sends upgrade confirmation notification.Part E – Downgrade SubscriptionInsurance user selects Downgrade Plan.System allows downgrade selection anytime.System schedules downgraded plan to activate after current plan expiry.Current plan remains active until expiry.User receives downgrade scheduling notification.Part F – Renewal SubscriptionInsurance user selects Renew Plan before expiry.User completes payment.Renewed plan is queued.System activates renewed plan automatically after current plan expires.Counters reset (no carry forward).System sends renewal confirmation notification.Part G – Subscription HistoryInsurance user navigates to Subscription History.System displays:Past and current plansPayment statusReceipts and invoicesUsage logsPart H – Access Control & Counters EnforcementInsurance user performs an action tied to subscription counters.System checks remaining counters.If counters are available, action is allowed.If counters are exhausted, system blocks action and displays upgrade/renew message.Unused counters do not carry forward to next cycle.Part I – Notifications & AuditSystem sends notifications for:Plan activationExpiry remindersRenewal confirmationUpgrade / DowngradePayment failureSystem logs all subscription actions in audit trail for compliance. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: Payment FailurePayment fails or is cancelled.Subscription is not activated.System displays failure message and sends notification.A2: Attempt to Use Feature Beyond CountersUser exceeds plan counter limit.System blocks the action.System suggests upgrade or renewal.A3: Expired SubscriptionSubscription reaches end date without renewal.System marks subscription as Expired.System restricts access to subscription-based features.User can renew their subscription through the app or manually pay amount to the Admin, they can activate their plan. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Subscription lifecycle is correctly managed (purchase, upgrade, downgrade, renewal).Access is enforced strictly based on counters and plan validity.Subscription history and invoices are stored.Audit trail is maintained for compliance. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Inactive Plan AccessIf a plan becomes inactive, it is not displayed for new purchases.EX-2: Unauthorized AccessIf user is not permitted or approved, subscription actions are blocked. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Subscription is mandatory to access core platform features.Payment gateway handles financial validation.Counter logic applies uniformly across all subscribed modules.HQ and Branch subscriptions are independent. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Branch subscriptions are separate from HQ subscriptions.This use case supports future enhancements like trial plans, discount coupons, and auto-renewal. |  |  |

My Account – Profile

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-INS-014 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how a logged-in user (Insurance, Garage, or Supplier) can view and update their account profile details, upload or update registration documents, and manage profile changes while ensuring that restricted and unique identifiers remain protected and approval workflows are enforced where required. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 19/11/2025 |
| Actors: | Insurance Company |  |  |
| Preconditions: | User must be registered, approved, and logged in.User account must be active.Admin approval workflow must be enabled for profile updates impacting compliance. |  |  |
| Triggers: | User navigates to My Account from the system menu. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View Profile DetailsUser opens My Account section.System displays all profile details captured during registration, including:Personal detailsCompany detailsContact informationUploaded documentsPart B – Edit Profile DetailsUser clicks Edit Profile.System enables editing for allowed fields only.User updates permitted profile details.System restricts editing of the following fields:Email IDLicense NumberAny other system-defined unique identifiersUser saves profile changes.Part C – Company Type RestrictionUser attempts to change Company Type (Insurance / Garage / Supplier).System blocks direct modification of Company Type.System displays a message stating that company type cannot be changed once the account is created.If business-critical profile details are updated that impact compliance, system flags the profile for Admin Review and Re-Approval before changes take effect.Part D – Admin Approval (If Applicable)If uploaded or edited details require re-verification:System marks profile as Pending Approval.Admin receives notification.Admin reviews updated profile or documents.Admin approves or rejects the changes.System updates profile status accordingly and notifies user. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: Edit Restricted FieldsUser attempts to edit Email ID or License number for an approved account.System disables the field and prevents saving changes. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | User profile is updated with valid, permitted information.Restricted identifiers remain unchanged.Updated documents are stored securely.Admin approval is enforced where required. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Unauthorized Profile AccessIf an unauthenticated user attempts to access My Account, system redirects to login.EX-2: Admin RejectionIf Admin rejects updated details, system restores previous approved profile data and notifies user with rejection reason. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Email ID and License Number are system-wide unique identifiers.Certain profile updates may require regulatory compliance review outside the system.Admin approval workflow is configurable. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | This use case ensures data integrity, regulatory compliance, and controlled profile changes.Future enhancements may include change request tracking and profile version history. |  |  |

Garage

Garage Onboarding & Registration

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-GRG-001 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines the end-to-end process for garage users to register on the platform, complete profile and compliance details, upload mandatory documents, and receive admin approval before gaining access to system functionalities. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Garage User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Garage user is not already registered with the same unique identifiers (Email, Mobile, License).Platform registration is active.Admin approval workflow is enabled. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Garage user clicks Sign Up from the website Garage login page. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Actor opens the registration link (via sales invitation or website self-registration).Actor enters Personal Details: Name, Email, and Contact Number.Actor enters Additional Details:Personal detailsCompany detailsGarage specialityRegistration and licensing detailsBank detailsUploads Trade License CopyUploads VAT CertificateSystem validates mandatory fields & document formats and sizes.Actor submits the registration form.System sends web and email notifications to Admin about new registration.Admin reviews the submitted details and documents.Admin manually verifies the license using the Licensing Authority website.Admin chooses to Approve or Reject the registration.If approved:System activates the Garage’s account and grants access to relevant modules.If rejected:Admin enters a rejection reason.System notifies the Garage through email notification with the reason and allows them to edit details and resubmit.Until approval is granted, garage user:Can log inCannot access operational modules of the platform. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Duplicate Registration AttemptUser attempts to register with an Email ID or Mobile Number already existing in the system.System blocks registration and displays validation error.A2 – Weak PasswordUser enters a password not meeting security standards.System displays password policy message and prevents submission.A3 – Incomplete Profile SubmissionUser submits registration without completing mandatory additional details.System prompts user to complete required fields before submission. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Garage user profile is either approved or rejected with reason.Approved garage users can access the platform.Rejected users can correct details and resubmit. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: System Failure during SubmissionIf system error occurs, registration is not saved and user is prompted to retry.EX-2: Admin Action TimeoutIf admin does not act, registration remains in Pending Approval state. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Email ID and Mobile Number are unique identifiers system-wide.Admin approval is mandatory for all garage registrations.Bank details are required for future payment processing. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Garage users cannot transact or participate in claims until approved.This use case aligns with regulatory and compliance requirements.Future enhancements may include auto-verification of licenses. |  |  |

Garage Claim Request for Quotation (RFQ) Management

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-GRG-002 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how a garage views claim requests sent by insurance companies, reviews claim details, accepts or rejects RFQ requests, prepares and submits repair quotations including parts and labour costs, uploads supporting documents, and receives notifications throughout the quotation lifecycle. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Garage User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.Insurance company must have sent a claim RFQ to the garage.Claim request must be in a valid RFQ state. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Garage user navigates to the Claim Requests / RFQ module or receives a notification for a new claim RFQ. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View Claim RFQ ListGarage user opens the Claim RFQ List.System displays all claim requests sent to the garage.Claim list shows the following details:Claim IDVehicle DetailsInsurance Company NameRequest DateClaim StatusSystem segregates claims based on status (status list to be provided by client).Part B – View Claim DetailsGarage user selects a claim from the list.System displays complete claim details including:Vehicle informationDamage photosRequest notes from insurerDocuments submitted by insurance companyPart C – Accept or Reject Claim RFQGarage user reviews the claim details.Garage user selects one of the following actions:Accept RFQReject RFQIf Rejected:Garage provides rejection reason.System updates claim status and notifies insurer.If Accepted:Claim moves to quotation preparation stage.Part D – Prepare QuotationGarage user adds required parts by selecting parts from the system.Garage enters unit price and quantity for each part.Garage enters Labour Cost for repair services.System calculates total quotation amount (parts + labour).Part E – Upload Supporting DocumentsGarage user uploads supporting documents such as:Estimate filesAdditional damage photosAny relevant repair documentsSystem validates file type and size.Part F – Submit QuotationGarage user reviews the quotation details.Garage user submits the final quotation.System updates claim status to Quotation Submitted.Insurance company receives notification of quotation submission.Part G – Activity Logging & NotificationsSystem logs all garage actions, including:Accept / Reject RFQAdding partsEntering labour costQuotation submissionGarage receives notifications for:New RFQ requestRFQ acceptance or rejectionQuotation selection by insurance |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Partial Quotation Save (Draft)Garage user saves quotation as draft without submission.System retains draft for later editing.A2 – Reject RFQ After ReviewGarage decides not to participate.Garage submits rejection reason.System updates status and notifies insurer. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Quotation is submitted or rejected by the garage.Insurance company can view and evaluate submitted quotations.All quotation actions are logged for audit. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Unauthorized AccessIf garage user attempts to access RFQ not assigned to them, system blocks access.EX-2: Incomplete Quotation SubmissionIf labour cost are missing, system prevents submission and prompts completion.System Error during SubmissionIf system failure occurs, quotation is not submitted and user is notified. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Status list for RFQ lifecycle will be provided by the client.Pricing entered by garage is exclusive of taxes unless specified.Once submitted, quotation can be edited only if insurance allows revision. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | RFQ handling is a critical garage interaction point with insurers.Transparent activity logs ensure auditability.This use case integrates with Claim Assignment and Payment workflows. |  |  |

Garage Auction Participation & Order Management

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-GRG-003 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how garage users view ongoing and upcoming auctions, review auction and item details, pay the required deposit, participate in bidding, track bids, and manage auction orders won through the platform, including payment, refund and notifications. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Garage User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.Auction must be active or scheduled.Deposit fee rules must be configured for auction items.Payment gateway must be available. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Garage user navigates to the Auctions module or receives auction notification. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View AuctionsGarage user opens the Auctions module.System displays all ongoing and upcoming auctions.Auction list displays:Auction TitleStart Date & TimeEnd Date & TimeItem CountReserve PriceAuction StatusPart B – View Auction DetailsGarage user selects an auction.System displays complete auction details including:Item informationAuction rulesReserve priceStart / End timePart C – View Bid DetailsGarage user views:Current highest bidBid historyBid timestampsPart D – Deposit Fee PaymentGarage user selects auction item(s) to bid on.System prompts for deposit fee payment.Garage user completes deposit payment through online payment gateway.System confirms successful deposit payment.Deposit amount is marked as refundable to the original payment source.Part E – Place BidGarage user enters bid amount.System validates that:Deposit payment is successfulBid amount is greater than current highest bidSystem accepts bid and updates bid history.System notifies other bidders (if applicable).Part F – Auction Closure & Order CreationAuction reaches end date & time.System determines highest bidder.System automatically creates an Auction Order for winning bidder.Garage user receives winning confirmation notification.Part G – Order List & DetailsGarage user navigates to Auction Orders.System displays all auction orders won by the garage with:Order IDAuction IDItem NameWinning PriceOrder StatusPayment StatusGarage user selects an order to view details including:Item informationDelivery detailsInvoicePayment requirementsPart H – Order PaymentGarage user completes payment for the auction order via online payment modes.System updates payment and order status accordingly.Part I – NotificationsSystem sends notifications to garage for:Deposit confirmationBid updatesAuction win confirmationPayment reminders |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Bid Lower Than Current Highest BidGarage user places a bid lower than or equal to current highest bid.System rejects the bid and displays validation message.A2 – Deposit Payment FailureDeposit payment fails.System blocks bidding and prompts retry.A3 – Auction LostGarage does not win the auction.Deposit refund is initiated to original payment source (as per refund rules). |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Garage successfully participates in auctions.Winning bids generate orders automatically.Payments and deposits are processed correctly.Notifications keep garage informed at every stage. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Unauthorized Auction Accessif garage is not approved or logged in, auction participation is blocked.EX-2: System Failure During BiddingBid is not recorded and garage is notified to retry. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Deposit fee refund timelines are governed by platform policy.Auction rules are visible to all participants.One auction order is generated per winning item. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Deposit mechanism ensures serious bidders only.This use case integrates tightly with Orders and Payments modules.Can be extended to support auto-bidding in future phases. |  |  |

Garage Hot Deal Purchase & Order Management

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-GRG-004 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how garage users view active hot deals published by insurers, filter and review item details, purchase hot deal items at fixed prices, complete payments, view orders, and how the system maintains audit logs for compliance and tracking. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Garage User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.Hot deals must be active and published by insurers.Inventory must have sufficient available quantity.Approved payment modes must be configured. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Garage user navigates to the Hot Deals module. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View Hot DealsGarage user opens the Hot Deals section.System displays all active hot deals.Hot deal listing shows:Item NameDeal PriceAvailable QuantityStart & End Date/TimeStatus (to be confirmed by client)Part B – Apply Advanced FiltersGarage user applies filters using one or more of the following:Part NamePart CategoryVehicle CompatibilityPrice RangeExpiry TimeHot Deal IDStatusSystem refreshes the hot deal list based on applied filters.Part C – View Hot Deal Item DetailsGarage user selects a hot deal item.System displays complete item details including:Part NamePart IDDeal PriceCompatibility (vehicle models)ConditionAvailable QuantityPart D – Purchase Hot Deal ItemGarage user selects the required Hot Deal.Garage user proceeds to purchase the hot deal.System will show the total hot deal items, their quantity and total price.Garage user confirms the order.Part E – Payment WorkflowSystem redirects the garage user to the payment gateway.Garage user completes payment using approved payment modes.System validates payment success.System automatically generates a Hot Deal Order.Part F – Order Listing & Order DetailsGarage user navigates to Hot Deal Orders.System displays all hot deal orders with:Order IDItem NameDeal PriceOrder DatePayment StatusDelivery StatusGarage user selects an order to view detailed information including:InvoicePayment informationDelivery informationPart G – Audit TrailSystem logs all actions related to hot deals including:Viewing hot dealsPurchase actionsPayment completionOrder creationAudit logs are stored for compliance and tracking purposes. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Insufficient QuantityGarage user selects quantity exceeding availability.System displays validation error and prevents checkout.A2 – Payment FailurePayment fails or is cancelled.Order is not created.System prompts garage user to retry payment. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Successful hot deal purchase results in order creation.Inventory quantity is reduced accordingly.Order appears in garage hot deal order list.Audit trail is maintained. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Unauthorized AccessIf garage user is not approved or logged in, system blocks access to hot deals.EX2 – System Error During TransactionTransaction is rolled back and no order is created. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Hot deal prices are fixed and non-negotiable.Delivery fulfilment is handled separately.Hot deal status values will be finalized by the client. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Hot deals enable fast procurement of parts and vehicles at fixed prices.This use case integrates with Inventory, Orders, and Payments modules.Future enhancements may include bulk purchase limits and expiry reminders. |  |  |

Garage Inventory Management (Parts & Vehicles)

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-GRG-005 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how garage users manage their internal inventory by adding parts and vehicles, maintaining quantity and pricing, viewing inventory listings, editing inventory details, and removing items that are no longer available, with strict access limited to garage users only. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Garage User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.Garage user must have inventory management access enabled. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Garage user navigates to the Inventory module. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Add Inventory ItemGarage user clicks Add Inventory Item.System prompts user to select Item Type:PartsVehicleIf Parts is selected:System displays item details in read-only mode.Garage user enters:Available QuantityUnit PriceIf Vehicle is selected:System displays vehicle details as configured.Garage user saves the inventory item.System adds the item to the garage inventory with an active status.Part B – Inventory ListingGarage user opens Inventory List.System displays inventory items with the following fields:Item NameItem IDItem Type (Parts / Vehicle)Quantity Available (static/manual entry)StatusPart C – Edit Inventory ItemGarage user selects an inventory item.Garage user edits permitted fields such as:Quantity AvailableUnit PriceStatusGarage user saves changes.System updates the inventory record.Part D – Delete / Remove Inventory ItemGarage user selects an inventory item that is no longer available.Garage user clicks Delete / Remove.System removes the item or marks it as inactive (as per configuration).Access ControlSystem ensures that only garage users can add, edit, or delete inventory items of their own except the inventory master details.Any unauthorized user action is blocked. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Invalid Quantity or PriceGarage user enters invalid or negative quantity/price.System displays validation error and prevents saving.A2 – Duplicate Inventory ItemGarage user attempts to add an item already existing in inventory.System displays duplication warning (handling as per configuration). |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Garage inventory is updated accurately.Inventory listing reflects current quantity and status.Removed items are no longer available for use. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Unauthorized AccessIf a non-garage user attempts inventory modification, system denies access.EX2 – System Error During SaveInventory changes are not saved and user is prompted to retry. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Inventory quantity is manually maintained by garage users.Pricing entered is exclusive of taxes unless defined otherwise.Inventory is independent of insurance inventory unless integrated later. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | This inventory is intended for garage operational use.Can be extended to support auto-deduction on job completion in future phases.Access control ensures data ownership and security. |  |  |

Purchase Parts for Garage Inventory

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-GRG-006 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how garage users purchase parts for their own inventory, including inventory validation before purchase, order placement, order tracking, replacement requests, reviews & ratings, and audit logging. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Garage User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.Required part must already exist in the garage inventory master.Garage user must have purchase permissions enabled. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Garage user initiates Purchase Parts action from inventory or parts catalog. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Inventory Validation & Purchase InitiationGarage user selects a part to purchase.System validates whether the selected item exists in the garage inventory.If item exists, system allows the user to proceed with purchase.Garage user enters purchase details:QuantitySupplier / InsurerGarage user confirms the order.Part B – Order Creation & ProcessingSystem creates a purchase order.System assigns a unique Order ID.System records order details including:Item NameSupplier / InsurerOrder DatePriceQuantityDelivery StatusPayment StatusPart C – Order Listing & TrackingGarage user navigates to Purchase Order Listing.System displays all raised and closed orders with required order details.Garage user selects an order to view full order summary.Part D – Replacement RequestGarage user identifies an issue with delivered parts (damaged, incorrect, or defective).Garage user raises a Replacement Request by providing a reason.System submits the replacement request for processing.Part E – Review & RatingAfter order completion, garage user submits ratings & reviews based on:Delivery TimeQualityAccuracyCommunicationSystem updates aggregated ratings for the part and supplier.Part F – Audit LoggingSystem logs all purchase-related activities including:Order creationOrder updatesReplacement requestsReviews & ratings |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Item Not Available in InventorySystem detects item does not exist in inventory.System restricts order placement.System displays notification instructing user to add the item to inventory first.A2 – Payment FailurePayment attempt fails.System updates payment status as failed.User is notified to retry payment.A3 – Invalid QuantityGarage user enters invalid or zero quantity.System displays validation error and blocks order submission. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Purchase order is successfully created and tracked.Inventory quantity can be updated post-delivery (manual or automated).Reviews and ratings contribute to supplier/part reputation. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | E1 – Unauthorized AccessNon-garage users attempting to place purchase orders are denied access.E2 – System Error during Order CreationOrder is not created and user is prompted to retry. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Inventory validation is mandatory before purchase.Replacement handling workflow is managed separately by supplier/insurer.Ratings are immutable once submitted. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Purchase orders are strictly for garage internal inventory usage.This use case supports traceability and compliance through audit logs.Can be extended to auto-update inventory on successful delivery in future phases. |  |  |

Garage – Insurance Management & Claim Interaction

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-GRG-007 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how garage users interact with associated insurance companies, including viewing insurance profiles, handling claim RFQs, sending quotations, tracking payments and invoices, receiving notifications, and maintaining audit trails. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Garage User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.Garage must be mapped as a network garage to one or more insurance companies.Insurance companies must be active in the system. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Garage user navigates to the Insurance section from the dashboard. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Insurance Listing & Profile ViewGarage user opens the Insurance List page.System displays all associated insurance companies.Insurance list displays:Company NameLogoContact PersonBranch (if applicable)EmailPhone NumberGarage user selects an insurance company.System displays detailed insurance profile including:Registered addressCommunication detailsOther company informationPart B – Claim Requests for Quotation (RFQ)Garage user views Claim Requests for Quotation received from the selected insurance company.System displays RFQ list with:Claim IDVehicle DetailsDamage TypeRequest DateStatusGarage user clicks on a claim RFQ.System navigates the user to full claim details page.Garage user prepares and sends quotation from the claim details screen.Part C – Payments & Invoice ManagementGarage user navigates to Payments & Invoices section for a selected insurance company.System displays:Payments receivedPayments pendingAssociated claim referencesUpon receiving payment for a completed claim:System allows garage to generate an Invoice.Generated invoice is sent to the insurance company for payment confirmation.Part D – Payment Summary & FilteringGarage user views Payment Summary.System allows filtering by:Date rangeBranchClaim statusPayment statusPart E – NotificationsSystem sends notifications to garage when:A new Claim Request for Quotation is receivedQuotation-related actions occurPayment status updates occurPart F – Audit TrailSystem logs all activities including:Financial transactionsInvoice generation and downloadsCommunication related to insurer payments |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – No Associated Insurance CompaniesSystem finds no insurance mapped to the garage.System displays an empty state with an informational message.A2 – No Claim RFQs AvailableSelected insurance company has no active claim RFQs.System displays “No Claim Requests Available”.A3 – Invoice Generation RestrictedPayment has not been received yet.System restricts invoice generation until payment confirmation |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Garage successfully views and manages insurance interactions.Quotations are sent against insurance claim RFQs.Payment and invoice records are maintained for compliance.Audit logs are created for all insurer-related activities. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | E1 – Unauthorized AccessNon-network garages attempting to access insurance details are denied.E2 – System Failure during Payment Data LoadSystem displays an error message and allows retry. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Only network garages can interact with insurance companies.Invoice format is standardized and system-generated.Payment reconciliation is handled outside this use case if required. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | This use case tightly integrates with Garage RFQ, Claims, and Payments modules.Audit trail is mandatory for financial and compliance reasons.Future scope may include dispute management and credit notes. |  |  |

Garage – Subscription Management

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-GRG-008 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how a Garage views, purchases, upgrades, downgrades, renews, and manages subscription plans, including payment handling, access control based on counters, notifications, and audit logging. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Garage User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Garage user must be registered, approved, and logged in.Admin must have configured and published subscription plans.Payment gateway must be active. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Garage navigates to the Subscription section from the dashboard. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View Subscription PlansGarage opens the Active Plans Listing page.System displays only Active & Published subscription plans.Each plan displays:Feature GroupPlan NamePriceBilling CyclePlan BenefitsCountersValidityInactive or unpublished plans are not shown.Part B – Purchase SubscriptionGarage selects a subscription plan.Garage proceeds to payment.System redirects supplier to the payment gateway.Garage completes payment using available payment options.Upon successful payment:System activates the subscriptionSystem displays payment receipt, transaction ID, and invoiceGarage receives subscription activation notification.Part C – Upgrade SubscriptionGarage chooses Upgrade Plan from current subscription details.System displays eligible higher plans.Garage selects a higher plan.System calculates price adjustment by deducting unused value of the current plan from the new plan price.Garage completes the differential payment.System activates the upgraded plan immediately.Garage receives upgrade confirmation notification.Part D – Downgrade SubscriptionGarage selects Downgrade Plan option.Garage chooses a lower plan.System schedules downgrade to activate after expiry of the current plan.System confirms downgrade scheduling to the supplier.Part E – RenewalGarage selects Renew Subscription before plan expiry.Garage completes payment.Renewed plan is scheduled to activate after current plan expiry.Garage receives renewal confirmation notification.Part F – Current Plan & HistoryGarage views Current Plan Details, including:Plan NameStart DateEnd DateCountersStatusGarage views Subscription History, including:Past plansPayment statusReceiptsUsage logsPart G – Access Control EnforcementSystem tracks feature usage against plan counters.If counters are exhausted:System restricts access to related featuresSystem displays usage limit messagePart H – Notifications & Audit TrailSystem sends notifications for:Plan activationExpiry remindersRenewalsUpgrades / DowngradesPayment failuresSystem logs all subscription actions for audit and compliance. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Payment FailurePayment fails at gateway.System displays failure message.Subscription is not activated.Garage receives payment failure notification.A2 – No Active Plans AvailableAdmin has not published any active plans.System displays “No subscription plans available”.A3 – Upgrade Not EligibleGarage selects a plan lower or equal to current plan.System restricts upgrade and shows validation message. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Garage has an active, upgraded, downgraded, or renewed subscription.Subscription usage is enforced based on counters.Payment and subscription history is recorded.Audit logs are maintained. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Access ViolationGarage attempts to use features beyond allowed counters → System blocks access.EX2 – System Error during Subscription UpdateSystem rolls back partial updates and notifies the user. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Subscription pricing and counter logic are defined by Admin.No unused counters are carried forward to the next cycle.All monetary calculations follow platform-defined proration rule |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Upgrade pricing adjustment logic must be clearly documented.Downgrades are deferred to avoid feature disruption.This use case integrates with Payments, Notifications, and Access Control modules. |  |  |

My Account (Garage) – Profile

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-GRG-009 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how a logged-in user can view and update their account profile details, upload or update registration documents, and manage profile changes while ensuring that restricted and unique identifiers remain protected and approval workflows are enforced where required. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Garage User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | User must be registered, approved, and logged in.User account must be active.Admin approval workflow must be enabled for profile updates impacting compliance. |  |  |
| Triggers: | User navigates to My Account from the system menu. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View Profile DetailsUser opens My Account section.System displays all profile details captured during registration, including:Personal detailsCompany detailsContact informationUploaded documentsPart B – Edit Profile DetailsUser clicks Edit Profile.System enables editing for allowed fields only.User updates permitted profile details.System restricts editing of the following fields:Email IDLicense NumberAny other system-defined unique identifiersUser saves profile changes.Part C – Company Type RestrictionUser attempts to change Company Type (Insurance / Garage / Supplier).System blocks direct modification of Company Type.System displays a message stating that company type cannot be changed once the account is created.If business-critical profile details are updated that impact compliance, system flags the profile for Admin Review and Re-Approval before changes take effect.Part D – Admin Approval (If Applicable)If uploaded or edited details require re-verification:System marks profile as Pending Approval.Admin receives notification.Admin reviews updated profile or documents.Admin approves or rejects the changes.System updates profile status accordingly and notifies user. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: Edit Restricted FieldsUser attempts to edit Email ID or License number for an approved account.System disables the field and prevents saving changes. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | User profile is updated with valid, permitted information.Restricted identifiers remain unchanged.Updated documents are stored securely.Admin approval is enforced where required. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Unauthorized Profile AccessIf an unauthenticated user attempts to access My Account, system redirects to login.EX-2: Admin RejectionIf Admin rejects updated details, system restores previous approved profile data and notifies user with rejection reason. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Email ID and License Number are system-wide unique identifiers.Certain profile updates may require regulatory compliance review outside the system.Admin approval workflow is configurable. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | This use case ensures data integrity, regulatory compliance, and controlled profile changes.Future enhancements may include change request tracking and profile version history. |  |  |

Supplier

Supplier Onboarding & Registration

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-SUP-001 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines the end-to-end process for Supplier users to register on the platform, complete profile and compliance details, upload mandatory documents, and receive admin approval before gaining access to system functionalities. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Supplier User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Supplier user is not already registered with the same unique identifiers (Email, Mobile, License).Platform registration is active.Admin approval workflow is enabled. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Supplier user clicks Sign Up from the website Supplier login page. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Actor opens the registration link (via sales invitation or website self-registration).Actor enters Personal Details: Name, Email, and Contact Number.Actor enters Additional Details:Personal detailsCompany detailsSupplier specialityRegistration and licensing detailsBank detailsUploads Trade License CopyUploads VAT CertificateSystem validates mandatory fields & document formats and sizes.Actor submits the registration form.System sends web and email notifications to Admin about new registration.Admin reviews the submitted details and documents.Admin manually verifies the license using the Licensing Authority website.Admin chooses to Approve or Reject the registration.If approved:System activates the Supplier’s account and grants access to relevant modules.If rejected:Admin enters a rejection reason.System notifies the Supplier through email notification with the reason and allows them to edit details and resubmit.Until approval is granted, Supplier user:Can log inCannot access operational modules of the platform. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Duplicate Registration AttemptUser attempts to register with an Email ID or Mobile Number already existing in the system.System blocks registration and displays validation error.A2 – Weak PasswordUser enters a password not meeting security standards.System displays password policy message and prevents submission.A3 – Incomplete Profile SubmissionUser submits registration without completing mandatory additional details.System prompts user to complete required fields before submission. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Supplier user profile is either approved or rejected with reason.Approved Supplier users can access the platform.Rejected users can correct details and resubmit. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: System Failure during SubmissionIf system error occurs, registration is not saved and user is prompted to retry.EX-2: Admin Action TimeoutIf admin does not act, registration remains in Pending Approval state. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Email ID and Mobile Number are unique identifiers system-wide.Admin approval is mandatory for all Supplier registrations.Bank details are required for future payment processing. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Supplier users cannot transact or participate in claims until approved.This use case aligns with regulatory and compliance requirements.Future enhancements may include auto-verification of licenses. |  |  |

Supplier – Request for Quotes (RFQ) Management

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-SUP-002 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how a Supplier views RFQs received from insurance companies, submits quotations with pricing, taxes, discounts, delivery and payment terms, manages quotation status, handles invoice creation, and processes replacement or refund requests. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Supplier User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Supplier must be registered, approved, and logged in.Insurance company must have sent an RFQ to the supplier.Supplier must have an active subscription (if applicable). |  |  |
| Triggers: | Supplier logs into the system and navigates to the RFQ sectionSystem receives a new RFQ from an insurance company |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View & Search RFQsSupplier opens the RFQ Listing page.System displays all RFQs received with the following details:RFQ NumberRFQ DateInsurance Company NameItem NamesQuantitiesRFQ StatusSupplier searches or filters RFQs using RFQ Number, Insurance Company, Date Range, or Status.Part B – View RFQ DetailsSupplier selects an RFQ from the list.System displays complete RFQ details including:RFQ Number and DateInsurance Company Name and Contact DetailsItem list with quantity, requested specifications, and delivery timelineAdditional RFQ details as provided by the clientPart C – Prepare QuotationSupplier enters item-level unit price for each requested part.Supplier adds applicable taxes, fees, and surcharges.Supplier applies discounts (percentage or fixed), if applicable.Supplier specifies payment terms (advance %, credit period, or full payment).Supplier specifies delivery terms, including delivery timeline, shipping method, and charges.Supplier adds additional notes/comments, if required.Part D – Submit QuotationSupplier submits the completed quotation.System updates quotation status to Pending.Insurance company receives notification of quotation submission.Part E – Quotation Decision & Invoice CreationInsurance company reviews the quotation.Based on insurer action, quotation status is updated to:Approved orRejectedIf quotation is Approved and payment is completed by insurance company:Supplier generates invoiceInvoice displays item summary, final price, tax, and delivery chargesPart F – Replacement & Refund ManagementSupplier receives replacement request within the allowed replacement tenure.Supplier reviews the replacement request and:Approves orRejects the request with comments/documentsIf replacement is not possible and item is refundable:Supplier initiates refund processingSupplier tracks refund status as Pending, Settled, or Rejected.Part G – Notifications & LoggingSystem sends notifications to supplier for:New RFQ receivedRFQ updatesInvoice payment confirmationReplacement or refund requestsSystem logs all RFQ, quotation, invoice, replacement, and refund actions. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – RFQ RejectedInsurance company rejects quotation.System updates status to Rejected.Supplier receives rejection notification.A2 – Partial RFQ ResponseSupplier cannot quote for one or more items.Supplier leaves price blank or marks item as unavailable (as per system rules).System validates and allows submission if permitted by configuration.A3 – Replacement RejectedSupplier rejects replacement request with justification.System notifies insurance company. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Quotation is submitted and tracked with defined statuses.Approved quotations result in invoice generation after payment.Replacement/refund requests are processed and logged.RFQ lifecycle is auditable. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – System Error During Quotation SubmissionSystem saves draft quotation (if enabled) or prompts supplier to retry.EX2 – Unauthorized AccessSupplier attempts to access RFQ not assigned to them → system blocks access. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | RFQ statuses and delivery timelines are configurable by the client.Replacement and refund tenure rules are defined by business policy.Invoice generation is allowed only after confirmed payment. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | RFQ evaluation and selection remain manual on insurer side.This use case integrates with Orders, Payments, Notifications, and Audit Trail modules.Exact RFQ detail fields will be finalized during FSD phase. |  |  |

Supplier – Inventory Management

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-SUP-003 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how a Supplier manages their inventory by viewing, searching, adding, editing, deleting, and reviewing inventory items, including access to deleted inventory records. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Supplier User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Supplier must be registered, approved, and logged in.Supplier must have access rights to the Inventory module. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Supplier navigates to the Inventory module from the dashboard. |  |  |
