| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Inventory ListingSupplier opens the Inventory Listing page.System displays all inventory items added by the supplier.Each inventory item shows:Item NameItem SKUUnit PriceAvailable QuantityStatusPart B – Search & FilterSupplier searches or filters inventory items using:Item NameSKUCategoryStatusPart C – Add InventorySupplier selects Add Inventory.System displays item master information as read-only.Supplier enters:Available QuantityUnit PriceSupplier saves the inventory item.System adds the item to the active inventory list.Part D – Edit InventorySupplier selects an existing inventory item.Supplier edits allowed fields such as:Unit PriceAvailable QuantityOther editable details (as configured)Supplier saves changes.System updates the inventory item.Part E – Delete InventorySupplier selects Delete for an inventory item.System removes the item from the active inventory list.System moves the item to the Deleted Inventory list (Recycle Bin).Part F – Deleted Inventory ViewSupplier navigates to Deleted Inventory.System displays all deleted inventory items for reference or audit.Part G – Inventory Detail PageSupplier opens an inventory item.System displays complete item details including all client-defined fields. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Invalid Data EntrySupplier enters invalid quantity or price.System shows validation error and prevents save.A2 – Delete RestrictionSupplier attempts to delete an inventory item linked to an active order.System restricts deletion and displays an error message. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Inventory data is updated accurately.Deleted inventory items are retained for audit/reference.System maintains current and historical inventory records. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – System Failure during Save/DeleteSystem rolls back changes and displays an error message. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Item master data is predefined and controlled by the system/client.Deletion is logical (soft delete) and not permanent.Only supplier users can manage their own inventory. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Restore functionality from Deleted Inventory may be added later if required.Inventory changes may impact RFQs, Orders, and Availability calculations.This use case integrates with RFQ and Order modules. |  |  |

Supplier Auction Participation & Order Management

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-SUP-004 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how Supplier users view ongoing and upcoming auctions, review auction and item details, pay the required deposit, participate in bidding, track bids, and manage auction orders won through the platform, including payment, refund and notifications. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Supplier User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Supplier user must be registered, approved, and logged in.Auction must be active or scheduled.Deposit fee rules must be configured for auction items.Payment gateway must be available. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Supplier user navigates to the Auctions module or receives auction notification. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View AuctionsSupplier user opens the Auctions module.System displays all ongoing and upcoming auctions.Auction list displays:Auction TitleStart Date & TimeEnd Date & TimeItem CountReserve PriceAuction StatusPart B – View Auction DetailsSupplier user selects an auction.System displays complete auction details including:Item informationAuction rulesReserve priceStart / End timePart C – View Bid DetailsSupplier user views:Current highest bidBid historyBid timestampsPart D – Deposit Fee PaymentSupplier user selects auction item(s) to bid on.System prompts for deposit fee payment.Supplier user completes deposit payment through online payment gateway.System confirms successful deposit payment.Deposit amount is marked as refundable to the original payment source.Part E – Place BidSupplier user enters bid amount.System validates that:Deposit payment is successfulBid amount is greater than current highest bidSystem accepts bid and updates bid history.System notifies other bidders (if applicable).Part F – Auction Closure & Order CreationAuction reaches end date & time.System determines highest bidder.System automatically creates an Auction Order for winning bidder.Supplier user receives winning confirmation notification.Part G – Order List & DetailsSupplier user navigates to Auction Orders.System displays all auction orders won by the Supplier with:Order IDAuction IDItem NameWinning PriceOrder StatusPayment StatusSupplier user selects an order to view details including:Item informationDelivery detailsInvoicePayment requirementsPart H – Order PaymentSupplier user completes payment for the auction order via online payment modes.System updates payment and order status accordingly.Part I – NotificationsSystem sends notifications to Supplier for:Deposit confirmationBid updatesAuction win confirmationPayment reminders |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Bid Lower Than Current Highest BidSupplier user places a bid lower than or equal to current highest bid.System rejects the bid and displays validation message.A2 – Deposit Payment FailureDeposit payment fails.System blocks bidding and prompts retry.A3 – Auction LostSupplier does not win the auction.Deposit refund is initiated to original payment source (as per refund rules). |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Supplier successfully participates in auctions.Winning bids generate orders automatically.Payments and deposits are processed correctly.Notifications keep Supplier informed at every stage. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Unauthorized Auction Accessif Supplier is not approved or logged in, auction participation is blocked.EX-2: System Failure During BiddingBid is not recorded and Supplier is notified to retry. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Deposit fee refund timelines are governed by platform policy.Auction rules are visible to all participants.One auction order is generated per winning item. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Deposit mechanism ensures serious bidders only.This use case integrates tightly with Orders and Payments modules.Can be extended to support auto-bidding in future phases. |  |  |

Supplier Hot Deal Purchase & Order Management

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-SUP-005 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how Supplier users view active hot deals published by insurers, filter and review item details, purchase hot deal items at fixed prices, complete payments, view orders, and how the system maintains audit logs for compliance and tracking. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Supplier User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Supplier user must be registered, approved, and logged in.Hot deals must be active and published by insurers.Inventory must have sufficient available quantity.Approved payment modes must be configured. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Supplier user navigates to the Hot Deals module. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View Hot DealsSupplier user opens the Hot Deals section.System displays all active hot deals.Hot deal listing shows:Item NameDeal PriceAvailable QuantityStart & End Date/TimeStatus (to be confirmed by client)Part B – Apply Advanced FiltersSupplier user applies filters using one or more of the following:Part NamePart CategoryVehicle CompatibilityPrice RangeExpiry TimeHot Deal IDStatusSystem refreshes the hot deal list based on applied filters.Part C – View Hot Deal Item DetailsSupplier user selects a hot deal item.System displays complete item details including:Part NamePart IDDeal PriceCompatibility (vehicle models)ConditionAvailable QuantityPart D – Purchase Hot Deal ItemSupplier user selects the required Hot Deal.Supplier user proceeds to purchase the hot deal.System will show the total hot deal items, their quantity and total price.Supplier user confirms the order.Part E – Payment WorkflowSystem redirects the Supplier user to the payment gateway.Supplier user completes payment using approved payment modes.System validates payment success.System automatically generates a Hot Deal Order.Part F – Order Listing & Order DetailsSupplier user navigates to Hot Deal Orders.System displays all hot deal orders with:Order IDItem NameDeal PriceOrder DatePayment StatusDelivery StatusSupplier user selects an order to view detailed information including:InvoicePayment informationDelivery informationPart G – Audit TrailSystem logs all actions related to hot deals including:Viewing hot dealsPurchase actionsPayment completionOrder creationAudit logs are stored for compliance and tracking purposes. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Insufficient QuantitySupplier user selects quantity exceeding availability.System displays validation error and prevents checkout.A2 – Payment FailurePayment fails or is cancelled.Order is not created.System prompts Supplier user to retry payment. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Successful hot deal purchase results in order creation.Inventory quantity is reduced accordingly.Order appears in Supplier hot deal order list.Audit trail is maintained. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Unauthorized AccessIf Supplier user is not approved or logged in, system blocks access to hot deals.EX2 – System Error during TransactionTransaction is rolled back and no order is created. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Hot deal prices are fixed and non-negotiable.Delivery fulfilment is handled separately.Hot deal status values will be finalized by the client. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Hot deals enable fast procurement of parts and vehicles at fixed prices.This use case integrates with Inventory, Orders, and Payments modules.Future enhancements may include bulk purchase limits and expiry reminders. |  |  |

Supplier – Subscription Management

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-SUP-006 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how a Supplier views, purchases, upgrades, downgrades, renews, and manages subscription plans, including payment handling, access control based on counters, notifications, and audit logging. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Supplier User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Supplier user must be registered, approved, and logged in.Admin must have configured and published subscription plans.Payment gateway must be active. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Supplier navigates to the Subscription section from the dashboard. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View Subscription PlansSupplier opens the Active Plans Listing page.System displays only Active & Published subscription plans.Each plan displays:Feature GroupPlan NamePriceBilling CyclePlan BenefitsCountersValidityInactive or unpublished plans are not shown.Part B – Purchase SubscriptionSupplier selects a subscription plan.Supplier proceeds to payment.System redirects supplier to the payment gateway.Supplier completes payment using available payment options.Upon successful payment:System activates the subscriptionSystem displays payment receipt, transaction ID, and invoiceSupplier receives subscription activation notification.Part C – Upgrade SubscriptionSupplier chooses Upgrade Plan from current subscription details.System displays eligible higher plans.Supplier selects a higher plan.System calculates price adjustment by deducting unused value of the current plan from the new plan price.Supplier completes the differential payment.System activates the upgraded plan immediately.Supplier receives upgrade confirmation notification.Part D – Downgrade SubscriptionSupplier selects Downgrade Plan option.Supplier chooses a lower plan.System schedules downgrade to activate after expiry of the current plan.System confirms downgrade scheduling to the supplier.Part E – RenewalSupplier selects Renew Subscription before plan expiry.Supplier completes payment.Renewed plan is scheduled to activate after current plan expiry.Supplier receives renewal confirmation notification.Part F – Current Plan & HistorySupplier views Current Plan Details, including:Plan NameStart DateEnd DateCountersStatusSupplier views Subscription History, including:Past plansPayment statusReceiptsUsage logsPart G – Access Control EnforcementSystem tracks feature usage against plan counters.If counters are exhausted:System restricts access to related featuresSystem displays usage limit messagePart H – Notifications & Audit TrailSystem sends notifications for:Plan activationExpiry remindersRenewalsUpgrades / DowngradesPayment failuresSystem logs all subscription actions for audit and compliance. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Payment FailurePayment fails at gateway.System displays failure message.Subscription is not activated.Supplier receives payment failure notification.A2 – No Active Plans AvailableAdmin has not published any active plans.System displays “No subscription plans available”.A3 – Upgrade Not EligibleSupplier selects a plan lower or equal to current plan.System restricts upgrade and shows validation message. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Supplier has an active, upgraded, downgraded, or renewed subscription.Subscription usage is enforced based on counters.Payment and subscription history is recorded.Audit logs are maintained. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Access ViolationSupplier attempts to use features beyond allowed counters → System blocks access.EX2 – System Error during Subscription UpdateSystem rolls back partial updates and notifies the user. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Subscription pricing and counter logic are defined by Admin.No unused counters are carried forward to the next cycle.All monetary calculations follow platform-defined proration rule |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Upgrade pricing adjustment logic must be clearly documented.Downgrades are deferred to avoid feature disruption.This use case integrates with Payments, Notifications, and Access Control modules. |  |  |

My Account (Supplier) – Profile

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-GRG-007 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how a logged-in user can view and update their account profile details, upload or update registration documents, and manage profile changes while ensuring that restricted and unique identifiers remain protected and approval workflows are enforced where required. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Supplier User |  |  |
| Preconditions: | User must be registered, approved, and logged in.User account must be active.Admin approval workflow must be enabled for profile updates impacting compliance. |  |  |
| Triggers: | User navigates to My Account from the system menu. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – View Profile DetailsUser opens My Account section.System displays all profile details captured during registration, including:Personal detailsCompany detailsContact informationUploaded documentsPart B – Edit Profile DetailsUser clicks Edit Profile.System enables editing for allowed fields only.User updates permitted profile details.System restricts editing of the following fields:Email IDLicense NumberAny other system-defined unique identifiersUser saves profile changes.Part C – Company Type RestrictionUser attempts to change Company Type (Insurance / Garage / Supplier).System blocks direct modification of Company Type.System displays a message stating that company type cannot be changed once the account is created.If business-critical profile details are updated that impact compliance, system flags the profile for Admin Review and Re-Approval before changes take effect.Part D – Admin Approval (If Applicable)If uploaded or edited details require re-verification:System marks profile as Pending Approval.Admin receives notification.Admin reviews updated profile or documents.Admin approves or rejects the changes.System updates profile status accordingly and notifies user. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1: Edit Restricted FieldsUser attempts to edit Email ID or License number for an approved account.System disables the field and prevents saving changes. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | User profile is updated with valid, permitted information.Restricted identifiers remain unchanged.Updated documents are stored securely.Admin approval is enforced where required. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: Unauthorized Profile AccessIf an unauthenticated user attempts to access My Account, system redirects to login.EX-2: Admin RejectionIf Admin rejects updated details, system restores previous approved profile data and notifies user with rejection reason. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Email ID and License Number are system-wide unique identifiers.Certain profile updates may require regulatory compliance review outside the system.Admin approval workflow is configurable. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | This use case ensures data integrity, regulatory compliance, and controlled profile changes.Future enhancements may include change request tracking and profile version history. |  |  |

Platform Administrator

Platform Administrator Onboarding

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-001 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how a Platform Administrator registers on the system, verifies their email, logs in securely, and manages password recovery following platform security standards. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Platform Administrator registration is enabled by the system.Admin has access to a valid email address and mobile number. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to the Admin Registration or Admin Login page. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Admin RegistrationPlatform Administrator opens the Registration page.Admin enters required registration details, including:NameEmail IDMobile NumberPasswordOther client-defined fieldsSystem validates the entered information.System creates the admin account in Pending Verification status.Part B – Email VerificationSystem sends an email verification link to the registered email ID.Admin verifies the email by clicking the link.System marks the email as verified.Admin account becomes eligible for first login.Part C – Admin LoginAdmin navigates to the Login page.Admin logs in using registered email ID and password.System authenticates credentials.On successful authentication, admin is redirected to the Admin Dashboard.Part D – Forgot PasswordAdmin selects Forgot Password on the login screen.Admin enters registered email ID.System sends a password reset link to the email ID.Admin verifies by opening reset link.Part E – Reset PasswordAdmin enters a new password.System validates password against security rules:Minimum lengthSpecial charactersUpper/lower case rulesSystem updates the password successfully.Admin receives confirmation and can log in with the new password. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Email Verification FailureAdmin is prompted to resend verification email.A2 – Invalid Login CredentialsAdmin enters incorrect email or password.System denies access and displays validation error.A3 – Password Rule ViolationAdmin enters a password that does not meet security rules.System displays password policy message and blocks reset. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Platform Administrator account is successfully created and verified.Admin can securely access the Admin Dashboard.Password reset actions are completed as per security policies. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX-1: System or Email Service FailureVerification or reset emails are not delivered → system logs error and allows retry. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Admin registration fields and validation rules are configurable by the client.Email verification is mandatory before first login. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Multi-factor authentication (MFA) can be added later if required.All admin login and password activities should be logged for security audit.This use case integrates with User Management, Security, and Audit Trail modules. |  |  |

Admin – Manage Insurance Companies

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-002 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how a Platform Administrator manages registered insurance companies, including viewing and searching listings, reviewing detailed profiles, monitoring analytics, approving onboarding and profile edits, activating or deactivating accounts, exporting data, and maintaining audit logs. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Platform Administrator must be registered, verified, and logged in.Insurance companies must exist in the system (registered or pending). |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to the Manage Insurance section from the Admin Dashboard. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Insurance Listing, Search & FilterAdmin opens the Insurance Listing page.System displays a list of all registered insurance companies with:Insurance Company NameDate of JoiningNumber of BranchesSubscription Plan NameAccount StatusAdmin searches insurance companies by Name, City, or Country.Admin applies filters using City, Country, Subscription Plan Name, and Status.Part B – View Insurance Company DetailsAdmin selects an insurance company from the list.System displays complete profile details, segmented into sections:Company InformationSystem shows:Company NameDate JoinedEmail IDMobile NumberAccount StatusSubscription InformationSystem displays:Active Plan NamePlan Start DatePlan End DatePlan TypePlan AmountBranch InformationSystem displays branch details including:Branch NameBranch IDAddressContact PersonPart C – Analytics & MetricsAdmin views Claim Analytics, including total number of claim requests.Admin views Auction Analytics, including total auctions created.Admin views Hot Deals Analytics, including total hot deals created.Admin views Garage Metrics, including:Network garagesNumber of claim requests per garageAdmin views Inventory Summary, including:Item NameUnit PriceQuantity AvailablePart D – Verification & Account ControlAdmin reviews new insurance onboarding requests, including:Uploaded documentsRegistration detailsAdmin approves or rejects the onboarding request.Admin reviews profile edit requests involving important details.Admin verifies and approves or rejects profile updates.Admin activates or deactivates insurance company accounts as required.Part E – Export Admin exports insurance company data into Excel or CSV format.System exports data based on applied filters.Part F – Audit LoggingSystem logs all admin actions including:ReviewApproval / RejectionActivation / DeactivationData export |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – No Records FoundAdmin applies filters resulting in no data.System displays “No insurance companies found”.A2 – Export FailureExport process fails due to system error.System displays error and allows retry.A3 – Rejection with ReasonAdmin rejects onboarding or profile edit request.Admin provides rejection reason.System notifies insurance company with reason. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Insurance company records are reviewed, updated, and controlled.Account status and subscription visibility are updated correctly.Analytics and reports reflect latest data.All admin actions are auditable. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Unauthorized ActionAdmin attempts an action without permission → system blocks and logs the attempt. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Analytics data is near real-time or refreshed at defined intervals.Subscription details are fetched from the Subscription module.Admin permissions are role-based. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Deactivation may restrict login and system usage for insurance users.Exported data should respect data privacy and compliance rules.This use case integrates with Claims, Subscription, Auction, Hot Deals, and Inventory modules. |  |  |

Admin – Manage Garages

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-003 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how a Platform Administrator manages registered garages, including listing and searching garages, viewing detailed profiles, monitoring subscriptions and claim activity, approving profile edits, activating or deactivating accounts, exporting data, and maintaining an audit trail. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Platform Administrator must be registered, verified, and logged in.Garages must exist in the system (registered or pending). |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to the Manage Garages section from the Admin Dashboard. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Garage Listing, Search & FilterAdmin opens the Garage Listing page.System displays all registered garages with the following information:Garage NameContact InformationDate of JoiningSubscription Plan NameNumber of Active Claim RequestsAccount StatusAdmin searches garages by Name, City, or Country.Admin applies filters using City, Country, Plan Name, and Status.Part B – View Garage Profile DetailsAdmin selects a garage from the list.System displays the complete garage profile, segmented into sections:Garage InformationSystem shows:Garage NameContact Person NameEmailMobile NumberAccount StatusSubscription InformationSystem displays:Active Plan NameStart DateEnd DatePlan TypePlan AmountPart C – Activity & AnalyticsAdmin views Claim Requests Summary, including:Number of accepted claim requestsNumber of rejected claim requestsAdmin views Associated Insurance Companies, including:Insurance Company NameNumber of claim requests received from eachAdmin views Parts Purchased Summary, including:Part NameQuantity PurchasedUnit PricePart D – Profile Verification & Account ControlAdmin reviews garage profile edit requests, including document, license, or contact detail changes.Admin approves or rejects the profile edit request with reason (if rejected).Admin activates or deactivates the garage account as required.Part E – Export Admin exports garage listing data to Excel or CSV format.System exports data based on applied filters.Part F – Audit LoggingSystem logs all admin actions including:ViewApproval / RejectionActivation / DeactivationExport |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – No Garage Records FoundAdmin applies filters that return no results.System displays “No garages found”.A2 – Export FailureExport process fails due to system error.System displays error and allows retry.A3 – Profile Edit RejectionAdmin rejects a profile edit request.Admin provides a rejection reason.System notifies the garage with the reason. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Garage records are reviewed and maintained accurately.Account status reflects admin actions.All activities are logged for audit and compliance. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Unauthorized Action AttemptAdmin attempts an action without proper authorization → system blocks and logs the attempt. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Subscription and claim metrics are refreshed periodically.Admin permissions are role-based.Exported data complies with platform privacy policies. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Deactivating a garage account restricts login and system usage.Profile edit approvals may impact live claims and RFQs.This use case integrates with Claims, Subscription, and Orders modules. |  |  |

Admin – Manage Suppliers

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-004 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case describes how a Platform Administrator manages registered suppliers, including listing and searching suppliers, viewing detailed supplier profiles, monitoring subscription and RFQ activity, verifying registrations and profile edits, activating or deactivating supplier accounts, exporting supplier data, and maintaining a complete audit trail. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Platform Administrator must be registered, verified, and logged in.Suppliers must exist in the system (registered, pending, or inactive). |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to the Manage Suppliers section from the Admin Dashboard. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Supplier Listing, Search & FilterAdmin opens the Supplier Listing page.System displays all registered suppliers with the following information:Supplier NameContact InformationDate of JoiningSubscription Plan NameNumber of RFQsAccount StatusAdmin searches suppliers by Name, City, or Country.Admin applies filters using City, Country, Plan Name, and Status (Active, Inactive, Pending, Rejected).Part B – View Supplier Profile DetailsAdmin selects a supplier from the list.System displays the complete supplier profile, segmented into sections:Supplier InformationSystem shows:Supplier NameDate JoinedEmail IDMobile NumberAccount StatusSubscription Plan DetailsSystem displays:Active Plan NameStart DateEnd DatePlan TypePlan AmountPart C – RFQ & Inventory OverviewAdmin views RFQ Details, including:Quote NumberQuote DateInsurance Company NameItem Details (quantity and unit price)RFQ StatusAdmin views Supplier Inventory Details, including:Item NameSKUUnit PriceQuantity AvailablePart D – Registration & Profile VerificationAdmin reviews new supplier registration requests, including business documents and compliance details.Admin approves or rejects the registration request.Admin reviews supplier profile edit requests (license, company name, email changes, etc.).Admin approves or rejects profile edit requests with reason (if rejected).Part E – Account ControlAdmin activates or deactivates a supplier account as required.System updates supplier account status immediately.Part F – Export& Audit LoggingAdmin exports supplier listing data into Excel or CSV format.System exports only the data matching applied filters.System logs all admin actions including:VerificationApproval / RejectionActivation / DeactivationExport actions |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – No Supplier Records FoundAdmin applies search or filters.System displays “No suppliers found”.A2 – Export FailureExport process fails due to system error.System displays error and allows retry.A3 – Registration or Profile Edit RejectionAdmin rejects registration or profile edit.Admin provides rejection reason.System notifies supplier with the rejection reason. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Supplier data is reviewed and maintained accurately.Supplier account status reflects admin actions.All activities are logged for audit and compliance. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Unauthorized Action AttemptAdmin attempts an action without proper authorization → system blocks the action and logs the attempt. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | RFQ and inventory data are updated in near real time.Admin permissions are role-based.Exported data follows platform data privacy policies. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Deactivated suppliers cannot participate in RFQs or submit quotations.Profile changes involving critical identifiers require admin approval.This use case integrates with RFQ, Inventory, and Subscription modules. |  |  |

Admin – Manage Claim Requests

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-005 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how a Platform Administrator views, searches, filters, and reviews all claim requests submitted on the platform. It includes accessing detailed claim information, exporting claim data, and ensuring transparency and control over claim lifecycle monitoring. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Platform Administrator must be registered, verified, and logged in.Suppliers must exist in the system (registered, pending, or inactive). |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to the Manage Claim Requests section from the Admin Dashboard. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Part A – Claim Request ListingAdmin opens the Claim Request Listing page.System displays a list of all claim requests with the following information:Insurance Company NameCustomer NamePolicy NumberDate of ClaimReason for ClaimClaim StatusPart B – Search & Filter ClaimsAdmin searches claims using:Policy NumberCustomer NameInsurance Company NameAdmin applies filters based on:Insurance CompanyCityCountryClaim Status (Pending, Assigned, Accepted, Rejected, Completed)Part C – View Claim DetailsAdmin selects a claim from the listing.System displays the complete claim request details, including:Claim InformationSystem shows:Customer NamePolicy NumberCustomer Contact InformationInsurance Company NameIncident & Evidence DetailsSystem displays:Witness details uploaded by insurer or claimantPolice report detailsAttached documents and imagesDetailed description of damagesParts & RFQ InformationSystem displays:List of required partsQuantities for each partRFQ linkage (if RFQ has been raised)Additional InformationSystem shows:Other relevant supporting documentsAdditional notes added by insurer or claimantPart D – Export ClaimsAdmin initiates export of claim data.System allows export in Excel or CSV format.Exported file includes only claims matching applied search and filter criteria. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – No Claims FoundAdmin applies search or filters.System displays message: “No claim requests found for the selected criteria.”A2 – Export FailureExport process fails due to system or network issue.System displays error message and allows admin to retry. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Admin successfully reviews and monitors claim requests.Exported claim data is available for reporting or audit purposes.No changes are made to claim data during view-only operations. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Unauthorized AccessIf admin session expires or permission is invalid, system redirects admin to login and logs the event. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Claim data is read-only for admin (no modification).RFQ linkage is visible only if initiated by insurer or system.Uploaded documents follow platform security and access rules. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Admin role is limited to monitoring, verification, and reporting, not claim execution.Claim statuses are controlled by insurer and workflow engines.Export functionality respects applied filters and data privacy policies. |  |  |

Admin – Manage Subscription

Admin – Manage Subscription Plans

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-006 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how the Platform Administrator creates, views, searches, edits, activates/deactivates, and deletes subscription plans, and controls their visibility on the frontend. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Admin is logged into the platform.Feature Groups exist (for plan creation). |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to Manage Subscription → Plans. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Plan Listing & SearchSystem displays all existing subscription plans with:Feature GroupPlan NamePricing TypePriceBilling CycleFrequencyStatusAdmin searches plans by Plan Name, Plan Type, or Billing Cycle.Add New PlanAdmin clicks Add New Plan.Admin enters: Feature Group, Plan Name, Pricing Type, Price, Billing Cycle, Frequency, Status.System validates all mandatory fields.Plan is saved as Draft or Published.Edit PlanAdmin edits a plan only if:Plan is in Draft, ORPlan is Published but not purchased by any user.System revalidates updated fields before saving.Delete PlanAdmin deletes a plan only if:Plan is in Draft state.Published plans cannot be deleted.Activate / Deactivate PlanAdmin activates or deactivates a plan.Inactive plans:Do not appear on frontend purchase screens.Remain usable for already subscribed users until expiry.Cannot be renewed. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Edit Restricted PlanIf Admin attempts to edit a plan that has already been purchased by one or more users:System displays validation message: “Plan cannot be edited as it is already subscribed by users.”Edit action is blocked.A2 – Delete Restricted PlanIf Admin attempts to delete a published plan:System prevents deletion.Admin is prompted to deactivate the plan instead.A3 – Deactivate Active PlanIf Admin deactivates an active plan:Plan becomes unavailable for new purchases.Existing subscribers continue until plan expiry. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Subscription plan configuration is updated successfully.Frontend visibility is controlled as per plan status.Existing subscribers retain access until subscription expiry. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX01 - Mandatory Field MissingIf required plan fields are not provided:System displays field-level validation errors.Plan is not saved.EX02 - Invalid Pricing ConfigurationIf pricing or billing cycle values are invalid:System rejects the configuration.Admin must correct the inputs.EX03 - System FailureIf system error occurs during save/update:Transaction is rolled back.Admin is notified with an error message. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Feature Groups must exist before creating subscription plans.Subscription pricing logic is handled by backend billing engine.Currency and tax calculations are managed externally or via configuration. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Deleting published plans is intentionally restricted to avoid subscription inconsistencies.Deactivated plans should be clearly labeled in Admin UI. |  |  |

Admin – Manage User Subscriptions

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-007 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how the Admin manages customer subscriptions including creation, modification, activation/deactivation, renewals, upgrades, downgrades, and payments. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Admin is logged in.Subscription plans exist.Users are registered on the platform. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to Manage Subscription → Users. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Subscription Listing & SearchSystem displays active subscriptions with:Company NameFeature GroupPlan NameBilling TypeAddress, City, State, Country, Pin CodeAdmin searches subscriptions by Plan Name, Plan Type, Billing Cycle.Create SubscriptionAdmin creates a new subscription by entering:User NameSubscription PlanBilling TypeStart DateStatusPayment MethodPayment Reference NumberPayment StatusEdit / Modify SubscriptionAdmin edits existing subscription details.System immediately updates user access based on changes.Delete SubscriptionAdmin deletes a subscription permanently.System immediately revokes user’s plan-based access.Activate / Deactivate SubscriptionAdmin activates or deactivates a user’s subscription.Deactivated plans are hidden from frontend for that user.Renewal, Upgrade & DowngradeAdmin tracks start date, expiry date, and renewal cycle.Admin manually upgrades or downgrades user plans.System adjusts access rules accordingly.Payment HandlingAdmin records payment via:Online system payment, OROffline payment entry. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Offline Payment EntryAdmin selects Offline Payment.System allows manual entry of payment reference number and status.Subscription becomes active only after payment status is marked successful.A2 – Manual Upgrade / DowngradeAdmin manually upgrades/downgrades user plan.System updates access permissions immediately or post-expiry (based on downgrade rules).A3 – Subscription DeactivationIf Admin deactivates an active plan:Plan becomes unavailable for new purchases.Existing subscribers continue until plan expiry. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | User subscription lifecycle is updated successfully.User access permissions reflect the latest subscription state.Payment and billing records are stored.Notification events are triggered where applicable. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX01 - Invalid User or PlanIf selected user or plan does not exist:System displays error.Subscription creation/edit is aborted.EX02 - Payment FailureIf payment fails:Subscription remains inactive or pending.Admin is notified.EX03 - Conflicting Subscription DatesIf expiry or start date overlaps existing subscription:System prompts Admin to correct date values. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Admin has full authority to override subscription lifecycle.Manual upgrades/downgrades do not require user confirmation.Payment reconciliation is handled externally if offline payments are used. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Immediate access revocation on delete is critical for security.Subscription changes must be reflected in real time. |  |  |

Admin – Manage Feature Groups

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-008 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case defines how the Admin manages Feature Groups used for subscription plan configuration. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Admin is logged into the platform. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to Manage Subscription → Feature Groups. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Feature Group Listing & SearchSystem displays Feature Groups with:IDNameDescriptionCreated OnStatusAdmin searches by Name, Description, Status, or keywords.Add Feature GroupAdmin creates a Feature Group by entering Name, Description, and Status.Edit Feature GroupAdmin updates Feature Group details.Activate / Deactivate Feature GroupAdmin activates or deactivates Feature Group.Inactive Feature Groups:Cannot be used for plan creation.Are not visible to users.Delete Feature GroupAdmin deletes Feature Group only if:It is not attached to any existing plan.System restricts deletion if linked to plans. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Feature Group Linked to PlanIf Admin attempts to delete a Feature Group already attached to a plan:System blocks deletion.Message displayed: “Feature Group is associated with an existing plan.”A2 – Deactivate Feature GroupAdmin deactivates Feature Group.Group becomes unavailable for new plan creation. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Feature Groups are created, updated, activated, or deactivated successfully.Feature Group visibility is updated in plan creation screens.Data integrity between Feature Groups and Plans is maintained. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX01 - Duplicate Feature Group NameIf Admin enters a duplicate Feature Group name:System displays validation error.Creation is blocked.EX02 - System ErrorIf save/update fails:No changes are committed.Admin is informed. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Feature Groups act as logical containers for plan features.Each Feature Group can be associated with multiple plans. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Feature Group deletion rules are strict to prevent orphaned plans.Deactivation is preferred over deletion for audit and reporting. |  |  |

Admin – Manage Review & Rating

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-009 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case allows the Platform Administrator to view, manage, moderate, edit, and delete reviews and ratings submitted by Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users against each other for claims and orders, ensuring platform transparency, data accuracy, and misuse prevention. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Admin is logged in with valid credentials.Reviews and ratings have been submitted by platform users.Admin has permission to manage reviews & ratings. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to Manage Review & Rating module from Admin Dashboard |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Admin accesses the Review & Rating Listing screen.System displays a list of all reviews with:Review IDUser NameUser TypeRating (1–5)Review TextOrder ID / Claim ID ReferenceDate AddedStatus (Active/Inactive)Admin searches reviews using keywords or IDs.Admin applies filters (Rating range, Reviewed Entity type).Admin selects a review to view full details.System displays:Reviewer profileReviewed entity (Insurance / Garage / Supplier)Claim / Order referenceTimestampsAdmin performs one of the following actions:Edit rating scoreDelete reviewActivate / Inactivate reviewSystem updates the review status/details.System updates aggregated ratings where applicable.System logs all actions in audit trail. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Edit Review RatingAdmin edits the rating score due to incorrect submission.System validates rating range (1–5).Updated rating is saved.Aggregated rating is recalculated automatically.A2 – Inactivate ReviewAdmin marks a review as Inactive instead of deleting.Review no longer appears in public views.Review remains stored for audit purposes.A2 – Delete ReviewAdmin deletes a review due to inappropriate or fraudulent content.Review is permanently removed.Aggregated rating is recalculated.A2 – Aggregated Rating ViewAdmin views cumulative ratings per entity.System displays average rating and total review count for:InsuranceGarageSupplier |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Reviews and ratings are moderated as per admin action.Aggregated ratings reflect latest approved reviews.Inactive or deleted reviews are excluded from frontend display.All actions are recorded in audit logs.Data integrity between reviews and claim/order references is maintained. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – System FailureIf system error occurs during edit/delete:Changes are rolled back.Admin is notified with error message. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Reviews are submitted only after valid claim/order completion.Each review is linked to a valid Claim ID or Order ID.Aggregated ratings are calculated based on non deleted reviews only. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Review moderation is critical to prevent misuse or biased ratings.Soft delete (Inactive) is preferred over hard delete where compliance requires record retention.Multi-flow review support ensures bidirectional transparency among all business entities.Future enhancement may include auto-flagging abusive language using AI moderation. |  |  |

Admin – Manage Contact Inquiry & Tasks

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-010 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case enables the Platform Administrator to manage contact inquiries submitted by platform users, convert inquiries into actionable tasks, manually create and manage tasks, track task status, and maintain an audit trail for all inquiry and task-related actions |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | User (Insurance/Garage/Supplier) has access to the Contact Inquiry form.Admin is logged in with appropriate administrative permissions.Notification services (email/system alerts) are active. |  |  |
| Triggers: | A user submits a Contact InquiryORAdmin navigates to Manage Contact Inquiry & Tasks module. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Contact Inquiry FlowUser submits a contact inquiry via the application form.System captures inquiry details and generates a unique Inquiry ID.System marks inquiry status as Open.Admin receives system notification for new inquiry.Admin opens Inquiry Listing screen.System displays inquiry list with:Inquiry IDNameEmailMobileSubjectSubmitted OnStatus (Open / Assigned / Closed)Admin selects an inquiry to view full details.System displays inquiry message and contact information.Task Management FlowFor admin Inquiry will be treated as a task or will create a task manually and will assign to an admin user.System updates inquiry status to Assigned.Assigned admin receives task notification.Admin updates task status and adds completion comments.Upon task completion, admin closes the task.System notifies the original user that the inquiry is closed. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Manual Task CreationAdmin creates a task without linking to any inquiry.Task is saved with status Open.Task appears in Task Listing.A2 – Delete InquiryAdmin deletes an inquiry.System moves inquiry to Archived state (if enabled).Inquiry is removed from active listings.A3 – Reassign TaskAdmin updates the assigned admin user.System sends notification to the new assignee.Task history is updated.A4 – Search & FilterAdmin searches inquiries or tasks using keywords.Admin filters inquiries by status (Open/Assigned/Closed).System refreshes listing accordingly. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Contact inquiries are properly tracked and managed.Tasks are created, assigned, updated, and closed successfully.Inquiry status reflects task lifecycle accurately.Notifications are sent at key stages.All actions are logged for audit and compliance. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Task Assignment FailureIf assigned admin user is inactive:System blocks assignment.Admin is prompted to select another user.EX1 – System FailureIf system error occurs during save/update:Transaction is rolled back.Admin is notified with an error message. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | All inquiries are non-financial and informational in nature.Inquiry deletion does not remove audit logs.Task closure implies inquiry resolution when linked. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | Inquiry-to-task conversion ensures accountability and traceability.Archive mechanism supports compliance without cluttering active data.Future enhancement may include SLA tracking and priority tagging.Tasks may later be extended to support attachments and due dates. |  |  |

Admin – Manage Auction

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-011 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case enables the Platform Administrator to view, search, filter, and inspect all auctions created across Insurance companies, Garages, and Suppliers. The Admin can review auction details, bidding activity, item-level information, and audit logs to ensure transparency, compliance, and platform governance. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Admin is logged in with valid administrative credentials.At least one auction exists on the platform.Auctions have been created by Insurance, Garage, or Supplier users.System has bid and auction activity data available. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to Manage Auction module from the Admin Dashboard. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Admin accesses the Auction Listing screen.System displays a complete list of auctions with the following fields:Auction Name / TitleCreated By (Insurance / Garage / Supplier)Start DateEnd DateAuction Status (Upcoming / Ongoing / Closed)Reserve PriceNumber of ItemsTotal Bids ReceivedAdmin searches auctions using keywords.Admin applies filters based on:StatusDate RangeCreated ByAdmin selects an auction from the list.System displays full Auction Details, including:Auction title and scheduleStarting bid and reserve priceSeller informationAuction terms and conditionsSystem displays Item-Level Details for the auction:Item NameSKUItem ConditionItem StatusReserve PriceHighest BidSystem displays Bid History with timestamps and bidder details.Admin navigates to Audit Trail section.System displays activity logs:Auction CreatedAuction EditedAuction ClosedWinner Declared |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – View Closed AuctionAdmin selects a closed auction.System displays final winning bid and winner details.A2 – View Auction Without BidsAdmin opens an auction with no bids.System displays “No bids placed yet” message. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Admin successfully views and audits auction activity.Auction and bid data remain unchanged (view-only).Platform transparency and compliance are ensured.Admin actions (view/search/filter) are logged for tracking. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Data Loading FailureIf bid or item data fails to load:System displays partial data with warning message.Error is logged for technical review. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Admin has read-only access to auctions (no edit/delete).Auction status is system-driven and cannot be modified by Admin.Bid history is immutable once auction is live or closed.All auctions follow a standardized auction lifecycle. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | This module is primarily for monitoring, compliance, and dispute resolution.Audit trail visibility supports legal and regulatory requirements.Future enhancement may include auction suspension or manual intervention rights for Admin.Reporting and analytics can be extended from this module. |  |  |

Admin – Manage Hot Deals

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-012 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case allows the Platform Administrator to view, search, filter, and inspect all Hot Deals created by Insurance companies, Garages, and Suppliers. The Admin can monitor deal performance, item details, pricing, availability, lifecycle status, and audit history for governance and operational oversight. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Admin is logged in with valid administrative credentials.At least one Hot Deal exists on the platform.Hot Deals are created by Insurance, Garage, or Supplier users.Item and purchase data are available in the system. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to Manage Hot Deals module from the Admin Dashboard. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Admin accesses the Hot Deals Listing screen.System displays a list of all Hot Deals with the following details:Hot Deal NameItem NameCreated By (Insurance / Garage / Supplier)Deal PriceStart DateEnd DateStatus (Active / Inactive / Upcoming / Expired)QuantityNumber of PurchasesAdmin searches Hot Deals using keyword-based search.Admin applies filters:StatusDate RangeCreated ByPrice RangeAdmin selects a Hot Deal from the list.System displays Hot Deal Details, including:Hot Deal NameDescriptionDeal PriceStart & End Date/TimeStatusCreated BySystem displays Item-Level Details linked to the Hot Deal:Item NameSKUQuantity AvailableUnit PriceItem ImagesItem SpecificationsAdmin navigates to the Audit Log section.System displays activity logs for the Hot Deal:CreatedEditedActivatedDeactivatedExpired |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – View Expired Hot DealAdmin selects an expired Hot Deal.System displays deal details in read-only mode.Purchase-related fields are locked.A2 – Hot Deal with Zero PurchasesAdmin views a Hot Deal with no purchases.System displays “No purchases yet” message. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Admin successfully views and audits Hot Deal information.No data modifications are made (view-only access).System logs admin access actions.Deal lifecycle remains unchanged. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Item Data Loading FailureIf item details fail to load:System displays partial deal information.Error is logged for technical investigation. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Admin has read-only access to Hot Deals.Hot Deal lifecycle (Active, Expired, etc.) is system-driven.Quantity and purchase count are updated in real time.Hot Deals are always associated with at least one item. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | This module is primarily used for monitoring promotional activity and compliance.Audit logs support dispute handling and operational reviews.Future enhancements may include manual deal suspension or override pricing controls for Admin.Analytics (conversion rate, revenue impact) can be layered on top of this module. |  |  |

Admin – Manage Admin Users, Roles, and Groups

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-013 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case enables the Super Admin to manage Admin Groups, Admin Roles, and Admin User Accounts within the platform. It covers creation, viewing, searching, filtering, editing, activation/inactivation, permission mapping, assignment, deletion controls, and audit logging to ensure secure access control and governance. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Super Admin is authenticated and logged in.Permission management framework is configured.At least one permission set exists in the system.Super Admin has access to User, Role, and Group management modules. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Super Admin navigates to Admin → Users, Roles & Groups from the Admin Dashboard. |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Manage Admin GroupsAdmin opens Admin Groups Listing.System displays groups with:Group NameDescriptionNo. of Users AssignedCreated OnStatus (Active/Inactive)Admin searches or filters groups.Admin creates a new group by entering:Group NameDescriptionStatusAssigned UsersAdmin edits group details and permissions.System maps permissions → users → groups.Admin activates/deactivates a group.Admin views group details including:PermissionsUsersCreated By / Created OnSystem logs all group-related actions.Manage Admin RolesAdmin navigates to Admin Roles Listing.System displays roles with:Role NameDescriptionAssigned Permissions CountUsers AssignedCreated OnStatusAdmin searches or filters roles.Admin creates a role by defining:Role NameDescriptionStatusPermissionsAdmin edits role permissions.Admin activates/deactivates roles.Admin views detailed role information.System restricts deletion if role is assigned.All role actions are logged.Manage Admin Accounts (Users)Admin opens Admin Account Listing.System displays:Admin NameEmailMobileAssigned GroupAssigned RoleCreated OnLast LoginStatusAdmin searches and filters admin users.Admin creates a new admin account by entering:NameEmailMobilePasswordGroupRoleStatusAdmin edits admin account details.Admin assigns or updates:GroupRoleAdmin activates/deactivates admin accounts.Admin views full admin user details.Admin user can reset password using Forgot Password.System logs all admin account actions. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Delete Group with Assigned UsersAdmin attempts to delete a group.System checks assigned users.If users exist → deletion blocked with message.A2 – Delete Role Assigned to UsersAdmin tries to delete a role.System prevents deletion and prompts reassignment.A3 – Assign Inactive Group or RoleAdmin attempts assignment.System blocks assignment and displays validation message.A4 – Deactivate Admin UserAdmin deactivates a user.User is immediately logged out and blocked from login.A5 - Password ResetAdmin user selects “Forgot Password”.System validates email and sends reset link. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Admin users, roles, and groups are updated as per actions.Access permissions are enforced immediately.Inactive users/groups/roles are restricted.Audit trail is recorded for all changes. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Duplicate Role or Group NameSystem rejects creation.Error message displayed.EX2 – Unauthorized AccessUser lacks permission.System blocks action and logs incident.EX3 – System Failure During SaveChanges are rolled back.Error message shown to admin.EX4 – Invalid Email or MobileValidation error shown.Admin must correct details. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Super Admin has full access across modules.Permission mapping follows a bottom-up approach.Group and role assignment affects access immediately.Email service is configured for password reset.Deletion restrictions are mandatory for security. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | This module forms the core security and access control layer.Audit logs support compliance and internal audits.Group + Role combination provides flexible permission control.Future enhancements may include:MFA for admin loginRole cloningTemporary access roles |  |  |

Admin – Manage Master Attributes

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-014 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case enables the Admin to centrally manage all master attributes required across the platform. This includes Items, Item Categories & Sub-Categories, Vehicle Hierarchy (Category → Type → Make → Model → Variant), Manufacture Origin, Damage Category & Sub-Category, and User Speciality.All master attributes are owned and controlled by Admin, ensuring data consistency across Inventory, Claims, RFQs, Auctions, and Hot Deals. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Admin is authenticated and logged into the platform.Admin has Master Data management permission.Attribute dependency hierarchy is configured (Vehicle → Make → Model → Variant).System supports audit logging. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to Master Attributes from the Admin Dashboard |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Manage Master ItemsAdmin views Item Listing with:Item NameSKU / CodeCategory & Sub-CategoryStatusAdmin searches and filters items.Admin views full item details.Admin activates/deactivates items.Inactive items are hidden from:InventoryClaimsRFQsAuctionsHot DealsAdmin exports item list.System logs all actions.Manage Item Categories & Sub-CategoriesAdmin views category and sub-category listings.Admin adds, edits, activates/deactivates categories.Admin deletes categories only if:No active items are linked.Inactive categories are hidden from creation forms.System maintains audit logs.Manage Vehicle Master Attributes(Category → Type → Make → Model → Variant)Admin manages:Vehicle CategoryVehicle TypeVehicle MakeVehicle ModelVehicle VariantAdmin can:AddEditActivate / DeactivateView detailsAdmin deletes attributes only if:Not linked to active inventory, RFQs, claims, auctions, or hot deals.Inactive vehicle attributes are hidden from inventory creation.All changes are logged.Manage Manufacture OriginAdmin views and manages manufacture origins.Admin links origin with vehicle hierarchy.Inactive origins are excluded from vehicle creation.Deletion is restricted if used.Audit trail is maintained.Manage Damage Categories & Sub-CategoriesAdmin manages damage categories and sub-categories.Admin controls activation/deactivation.Inactive damage attributes are hidden from claim flows.Deletion restricted if referenced.System logs all actions.Manage SpecialityAdmin manages user specialities.Admin assigns or removes speciality from users.Speciality updates reflect immediately in system workflows.User-Initiated Attribute RequestInsurance / Garage / Supplier requests a missing item/attribute.System creates a pending request.Admin reviews request.Admin approves or rejects.Approved attributes become created and activated.Action is logged. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Delete Attribute in UseAdmin attempts deletion.System blocks action and displays dependency message.A2 – Deactivate AttributeAttribute becomes unavailable in creation forms.Existing references remain intact.A3 – Duplicate Attribute CodeSystem prevents save.Validation error shown.A4 – User Attribute Request RejectedRequest marked as rejected.User notified with reason. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Master attributes are updated centrally.Attribute visibility reflects status immediately.Dependency integrity is preserved.Approved attributes are available platform-wide.Complete audit trail is maintained. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Unauthorized ActionAdmin lacks permission.System blocks action and logs attempt.EX2 – System Error During SaveTransaction rolled back.Error notification shown. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Only Admin can manage master attributes.Deactivation is preferred over deletion.Attribute hierarchy is mandatory.User-raised requests do not auto-approve.Audit trail is mandatory for compliance. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | This module is the foundation of all transactional workflows.Attribute integrity directly impacts:ClaimsRFQsAuctionsInventoryHot Deals |  |  |

Admin – Manage Order

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-015 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case allows the Admin to centrally view, monitor, and audit all orders placed across the platform between Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users, including Auction Orders and Hot Deal Orders. The Admin has full visibility into order lifecycle, payment status, fulfillment progress, replacements, and refunds for governance and dispute handling. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Admin is logged into the platform.Admin has Order Management permission.Orders exist in the system.Payment and shipment statuses are already updated by respective workflows.Audit logging is enabled. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to Orders from the Admin Dashboard |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Order Listing & ClassificationAdmin views a consolidated Order Listing.System displays orders with classification by type:Insurance → SupplierGarage → SupplierInsurance → GarageAuction OrdersHot Deal OrdersEach order record shows high-level details (Order ID, Buyer, Seller, Order Type, Date, Status).Search & Filter OrdersAdmin searches orders using standard search parameters.Admin applies filters (order type, status, date range, buyer, seller, etc.).System refreshes the listing based on applied criteria.View Order DetailsAdmin selects an order.System displays full order details including:Buyer informationSeller informationItem details (name, SKU, quantity, unit price)Shipping informationBilling informationPayment method & payment statusTaxes, discounts, and final amountOrder creation and fulfillment datesOrder Activity TimelineAdmin views the Order Activity Timeline, showing:Order PlacedPayment ReceivedOrder ProcessedDispatchedDeliveredReplacement Requested / Approved / CompletedRefund Initiated / Completed (if applicable)Export OrdersAdmin exports:Order listingIndividual order detailsSystem generates Excel/CSV files respecting applied filters.Audit & TraceabilitySystem logs all admin views and exports for audit compliance. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Order with Replacement or RefundOrder contains replacement or refund requests.Admin can view:Reason for replacement/refundApproval statusAssociated documentsTimeline reflects additional steps.A2 – Auction or Hot Deal OrderOrder type is Auction or Hot Deal.System displays:Auction ID / Hot Deal IDWinning bid price or deal priceAuction/Deal validity timelineA3 – Partial FulfillmentOrder contains multiple items.Some items are delivered while others are pending.Timeline reflects item-level fulfillment. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Admin gains complete visibility into order lifecycle.Order data remains unchanged (read-only).Exported files are generated successfully.Audit logs capture admin access and actions. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Order Data Not FoundAdmin opens an invalid or deleted order.System displays error message and redirects to listing.EX2 – Export FailureSystem fails to generate export file.Error message shown and logged.EX3 – Unauthorized AccessAdmin lacks permission.System blocks access and logs the attempt. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Admin has read-only access to orders.Order modification is handled only via transactional workflows.Replacement and refund approvals are managed in respective modules.Order timeline is system-generated and immutable.Export functionality respects data access rules. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | This module is critical for:Dispute resolutionCompliance & auditPerformance monitoringRecommended future enhancements:Order-level annotations/comments for AdminSLA breach indicatorsAdvanced analytics dashboard (order value, delays, disputes) |  |  |

Admin – Manage Payments

| Use Case ID: | PH-UC-ADM-016 |  |  |
|---|---|---|---|
| Description | This use case enables the Admin to centrally view, monitor, search, filter, and export all payment and refund transactions occurring across the platform between Insurance, Garage, and Supplier users. The Admin can differentiate payments based on purpose (orders, subscriptions, auctions, hot deals, etc.) and track refunds for audit, compliance, and dispute resolution. |  |  |
| Creator: | Anubhab Ganguly | Date Created: | 17/12/2025 |
| Actors: | Platform Administrator |  |  |
| Preconditions: | Admin is authenticated and logged into the platform.Admin has Payment Management access rights.Payments and refunds exist in the system.Payment gateway integration is active.Audit logging is enabled. |  |  |
| Triggers: | Admin navigates to Payments from the Admin Dashboard |  |  |
| Basic Workflow: | Payment ListingAdmin views a consolidated Payment Listing.System displays all payments made across the platform.Each payment record displays:Transaction IDPayer (Insurance / Garage / Supplier)PayeePayment AmountPayment DatePayment StatusPayment Type DifferentiationSystem categorizes each payment by purpose, such as:Order PaymentsAuction PaymentsHot Deal PaymentsSubscription PaymentsClaim-related PaymentsAdmin can clearly identify why a payment was made.View Payment DetailsAdmin selects a payment record.System displays full payment details including:Transaction IDOrder Reference IDInvoice Number(s)Payment Gateway Reference NumberPayment MethodPayer and Payee detailsTaxes, discounts, and final amountPayment status and timestampsSearch & Filter PaymentsAdmin searches payments using standard search parameters.Admin applies filters such as:Date rangePayment statusPayment typePayer / PayeeSystem refreshes listing accordingly.Refund ListingAdmin navigates to Refund Listing.System displays all refund transactions with:Refund IDOriginal Payment IDOrder IDRefund DateRefund AmountRefund ReasonRefund StatusExport Payments & RefundsAdmin exports:Payment listingPayment detailsRefund listingSystem generates CSV/Excel files based on applied filters.Audit LoggingSystem logs all admin access, views, and export actions. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | A1 – Partial RefundRefund is issued for part of the payment.System displays refunded amount and remaining balance.A2 – Failed or Reversed PaymentPayment status shows Failed / Reversed.Admin can view gateway failure reason and timestamps |  |  |
| Postconditions: | Admin gains complete visibility into all payment and refund transactions.No payment data is modified (read-only).Exported files are successfully generated.Audit logs capture admin activity. |  |  |
| Exceptional Flow: | EX1 – Export FailureExport process fails.System displays error message and logs incident.EX2 – Unauthorized AccessAdmin without permission attempts access.System blocks action and logs attempt. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | Admin has read-only access to payment data.Payment initiation and refunds are managed by transactional workflows.Payment gateway is the single source of truth for transaction status.Refunds follow configured refund policies and tenure.Currency and tax calculations are handled at transaction time. |  |  |
| Notes & Comments | This module is critical for:Financial auditsRegulatory complianceDispute and reconciliationFuture enhancements may include:Payment reconciliation reportsSettlement cycle trackingAutomated anomaly detection |  |  |
